entries.name: Consumable
entries: A reward that is permanently expended upon use.

entries.name: Cursed
entries: This reward can be removed with {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB}, or has another detrimental effect.

entries.name: Exotic
entries: A reward that can only be found or received under certain circumstances.

entries.name: Unique
entries: A reward where a very limited number of instances exist.

entries.name: Material
entries: Material is an Item Property tag. An item can have only one Material property at a time. The Material property represents the material the item is primarily composed of.

entries.name: Mutation
entries: Mutations are talents or perks that have physical changes to the body. They are removed if the mutated part is removed, replaced, or changed. Spells such as Alter Creature can remove mutations.

entries.name: Nonmagical
entries: Rewards tagged nonmagical function in an {@spell Antimagic Field|XPHB}. For a reward to be tagged nonmagical, it cannot have any Magic actions.

entries.name: Renown
entries: Rewards with the Renown tag can only be gained through gaining Renown with a faction. Renown tags can specify the group they are associated with in parenthesis after the tag.

baseitem.name: ![Bingus][image1]
entries: {@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Anarchic Water
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Anarchic Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take {@dice 2d8} damage if it is a Construct. Roll a {@dice d8} for the damage type: 1. Acid; 2. Cold; 3. Fire; 4. Force; 5. Lightning; 6. Poison; 7. Psychic; 8. Thunder.
{@i Made by Aris}

baseitem.name: Axe
entries: Working for 10 minutes, you can use an Axe to cut down a tree less than 5ft in diameter. Working for 1 hour, you can use an Axe to dig a hole that is 5 feet on each side in wood or similar material.
{@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Axiomatic Water
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Axiomatic Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take {@dice 2d8} Force damage if it is an Aberration or an Elemental.
{@i Made by Aris}

baseitem.name: Bathaerel
entries: Also known as "rustbanish", Bathaerel is a purplish powder or gravel made of certain rocks from the Abyssal layers of Rauwend and Voorz'zt. When the rock is agitated in a bag with rusty metal items or mixed into a paste with water, crushed raw potato or grist, and certain green-shoot plants, the resulting mixture stops rust and removes it, leaving behind clean metal. However, it doesn't replace metal already lost to rust.

One pound of this paste can coat one item. Applying the paste takes 1 minute. All rust on the item is removed, and for one hour, the item cannot rust, including from rusting effects like Rust Monsters produce.
{@i From S3 & Dragon Magazine #421 in an article by Ed Greenwood; Ported by Aris}

baseitem.name: Boomerang
entries: {@i {@dice 1d6} Bludgeoning}
{@i Finesse, Thrown (Range 30/90)}
{@i Mastery: Slow}
A Boomerang returns to the wielder's hand immediately after it is used to make a ranged attack.
{@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Bubble Weed
entries: While you hold this Bubble Weed when you’re submerged in water, it can supply you with breathable air for 1 minute before withering.
{@i From Rain World}
{@i Made by Chrys}

baseitem.name: Cherrybomb
entries: As an action, you can throw the Cherrybomb at a point within 20 feet. The Cherrybomb emits a loud popping sound, and each creature within a 5-foot Emanation of the Cherrybomb must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or be Deafened until the end of their next turn.
{@i From Rain World}
{@i Made by Chrys}

baseitem.name: Cow Tools
entries: {@b Ability:} Constitution
{@b Utilize:} Moo (DC 20)
{@b Craft:} Milk
{@i Made by Denny}

baseitem.name: Crab Suit
entries: {@i To create a cheaper, more easily produced version of the popular Fish Suit, gnomish inventor Nadul DaRoni created the Crab Suit, a suit of armor made to handle deep water pressure.}

This bulky suit, which fully encases your head and body, takes 10 minutes to don or doff. While worn, it enables you to breathe in an airless environment and renders you immune to the harmful effects of any gas that surrounds you for up to 8 hours.
Once worn for 8 total hours, the suit's property is unusable unless the suit is recharged for 1 hour in an elemental air node (or dedicated Crab Suit Recharger).
The Crab Suit allows the user to withstand depth pressures of up to 750m, with more resistant models able to withstand even greater pressure.
{@i Made by Patrick}

baseitem.name: Darkvision Powder
entries: This plain gray powder clings to surfaces and cannot be seen with normal vision from more than 10 feet away. It glows brightly when viewed with darkvision, and thus is useful for creatures that have darkvision and wish to write messages that other creatures can’t read. Each vial contains enough powder for a message of up to 25 words. By adding a mixture of spider silk oil, an ink can be created with the same properties as the powder.
{@i This item can be crafted using Alchemy Tools.}
{@i From S3, 3.5e Drow of the Underdark, ported by Aris}

baseitem.name: Diving Mask
entries: Comprised of an airtight mask, goggles, and an air tank, the Diving Mask was made as a portable solution to air deprivation and light underwater travel.

The Diving Mask allows the user to connect to an air tank, expanding their breathing capacity and prolonging suffocation. A full air tank connected to a diving mask provides 6 hours' worth of breathable air.
{@i Made by Patrick}

baseitem.name: Droth
entries: Also called "demon's blood", Droth is a black, sticky substance made from the blood of Manes and the boiled down chitin of Dhergoloths. When smeared on the eyes, it cures certain sorts of blindness in some individuals, and when ingested (its taste is generally described as saldy and horrible), it can help to cure certain contagions. When smeared on acid-drenched items or living creatures, it cuts the corrosion done by the acid in half. Ingested or smeared, droth stops all green slime effects instantly, and turns back existing slime into the living material it was before being slimed.

Ingesting or smearing Droth takes a Utilize action. When you smear Droth on your eyes, you gain Advantage on saving throws to end the Blinded condition for 1 hour. When you ingest Droth, you have Advantage on saving throws made to end the effects of a Magical Contagion for 1 hour.

Droth can be applied to a creature or object with acid on it as a Utilize action. The effect causing the acid will deal half damage until it is removed. In addition, if a patch of green slime touches an object or creature with Droth applied, or Droth is applied to a patch of green slime, the slime effect ends instantly, and it reverts to the living material it was before being slimed.
{@i From S3 & Dragon Magazine #421 in an article by Ed Greenwood, ported by Aris}

baseitem.name: Faeorran Wine (Bottle)
entries: {@i This wine is made from Sweet Faeorra, a rare grape-like fruit found in the feywild. The wine is a deep red, very sweet, and very high in alcohol content.}
A creature that drinks an excessive amount of this wine must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it becomes Poisoned for 1 minute.

Whenever it ends its turn while Poisoned in this way it moves 10 feet in a random movement. Roll {@dice 1d4} for the direction: 1, north; 2, east; 3, south; or 4, west. If it collides with an object or creature within this movement, it falls Prone. This movement provokes Opportunity Attacks.
{@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Gummy Apple
entries: Mmmmmm Yummy appl. Eating this is food. Yumm
{@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Iconograph
entries: This curious box allows you to make instantaneous paintings. Point the small glass lens at the desired subject, press the button on the top, and a small, incredibly accurate painting is ejected a few moments later.

The box contains a set of painting supplies and a very, very short imp who produces the paintings. The imp is focused on hyperrealism, and can only paint what they see. Painting supplies and safe working conditions must be regularly provided to the imp, lest they submit their two tenday notice and return to the Hells.
{@i Repassing from S3, by Ryan Gabrillo}

baseitem.name: Messenger Pigeon
entries: {@i Made by Patrick}

baseitem.name: Moonhoney
entries: The substance with the unlikely name of moonhoney is actually the dung of ground-worms that dwell on many Abyssal layers (and which are eaten by many demons). It is a smokey-tasting, nourishing, even rib-sticking treat to most humanoids. Its name comes from its consistency and appearance (both of which are rather like the honey produced by wild bees), and the fact that when bathed in moonlight, it momentarily acquires a rough, fleeting sweetness. Moonhoney doesn't spoil unless it is scorched in open flame or soaked in citrus juices, and so it is an ideal trail food for wayfarers of all kinds, who can readily carve into handy chunks. It is prized for its property of neutralizing almost all known poisons active within those who eat it.
Consuming a chunk of moonhoney rids you of the poisoned condition.
{@i From S3 & Dragon Magazine #421 in an article by Ed Greenwood, Ported by Aris}

baseitem.name: Paincone
entries: As an action, you can throw the Paincone at a point you can see within 30 feet. Each creature within a 5 foot-Emanation of the Paincone must make a DC 11 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is Restrained until the end of their next turn. On a success, the creature’s Speed is halved until the end of its next turn.
{@i From Rain World}
{@i Made by Chrys}

baseitem.name: Paxel
entries: The Paxel is a tool that combines the utility of a shovel, an axe, and a pickaxe. Working for 1 hour, you can use the Paxel to displace a 5ft Cube of soil, stone, or wood.
{@i From WM3, Made by Jarod}

baseitem.name: Pickaxe
entries: Working for 1 hour, you can use a Pickaxe to mine a hole that is 5 feet on each side in soft rock, or 8 hours for hard rock. Using a Pickaxe gives you Advantage on Strength checks to remove gems and minerals from stone.
{@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Rabbit
entries: {@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Raceless Stone
entries: I didn't saved the life of anybody, a stone with the symbol of nothing carved into it. The stone is an exotic mundane item that doesn't requires attunement, and I can't attune to it.

As an action, I can use the stone to not summon the spirit of an orc, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit  doesn't use an stat block and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit doesn't understand any language I speak and doesn't obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Inspired by Orc Stone, Made by Denny}

baseitem.name: Replica Ruinstone
entries: A fiery red crystal the size of a human palm.
Gemstones are small, lightweight, and easily secured compared to their same value in coins.
{@i Made by Chrys}

baseitem.name: Sheet Music
entries: {@i This piece of parchment teaches the reader how to play a specific written piece of music, so long as the user knows the notation.}

When you attempt to play the song written on this piece of sheet music and you are Proficient with the instrument you are playing, any roll of 4 or lower on the {@dice D20} roll is treated as a 5 instead. Any given piece of sheet music must depict a specific song, and its benefits only apply to playing the given song.
This item may be copied and printed at a scriptorium.
{@i Made by Patrick}

baseitem.name: Shocking Filament
entries: You can take 1 minute to apply this filament to a melee weapon within 5 feet of you. After the filament is applied, the next time you score a Critical Hit with this weapon, the hit deals an additional {@dice 1d8} Lightning damage. A weapon can only have one filament applied to it at a time.
{@i Made by Chrys}

baseitem.name: Spore Puff
entries: As an action, you can throw this Spore Puff at a point within 30 feet of you. Each creature within a 15-foot Emanation of the Spore Puff must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or be Poisoned until the end of its next turn. Arthropods are particularly susceptible to Spore Puffs, and take {@dice 3d6} Poison damage on a failed save.
{@i From Rain World}
{@i Made by Chrys}

baseitem.name: Stiletto-heeled Boots
entries: These boots can be wielded as a dagger while on your feet. However, they have a -2 to hit, unless the boot is wielded in a hand, you are airborne, or the attack is made against a Prone creature.
{@i Made by Aris}

baseitem.name: Stone Stone
entries: It's a stone.
{@i Made by Tyler}

baseitem.name: Swim Clothes
entries: These clothes are treated to be water resistant, quick drying, stretchy, and low absorption, great for swimming in!
{@i Made by Landon}

baseitem.name: Teeth Bomb
entries: As an Utilize action, you can light a Bomb and throw it at a point up to 60 feet away, where it explodes. Each creature in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point makes a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 6d6} Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
{@i Made by Marvin}

baseitem.name: Thinker's Tools
entries: {@b Ability:} Intelligence
{@b Utilize:} Ponder (DC 20)
{@b Craft:} Book (Fiction)
{@i Made by June}

baseitem.name: Traveler's Dust
entries: Traveler's dust is transported and sold in the form of tiny roseate crystals, each a bit bigger than a grain of salt. Traveler's dust is used by dropping a single grain of the drug onto the eye and letting it dissolve.

When you dissolve a crystal in your eye, your vision becomes hazy with red, and you see a breathtaking vista lining a winding, Crimson Road. This vision is from your astral body, as your soul makes a journey.

While your astral form strides upon the Crimson Road (you are compelled to, and cannot resist the urge), all sorrows sink beyond recall, while all joys are raised like blazing stars. You feel alive, powerful, changed.

One trip of the drug lasts an hour, though more consumption can continually prolong the experience. At the end of every hour while under the effects of this drug, make a DC 20 Charisma saving throw. Once you reach 5 failures from this drug, your astral body crosses the precipice of the tooth-lined pit at the end of the Crimson Road. The soul falls into the Abyss, where your mind and soul are devoured by awaiting demons. You die, and your soul is destroyed. You can only be revived by a True Resurrection or Wish spell.
While travelling, you are only vaguely aware of your actual surroundings. Your hands tremble, your speech is slurred, and your eyes are the color of blood.
Resuming the drug at any point after a trip causes your astral body to resume from where it left off.

The drug is incredibly addictive, and if you are deprived of the substance for more than eight hours after having experienced the drug, you become violent, first towards others around you, and then to yourself.
GMs should not give this drug out lightly, and should be careful that the player is okay with receiving the drug, and that portrayals of the experience are sufficiently content warned.
{@i From S3; By Aris}

baseitem.name: Unholy Water
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing a flask of Unholy Water. Target one creature you can see within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or take {@dice 2d8} Necrotic damage if it is a Celestial or a Plant.
{@i Made by Aris}

baseitem.name: Urchin Venom
entries: A creature subjected to Urchin Venom must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition for 1 minute.
{@i Made by Aris, Adapted from 3.5e There Is No Honor}

baseitem.name: Vrock Dust
entries: This powder made from the dried spores of a vrock provides a potent, pleasurable high when inhaled. A creature that consumes this drug must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature is Poisoned for the next 10 minutes. While Poisoned in this way, the creature is Vulnerable to Radiant damage and automatically fails any saving throw inflicted by a Celestial or Fiend.
A creature addicted to Vrock Dust experiences potent cravings for more Vrock Dust, and will often do anything it can to obtain additional doses of the drug.
{@i Credit to FungusAmongUs's Abi-Dalzim’s Abhorrent Adventurers}
{@i Repassed from WM3}

baseitem.name: Juggling Club
entries: {@i Made by June}

item.name: +1 Tool
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to ability checks made using this tool.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Abjurer's Necklace
entries: This enchanted amulet is popular among the wizards of Waterdeep. You can use the amulet to cast the shield spell. Once cast, the amulet can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i From S3 and Adapted from Warriors of Waterdeep by Aris}

item.name: Adamantine Axe
entries: An Adamantine Axe is twice as efficient as a regular axe, being able to cut down trees up to 10ft in diameter in 10 minutes, and clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Adamantine Paxel
entries: An Adamantine Paxel is twice as efficient as a regular Paxel, clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Adamantine Pickaxe
entries: An Adamantine Pickaxe is twice as efficient as a regular pickaxe, clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Adamantine Shovel
entries: An Adamantine Shovel is twice as efficient as a regular shovel, clearing out twice the amount of material in the same amount of time
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Amulet of Perceived Health
entries: While wearing this amulet, creatures are unable to tell if you are Bloodied.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Appraiser's Glass
entries: The Appraiser's Glass functions as a Magnifying Glass. As a Magic action while holding the glass, you can spend cast Approximate.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Barbecue Hammers
entries: Made with love... and {@i hammers}.
As a Utilize Action, you can eat it.  When you consume the hammers, you must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, you take {@dice 2d4} Bludgeoning damage.
The hammers provide as much nourishment as 1 day of Rations
{@i Made by Marvin}

item.name: Beast Mode Indicator Vestments
entries: This shirt reads “Beast Mode: Off”. As you enter a temporarily heightened state, such as a Rage or a physical transformation, you may choose to rip this shirt off to reveal another shirt underneath that reads “Beast Mode: On”. Once this state ends, the destroyed shirt reappears on you, undamaged.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Bingufier
entries: ![Bingus][image3]

This machine is large enough for Medium and smaller creatures to pass through it. Any creature that willingly passes through it, becomes bald (losing all hair on their body) and loses the ability to grow hair. This can only be undone with Wish, or by the Debingufier.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Boots of a Zephyr
entries: {@i +1 Aura. These babies have NO mechanical benefit.}
While you wear these boots, you can take a Magic action to appear to float 4 inches above a surface. This effect lasts until you take another Magic action to end it.
{@i Made by Althaea}

item.name: Boots of Orcish Kind
entries: While you wear these boots, your steps make a bunch of sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Boots of Stomping
entries: While you wear these boots, you can pass through the space of Prone creatures as if they were Incapacitated. When you pass through the space of a Prone creature, you can choose to deal {@dice 1d4} Bludgeoning damage to it. A creature takes this damage no more than once per turn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Bottle of Boundless Kaeth
entries: This metal bottle carries delicious, warm kaeth. The bottle comes with a stopper, which is attached to the bottle by a little chain. Even when open, the bottle won't accept any liquid other than the kaeth it produces. The kaeth inside is always comfortably warm, and none of the heat can be felt through the bottle.

Each time you drink the kaeth, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, the bottle refuses to dispense kaeth for the next hour. If you pour kaeth from the bottle, rather than drinking from it, the kaeth vanishes the moment it leaves the bottle.
{@i From Strixhaven: A Curriculum of Chaos}

item.name: Cheese With Nothin’
entries: {@i “Nothin’?”}
Sometimes erroneously referred to as a simple stick of cheese, this pizza with extra cheese has no dough or sauce. Despite being a very simple pizza, it serves as a day’s rations for up to 8 people.
{@i Made by Miel}

item.name: Cinnabar Eye Cusps
entries: These are pink lenses of polarized crystal that fit comfortably over your eyes. When worn in an area with an intense concentration of faerzress, the eye cusps enhance your vision. If you lack darkvision, you gain darkvision out to 120 feet. If you have darkvision, your darkvision extends through the entire faerzress-infused area.
{@i From 3.5e Drow of the Underdark, ported by Aris}

item.name: Coin of Delving
entries: This scintillating copper coin sheds dim light in a 5-foot Emanation. If dropped a distance greater than 5 feet, the coin issues a melodious ringing sound when it hits a surface. Any creature that can hear the chime can determine the distance the coin dropped based on the tone.
{@i From Explorer’s Guide to Wildemount}

item.name: Commoner Stone
entries: I saved the life of a commoner, who gave me a stone with a name carved into it. The stone is a Common magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of a Commoner, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Commoner stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands Common and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Cowboy Beer
entries: When drunk, make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, you become Poisoned for an hour. While Poisoned in this way, you do not have Disadvantage on weapon attacks made with pistols, muskets, or whips, and instead of Disadvantage on Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks involving horses, you have Advantage. You additionally automatically pass any checks to not fall off a creature you are mounting.
{@i Repassed from S3, Made by June}

item.name: Cradle of the Bingus Scion
entries: This box has 15 AC, 10 Hitpoints, and Immunity to Psychic and Poison damage. When destroyed, it releases ![Bingus][image1].
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Dazzle Cake
entries: When you consume this cake as a Magic action, sparkles and iridescent light coats your figure for 10 minutes. For the duration, whenever you engage in conversation with a creature, that creature has a -5 penalty to Wisdom (Perception) checks and Passive Perception.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Deck of Dusk
entries: {@i Created in the image of the Deck of Some Things, this deck of ivory or vellum cards bestows an assortment of minor benefits and penalties on those who draw from it. Most (75 percent) of these decks have only thirteen cards, but the rest have twenty-two.}

{@i Before you draw a card, you must declare how many cards you intend to draw, then draw them randomly. Any additional cards drawn have no effect. Unless a card states otherwise, as soon as you draw a card from the deck, its magic takes effect. You must draw each card you declared no more than 1 hour after the previous draw. If you fail to draw the chosen number, the remaining number of cards fly from the deck and take effect simultaneously.}
{@i Unless it is the Mystery card, a drawn card immediately takes effect, fades from existence, and reappears in the deck, making it possible to draw the same card multiple times.}
A ♦              | Dusk{@i
K ♦              | Dusk
Q ♦              | Dusk
J ♦              | Dusk
2 ♦              | Dusk{@i
A ♥              | Dusk{@i

Q ♥              | Dusk
J ♥              | Dusk
2 ♥              | Dusk{@i
A ♣              | Dusk{@i
K ♣              | Dusk
Q ♣              | Dusk
J ♣              | Dusk
2 ♣              | Dusk{@i
A ♠              | Dusk{@i
K ♠              | Dusk
Q ♠              | Dusk
J ♠              | Dusk
2 ♠              | Dusk{@i
Joker (with™)    | Dusk{@i
Joker (without™) | Dusk

{@b Dusk.} This card supernaturally saps your energy. You have Disadvantage on Initiative rolls. This effect lasts until you finish a Long Rest, but it can be ended early by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Debingufier
entries: ![][image4]

This machine is large enough for Medium and smaller creatures to pass through it. Any creature that willingly passes through it, becomes covered in hair and gains the ability to grow hair. If a creature is under the effects of a Bingufier, this machine instead undoes the changes.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Destination Orb
entries: Destination Orbs are engraved with the sigil sequence of a Teleportation Circle. A creature who holds it immediately learns the sequence. Whenever a creature holding the orb casts Teleportation Circle targeting the sigil sequence engraved in the orb, it can choose to crush the orb instead of expending a spell slot.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Distilled Effervescence
entries: When you drink this potion, you are Effervescent for the next 10 minutes. Additionally, while Effervescent, every creature that starts their turn within 30 feet of you knows that you are Effervescent.
{@i From WM3, Made by Percy}

item.name: Doublet of Dramatic Demise
entries: This striking satin jacket allows you to make the most of your dying moments. When you are Unconscious with 0 Hit Points while wearing this doublet, you can speak and communicate.
You make Death Saving Throws as normal, but if you roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, you swoon with a loud gasp and die instantly.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Duck Foot
entries: When you drink this brackish-smelling potion, your hands and feet begin to tingle. Within seconds, they turn a bright orange and become webbed like a mallard's feet. For the next hour, you have a Swim Speed of 40 feet and are able to make a very convincing quacking sound.
{@i From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass}

item.name: Ek-Sel Slate
entries: A small clay tablet. There are rows intersecting columns, with cuneiform written in some of the boxes. It is a tabular account of livestock and feed.

When holding this tablet, you feel the intelligence and logic of the long-lost denizens of Ek-Sel flow through you, and you gain advantage on checks towards solving mathematical problems and equations.
{@i Made by Sam}

item.name: Elf Stone
entries: I saved the life of an elf, who gave me a stone with the symbol of the Evermeet island carved into it. The stone is a common magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As an action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of an elf, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Wood Elf or Drow Commoner (chosen when you receive this stone) stat block and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Everburning Torch
entries: When this torch is ignited, it cannot be extinguished unless a creature within 5 feet of the torch uses a Magic action to do so.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Fish Lighter
entries: This Tiny automatic torch is shaped like a fish, and is fitted with extremely bright magical lights. A creature holding this item can use a Utilize action to make it start or stop shedding Bright Light for 40 feet and Dim Light for 40 feet further. While it is shedding light, it also emits an extremely loud noise of a screaming human, which can be heard from up to 300 feet away.
Any creature occupying the same space as this object while it is shedding light is Blinded and Deafened until it is either no longer within 5 feet or the torch is deactivated.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Forti-brest
entries: When you consume this paris-brest as a Magic action, the residual goodness baked into its bread protects you from evil. For the next minute, Fiends and Undead have Disadvantage on attack rolls made against you.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Fragile Weapon
entries: {@i This weapon is barely able to hold itself together, falling apart if the wrong person holds it. Pretty much every weapon in the world is one of these.}
Whenever a creature wielding one of these dies, the weapon immediately breaks apart and turns to dust.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Frostine Weapon
entries: {@i This weapon is made from Frostine crystals, a cream colored gemstone that is perpetually cold.}
This weapon is cold enough to keep the temperature within 5 feet of it at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (0 degrees Celsius). It deals Cold damage instead of its normal type.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Gambling Coin
entries: As a Bonus Action you cam flip this coin in the air. The next attack roll with a weapon you make before the start of your next turn deals an additional {@dice 3d6} damage if it scores a Critical Hit. If you roll a 1 on the {@dice d20}, the attack instead hits you and deals an additional {@dice 3d6} damage.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Gizmo!
entries: {@i A strange assortment of magic runes and metal latticework. It's been given a haphazard shell and a safety seal of approval.}
While holding a Gizmo!, you can use it to cast either Prestidigitation or Thaumaturgy. After doing so, it falls apart.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Glasses of Transcription
entries: These boxy reading glasses have a black stripe along the bottom of each lens. When worn, they attempt to caption speech and noises relevant to the wearer, causing letters to appear in the black box. Speech is written in the language it is spoken in, while sound effects are captioned in a language familiar to the reader. Muffled speech, overlapping noises, and other forms of interference may impair the Glasses' ability to subtitle the world.

item.name: Heroic Costume
entries: {@i With the popularity of Liar's Night continuing to grow across Toril, people have begun to create a variety of magical costumes. This costume was made to imbue confidence in the wearer, depicting a famous adventurer or fantastical hero.}

While wearing this costume, you can use a Magic action to cast Heroism. When cast in this way, it has a range of Self and does not grant Temporary Hit Points. Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the costume again until the next sunset.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Hog of Cranking
entries: {@i Bear Witness!}

As a Magic action, you may begin cranking your hog. While cranking your hog, you cannot take reactions, as you are fully focused on cranking your hog. At the beginning of your next turn after beginning to crank your hog, 1 Boar appears in the space where the Hog of Cranking was (or within 5 ft. of that space if the space it occupied is currently occupied by another creature). You stop cranking your hog when the Boar is summoned, upon which the hog becomes non-magical. This boar is friendly to you and your allies and hostile to any creatures you may be hostile towards when you begin to crank your hog. The boar continues to fight said hostile creatures until it or the hostile creatures die or are knocked Unconscious. If no more hostile creatures remain, the boar acts as typical for a boar, and it no longer follows an obligation to be friendly towards you or your allies.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Intoxicating Aroma
entries: When consumed, you smell of an expertly crafted perfume. The effects of this potion last for 1 year.
{@i From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass}

item.name: Joy Éclair
entries: When you consume this Éclair as a Magic action, euphoria fills your mind. You have Advantage on Charisma (Persuasion and Performance) checks and Disadvantage on Charisma (Deception and Intimidation) checks for the next 10 minutes.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Kobold Wristbow
entries: Something about this cobbled-together, wrist-mounted, bow-like contraption looks wrong. It shouldn't hold together. There's nothing even holding the string in place. It must be magic. There's no writing on it but, if there was, you feel it'd be written as: "Kobbold RystBo."
{@b Handsfree.} The kobold wristbow does not require a free hand to hold or fire.

{@b Quickshot.} In response to your mental command, you can release the bowstring and make a ranged weapon attack with the loaded wristbow as a bonus action. You must reload a wristbow before firing again.
{@b Reload.} You can reload the kobold wristbow using either a bonus action or an action. This requires at least 1 free hand.
{@i From Heliana's Guide to Monster Hunting}

item.name: Lawn Mower
entries: As a Utilize action, you can pull the cord on the lawn mower. Make a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check. On a pass, the lawn mower starts.

While on, the blade on the bottom of the lawn mower starts spinning, cutting through all nonmagical grass, weeds, and other small plants it touches. If a creature starts it turns touching the blade of the lawn mower, it takes 1 Slashing damage. You can use a Utilize action to turn off the lawn mower.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Lesser Bag of Spider Holding
entries: This silk bag writhes, as if containing swarms of crawling creatures inside.

This woven silk bag designed for transporting Spiders, the official currency of Menzoberranzan. If you press it to an Object or part of your body as a Magic action, the bag stays attached there for up to 8 hours or until a creature uses a Magic action to remove it.

This bag can contain up to 10,024 Spiders. Spiders in the bag cannot escape unless you remove them. As a Magic action, you can reach into the bag and remove a spider silk pouch containing any number of spiders from your Bag of Spider Holding. The silk pouch lasts for up to 8 hours, or until a creature takes a Magic action to dissolve the silk.
Spiders in the Bag of Spider Holding do not require food, water, or sleep, and they cannot die while within the bag, and they do not attack one another.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Loud Buzzer
entries: As a Utilize action, this red button can be pushed to make a loud noise that can be heard clearly by all creatures within 60 feet and heard faintly by all creatures within 120 feet. Additionally it can be thrown as an action to a point you can see within 60 feet, making a noise as if it was pushed when it lands.
{@i Made by June, Repassed from WM3}

item.name: Lounge of Floating
entries: This hovering couch allows even the most injured of creatures to retain their mobility. While sitting, lying, reclining, or otherwise resting on this seat, you gain a 20 foot hovering speed from the lounge carrying you around. It cannot hover more than 5 feet above the ground or other solid surfaces, and if it exceeds this height, it plummets until it strikes a solid surface, dealing fall damage to the creature upon it. Only one creature of a size no greater than large can sit upon the lounge at a time. If more than one creature rests on the lounge, it sets itself on the ground as gently as it can. A single creature of up to tiny size, such as a pet cat or crab, can rest on the couch along with its primary occupant without causing it to cease hovering.
If more weight is placed on the Floating Lounge than the primary occupant's carrying capacity (not including the occupant themselves), it floats to the ground as gently as possible.
Paralysis caused by magic may, at the DM's discretion, also halt the lounge. Paralysis caused by physical injury, poison, or illness typically does not halt it.
The mental effort required to command the Floating Lounge is too great to permit resting while it hovers.
A surface held aloft by telekinesis, another creature, or most other magical means is not sufficiently solid for the lounge. (Please don't GMod physics this thing).

item.name: Mend-O
entries: {@i Developed on Mechanus, this vial of silvery liquid is able to repair anything it touches. Shaking the vial reveals it is full of many small flakes of metal.}

A vial of Mend-O can be applied to a tear in an object, or a damaged item. Applying Mend-O takes 1 minute, after which it magically seeps into whatever it is applied to, causing it to receive the benefits of a casting of Mending.

Alternatively, as a Bonus Action, you can drink it or administer it to another willing creature within 5 feet of yourself. If the creature that drinks the Mend-O is a Construct, it regains {@dice 2d4+2} Hit Points. If the creature is not a Construct, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 14 ({@dice 4d6}) Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Message in a Bottle
entries: When you cast the Message spell, you can choose to cast the spell into this bottle, provided it is empty and unsealed. When you speak the message, a scroll of paper with your message appears in the bottle, and a cork appears, sealing the bottle.

As a Utilize action, a creature can open this bottle while a message is contained within. That creature hears the stored message as if it was cast targeting them. Then, the message and cork disappear.
When found, there's a 75% chance a message is already stored in the bottle.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Mini-Mii
entries: It's a little construct of a person. As a Magic action, a Construct can install this Mini-Mii inside of them. While a Construct has one installed, they have the personality of the Mini-Mii. A Construct can eject a Mini-Mii as a Magic action. If a Construct has multiple installed, their behavior might become....complicated. When not installed, it has the statistics of a Tiny Servant, though retaining its personality.
{@i Made by Landon}

item.name: Modron Earpiece
entries: A bronze, clockwork earpiece with a rudimentary microphone attached.

When speaking Common into the audio receptor, the earpiece converts the Common speech into Modron, emitting a sequence of clicks and whirs. When Modron is spoken and can be heard by the earpiece, the earpiece translates Modron into Common, emitting what is said but in Common into the ears of the wearer.
Mechanically, Common speech of the wearer can be heard as Modron, and Modron spoken in the vicinity of the wearer can be heard as Common by the wearer.
{@i Made by Sam}

item.name: Mortedella
entries: A pork sausage flavored with bone fragments of an unknown origin, empowering it with necromantic energy. As a Magic action, you can consume the sausage, summoning a zombie within five feet of you. This zombie is friendly to you, and will follow your instructions to the best of its ability, taking its turn after yours.
{@i Made by Ryan}

item.name: Mystery Potion (Common)
entries: {@i Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.}
When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 10 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.
{@dice 1d4} | Potion Effect
1   | Potion of Climbing
2   | Potion of Comprehension
3   | Potion of Healing
4   | Potion of Watchful Rest
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Noble Weapon
entries: A weapon furnished with platinum and gold, giving it a regal presence. It shines brighter in the hands of those with great conviction.
{@b Glory Strike.} When you score a Critical Hit with this weapon, you deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage. This extra damage is not doubled by the Critical Hit.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Orb of Space
entries: While holding the orb, you can take a Magic action to determine whether you are on the Material Plane. This property functions only on the Material Plane.
{@i Made by Tyler}

item.name: Ornate Costume
entries: {@i With the popularity of Liar's Night continuing to grow across Toril, people have begun to create a variety of magical costumes. This costume was made to impress others, sewn with fancy techniques and wealth.}

While wearing this costume, you can use a Magic action to cast Charm Person (Save DC 10). When cast in this way, it requires Concentration and has a duration of 1 minute. Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the costume again until the next sunset.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Paint Cover
entries: As a Magic action you can place or remove this cover from a melee weapon you are holding. While the cover is on, the weapon deals no damage, instead leaving a visible colored mark onto the target. The mark fades after 8 hours, or until a creature spends 1 minute to clean it.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Paintball
entries: Attacks using this ammunition deal no damage, instead leaving a visible colored mark onto the target. The mark fades after 8 hours, or until a creature spends 1 minute to clean it.
This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of one hundred or five hundred pieces.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Paintball Gun
entries: The Paintball Gun magically produces Paintballs as ammunition. It is unable to fire any other ammunition.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Painter’s Sun Hat
entries: This well-made hat can come in any shape, size, or form and from casual inspection seems to be mundane in nature. However, when the hat is placed on the ground as a Magic action and its command word is spoken, it begins to float into the air. The hovering hat then produces an illusion of a humanoid of your choice that you are familiar with, with the caveat that the illusion is wearing the hat. The figure can't move and fails to hold up to physical inspection. The illusion remains until the hat is moved out of place, or until the command word is spoken once more, causing the hat to float back to the ground. If a creature uses the Study action to examine the illusion, a DC 10 Intelligence (Investigation) check reveals the figure is an illusion.
{@i From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass}

item.name: Paradise Plumage
entries: After drinking this potion, you are instantly adorned with striking and distinctive plumage. You can control it to create dazzling displays of shimmering feathers, iridescent colors, and stunning patterns. When you do so, you gain Advantage on all Charisma (Performance) checks made to distract or amaze. The effects of this potion last 1 hour.
{@i From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass}

item.name: Periapt of the Minor League Gamer
entries: Before you make an attack with a ranged weapon that you are proficient with, you can declare that you are doing a "360 No Scope." You roll your attack roll four times and take the lowest roll.

item.name: Pig Snout
entries: After drinking this pink pastel potion, there is a brief uncomfortable transformation, where the imbiber grows a beautiful, snuffling pig snout. For the next hour, you become very fond of truffles, perhaps even to the point of belligerence, and you have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.
{@i From Obojima: Tales from the Tall Grass. Made by Chrys}

item.name: Plushie of Scent
entries: A tiny, soft plushy that emits a particular scent.
This plushy emits a particular scent that can be perceived up to 10 feet away.  You can change the scent to a scent of your choice with a Magic action.
{@i Made by Marvin}

item.name: Plushie of Squeaking
entries: A tiny, soft, squeezable plushy.
When you squeeze this plushy as a Utilize action or a Bonus Action, it emits a squeaking sound that can be heard by creatures up to 10 feet away.
{@i Made by Marvin}

item.name: Plushie of Warmth
entries: A tiny, soft plushy that emits warmth.
The plushy generates enough dry heat to keep the temperature within 10 feet of it at 95 degrees Fahrenheit (35 degrees Celsius).
{@i Made by Marvin}

item.name: Pole of Angling and Collapsing
entries: While holding this 10-foot pole, you can speak a command word and transform it into a fishing pole with a hook, a line, and a reel. Speaking the command word again changes the fishing pole back into a normal 10-foot pole.

While holding this 10-foot pole, you can use an action to speak a command word and cause it to collapse into a 1-foot-long rod, for ease of storage. The pole's weight doesn't change. You can use an action to speak a different command word and cause the rod to revert to a pole; however, the rod will elongate only as far as the surrounding space allows.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Pot of Growing
entries: This 10-pound pot can grow any small nonmagical plant. It magically provides nutrients and water to the plant.

As a Magic action, you can touch the pot and cause a glass dome to appear over the plants growing in it. While the dome is up, the plant is treated as if it was planted in its proper environment, with the dome providing sunlight, temperature, humidity, and all other environmental factor control. You can remove the dome as a Magic action.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Potion of Air Breathing
entries: You can breathe above water for 24 hours after drinking this potion.
This potion smells and tastes like dirt.
{@i Made by Gavin}

item.name: Potion of Emotional Instability
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain a random condition from the table. This condition lasts for 1 minute.

When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with throwing this potion. Target one creature within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or gains the effects of drinking the potion. At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can make a DC 11 Constitution saving throw, ending the condition on a success. The potion is destroyed after throwing it.
{@dice 1d6}
1 | Blissful
2 | Confused
3 | Depressed
4 | Enraged
5 | Frightened
6 | Panicked
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Potion of Foreseen Doom
entries: When you drink this potion, you are able to foresee doom for a moment. You learn half a second before a creature dies that it is going to. This is not enough time to do anything in reaction to this knowledge. This ability lasts for 1 hour.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Potion of Tail Growth
entries: When you drink this potion, you grow a tail.  If you already have a tail, you grow another one.
You can only have as many tails equal to your Proficiency Bonus.
{@i Made by Marvin}

item.name: Potion of Tail Reduction
entries: When you drink this potion, you lose a tail.
Made by Marvin

item.name: Potion of Tremorsense
entries: When you drink this potion, you have Tremorsense out to 20 feet (unless it is already higher) for 10 minutes. You must be in contact with stone, mud, or dirt to use this Tremorsense.
This potion is a grey liquid with dark particles floating within it.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Pronoun Pin
entries: This round metal pin can be attached to or detached from any article of clothing as a Magic action. While the pin is worn, it displays the current pronouns of the wearer. Any creature that understands at least one language can read the pin.
Additionally, you can cast Detect Pronouns while wearing the pin. Once you have done so, you cannot cast it again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Psychic Lance
entries: This lance appears to be made out of shimmering glass. It leaves no physical wounds when it strikes, instead passing right through its targets. Despite this, any would-be wielders can hold it without issue.
{@b Ψ-Weapon.} This weapon deals Psychic damage instead of Piercing damage.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Punko Fop
entries: This odd little statuette represents a creature or person, decided when the item is given out. The proportions are entirely absurd, with the head larger than the entire rest of the body and unsettling blank eyes.
The statuette does not do anything noticable. Some rumors claim that it functions as "bitch repellant", but whether this is magic or mundane is unknown.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Quicktimer
entries: You can use this timepiece to take a Reaction when you have already taken a Reaction before the start of your next turn. The Reaction cannot be used to cast a spell. The quicktimer cannot be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Receiving Stone
entries: When a creature who has seen this stone casts Sending, they can target the Receiving Stone instead of a creature. The creature holding the Receiving Stone receives the Sending and may reply normally. If no creature is holding the stone, the spell fails.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Redsmith Crucible Set
entries: This set of magic smith's tools from the dwarven Redsmith family includes a stone crucible that's been enchanted with minor transmutation magic. If you are Proficient with smith's tools or are a dwarf, you can use a Magic action to speak the tools' command word and lower a nonmagical weapon made of metal into the crucible and hold it there for 10 minutes. At the end of that time, make a DC 10 Strength check using the smith's tools. On a success, the weapon is magically reforged. A reforged weapon counts as magical, and when you pull the reforged weapon from the crucible, you can change it into another type of weapon that shares the same properties as the original one. For example, a light hammer could become a handaxe, or a longsword could become a warhammer. These benefits last for 24 hours, at which point the weapon becomes nonmagical once more and returns to its original form.
Once this property of the tools has been used, it can't be used again for 24 hours. In the meantime, the tools can still be used as magic smith's tools.
{@i From the Griffon's Saddlebag 2.}

item.name: Redsmith Hammer
entries: This dwarven smithing hammer has runes running from end to end. When beaten against an anvil, the runes around the hammer glow a bright red and emanate heat, allowing a blacksmith to use the hammer to mold weapons and armor using only the hammer and anvil.
{@i From the Griffon's Saddlebag 2}

item.name: Rescue Ring
entries: Whenever you start your turn underwater and you are Unconscious, the Rescue Ring causes you to rise 60 feet toward the surface. The Rescue Ring prevents you from sinking deeper while you are Unconscious. Once you regain consciousness, the Rescue Ring becomes inoperable and is consumed, unless you die before regaining consciousness.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Sacrificial Dagger
entries: The dagger is covered in abyssal runes, depicting commitments of evil made in service of a demon lord.

The wielder may take a Bonus Action to cut itself with the dagger, dealing {@dice 1d4} Piercing damage to itself that cannot be reduced. The wielder's Hit Point maximum is also reduced by this amount. The dagger then gains +1 to attacks and damage for 1 minute.
Additionally, while holding this dagger, you gain a +3 bonus to Intelligence (Religion) checks made about rituals that require a knife.
{@i Made by Aris, repassed from WM3}

item.name: Scalding Orb
entries: While holding this orb, your Speed is increased by 5 feet and you are Burning. As a Bonus Action, you can throw this orb to a willing creature within 60 feet of you, which causes you to stop Burning.
{@i Inspired by Mewgenics. Made by June}

item.name: Scary Soda
entries: {@i This bubbly soda tastes like freshly picked grapefruit with a hint of lime. It's a pretty good soda, but the effects aren't pleasant.}
When you drink the soda, you become Frightened of it for one minute. A creature Frightened by the soda is inclined to scream.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Self-Replicating Truffle
entries: {@i As a Magic action while holding a Self-Replicating Truffle, you can cause a truffle (1/4lb of rations) to appear next to it. Once you use this ability, it can't be used again until the next dawn.}
You can also eat a Self-Replicating Truffle. It counts as 1 lb of rations.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Shield Gauntlets
entries: Unlike a regular shield, a hand with this equipped counts as a free hand but does not passively confer a bonus to AC.
If a hand wearing these gauntlets is free, you can use a Bonus Action to gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. You do not gain this benefit if you have another shield donned.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Sinister Potion
entries: {@i Often forbidden to sell, this potion seems Sinister to whoever dares to lay their eyes upon it.}

When you would drink the potion, you must first make a DC 10 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the act is too scary for you, and you waste whatever action or Bonus Action you used to attempt to drink it. On a success, you drink the potion and you also gain its Sinister quality, gaining a +5 bonus to Charisma (Intimidation) checks for the next minute.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Slot Machine
entries: {@i This large machine makes a lot of noise and has bright lights all over it. On the side there is a massive lever inviting all who see it to try their luck.}

As a Magic action you can put 6 GP into the machine and pull the lever. After {@dice 1d4} rounds, {@dice 1d10} GP pops out of the machine. Once used, the Slot Machine cannot be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Spellbook Jacket
entries: As a Magic action, this jacket can be placed onto a spellbook. While on a spellbook, the spellbook functions as an Enduring Spellbook. As a Magic action, the creature who placed the jacket on the spellbook can remove the jacket
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Spooky Costume
entries: {@i With the popularity of Liar's Night continuing to grow across Toril, people have begun to create a variety of magical costumes. This costume was made to scare all who see it, depicting a vile monster or other terrifying figure.}
While wearing this costume, you can use a Magic action to cast Cause Fear (Save DC 10). Once the spell is cast, it can't be cast from the costume again until the next sunset.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Ssissuragaaaaggg's Horn
entries: As a Magic Action you can blow the horn to summon an Abyssal Chicken in an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet. The Abyssal Chicken disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or after 1 minute.
The Abyssal Chicken is hostile to all creatures, including you. It rolls its own Initiative and acts on its own turns. It pursues and attacks the nearest creature to the best of its ability.
Once you use the horn, it can't be used until next Dawn.
{@i Adapted from Dungeon Crawl Classics - Quick Start Rules & Starter Adventure. Made by Jackson}

item.name: Stinky Cheese
entries: {@i A delicacy to rodents. It smells quite foul, but tastes very good. It's known to cause scuffles and violent crime.}
A creature that eats these cheese must make a DC 11 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save the creature gains an uncontrollable desire to eat all stinky cheese it sees for the next hour.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Stupid Floaties
entries: {@i Yay, floaties!}

As a Magic action, you can inflate them. While inflated and you are wearing them, you have a Swim Speed equal to your Speed, but you cannot swim deeper underwater. If you take Piercing damage while they are inflated, they pop and they are destroyed. You can also take a Magic action to deflate the floaties.
{@i Made by Landon}

item.name: Tasty Goop
entries: When you drink this potion, you transform into a 5-foot square puddle of Green Slime. Your gear melds into the new form. You can't use or otherwise benefit from any of that equipment. While transformed, you are Incapacitated and Blinded.
This transformation lasts for 1 hour, or until you end it (no action required). If the patch of Green Slime would be destroyed, the form immediately ends.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Tespian Elixir
entries: {@i ‘Tastes good!’}

If your alignment is Lawful or Neutral on the Lawful-Chaotic axis, you regain {@dice 2d4} + 2 Hit Points when you drink this potion. Otherwise, you take {@dice 2d4} + 2 Radiant damage that cannot be reduced in any way.
Whatever its potency, the potion’s glowing white liquid glimmers when agitated.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: The CATalog
entries: This book is the go-to book for all information on cats.

The CATalog is a nonfiction book on types of cats. As a Study action, you can make a DC 10 Investigation check to learn the game statistics of a cat you are aware of. (A GM can rule that the DC is higher for more obscure cats).
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Undertaker's Axe
entries: You cannot knock a creature out with this axe. When you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with the Undertaker's Axe, you chop off their head, or another equivalent body part, if any.
{@i Made by Gavin}

item.name: Unreliable Weapon
entries: When you hit with an unreliable weapon, instead of adding an ability modifier to damage rolls, you deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of the same type.
{@i Made by Gavin}

item.name: Vulture Mask
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast Cause Fear with it (DC 10). The mask can't cast this spell again until the next dawn.
![][image5]
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Weapon of The Mariner's Catch
entries: This magic weapon deals an additional {@dice 1d4} damage of its type when it is used to attack a beast with a Swim Speed. Any beast damaged in this way remains pristine and fully intact for the sake of cooking and consuming.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Woke Beer
entries: When drunk, make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, you become Poisoned for an hour. While poisoned in this way, you can cast Detect Pronouns at will, and it doesn't require Concentration.
{@i Repassed from S3, Made by June}

item.name: +2 Tool
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to ability checks made using this tool.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: 40 gallons of liquid or up to 4 cubic feet of dry goods Stone
entries: I saved the life of 40 gallons of liquid or up to 4 cubic feet of dry goods, who gave me a stone with the symbol of a barrel carved into it. The stone is an uncommon magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As an action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of 40 gallons of liquid or up to 4 cubic feet of dry goods, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Aberration Stone
entries: I saved the life of an aberration, who gave me a stone with an indescribable symbol (just a bunch of lines) carved into it. The stone is an Uncommon magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of an aberration, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Grell stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Adamantine Psychic Blade Gauntlets
entries: Each of these heavy leather gloves bears a violet crystal set on a small plate of adamantine.
These gloves enhance the psychic blades of Soulknifes. When you hit an object with an attack with your psychic blades, you deal force damage instead of psychic damage, and your hit is a Critical Hit.
{@i Inspired by Magic Item Compendium 3.5e, Made by Aris}

item.name: Adgrac Cavebraids' Steel Boots of the Unstable Mineshaft
entries: Specially designed by Cavebraid himself. These boots are designed to help dwarven miners detect cave-ins before they occur.
While wearing these boots, you have a Tremorsense with a range of 20 feet. When you use your Stonecunning ability, your Tremorsense range increases to 60 feet.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Anchor
entries: This greataxe is a massive anchor on a chain, and as such, deals Bludgeoning or Piercing damage instead of Slashing.
When you make an attack with it on your turn, your reach with the weapon is increased by 10 feet. When you hit a target with this weapon, you can choose to pull the target up to 10 feet towards you.
While holding this weapon, effects cannot forcibly push you or knock you Prone against your will.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Bag of Boars
entries: {@i This bag made from gray cloth appears empty. Reaching inside the bag, however, reveals the presence of a small, pigskin object.}

You can take a Magic action to pull the pigskin object from the bag and throw it up to 20 feet. When the object lands, it transforms into a creature you determine by rolling on the table below. See the Monster Manual for the creature's stat block. The creature vanishes at the next dawn or when it is reduced to 0 Hit Points.

The creature is Friendly to you and your allies, and it acts immediately after you on your Initiative count. You can take a Bonus Action to command how the creature moves and what action it takes on its next turn, such as attacking an enemy. In the absence of such orders, the creature acts in a fashion appropriate to its nature.
Once three pigskin objects have been pulled from the bag, the bag can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@dice 1d8}    Creature
1    Pig (the Trade Good)
2      Boar
3      Boar
4      Boar
5      Boar
6      Giant Boar
7      Giant Boar

8      Gorthok the Thunder Boar - if the pigskin transforms into Gorthok, the bag cannot be used again for 7 days. At the end of each of Gorthok's turns while he is bloodied, you must make a DC 15 animal handling check. On a failure, Gorthok is now hostile to you and your allies and doesn't listen to your orders. He targets you in particular.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Bead of Night
entries: A concentrated pearl of pure black. It feels like rippling glass.
{@b Fragile.} A Bead of Night has 1 hit point and 10 AC. A creature holding it can take a Utilize Action to crush it, destroying it.
{@b Throw.} Alternatively, as a Utilize Action, you can throw a Bead of Night to a spot you can see within 30 feet of you, destroying it.
{@b Darkness.} Whenever a Bead of Night is destroyed, it casts Darkness on the point that it was destroyed at (no Concentration required). When cast in this way, it lasts 1 minute instead of 10.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Belt of One Mighty Blow
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can activate this item, and the next time you hit with a melee weapon attack before the end of your turn, you deal extra damage determined by the type of weapon. A light weapon deals an extra {@dice 1d8} damage, a two-handed weapon deals an extra {@dice 3d6} damage, and all other attacks deal an extra {@dice 2d6} damage.
Once used, this item can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i From S3 & 3.5e}
{@i Original item by Aris}

item.name: Bent Silver Spoon
entries: {@i This silver spoon has strange properties that channel psychic power.}

The spoon enhances the flavor of any soup or similarly spoonable dish by drawing out the more nuanced flavors that are often overlooked. It also tarnishes and dim when touched by a poison. If tarnished this way, the spoon remains so until the next dawn, when its normal luster magically reappears.

The spoon can be used as an Arcane Focus, and while you hold it, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your Sorcerer spells and your psionic features, such as Telekinetic.

If you are holding the spoon and you roll psychic damage, you can focus your attention on the spoon to deal maximum damage on one of the damage dice. Once this feature has been used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Adapted From TGS2 by Landon}

item.name: Blast Disk
entries: As a Magic action, you can activate this blast disk and place it on an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. When you activate the blast disk, you can set the trigger to either proximity or a timer.
{@b Proximity.} The next creature of small size or larger that enters the space of the blast disk triggers an explosion.

{@b Timer.} You can set a number of rounds until the disk explodes. This number can't be less than 1 or greater than 10. At the start of your turn after that many rounds have passed, the disk explodes.

When the disk explodes, all creatures within 10 feet of the disk must make a DC 14 Dexterity save. The creature takes {@dice 5d6} fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success. This explosion also damages objects in the area, and destroys this item.
{@i From 3.5e and S3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Boots of Snowsense
entries: While wearing these boots you have a Tremorsense of 30 feet. You must be on snow to use this Tremorsense.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Brooch of Night's Vigil
entries: While wearing this brooch, you gain the following benefits:
{@i  You gain Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet if you don't already have Darkvision.
{@i  You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception and Survival) checks at night.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Cattering Ram
entries: {@i The Cattering Ram is an adamantine tube that, through the power of magic, was partially polymorphed into a Cat. It appears to be a Calico cat, having ears, whiskers, paws, a tail, and an expressive face, all while retaining its original tubular shape.}
You can receive assistance from the Cattering Ram to break down doors. When doing so, you gain a +4 bonus and Advantage on the Strength check (This does not stack with a normal Portable Ram).

{@b Sentience.} The Cattering Ram is a sentient, Chaotic Neutral portable ram with an Intelligence of 3, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 7. It has a Speed and Climb Speed of 40. The Cattering Ram is a lazy and chaotic entity. It enjoys walking around and ramming into random objects and creatures nearby. The Cattering Ram can change its expression and create audible Cat noises as it wishes.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Cleats of Stomping
entries: While you wear these boots, you can pass through the space of Prone creatures as if they were Incapacitated. When you pass through the space of a Prone creature, you can choose to deal {@dice 1d6} Piercing damage to it. A creature takes this damage no more than once per turn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Clockwork Spurs
entries: {@i These spurs have a cog for a rowel, that rotates and makes clicking noises at timed intervals.}
While wearing these spurs and mounting a creature, you gain the following abilities:
{@i Perfect Command{@b }.} As a reaction when your steed would make an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can forgo rolling to get a 10 on the die.
{@i Maintain Order{@b }.} Your mount has Immunity to the charmed and frightened conditions.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Coalescence Creator
entries: {@i A magical bowl that shreds and mixes edible organic material placed within.}

After placing a pound of two or more different sources of edible organic material within (to a maximum of 5 lbs.), you can spend 10 minutes to create a Smoothie with this bowl. A creature can consume this Smoothie as a Utilize Action. Consuming this Smoothie restores 1 Hit Point, and the Smoothie provides enough nourishment to sustain a creature for one day.
A Smoothie created this way loses its magical properties if it isn't consumed within 24 hours.
{@i Made by Marvin}

item.name: Combat Sword Sheath
entries: This sword sheath is designed to be used effectively in combat even while a sword is sheathed in it.

When a sword is sheathed in this sheath, it deals Bludgeoning damage, instead of the normal damage type of the weapon. While a sword isn't sheathed in it, it can be used as a club (or greatclub, if its a greatsword sheath)
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Corroded Circuit
entries: A metal wafer with impossibly small magic sigils running across it. It's half-melted, rusted, and blown to bits. You aren't {@i really} gonna stick this thing in your head, right?

{@i Integrated{@b }.} While you live, this item can't be removed from you against your will. However, once installed, it takes a Bastion Turn to safely unattune from it, as it is a very delicate process.
{@i Bludgeon Protocol{@b }.} You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with Unarmed Strikes.

{@i Overclock{@b }.} As a Bonus Action, you can trigger a dangerous overclock, driving your body past its limits for 1 minute. For the duration, your Unarmed Strikes deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Force damage on a hit, and your movement speed increases by 10 feet. Once used, this Bonus Action can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Directive Corruption{@b }.} Alien instructions jab into your mind, filling your thoughts with confused, garbled messages. You have Disadvantage on Intelligence saving throws.
(Note: you cannot attune to both this and an Eldritch Claw Tattoo.)
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Crystalline Tool
entries: {@i This tool is sculpted out of fragile magic crystals. They are carved with very small runes that channel the magic of the crystals into the wielder allowing them to perform much better than they normally would.}

You gain a +5 bonus to ability checks made using this tool. After making the check, if you are Proficient with the tool roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 1 the tool is destroyed. If you are not Proficient with the tool, it's destroyed once.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Denny Stone
entries: I saved the life of Denny, who gave me a stone with the symbol of Denny carved into it. The stone is an uncommon magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As an action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of Denny, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Doom Croaker's Staff
entries: {@i Doom comes for us all}
This staff deals Bludgeoning, Radiant, or Necrotic damage (Chosen when you attune).

As a Bonus Action while holding the staff, you can foresee and speak a prophecy. All creatures in a 30-foot Emanation must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a creature hears a prophecy of doom and becomes Frightened of you for 1 minute. On a success, a creature hears a prophecy of its greatness gaining Advantage on the next attack roll it makes in the next minute. A Frightened creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until the next dawn.
{@i Based off the Ravenfolk Doom Croaker from Tome of Beasts 1. Made by June.}

item.name: Dream Token
entries: {@i This token contains a sliver of a psionic crystal. Most are worn as brooches, but others take the form of coins or keys.}

When you sleep with it on your person, you remain fully lucid when you dream, and when you wake, you clearly remember everything that transpired. As an action within ten minutes of you waking, you can bind the dreams you experienced while you slept to the key, which remains bound until the next dawn. While bound, any creature who sleeps with this token on its person experiences the bound dreams instead of creating its own.
{@i Inspired by an item from Exploring Eberron. Made by Aris}

item.name: Dream Viewer
entries: As a Magic action, you can use this lens to begin viewing the dreams of an Asleep creature within 5 feet of you (if it has any). This requires Concentration, as if it was a spell. Your Concentration also breaks if you are no longer within 5 feet of the creature or if the creature is no longer Asleep. This is considered divination magic.
{@i Made by Landon}

item.name: Drow House Insignia
entries: {@i Each drow house insignia is keyed to a specific noble house of a particular drow city, and is wrought to reflect the house that created it. Some might be stylized versions of crossbows, javelins, maces, or even just spiders.}
Insignias come in one of two variants: Spell or Transmitting.

{@b Spell.} You can use the insignia to cast a single Level 1, 2, or 3 spell determined at its creation, with the more powerful spells reserved for drow of significant station or influence. Once the insignia casts the spell, it cannot cast it again until 24 hours have passed. These insignia typically contain one of the following spells: Blur, Enhance Ability, Comprehend Languages, Feather Fall, Jump, Levitate, Shield, Spider Climb, Unseen Servant, and Water Breathing.

{@b Transmitting.} As a Magic action, you can speak a command word and utter a short message of no more than 50 words or less into the insignia, which transmits it up to 1 mile away to another creature wearing an insignia of the same house. The recipient hears the message in its mind. To transmit the message, you must name the intended target as part of the command word. Should the target not be wearing its drow house insignia, the message is lost, but you have no way of knowing whether it successfully transmits or not. The insignia can transmit messages three times, and regains a use every 8 hours.

Since each insignia is slightly different—even those from the same noble house—a person aware of the unique shape of an individual insignia can magically locate that amulet with a scrying or similar spell.
{@b Identifying an Insignia.} A DC 15 Intelligence (History) check reveals the house to which an insignia is keyed. Drow gain a +5 bonus on these checks.
{@i From 3.5e Drow of the Underdark, ported by Aris}

item.name: Dude’s Rock
entries: {@i Hell yeah, brother.}

Carved from the burning mass of a fallen star, this rock is striated with streaks of red, white, and blue. This rock has 2 charges. When you hold it, you can use a Magic action to expend a charge and choose an effect from the following list:

{@i  Manifest a paper plate with one medium-well hamburger, eight buffalo wings, and two hot dogs in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the rock. Consuming the food as a Utilize action grants 5 Temporary Hit Points.

{@i  A six-pack of beer bottles appears next to the rock. The taste is identical to Anheuser-Busch Natural Light. Consuming all six beers as a Utilize action gives you Advantage against the next saving throw you make against the Frightened condition within the next hour.

{@i  The rock plays an otherworldly tune. The exact song is up to the discretion of the GM, but must fit under the umbrella of “Dad Rock.” You have Advantage on the next Unarmed Strike you make within the next hour.
The rock regains any expended charges at dawn.
Made by Ryan Gabrillo, Repassed from WM3

item.name: Edible Cement
entries: {@i Om nom nom nom nom} 🍰🦭.

When you eat the cement you become heavy for 1 hour. While heavy, you weigh an additional 8,000 pounds, are Slowed and are unable to swim. You are unable to be moved by any means other than your speed and teleportation.
If you are a seal, you can choose to be immune to the effects of the cement except for the increased weight.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Elf Stone
entries: I saved the life of an elf, who gave me a stone with the symbol of the Evermeet island carved into it. The stone is an common magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As an action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of an elf, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Wood Elf or Drow Commoner (chosen when you receive this stone) stat block and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Emergency Wound Closure Express
entries: Whenever you are reduced to 0 Hit Points and become Unconscious while wearing this amulet, you are immediately stabilized. Once the amulet is used this way, its magic is expended, and the item is consumed.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Enchanted Book: [Property]
entries: A creature can use an Enchanted Book in order to apply a non-Material Minor Item Property to an item. The amount of time it takes depends on the rarity of the item. Once an Enchanted Book is used, it becomes magically consumed. If an item does not meet the prerequisites for the Item Property or already has three or more Item Properties applied to it, it does not gain the Item Property and the book is still consumed.
The Item Property the manual gives is chosen by the GM when it is given out.
Common: 1 day
Uncommon: 2 days
Rare: 3 days
Very Rare: 4 days
Legendary/Legacy: 5 days
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Ender Pearl
entries: As a Utilize action, a creature can throw the pearl up to 60 feet; if the pearl lands in an unoccupied space big enough to contain the creature, the creature teleports to that space, along with any equipment it is wearing or carrying, and the pearl disappears.
{@i Made by Chrys in S3, Sourced from partnered content: Monstrous Compendium Volume 3: Minecraft Creatures}

item.name: Expanding Shield
entries: This shield is made of many connected gears, allowing it to expand to block off a space.

As a Magic action, you can activate the shield, doffing it and placing it onto the tile you occupy. When you do so, choose a direction (North, East, South, or West). The shield expands, transforming into a 5 foot wide and 5 foot tall blockade in the direction you chose.

{@i Blockade.} The blockade is a Medium object with 17 AC and 21 ({@dice 6d6}) Hit Points. It has Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage, and Vulnerability to Thunder damage. Nothing is able to pass through the blockade. The blockade regains all Hit Points at dawn.

The blockade is immobile, able to hold up 5,000lbs of weight. More weight causes the blockade to deactivate, transforming back into a shield. A creature can take a Utilize action to make a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check, moving the fixed rod up to 10 feet on a successful check.
When the block is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it deactivates transforming back into a shield. It is unable to be reactivated until the next dawn.

As a Magic action, the creature who activated the shield can speak its command word, deactivating the blockade transforming it back into a shield. If the creature is within 5 feet of the blockade, it can don the shield as part of the same action.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Focus Circlet
