entries: You have a +3 bonus to Constitution saving throws made to maintain Concentration.
{@i Adapted from The Griffin's Saddlebag 2 by June}

item.name: Fool's Scepter
entries: {@i The Nilbog spirit loves convincing goblins to make these funny staffs.}

As a Magic action, you can use the scepter to mock a creature within 60 feet. It must make a DC 12 Wisdom saving throw. On a fail it takes {@dice 2d4} Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on its next Attack Roll before the end of its next turn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Frozen Memories
entries: {@i You can feel the memories of bygone beings resonating in this shard of black ice.}

While holding this shard, you can draw on the memories stored in the ice as a Magic action. Choose one skill in which you lack proficiency. For the next hour, you have proficiency in the chosen skill. Drawing upon these memories takes a toll on your own. After the hour is up, you lose proficiency in two randomly determined skills. You can only gain one proficiency in a skill at a time.
If you have less than two proficiencies in a skill, you cannot use this item. You regain all lost proficiencies after completing a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Gambler’s Fallacy
entries: {@i Bonk. Damn it didn’t work. Bonk. Damn it didn’t work. Bonk. Damn…}

This weapon deals 0 damage on a hit. When you roll a 20 on the {@dice d20} attack for the attack roll, the weapon deals an additional {@dice 10d6} Bludgeoning damage. You treat any roll other than a 20 on the {@dice d20} attack as a 1.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Gelatin Cube
entries: {@i By mixing a Gelatinous cube, hot salt water, and various goops you can make a sticky, acidic, and volatile gel. Using magic to shrink a chunk of it into a 2 inch cube allows it to become stable, until it is released again.}

As a Magic action, you can activate the cube and throw it to a point up to 60 feet away. Upon landing, it expands into a 10ft Cube. If the space it is thrown is smaller than this, it expands to fill the space. The Cube is partially transparent, allowing creatures to see through it as normal while still being visible. Attacks and spells can be made through the Cube as if it wasn't there. The area covered by the Cube is difficult terrain. The Cube evaporates after 1 minute.

When it expands, every creature in the area of the Cube must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed saving throw, the creature takes 7 ({@dice 2d6}) Acid damage, and becomes engulfed by the Cube. On a successful one, the creature takes no damage, but is moved to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of itself that is not inside the Cube. If there is no unoccupied space available or the creature chooses to not move, it suffers the effects of failing the saving throw.

When a creature enters the Cube's area or ends its turn there, it must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 7 ({@dice 2d6}) Acid damage and becoming engulfed on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature can only take this damage once per turn.

{@b Engulfed{@i .}} Any creature engulfed by the Cube is suffocating, can't cast spells with a Verbal component, and has the Restrained condition. An engulfed creature can try to escape by taking an action to make a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the creature escapes and enters the nearest unoccupied space, no longer being engulfed.

As an action, a creature within 5 feet of the Cube can attempt to pull out an engulfed creature by succeeding on a DC 13 Strength (Athletics) check, moving the creature to the nearest unoccupied space on a success, causing it to no longer be engulfed. The puller takes 7 ({@dice 2d6}) Acid damage whether or not it succeeds on the check.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Giant Stone
entries: {@i I saved the life of an giant, who gave me a stone with the symbol of the Ordning carved into it. The stone is an Uncommon magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can attune to it.}

{@i As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of an giant, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Young Fire Giant stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.}
{@i After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.}
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Gork Stone
entries: I saved the life of Gork, who gave me a stone with the symbol of the Many-Arrows tribe (a humanoid skull pierced by three arrows) carved into it. The stone is an uncommon magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of a Gork warrior, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i I saved the life of June, who made this reward with inspiration from the Orc Stone from Icewind Dale carved into its writing.}

item.name: Gun Buddy
entries: {@i A little goober who hangs from a weapon to manage your ammunition.}

As a Magic action you can attach a Gun Buddy to any weapon with the Ammunition property. While attached, you can ignore the Loading property of the weapon and you don't require a free hand to draw or load ammo into the weapon.

{@b Sentience.} A Gun Buddy is a sentient Unaligned object with an Intelligence of 6, a Wisdom of 6, and a Charisma of 14. It has hearing and Blindsight out to 5 feet (it is blind beyond). A Gun Buddy loves nothing more than guns and ammunition, and will always try to grab some if it is in reach and load it into the nearest weapon. Other than that they have fun by swinging around.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Harpoon of Reeling
entries: This magic javelin is attached by a rope, and immediately after the attack, the weapon flies back into your hand.
If you possess the weapon mastery feature and have selected the Javelin as one of your masteries, this javelin has the unique 'Pull' weapon mastery.
{@i Pull.} If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can pull the creature up to 10 feet straight towards yourself if it is Large or smaller.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Haruspex's Knife
entries: You can cast Augury from this Dagger if you also use the entrails of a Medium or larger Beast, Giant, Humanoid, or Monstrosity slain within the last 10 minutes. This Dagger cannot be used this way again until the next moon rise, and entrails used this way cannot be used by another Haruspex's Knife.
{@i Made by Landon}

item.name: Headdress of the Serpent King
entries: This gold and emerald green headdress channels old and twisting serpentine magic to your bidding. If you take Poison damage while wearing this headdress, that damage is reduced by your Constitution modifier.

While wearing the headdress, ordinary snakes and snakes with a challenge rating of 1/4 or lower are at least indifferent toward you and will not attack you unless you threaten or harm them. In addition, you can use an action to cast Animal Messenger from it, but can only target snakes with it. The DC for casting Animal Messenger is 13.

{@b Curse.} This headdress is cursed, and equipping it extends that curse to you until you are targeted by Remove Curse. Removing the headdress fails to end the curse on you. While cursed, your speech is slightly changed: you hiss slightly when pronouncing "s" sounds.
{@i Slightly changed from The Griffon's Saddlebag Book 2 by Sam}

item.name: Holy Hand Grenade
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with lighting the Holy Hand Grenade and throwing it to a point up to 60 feet away. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 6d6} Radiant Damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
{@i Inspired by Terraria, Made by June}

item.name: Horn of Plenty
entries: {@i This horn is full of delicious food and celebratory gifts. Anyone would be grateful to have a chance to take from it}
This horn has 3 charges, which it regains daily at dawn.

As a Bonus Action, you can expend a charge to eat from the horn, or feed food from it to another creature within 5ft of yourself. The creature that eats from the horn regains {@dice 3d4+3} Hit Points.
As a Magic action, you can expend all 3 charges to cast Goodberry.
{@i Inspired by Terraria, Made by June}

item.name: Horns of Taurus
entries: These massive black horns are incredibly difficult to hold on your head, but imbue you with a building rage.
{@b Bulky.} Your speed is reduced by 10 feet.

{@b Building Rage.} While in initiative, your speed increases by 15 feet at the end of each of your turns, up to a maximum of 60 movement speed. Your speed resets if you are Incapacitated or you leave Initiative.
{@b Raging Charge.} At the start of your turn, if you have 60 feet of movement speed, you gain the following until the end of your next turn:

items: You cannot cast spells, concentrate on spells, or make ranged attacks.
The first melee attack you hit as part of an Attack action deals an extra {@dice 1d8} damage of the weapon's damage type.

entries: Once this effect triggers, it cannot be triggered until you roll Initiative again.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.}
{@i By Miel}

item.name: Humanoid Stone
entries: I saved the life of a humanoid, who gave me a stone with a letter in Common carved into it. The stone is an Uncommon magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of a humanoid, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Commoner stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Ellie}

item.name: Hunter Hunter Stone
entries: I saved the life of a villain stone, who gave me a stone with the symbol of a Ruinstone carved into it. The stone is an Uncommon magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can Attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of a Villain Warrior, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
Each use of the stone has a terrible side effect: someone known to the wielder is also erased from reality. After I use the stone, roll a {@dice d20}: on a 1-20, Alakmur'ss disintegrates.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Immortality Machine™
entries: This gnometech machine promises to let you live to see the future! Just set a time and place it unto your head and it will ensure{@i  that you live to that date!
{@i }Gnometech is not responsible for any complications that arise from misuse of the machine. The machine must remain undisturbed for the entirety of its use.{@i

The Immortality Machine is a hat. When worn, a willing creature can use a Magic action to set a target date and activate the machine. The creature becomes Petrified until the set date, unless the machine deactivates before then.
The machine deactivates if any of the following occurs:
{@i  It is removed from the creatures head
{@i  Dispel Magic is cast on it
{@i  It enters an Antimagic field
{@i  The creature takes damage
{@i  The creature is moved 5ft or more
{@i  The temperature of the room is ever below 32 degrees Fahrenheit (0 degrees Celsius) or above 95 degrees Fahrenheit (35 degrees Celsius)
{@i  It comes in contact with a liquid
{@i  It is touched by a creature
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Jester Hat
entries: While wearing this hat, you can use a Magic action to cast Tasha's Hideous Laughter (Save DC 13). It can be used twice, regaining all uses daily at dawn.
Whenever you make a Charisma (Performance) check, you can add {@dice 1d4} to the roll.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Lesser Polymorph Potion
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effects of the Polymorph spell for 1 hour (no concentration required). The Beast you turn into is chosen by rolling on the following table and ignores the Challenge Rating restriction.
When you transform, you gain a max number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 10 + twice your level (or 10 + twice your Challenge Rating if you don't have a level).
{@dice 1d64}
1  | Ape
2  | Baboon
3  | Badger
4  | Bat
5  | Black Bear
6  | Blood Hawk
7  | Boar
8  | Brown Bear
9  | Camel
10 | Cat
11 | Constrictor Snake
12 | Crab
13 | Crocodile
14 | Deer
15 | Dire Wolf
16 | Draft Horse
17 | Eagle
18 | Elk
19 | Frog
20 | Giant Badger
21 | Giant Bat
22 | Giant Centipede
23 | Giant Crab
24 | Giant Fire Beetle
25 | Giant Frog
26 | Giant Goat
27 | Giant Hyena
28 | Giant Lizard
29 | Giant Octopus
30 | Giant Rat
31 | Giant Seahorse
32 | Giant Spider
33 | Giant Toad
34 | Giant Venomous Snake
35 | Giant Wasp
36 | Giant Weasel
37 | Giant Wolf Spider
38 | Goat
39 | Hawk
40 | Hyena
41 | Jackal
42 | Lion
43 | Lizard
44 | Mastiff
45 | Mule
46 | Octopus
47 | Owl
48 | Panther
49 | Piranha
50 | Pony
51 | Pteranodon
52 | Rat
53 | Raven
54 | Reef Shark
55 | Riding Horse
56 | Scorpion
57 | Seahorse
58 | Spider
59 | Tiger
60 | Venomous Snake
61 | Vulture
62 | Warhorse
63 | Weasel
64 | Wolf
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Lil' Locksmith
entries: {@i I'm on it, boss!}

The Lil' Locksmith is sentient and has the personality of a locksmith artisan. Its alignment is determined by rolling on the alignment table in the "Sentient Magic Items" section in the Dungeon Master's Guide (or determined by the GM granting this item). Regardless of its alignment, the Lil' Locksmith uses the 'Commoner' stablock, with the exception that the Lil' Locksmith is of the tiny size category, and has Expertise in thieves' tools. The Lil' Locksmith has its own goals and motivations, and might require an incentive to take orders from a wielder, such as payment, a task, or some other form of compensation.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Liquid Locks
entries: {@i 'Oh Annam, why can male giants grow hair but not I?' - Eglitham 'Stonejumper' Gathakanathi, Male Goliath Cleric}

When you drink this potion, if you are a member of a species that is biologically incapable of growing hair or you are a variant of one that is biologically incapable of growing hair (i.e. Male Goliaths), you grow hair from your scalp. You determine minor characteristics about the hair such as its length, texture, and color.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Mak’Arrel’s Riffmaker
entries: {@i Riff}. You can take a Magic action to strum the cittern and cause an illusory riff to emanate from it.

{@i Cacophony}. This cittern has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} + 1 charges daily at dawn. While wielding this cittern, you can cast a spell from it. A spell cast from the cittern has a save DC of 13. Choose the spell from the following list: Shatter (2 charges), Thunderwave (1 charge).
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Mystery Potion (Uncommon)
entries: {@i Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.}
When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 15 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.
{@dice 1d100} | Potion Effect
1-3   | Potion of Climbing
4-6   | Potion of Comprehension
7-9   | Potion of Healing
10-12 | Potion of Watchful Rest
13-15 | Bottled Breath
16-18 | Oil of Slipperiness
19-21 | Philter of Adoration
22-24 | Potion of Acid Resistance
25-27 | Potion of Advantage
28-30 | Potion of Animal Friendship
31-33 | Potion of Cold Resistance
34-36 | Potion of Fire Breath
37-39 | Potion of Fire Resistance
40-42 | Potion of Force Resistance
43-45 | Potion of Greater Healing
46-48 | Potion of Growth
49-51 | Potion of Hill Giant Strength
52-54 | Potion of Lightning Resistance
55-57 | Potion of Necrotic Resistance
58-60 | Potion of Poison
61-63 | Potion of Poison Resistance
64-66 | Potion of Polychromy
67-69 | Potion of Psionic Fortitude
70-72 | Potion of Psychic Resistance
73-75 | Potion of Pugilism
76-78 | Potion of Radiant Resistance
79-81 | Potion of Thunder Resistance
82-84 | Potion of Water Breathing
85-00 | Roll Again
{@i Made by June}

item.name: NR-G Sports Soda
entries: {@i “A quick toxicology test on some of the victims [of this drink] has come back positive for um... all kinds of stuff, really. Methadone, phencyclidine, tetrahydrocannabinol, a variety of opiates, benzodiazepines, barbiturates, amphetamines... the list just goes on and on and on.”}
When you drink this potion, your Speed increases by 10 feet for 1 hour.
{@i From Risk of Rain 2, Made by Althaea}

item.name: Ooze Stone
entries: I saved the life of an Ooze, who gave me a stone with acidic slime on it. The stone is an Uncommon magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of an Ooze, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Plasmoid Boss stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands Common and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to goop.
{@i Made by Ellie}

item.name: Pendant of Bloody Gust
entries: {@i "May your rage bring haste.”}
A blood agate in the shape of a curling mote of wind, fashioned into a necklace or ring. It reacts to the presence of its holder's blood.
While you are Bloodied, you gain 10 additional movement speed.

Additionally, as a Magic action while you are bloodied, or as a Reaction to gaining the bloodied condition, you can cast Haste using this stone, targeting yourself. When you do so, the stone becomes nonmagical.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}
{@i Made by Miel}

item.name: Philter of Brotherly Love
entries: The next time you see a creature within 10 minutes after drinking this philter, you become Charmed by that creature for 1 hour. If this creature has at least one language, you believe them to be a close friend of several years. You are easily convinced to do favors for said creature on the grounds of "That's my fucking boy right there." This potion's amber-hued, sparkling liquid contains one easy-to-miss bubble shaped like an eagle. The effects of this potion can be negated by consuming one standard drink.
{@i Made by VP Ryan Gabrillo}

item.name: Pickle Stone
entries: I saved the life of a pickle, who gave me a stone with the symbol of a pickle carved into it. The stone is an uncommon magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As an action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of a pickle, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Poofy Snowball
entries: As a Magic action you can cast Snow Cloud (Save DC 13) by throwing the ball at a place within 60 feet.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Powdered Orc Stone
entries: I saved the life of a powdered orc, who gave me a powdered stone with the symbol of the Many-Arrows tribe (a humanoid skull pierced by three arrows) carved into it. The powder is an uncommon magic item that requires attunement, and only I can attune to it.

As an action, I can use the powder to summon the spirit of a powdered orc warrior, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the powder is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Pointe Stilettos
entries: You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with these weapons.

These two rapiers transform into boots when you put them on your feet as a Utilize action. While they are boots, each can be wielded as a rapier while on a foot. You can transform them back into a more normal rapier form as Utilize action.

They also have the Thrown (20/60) property in either form, and don't require a hand to be thrown while they're boots. In either form, they magically return at the end of your turn if what was used to throw them is free, whether that's to your hand or your feet. When they do so, you must succeed on a DC 5 Dexterity Saving Throw or take {@dice 1d4} Piercing damage from them.
{@i Original Item by Aris}

item.name: Rainbow Necklace
entries: When found, this necklace contains {@dice 1d6+3} rainbow beads. As a Utilize action, or as part of the Attack action by replacing an attack, you can remove bead from the necklace and throw it at a point within 60 feet. The bead breaks, providing Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet at the point for the next minute. This light is sunlight.
This necklace produces Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet. The light from this necklace slowly changes between the colors of a rainbow.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Rat King's Crown
entries: {@i This crown is made of matted fur from many different rats. Rats view those who wear the crown as powerful, but not a voice of authority.}
While wearing this crown, Hostile rodents become Indifferent to you. If you deal damage to one of these creatures, this effect is suppressed for 1 minute.

{@b Rodent Influence.} As a Magic action while wearing the crown, you can cast Dominate Beast (Save DC 13), targeting a rodent that is not Hostile to you. When cast this way, the spell lasts up to 1 hour, but once the spell ends an affected creature becomes immune to it for 24 hours.

{@b Conjure {@i RATS}.} As a Magic action you summon a Swarm of Rats at a point within 60 feet of yourself. It is Indifferent to you and your allies. This Swarm of Rats disappears after a day or once it drops to 0 Hit Points. You cannot use this ability until the previous Swarm of Rats created by it disappears.

{@b Cursed.} Your teeth and hair begin to grow rapidly. Each day you must spend 1 hour gnawing on an object to prevent your teeth from exceeding their natural length. If you let your teeth grow unimpeded for 3 days in a row, you transform into a Giant Rat as if through polymorph. This form lasts until you have no Temporary Hit Points left, at which point you die and revert form. You can't remove the crown or end your attunement unless you have done an hour of gnawing within the last 24 hours.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Rimetouched Glove
entries: Bound into this glove is the spell Frost Fingers. This glove has 5 charges and regains all expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing this glove, you can expend 1 charge to cast the spell Frost Fingers at the 1st Level. The spell’s saving throw DC is 13.
While wearing it, this glove can serve as a spellcasting focus.
{@i Made by Sam}

item.name: Scallywag's Sash
entries: {@i This harness, belt, or sash holds up to four flintlock pistol holsters, making for easy swapping between loaded firearms in a fight.}

While wearing the Scallywag's Sash, you may stow up to four pistols in its holsters. When you take the attack action on your turn, you may draw a pistol from the Sash before you make an attack, and stow it in the sash once the attack resolves, independently from the Equipping and Unequipping Weapons part of your attack. This benefit only applies to flintlock (Renaissance) pistols.
{@i Made by Patrick}

item.name: Second Edition Crowbar
entries: {@i “Crowbar/prybar/wrecking bar allows for both prying and smashing! Carbon steel, so it should last for a very long time, at least until the 3rd edition arrives.”}
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
When you hit a creature with Hit Points equal to its Hit Point maximum, this weapon deals an additional {@dice 1d8} damage.
You can use this as a Crowbar.
{@i From Risk of Rain, Made by Althaea}

item.name: Shackles of Silence
entries: You can take a Utilize action to place these shackles on a creature that has the Incapacitated condition. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. The bound creature is Silenced and Deafened.

You and any creature you designate when you use the shackles can take a Utilize action to remove them. Once every 30 days, the bound creature can make a DC 20 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the creature breaks free and destroys the shackles.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Shawl of the Reaper
entries: {@i Reaper's Training}. When attuned to this shawl, you have Proficiency with Sickles and Glaives.
{@i Strength from Harvest}. When you reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points with a Sickle or Glaive, you gain {@dice 2d4} Temporary Hit Points.
{@i Blessing of Myrkul}. When you succeed on a saving throw against a spell from a spellcaster, the spellcaster takes Necrotic damage equal to twice the spell's level.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Shield of Blinding
entries: This shield has three charges. When a creature within 5 feet of you misses you with a melee weapon attack, you may use your reaction to expend one charge of the shield. That creature must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be Blinded until the end of its next turn. This shield regains {@dice 1d3} charges daily at dawn.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Slugger
entries: When you score a Critical hit with this weapon, you can choose to summon a Giant Slug within 5ft of yourself. It uses the Giant Snail statblock, however it lacks the Shell Defense action.

The Giant Slug is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the Giant Slug shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger. The Giant Slug disappears when it takes damage or at the start of your next turn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Soothing Tonic
entries: The effects of this drink lasts 1 minute. A creature who is Charmed or Frightened when they consume this drink as a Magic action has the condition suppressed for the duration and has Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened condition until the effect ends.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Sporophore
entries: As a Magic action, you can use this sporophore to spread spores in 5 foot square on a surface within 5 feet of you. The spores are of a fungi of your choice, and after 10 minutes, it fully sprouts, provided the area is in dim light or darkness for the entire duration. The fungus can be one of the following:
{@i  Bile Lichen
{@i  Brown Mold
{@i  Yellow Mold
{@i  Russet Mold
{@i  Fireball Fungus
{@i  Violet Fungus
{@i  Shrieker Fungus
{@i  Gas Spore Fungus
{@i  Myconid Sprout
{@i  Campestri
{@i  Jammer Leech
{@i  An Edible Fungi
{@i  An Exotic Fungi
A sporophore only contains enough spores for one fungi. It regains its spores daily at dusk.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Stratagem Ball: OPS
entries: Stratagem Balls are handheld beacons that can be thrown to call down various forms of support. This one has been modified for long-range transmit, allowing use outside of SEAF operations. As a Bonus Action, you can throw a Stratagem Ball up to 60 feet away. Stratagem Balls can only be used outdoors.

After throwing the Ball, an artillery shell lands at the same spot at the start of your next turn. Any creature in a 5-foot Emanation must make a DC 13 Dexterity save or take {@dice 6d6} fire damage, or half on a success.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Stratagem Ball: ESR
entries: Stratagem Balls are handheld beacons that can be thrown to call down various forms of support. This one has been modified for long-range transmit, allowing use outside of SEAF operations. As a Bonus Action, you can throw a Stratagem Ball up to 60 feet away. Stratagem Balls can only be used outdoors.

After throwing the Ball, a strafing run lands at the end of your turn. Any creature in a 5-foot wide, 20-foot long line starting from the ball and pointing away from you must make a DC 13 Dexterity save or take {@dice 4d4} Piercing damage, or half on a success.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Stratagem Ball: ENS
entries: Stratagem Balls are handheld beacons that can be thrown to call down various forms of support. This one has been modified for long-range transmit, allowing use outside of SEAF operations. As a Bonus Action, you can throw a Stratagem Ball up to 60 feet away. Stratagem Balls can only be used outdoors.

After throwing the Ball, a napalm strike lands at the start of your next turn. Rr napalm lands in a 5-foot wide, 30-foot long line originating from the ball in a direction of your choice. Any creature in the napalm when it lands must make a DC 13 Dex save or take {@dice 4d8} fire damage, or half on a success. The napalm remains for one minute, and any creature that enters the napalm or starts its turn there takes {@dice 1d8} Fire damage.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Sturgis Stone
entries: I saved the life of a Sturgis, who gave me a stone with the symbol of a lumberjack carved into it. The stone is an Uncommon magic item that requires Attunement, and only I can Attune to it.

As a Magic action, I can use the stone to summon the spirit of a Sturgis Warrior, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
After the stone is used three times, it turns to dust.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Tactical Rock Pouch
entries: {@i Many underestimate the power of having a good rock between you and the mouth of a dragon.}

Whenever you roll Initiative and are not surprised, you can choose to deploy {@dice 2d4} tactical rocks from the pouch. You throw each of these to an unoccupied space within 120 feet of yourself, where they expand becoming Medium sized boulders (Shown below). The boulders disappear after 10 minutes.
{@b Boulder}
{@i Medium Object}
Armor Class: 17
Hit Points: 50
Damage Immunities: Poison, Psychic
Each boulder fills a 5ft Cube, and is large enough to provide up to Three-Quarters Cover to a standing Medium sized creature.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Tall Hat
entries: {@i These hats are almost always found in their smallest form, but through bloodlust and the love of the game, you can achieve hat perfection!}

This hat starts being 5 inches tall. Every time you kill a creature with an Intelligence of 2 or higher while wearing this hat, it grows 1/2 inch taller. As the hat grows taller, it gains new effects, retaining the effects of all heights smaller than it.

{@b 5 inches:} {@i Your hat is of normal size.} Creatures who view your hat think that it is a normal thing to wear. Creatures who immune to the charmed condition or mind altering effects are allowed to form their own opinions. Because of the heft of the hat, you have -{@dice 1d4} to all Initiative rolls.

{@b 10 inches:} {@i Your hat is starting to become quite tall.} You have -1 to all stealth checks you make, unless you are invisible or otherwise fully hidden. As a Magic action you can tip your hat at a creature within 30ft of yourself. The target must make a DC 5 Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed by you until the start of your next turn. Once you use this ability, you can't until the next dawn.

{@b 15 inches:} {@i This is a massive hat!} Whenever a creature rolls exactly 1 above your AC, your hat gets hit causing the attacker to take notice of its elegance. When your hat is hit, the attacking creature must make a DC 5 Charisma saving throw or be Charmed by you until the end of your next turn. A creature that passes this save is immune to it for 24 hours.

{@b 24 inches:} {@i Your hat is comically large.} As a Magic action you can pull your hat down onto yourself (Even if you are larger then it) causing you to go inside of it for a moment. While inside of the hat, you are considered to have half cover. Once you are hit, you are forced out of your hat (yet it remains on your head). You can only take this Magic action once per day.
{@b 36 inches:}  {@i At 3 feet tall, your hat is too much to handle.} Whenever you fail an Acrobatics check by 5 or more, your hat falls off of your head.
{@b 48 inches} {@i Four feet!} Your hat catches in the wind, causing it to become even more nimble. You can take any of the previous Magic actions as a Bonus Action.

{@b 60 inches} {@i FIVE WHOLE FEET!} When you enter a space that a creature one size larger of you can't fit into (or would have to squeeze into), you hat is knocked off of your head. You gain -{@dice 1d4} to any check involving fitting into a small space.

{@b 120 inches} {@i TEN FEET!!!!!!!!} By reaching ten feet, you have achieved perfection in your hat causing it to turn gold. When you put your hat on the ground, you can climb it as if it were a 10ft tall ladder, or use it as a 10ft pole. Selling a hat that is golden magically nets you an extra 500gp no matter what price you sold it for, however, the hat permanently loses its golden color.

{@b 25,000 miles} {@i Your hat is now large enough to stretch across all of Toril} As a Magic action while wearing the hat, you can launch yourself through it, teleporting yourself anywhere on the same planet, as if cast by the Teleport spell. Once you use this ability you can't use it again until the next dawn.

{@b Curse:} Whenever this hat is knocked off of your head, you take {@dice 1d4} psychic damage and must make a DC 10 Wisdom saving through or be charmed by the hat. On a fail, you must spend your next turn trying to pick up the hat.
Made by June, Repassed from WM3

item.name: The Belt
entries: {@i "Speed up!"}
A manifestation of a scared mind, this leather belt is exceptionally long.

You can take a Magic action to crack the belt. When you do so, creatures of your choice within 30 feet must make a DC 14 Wisdom saving throw or become Frightened by you for 1 minute. Creatures that can't hear automatically pass the save.
Creatures that are Frightened in this way have their speed increased by 10 feet. Frightened creatures can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success.
Once you use this ability, it can only be used again after an hour.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.}
{@i Repassed From S3, by Miel.}

item.name: Throwing Dynamite
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your attacks with lighting the Throwing Dynamite and throwing it to a point up to 60 feet away. Each creature in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point must make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 4d6} Fire Damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Trap Powder
entries: {@i This bag is full of magical dust. When released, the dust explodes quickly covering every nearby object and sneaking its way into traps.}

As a Magic action, you can open the bag to cast Find Traps. When cast in this way, it has a range of Self and only senses traps within 60 feet of the caster. The dust glows red for a moment if it detects a trap, filling the space with Dim Light until the end of your turn. The dust magically disappears at the end of your turn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Umbral Hood
entries: A black, silky cloak. Thin etchings are traced along its fabric, leading to a metallic device at the cloak's heart.
{@b Shadow Circuit.} As a Magic Action, you can activate the cloak, causing its fabric to bleed blackness. You gain the following benefits for 1 hour:

{@b - Darkrhythm.} You can feel the ebb and flow of shadows, as well as whatever may disturb them. While you are in Dim Light or Darkness, you have Blindsight with a range of 15 feet. This Blindsight does not extend into areas not in Dim Light or Darkness.

{@b - Shroud of Night.} The cloak seems to melt into darkness, and you along with it. While you are in Dim Light or Darkness, Wisdom (Perception) checks made to perceive you have Disadvantage, and you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
Once used, this item can't be used again in this way until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Vial of Undead's Bane
entries: This magical vial of oil replenishes its contents daily. Once a day over the course of one minute, you may coat one simple or martial weapon with this alchemical oil. For the next hour or until you hit with the weapon 10 times, the weapon deals an extra {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage when you hit an Undead creature.
Only one oil can be active on a weapon at a time. Applying a new oil ends the previous one. Once it has been applied to a weapon, you may not do so until the next dawn.
{@i Inspired by the Witcher, Made by Tyler}

item.name: +3 Tool
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to ability checks made using this tool.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Amulet of Immutability
entries: {@i This adamantine amulet protects you from magic that would threaten to alter your form against your will.}
While wearing the amulet, you have Advantage on saving throws against Transmutation spells. Additionally you are unable to shape-shift or have your form changed against your will.
{@i Adapted from The Griffin's Saddlebag 2 by June}

item.name: Armor of the Woodland
entries: {@i The elves of ancient times constructed these armors from the leaves and bark of birches, oaks, and maples.}
While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class. While you are traversing through a forest, your Speed increases by 10 feet and you leave no tracks.
You can cast Tree Stride three times a day. Expended uses are regained daily at dawn.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Asura Shield
entries: Elite asura soldiers are known to bear this shield in times of war.
While wielding this spiked shield, you can take the Attack action with it as if it was a Martial Melee Weapon. If you have Proficiency in Shields, then you are Proficient with this shield.
Attacks with this shield deal {@dice 2d6} Piercing damage. If the target creature is a Fiend or Undead, it takes an additional {@dice 1d6} Fire damage plus {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage.
A Fiend or Undead creature that touches the shield takes {@dice 1d6} Fire damage plus {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage and takes the damage again each time it ends its turn holding or carrying the shield.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Automapper
entries: This magical quill is able to create maps for you, freeing up your hands for important adventuring duties.

The automapper is able to draft rudimentary maps as you travel, as if through Cartographer's Tools. It is able to scribe a visible area up to 150 feet away from you. If you are traveling at a Fast pace, the automapper is unable to keep up with your movement, not producing a map.
The automapper creates maps in 1 square mile pages. It can be enabled or disabled as a Magic action.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Bells of Orthys
entries: When played, these bells ring with off-kilter, faint ethereal music that to those that hear it, believe it to originate from somewhere far away, in the opposite direction of the bells. All creatures within a mile can hear this music.

{@b Call of Carceri}. As a Magic action, you can play this bell and target a creature within 1000 feet that can hear the bell's music. The creature must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is Charmed. While Charmed, the creature believes that following the music leads to its heart's desire, and it must spend 2 feet of movement for every 1 foot it moves when moving towards you. This Charm effect lasts 1 minute. If a creature passes its save, it is Immune to this property for 24 hours and cannot hear the music. Demodands automatically pass their save.
If a Bard casts a spell as an action while holding this item, they can play the bell as part of that action.
{@b Sin Swamp}. As a Magic action, you can cast either the Insect Plague or Transmute Rock (rock to mud) spells. This property of the bells can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@b Curse}. While Attuned to this item, you have the following flaw: "I care most for myself, and I am willing to use deceit and betrayal to achieve my goals."
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Black Candle
entries: This black wax candle burns with a faint, shadowy flame that sheds no light. The wax never diminishes, and the cold iron chamberstick that holds it is perpetually cool to the touch.

While you are attuned to the Black Candle and are holding it, the candle automatically ignites and remains lit. It continues to burn for up to 8 hours when not held, upon which the flame extinguishes. While the candle is lit, you are immune to the effects of any curses affecting you. This does not prevent you from being cursed—any curses you gain while the candle is lit take effect once the flame is extinguished.

In addition, while holding the lit candle, you can end your attunement to a cursed magic item at will, as long as it can be ended by a remove curse spell. This property does not remove the curse itself, nor does it end any other magical effects of the cursed item.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.}
{@i Adapted from S3, Made by Miel}

item.name: Blindfold of True Darkness
entries: You gain a Blindsight of 30 feet while wearing this blindfold, and you are Blind beyond that radius. You are also immune to gaze weapons. You can't use vision in any way while wearing this blindfold. Donning or doffing this blindfold takes 1 minute to do.
{@i From 3.5e and S3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Bone Heart
entries: {@i A fragile vessel for health, a bony cage that increases vitality while it remains intact.}

{@b Fragile Vitality.} When you finish a Long Rest, you gain 5 temporary Hit Points. When your temporary Hit Points are reduced to 0 due to taking damage, you may negate any additional damage dealt to your Hit Points, upon which the Bone Heart crumbles and is consumed.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance.}
{@i Made by Miel}

item.name: Bottomless Bag of Bullets
entries: This bag contains an endless number of mundane Firearm Bullets and Sling Bullets. Any ammo drawn from the bag disappears after it hits a target, or 1 minute after it is removed from the bag.
You can draw any item the bag contains as if doing so from a regular bag or pouch.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Candy Coaster
entries: {@i The Candy Coaster is Santa's second choice vehicle behind his signature sleigh. This classic two wheeled vehicle is covered in red and white stripes.}

The Candy Coaster functions as a vehicle (Statblock below). When the Candy Coaster reaches 0 Hit Points, it isn't destroyed instead breaking into pieces. A creature Proficient with Smith's Tools can repair the Candy Coaster causing it to regain all its Hit Points as part of a Long Rest. This costs components worth 200 gp which can be bought in any major city.
{@b Candy Coaster}
{@i Large vehicle (500 lb.)}
Creature Capacity: 1 Large creature
Cargo Capacity: 100 lb.
Armor Class: 13
Hit Points: 30 (damage threshold 5)
Speed: 60 ft.
STR: 14
DEX: 16
CON: 10
INT: 0
WIS: 0
CHA: 0
{@b Damage Immunities:} Poison, Psychic
{@b Condition Immunities:} Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Frightened, Petrified, Poisoned, Unconscious
Traits:
{@b Jump.} With a 20-foot running start, the Candy Coaster can Long Jump 40 feet
{@b Prone Deficiency.} If the Candy Coaster falls Prone, it can't right itself and is Incapacitated until pulled upright.
Actions:
{@b Helm (Requires 1 Crew and Grants Half Cover).} Drive and steer the Candy Coaster.
Reactions:
{@b Juke.} If the Candy Coaster is able to move, the driver can use its reaction to grant the Candy Coaster Advantage on a Dexterity saving throw.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Circle of Abyssal Calling
entries: This circle of abyssal runes are forged of silver and inlaid in the ground, and are 10 feet in radius.

A creature that can speak Abyssal can perform a 1 hour ritual to call upon a Demon. You must provide a sacrifice worth at least 3600 gp, or a soul, which is consumed by the ritual. When the ritual is performed, you must speak the name of the Demon, and then make an Arcana or Religion check. The DC for this check is 10 + the CR of the Demon. If you succeed, you cast Planar Ally summoning the named Demon. If you fail, you cast contact other plane targeting the Demon you named.
If the Demon you targeted is a demon lord, it can choose to send an underling in its place.
If you speak the true name of the Demon, the DC for the Religion or Arcana check becomes equal to the CR of the Demon.

When you summon a Demon in this way, it has no actual physical presence, it is merely a manifestation of its will given temporary physical form. It cannot leave the circle in this form, except to return to its home plane via the spell. This manifestation cannot be affected by spells or effects, it can merely converse and make deals.

The Demon at any time can choose to appear in physical form, replacing its manifestation. If in physical form, it has no such restraints, and is only beholden to the rules of the spell. A Demon may do this after a deal has been met, though it's just as likely to do so to attack an unprepared summoner, or a summoner that has upset it.
{@i From WM3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Cloak of the Embrace
entries: {@i You're alone. Let the chorus of arms help you.}

While a creature is Grappled by your Unarmed Strike, it is also Charmed by you. At the end of each of its turns, the creature can make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw, ending the Charmed condition on a success. A creature that passes the save is immune to the cloak for 24 hours.
{@i Made by Althaea & June}

item.name: Crystal Shield
entries: While holding this Shield, you have a +4 bonus to Armor Class, in addition to the Shield's normal bonus to AC.

The AC bonus this shield provides decreases by 1 each time an attack misses you while you have this shield equipped. When the bonus this shield provides reaches 0, the shield shatters and is destroyed.
{@i Inspired by Barony, Made by Patrick}

item.name: Dagger of Terror
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

You can take a Bonus Action to channel dead spirits around the dagger. The spirits remain for 1 minute or until an attack using this weapon hits a creature. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or take {@dice 2d10} Psychic damage and have the Frightened condition for 1 minute. The weapon can’t be used this way again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Aris, Based on Dagger of Venom}

item.name: Dagger of Torment
entries: {@i Few of these blades remain, as many heroes of the past worked to destroy the majority of these terrible weapons.}

{@b Glimpse of Dread}. When you hit an attack against a creature using this weapon, you can force that creature to make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature perceives you as an animated corpse, often that of the creature's loved ones, and is Dazed until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature three times. Expended uses are regained nightly at midnight. Creatures with Truesight or have Immunity to the Frightened condition are Immune to this feature.

{@b Visions of Torment}. You can take a Magic action to curse a creature that has been under the effects Glimpse of Dread in the past hour. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be plagued by visions of what they've seen. An affected creature suffers a -2 to all {@dice D20} Tests for the next hour. A casting of Remove Curse ends the effect. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until midnight.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Disposable Stun Prod
entries: {@i “A single-use, short-range high-voltage electroshock weapon. Can be used one-handed.”}
As a Magic action, all creatures in a 5-foot Emanation originating from you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become Stunned until the start of your next turn.
{@i From SIGNALIS, Made by Althaeai}

item.name: Divorce Papers
entries: A priest can read this scroll while within 30 feet of one or more creatures that have been married to each other through the Ceremony spell. This ritual takes 8 hours and a lot of court filings beforehand (a bastion turn to do so).

On the completion of the ritual, any willing and affected creatures chosen within 30 feet are no longer married via the Ceremony spell, losing all benefits the marriage provides. In addition, this counts as the creatures being "widowed" and the creatures they were married to being "widowed" as well.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Dragonblood Soup
entries: When you drink this soup, you have Resistance to Fire damage for 1 hour, +1 to your AC while you aren't wearing armor, and once during the hour, you can unleash a breath weapon. Each creature in 15-foot Cone originating from you makes a DC 13 Dexterity Saving Throw, taking {@dice 7d6} Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful save.

item.name: Dryad's Sandals
entries: You can move through Difficult Terrain composed of vegetation, such as foliage and thorns, without it costing extra movement.

Additionally once per turn while wearing these boots, you can walk into any inanimate plant of a size category larger than you, and immediately exit out of another plant of that size or larger within 60 ft of yourself by using 5 feet of movement.
{@i Taken (accidentally) from Pathfinder, Made by June}

item.name: Environment Emulator
entries: {@i Originally designed by a group of kobold tinkerers, this device has seen widespread use for its simulation ability. Many report that it is the most practical way to get hands-on training in any environment, often used for exercise, spars, and military training. Some have attempted to use it as a home, but its illusionary nature and bad upkeep time results in this being impractical.}

It takes 8 hours to enable an Environment Emulator. While enabled, the emulator is immovable and indiscernible. A creature can spend 10 minutes to disable it. If Dispel Magic is cast on it, it also disables.

While enabled, a creature can spend 1 minute to cast Mirage Arcane centered on the emulator. When cast in this way, it can only cover a 200 foot square and the spell has a duration of 10 minutes. The emulator glows blue if a creature within the illusion is using Truesight to see through it.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Frostine Armor
entries: {@i A set of armor made out of pure Frostine crystals.}

This armor is cold enough to keep the temperature within 5 feet of it at 32 degrees Fahrenheit (0 degrees Celsius). While wearing this armor you have Resistance to Cold damage and automatically pass any saves against cold weather.
A creature wearing the armor can take a Magic action to cast Rime's Binding Ice (DC 14). This property of the armor can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Gambit's Die
entries: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one attack with rolling a {@dice d6}. All creatures that can see you within 120 feet are aware of what number you rolled. Each time you take damage before the start of your next turn, roll another {@dice d6} replacing the previous roll. At the start of your next turn an effect occurs based on the number rolled.
Roll | Effect
1    | Confusion. You become Confused until the start of your next turn.
2    | Boulder. You create a Boulder (See Tactical Rock Pouch) in a space within 5 feet of yourself.
3    | Lazer. All creatures in a 30-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 4d6} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.
4    | Quake. Creatures of your choice in a 20-foot-Emanation must make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. Failure:  {@dice 3d12} Bludgeoning damage and falls Prone. Success: Half damage only

5    | Storm. A orb of electricy flies out of your to a point of your choice within 30 feet. All creatures in a 10-foot-Sphere centered on the point must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. Failure: {@dice 2d10} Lightning damage and Stunned until the end of its next turn. Success: Half damage only.
6    | Wrath. Creatures of your choice in a 60-foot-Emanation of you must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. Failure: {@dice 8d6} Fire damage and starts Burning. Success: Half damage only.
{@i Inspired by Mewgenics. Made by June.}

item.name: Gray Zapinator
entries: {@i The pistol shoots beams of light instead of bullets. It might be broken.}
This pistol produces its own ammo and deals Radiant damage.

Every time you make an attack with this weapon, roll on the table for what effect occurs. If you roll multiple times, apply any rolled effects in the order you roll them. Multiple of the same effect stack, however a creature only makes a saving throw once taking all the stacked effects on a failure. Whenever you shoot this weapon, the GM can decide it jams and stops functioning until the next dawn.
{@b {@dice 1d20}}
1 - {@i The beam flies off randomly.}
The attack misses and you stop rolling effects
2 - {@i The beam shoots backwards out of the gun.}
You make the attack against yourself instead of its original target
3 - {@i The beam teleports towards a random creature.}
The target of the attack roll is changed to a random creature within the normal range of the weapon
4 - {@i The beam moves erratically.}
Roll {@dice 1d4} and subtract it from the attack roll
5 - {@i The beam grows dimmer.}
If the attack hits, roll {@dice 1d6} and subtract it from the damage roll (to a minimum of 0)
6 - {@i The beam glows green.}
If the attack hits, the target gains {@dice 1d10} Temporary Hit Points (After taking the damage).
7 - {@i The beam has a magnetic effect.}
If the attack hits, the target is pushed {@dice 1d4} times 5 feet directly away from you
8 - {@i The beam is extra strong.}
If the attack hits, the target is pulled {@dice 1d4} times 5 feet directly towards you
9 - {@i The beam slows down.}
If the attack hits, the target doesn’t suffer any of its effects (including damage) until the start of your next turn
10 - {@i The beam teleports repeatedly around the target.}
If the attack hits, All creatures within a 15ft Emanation of the target must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 1d10} Radiant damage on a fail
11 - {@i The beam quickly pierces through multiple creatures.}
If the attack hits, All creatures in a 90 foot Line from you in the direction of the target must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 1d10} Radiant damage on a fail
12 - {@i The beam moves precisely.}
Roll {@dice 1d4} and add it to the attack roll
13 - {@i The beam glows brighter.}
If the attack hits, it deals an additional {@dice 1d6} damage
14 - {@i The beam becomes partially incorporeal.}
The attack ignores all cover (Including Total Cover, allowing random targets to be selected through walls)
15 - {@i The beam splits into a second beam.}
If the attack hits, you immediately make another attack with the weapon against the same target
16-19 - Roll twice more
20 - Roll thrice more.
{@i Inspired by Terraria, Made by June}

item.name: Greater Piwafwi
entries: This dark spider-silk cloak is made by drow. It is a cloak of elvenkind. It also grants Resistance to Fire damage while you wear it. It loses its magic if exposed to sunlight for 1 hour without interruption.
While you wear this cloak, Wisdom (Perception) checks made to perceive you have Disadvantage, and you have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
{@i From Out of the Abyss}

item.name: Heartsteel
entries: The surface of this gemstone is thick and slightly jagged, and at the center sits a glowing heart-shaped core that beats slowly.

{@b Colossal Consumption.} When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack or Unarmed Strike on a creature that you hit with a melee weapon attack or Unarmed Strike on your last turn, you can choose to deal an additional {@dice 2d6} damage of your weapon's type. When you do so, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the additional damage dealt.
A creature cannot be affected by Colossal Consumption more than once.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Hellion Helm
entries: A helmet fashioned in the likeness of a Fire Hellion, it carries an infernal warmth.

Other creatures have Disadvantage on Intelligence (Investigation) and Wisdom (Insight) checks made to discern your true identity or intentions. Additionally, you can add {@dice 1d4} to Wisdom (Perception) checks.

{@b Infernal Searing.} When you make an melee attack roll against a creature and hit it while wearing this helmet, the target takes an additional {@dice 1d4} Fire damage and can't regain Hit Points until the start of your next turn if it is damaged by this Fire damage.
{@i Made by Landon}

item.name: History's Reflection
entries: {@i The exact appearance of this item varies from item to item. Whether the case, it is an object that can read the imprints of time.}

As a Magic action while holding this item, you can cast Legend Lore from it. To cast the spell in this way, you choose a place of great history that you're currently in. The information about the place, provided it is of legendary importance, is displayed on or from the item as if history was being replayed, though it may be abstract or symbolic. If the place is not of legendary importance, then the spell fails.
Once used, the item cannot use that area again for 1 year.
{@i Variant of Lorekeeper's Tome by Aris. Made by Landon}

item.name: Inquisitor's Notebook
entries: While holding this notebook, you can cast Legend Lore from it. To cast the spell in this way, you must choose a creature that you can see within 10 feet of you. The information about that creature, provided it is of legendary importance, appears written in the pages of the notebook.
Once used, the notebook cannot use that creature again for 1 year.
{@i Variant of Lorekeeper's Tome by Aris. Made by Landon}

item.name: King Vulture Mask
entries: While wearing this mask, you can cast Fear with it (DC 15). The mask can't cast this spell again until the next dawn.
{@i From Rain World}
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Luden’s Echo
entries: You have a +1 bonus attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
This weapon has 6 charges. It regains {@dice 1d6} + 1 expended charges daily at dawn.

{@b Echo.} When you deal damage with a spell you can expend a charge and choose for the attack to echo to up to 2 creatures of your choice within 15 feet of the original target. The selected creatures take {@dice 2d6+2} Force damage. Once you use this ability, you cannot use it until the start of your next turn.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Merrenoloth’s Oar
entries: Made of special wood that grows only in the Lower Planes, this long oar is carved with ancient symbols of death from countless cultures.

While you hold the oar, you have Advantage on Dexterity checks to maintain your balance while on a watercraft. In addition, as a Bonus Action while you hold the oar, you can learn the depth of any water or liquid within sight, and the distance and direction to the closest land while you are on open water.

The merrenoloth’s oar can be used as a weapon. It is treated as a +1 Lance when so wielded, and it can be used one-handed while you are on board any watercraft. It deals Fire damage instead of Piercing.
{@i Adapted from Dragon+ by Aris}

item.name: Megaton
entries: A maul of colossal proportion, like an entire planet held aloft by a metal rod. Its rectangular hammerhead is segmented by lines of thrumming energy, indicating the advanced technology lurking within it.

{@b Colossus Circuit.} Whenever you enter your Rage, if your size increases from either your Giant's Havoc or Demiurgic Colossus feature, this weapon increases in size alongside you and gains the following benefits for the duration of the Rage:

items: {@b Weight of the World.} When you hit a creature with an attack using this weapon, the weapon deals an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of the same type as its normal damage. This extra damage is increased to {@dice 2d6} if the hit creature is smaller than you.

{@b Atom Smash.} As a Magic action, you can attempt to deal a skull-shattering blow. Make a melee weapon attack using Megaton against a creature that is smaller than you within range. On a hit, they suffer the normal effects of the attack and additionally must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a fail, it is Slowed until the end of your next turn. Once used, this item can't be used again in this way until the next dawn.

entries: {@i Made by Christopher}
item.reqAttune: by a Path of the Giant Barbarian

item.name: Mystery Potion (Rare)
entries: Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.
When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 20 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.
{@dice 1d100} | Potion Effect
1-2   | Potion of Climbing
3-4   | Potion of Comprehension
5-6   | Potion of Healing
7-8   | Potion of Watchful Rest
9-10  | Bottled Breath
11-12 | Oil of Slipperiness
13-14 | Philter of Adoration
15-16 | Potion of Acid Resistance
17-18 | Potion of Advantage
19-20 | Potion of Animal Friendship
21-22 | Potion of Cold Resistance
23-24 | Potion of Fire Breath
25-26 | Potion of Fire Resistance
27-28 | Potion of Force Resistance
29-30 | Potion of Greater Healing
31-32 | Potion of Growth
33-34 | Potion of Hill Giant Strength
35-36 | Potion of Lightning Resistance
37-38 | Potion of Necrotic Resistance
39-40 | Potion of Poison
41-42 | Potion of Poison Resistance
43-44 | Potion of Polychromy
45-46 | Potion of Psionic Fortitude
47-48 | Potion of Psychic Resistance
49-50 | Potion of Pugilism
51-52 | Potion of Radiant Resistance
53-54 | Potion of Thunder Resistance
55-56 | Potion of Water Breathing
57-58 | Elixir of Health
59-60 | Oil of Etherealness
61-62 | Potion of Clairvoyance
63-64 | Potion of Diminution
65-66 | Potion of Fire Giant Strength
67-68 | Potion of Frost Giant Strength
69-70 | Potion of Gaseous Form
71-72 | Potion of Heroism
73-74 | Potion of Invisibility
75-76 | Potion of Invulnerability
77-78 | Potion of Mind Control (beast)
79-80 | Potion of Mind Control (humanoid)
81-82 | Potion of Mind Reading
83-84 | Potion of Stone Giant Strength
85-86 | Potion of Superior Healing
87-00 | Roll Again
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Nos Erac Ekat
entries: A paintbrush fashioned from a crooked bone, with frayed brushhairs grasping out like fingers. It fits in your hand perfectly.
{@b Vivid Paints.} you have a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with your Psychic Blades.
{@b Violet Bloom.} You have a mental palette of red and blue that you can splash across your masterworks. Whenever you manifest a Psychic Blade, you can change its damage type to Cold or Fire damage.
{@b Vantablack.} While holding Nos Erac Ekat, you can cast Creation, as you paint the object from pure black. Once used, this item can't be used again in this way until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Panic Button
entries: A Panic Button is a small wooden stick about the size and shape of a dagger hilt, with a red button inset on the end. As a Magic action, you can press the button to cast Hornung’s Random Dispatcher on yourself. Once used, it loses its magic.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Periapt of the Major League Gamer
entries: {@i But my hope will never die.}
A sacred relic from the infamous Phase Clan, this golden amulet depicts a Shiba Inu wearing a fedora. While wearing it you gain the following effects:

{@b Oh Baby} - Before you make an attack with a ranged weapon that you are proficient with, you can declare that you are doing a "360 No Scope." You roll the {@dice d20} four times and take the lowest roll. If that attack hits, you automatically score a critical hit. The roll cannot be affected by features that alters the results of {@dice d20} tests, such as trance of order, portents, clockwork amulets, etc.

{@b Get The Camera} - As a Magic action, you can create an illusion within 10 feet of you that takes on the form of a small scene. The illusion forms into a moving image that reenacts the last 15 seconds of a creature you recently killed's life. While the illusion is broadly accurate, it is incredibly stylized, complete with sound effects, slow-motion sequences, and additional visual effects (such as explosions or a man saying "Wow.").
{@b Confirmed} - You have advantage on ability checks to decode the meaning behind symbols, ciphers, and other codes that are related to secret societies.
{@b Every Day} - Consuming cannabis gives you 69 temporary hitpoints. Once this property has been used, it cannot be used again until 42.0 days have passed.
{@i Made by Ryan}

item.name: Pipe of Grief
entries: This long smoking pipe brings sorrowful memories when smoked.

As a Magic action while the pipe is filled with tobacco and lit, you can release a puff of magical smoke that takes the form of a 5 foot Emanation centered on you. The cloud remains for {@dice 3d8} minutes, or until a strong wind disperses it. A creature that ends its turn in the cloud must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is filled with grief, and has disadvantage on {@dice D20} tests for 1 minute. A grieved creature can repeat the saving throw if it ends its turn outside of the cloud. After three successful saving throws, the grief effect ends.

Creatures immune to the Poisoned condition are immune to this effect, as well as creatures that don't need to breathe. Once used, a magical cloud cannot be produced from this pipe again until the next dawn.
{@i From Book of Vile Darkness (3.5e)}
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Polymorph Potion
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effects of the Polymorph spell for 1 hour (no concentration required). The Beast you turn into is chosen by rolling on the following table and ignores the Challenge Rating restriction.
When you transform, you gain a max number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 30 + twice your level (or 30 + twice your Challenge Rating if you don't have a level).
{@dice 1d85}
1  | Ape
2  | Baboon
3  | Badger
4  | Bat
5  | Black Bear
6  | Blood Hawk
7  | Boar
8  | Brown Bear
9  | Camel
10 | Cat
11 | Constrictor Snake
12 | Crab
13 | Crocodile
14 | Deer
15 | Dire Wolf
16 | Draft Horse
17 | Eagle
18 | Elk
19 | Frog
20 | Giant Badger
21 | Giant Bat
22 | Giant Centipede
23 | Giant Crab
24 | Giant Fire Beetle
25 | Giant Frog
26 | Giant Goat
27 | Giant Hyena
28 | Giant Lizard
29 | Giant Octopus
30 | Giant Rat
31 | Giant Seahorse
32 | Giant Spider
33 | Giant Toad
34 | Giant Venomous Snake
35 | Giant Wasp
36 | Giant Weasel
37 | Giant Wolf Spider
38 | Goat
39 | Hawk
40 | Hyena
41 | Jackal
42 | Lion
43 | Lizard
44 | Mastiff
45 | Mule
46 | Octopus
47 | Owl
48 | Panther
49 | Piranha
50 | Pony
51 | Pteranodon
52 | Rat
53 | Raven
54 | Reef Shark
55 | Riding Horse
56 | Scorpion
57 | Seahorse
58 | Spider
59 | Tiger
60 | Venomous Snake
61 | Vulture
62 | Warhorse
63 | Weasel
64 | Wolf
65 | Allosaurus
66 | Ankylosaurus
67 | Archelon
68 | Elephant
69 | Giant Ape
70 | Giant Boar
71 | Giant Constrictor Snake
72 | Giant Crocodile
73 | Giant Scorpion
74 | Giant Shark
75 | Giant Squid
76 | Hippopotamus
77 | Hunter Shark
78 | Killer Whale
79 | Mammoth
80 | Plesiosaurus
81 | Polar Bear
82 | Rhinoceros
83 | Saber-Toothed Tiger
84 | Triceratops
85 | Tyrannosaurus Rex
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Potent Pressure Capsule
entries: This small capsule is made of beeswax blended with sand and a variety of enchanted water plants. A creature who consumes a pressure capsule ignores the effects of swimming at depths greater than 100 feet. This effect only ends when the creature dies or is targeted with Dispel Magic or a similar effect.
{@i Made by Ellie}

item.name: Potion of Poison Immunity
entries: When you drink this potion, you have Immunity to Poison damage and the Poisoned condition for 1 hour.
This potion looks like a golden-hued liquid, and glows faintly in darkness.
{@i Made by Gavin}

item.name: Potion of Treasure Hunting
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the ability to sense nearby treasure for an hour. You sense the most valuable stash of Treasure or Coins within 1,000 feet. You sense its approximate value, the direction to its location, its distance, and if it's in motion you know the direction of its movement. You can choose to exclude treasure you are aware of from your sense.
This spell can't locate an object if any thickness of lead blocks a direct path between you and the object or if the treasure is warded from Divination magic.
{@i Adapted from 2e from June.}

item.name: Potion of Vigor
entries: When you drink this potion, you regain {@dice 1d6} + 4 expended Hit Dice. You can choose to immediately roll one of these Hit Dice, and regain Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier.
This brown potion tastes of a hearty stew.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Ring of Adamantine Touch
entries: While wearing this adamantine ring, your Unarmed Strikes are treated as though wielding an adamantine weapon; whenever you hit an object with an Unarmed Strike, the hit is considered a Critical Hit.
{@i From 3.5e, Repassed from WM3, Ported by Aris}

item.name: Ring of Broken Vows
entries: {@i It twists on itself.}

Once on each of your turns when you take the Attack action, you can replace one attack with a melee Spell Attack (Your Sanity modifier plus your proficiency bonus to attack). On a hit, the target takes {@dice 3d6} Psychic damage. On a miss, you take the Psychic damage instead.
You cannot use this ring if your GM isn't using optional Sanity rules.
{@i Based on Betrayal at the House on the Hill, Made by Aris}

item.name: Ring of Force Shield
entries: As a Bonus Action while the hand you wear this ring on is empty, you can generate a shield-sized (and shield-shaped) Wall of Force that stays with the ring and can be wielded by the wearer as if it were a shield. The shield lasts until you hand is no longer empty or you dismiss the shield (no action required).

item.name: Ring of Gnomekind
entries: {@i Legends hold that Garl Glittergold himself handed down these simple rings to his children (He didn't).}

{@i Gnome}. The Ring of Gnomekind transforms you into an average looking gnome of average height and weight. The ring chooses the physical characteristics of the form, such as age, gender, skin color, hair color, and voice. Except for size, your statistics and species traits don't change, nor do items worn or carried by you.
{@i Gnomish}. You know Gnomish.

{@i Gnomish Cunning}. Your Intelligence score increases by 2, to a maximum of 20. You have a +2 bonus to Dexterity (Tinker's Tools) checks, and a +1 bonus to your spell save DCs for your Illusion spells.
{@i Friend of Gnomekind}. You have Advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to interact with gnomes and svirfneblin.
{@i Gnomish Darkvision}. You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Ring of Mystery Keys
entries: This key ring always has many Mystery Keys on it. Inserting a key from the key ring into a lock has a 5 percent chance of unlocking it. If the first inserted key doesn't unlock the lock, none of the keys on the ring can. Once the key ring has unlocked a lock, it cannot unlock another lock for {@dice 1d4} hours.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Roulette Wheel
entries: While holding this shield, you have a +1 bonus to AC. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.
As a Bonus Action you can spin the wheel, your AC is increased (or decreased) by {@dice 1d8-4} until the end of your next turn.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Skin of Adhesion
entries: While wearing this tar-covered armor, you have a +1 bonus to your AC. When you attune to this, it melds into your skin, making you naturally excrete a sticky tar. You still retain the benefits of wearing the armor. While attuned, you gain the following benefits:

When you are hit with a Melee Attack with a weapon, you can take a Reaction to force the wielder of the weapon to make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, the weapon becomes attached to your skin until you die or release it with a Bonus Action. A creature can take an Action to pry the weapon off by succeeding on a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check. Once you use this feature 3 times, you cannot do so again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Step Two
entries: One vial of this oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature, along with the equipment it's wearing and carrying (one additional vial is required for each size category above Medium). Applying the oil takes 10 minutes. The affected creature gains the following benefits for 8 hours.
The affected creature has a Fly Speed equal to its walking speed while it is under rainfall.,
The affected creature is buoyant while underwater, rising 30 feet toward the surface at the start of each of its turns.,
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Stone of Sending
entries: You can cast the Sending spell from this stone once per day.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Swarm Slayer
entries: {@i The following is engraved in Elvish into the side of the weapon: "When faced with one spider, one bullet will do. When faced with a hundred spiders, you're gonna need a hundred bullets."}

The Swarm Slayer is a Martial weapon that deals {@dice 5d4} Piercing damage on a hit and has the following properties: Ammunition (Range 20/120 ft.; Firearm Bullet), Firearm, Loading, Heavy, Two-Handed, Mastery: Graze. Every time you make an attack with the Swarm Slayer, it expends 9 Firearm Bullets in addition to the ammunition loaded.

{@b Spray.} When you take the Attack action, you can replace an attack with this weapon with spraying aimlessly in front of you. Each creature in a 30-foot Cone must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 5d6} Piercing damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you spend one minute to load 100 Firearm Bullets into the weapon.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Sunscorcher
entries: {@i Wielded by Morninglords and Silverstars alike, the glassteel of this blade refracts the light of both the sun and moon to weaponize the sky to the user's benefit.}
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When attacking a creature in sunlight or moonlight, the creature takes an extra {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage.
{@i Repassing from WM3, Made by Patrick}

item.name: Swarmkeeper's Rapture
entries: This ornate staff of bone drones with thrums of the swarms of locusts within has 5 charges and regains {@dice 1d6} + 2 charges daily at dawn. As a Magic action, you can do one of the following:

{@b Rapture}. You can expend 1 charge of the staff to call forth the rapture within. Choose one creature within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be unable to take actions during its next turn.

{@b Hypnotic Swarm}. You can expend 3 charges and target a creature within 30 feet of you. A swath of locusts swarms that creature with its dazzling shells. That creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be affected as though it was under the effect of Hypnotic Pattern.
{@b Locust Swarm}. You can expend 5 charges of the staff to cast Insect Plague (DC 15).
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: The Painting of Mara Jay
entries: This painting is an 8 foot long landscape of an open field near Raven's Bluff. A small town can be seen on the horizon line, and bison are grazing the grasses. The painting is signed by prodigy artist Mara Jay.
While The Painting of Mara Jay is placed in a bastion facility, it gains the following benefit:
{@i Inspired.} Taking a bastion turn in the facility where the painting resides grants you a +{@dice 1d4} bonus to any {@dice D20} Tests within the facility.
You can only move the painting once per week, at the start of the new bastion turn.
{@i Made by Gavin}

item.name: Throwing Axe
entries: You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
{@i Hunter's Impulse.} At the start of your first turn of each combat, if you take the Attack action on that turn, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action.
{@i Adapted from STS2 by Landon}

item.name: Tobor
entries: This Chest of Preserving has been outfitted with wheels and magic-induced locomotion, allowing it to act as a delivery unit for whatever it contains.

As a Magic Action while within 15 feet of it, you can verbally command it to travel to a location you are familiar with or to stop traveling (if it is already en route). The location must be within 1 mile of its current location, unmoving, and on the same plane of existence. The target location must be clearly described, and effects that block divination magic block its pathfinding.

It has a movement speed of 15 feet per turn and is blocked by Difficult Terrain, objects, creatures, and vertical drops. If for some awful reason it enters initiative, it takes its movement at the end of the round, but does not have a turn of its own (as it is not a creature).
If it cannot find a path to its location after it has begun to travel, it will stop moving and wait until a feasible path exists.
It's unclear why it's called a Tobor.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Tome of Language Imparted
entries: After reading this scroll, book, or tome (typically takes an hour, but a GM could rule it takes days or even weeks), you are magically imparted a language. You gain that language, and can speak it at a natural level. The language is obscure. It cannot be any of the standard or exotic languages in the phb, nor can it be Druidic or Thieves' Cant.
Once a language has been magically imparted, the tome becomes nonmagical, though it might contain nonmagical lessons in the language.
Examples of adequate languages: Ice Toad, Gnoll, Giant Eagle, Chondathan, Worg...

It is said that the final ingredient of crafting this item is a fluency in a language. A creature fluent in a language gives up the language in its entirety, to be passed on to another. The language cannot be learned by that creature again.
{@i From S2 & S3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Tome of Mystra's Cataclysmic Destruction
