entries: This weathered tome of history bound by auspicious prayer seals to various gods is only but a long-gone shadow of its formerly powerful self, dyed and dissected by mortal desire. As a Bonus Action while holding the tome you may choose the following magical effects to occur, causing the book to disintegrate into nothingness immediately afterwards.
{@b Detect Outsiders.} You gain the benefit of the Tracker's Sense Talent against Aberrations for the next hour.
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Unquenchable Heart
entries: The modified core of a mighty machine, now yours. When inside your chest, you can feel it burn with conviction.
{@b Integrated.} While you live, this item can't be removed from you against your will. However, once installed, it takes a Bastion Turn to safely unattune from it, as it is a very delicate process.
{@b Charged.} The heart has 6 charges, and it regains all expended charges daily at dawn. You can spend these charges on the following:

items: {@b Voice Circuit.} You can expend 1 of the heart's charges to project your voice to be heard clearly by all creatures within 1 mile of yourself, regardless of intervening noise, for 1 minute. Magical silence, 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood blocks this projection.

{@b Rapid Barrier Energization.} At the start of your turn, if your Arcane Ward has 0 hit points, you can expend 3 of the heart's charges to have it regain a number of hit points equal to twice your Intelligence modifier.

entries: {@i Loosely inspired by Draedon's Heart from the Terraria Calamity mod. Voice Circuit taken and modified from Ring of the Orator. Made by Christopher}

item.name: Unseen Fog
entries: This small canister contains thick and obscuring fog, enchanted to be invisible. You can use a Utilize action to activate it, at which point the item casts Fog Cloud (no concentration required) centered on itself. This fog has no effect on any creature that does not have Truesight, as it cannot see it.
Once used, this item cannot be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Unsettling Lamp
entries: The lamp functions as an Everbright Lantern.

Whenever a creature within 30 feet of the lamp makes a saving throw against an effect you control, you can give that creature Disadvantage on that roll. That creature is then Immune to this effect of Unsettling Lamp for 24 hours.
{@i Adapted from STS2 by Landon}

item.name: Unyielding Gauntlets
entries: While wearing these gauntlets, you are under the following effects:
{@i  If you are moved in a way other than teleportation while Grappling a creature, you can choose to move the creature with you.
{@i  If a creature that you are Grappling is moved in a way other than teleportation, you can choose to move along with it.
{@i Made by V}

item.name: Vambraces of Evil’s Warding
entries: While wearing these vambraces, when you are targeted by a ranged attack from a Fiend or Undead, you get a +2 bonus to your AC against the attack.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Voidwalker Armor (Variant)
entries: This black studded leather armor bears a red sheen. While wearing this armor, you can use a Bonus Action to summon a projection of yourself in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of yourself. The projection is a translucent copy of you that has Immunity to all damage and conditions, and you can make attacks and cast spells with a range other than self as if standing in the projection’s space. The projection disappears at the end of your turn. Once you use this Bonus Action, it can’t be used again until the next dawn.

{@b Curse.} This armor is cursed, and attuning to it extends the curse to you. As long as you remain cursed, you’re unwilling to part with the armor. Taking it off fails to end the curse. While cursed in this way, you feel disconnected from your body, continuously hearing whispers that call for you to join them. Whenever you finish a Long Rest while cursed, you must make a DC 11 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you gain no benefit from that Long Rest and your Charisma score decreases by 2. You die if this reduces that score to 0.
{@i From the Book of Many Things, with the curse nerfed to not require a whole rescue session}

item.name: Weapon of Fleshgrinding
entries: When you make an attack with this weapon against a creature and hit, you can verbally command this weapon to "grind" and release it from your grasp. At that time, the weapon animates and continues to grind itself into the foe's flesh. At the start of each of your turns thereafter, it deals damage to the foe as if you had successfully hit with the weapon. The weapon ceases to grind after it has dealt this damage 5 times, where it then falls to the ground.

The foe or another creature with a free hand within reach of it can attempt to rid the foe of the grinding weapon by making a Strength (Athletics) check (DC 20, uses its object interaction). If a creature succeeds, it is now holding the Weapon of Fleshgrinding and can use it.
{@i From 3.5e Book of Vile Darkness, originally adapted by Aris}

item.name: Nalfeshnee's Visage
entries: A mask of a Nalfeshnee, it holds remnants of its hideous power.

Other creatures have Disadvantage on Intelligence (Investigation) and Wisdom (Insight) checks made to discern your true identity or intentions. Additionally, you can add {@dice 1d4} to Charisma (Intimidation) checks.
{@b Pursuit.} Immediately after you hit an enemy with an Opportunity Attack, you can move up to half your Speed as part of that Reaction.

{@b Horror Nimbus.} As a Magic Action, you can create a 15-foot Emanation originating from you. Each creature within that Emanation makes a Wisdom Saving Throw: DC 15. Failure: A target takes {@dice 8d6} Psychic damage, and the target has the Frightened condition for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until it ends its turn with you out of line of sight. Success: The target is unaffected. This item cannot be used this way again until next dawn.
{@i Made by Landon}

item.name: Weakening Shackles
entries: You can take a Utilize action to place these shackles on a creature that has the Incapacitated condition. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. Once every minute, a shackled creature must make a DC 15 Strength saving throw. On a failure, the creature's Strength score is reduced by {@dice 1d4} (to a min of 1).

You and any creature you designate when you use the shackles can take a Utilize action to remove them. Once every 30 days, the bound creature can make a DC 30 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the creature breaks free and destroys the shackles.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Wing-graced Necklace
entries: The Interloper god has decided to grant you a boon.
{@b Light Wings}. You gain a Fly Speed equal to your Speed.
{@b Zeus’ Bane.} You gain a +2 to Dexterity saving throws to avoid Lightning damage.
{@i Made by Krypt}

item.name: Acorn of the Ice Squirrel
entries: This acorn is imbued with the diabolical power of ice. It can grant you great power, at a price.
{@b Diabolical Resistance.} You are Immune to Cold damage and Resistant to Fire and Poison

{@b Curse.} This item is cursed. While attuned, you take a -2 penalty to Dexterity Skill Checks and Saving Throws. You can't voluntarily end your attunement to it. If you're targeted by a spell that ends a curse, your attunement to the acorn ends.
{@i Made by Andrew}

item.name: Amulet of Greater Spirit Channeling
entries: This amulet features a central sapphire gemstone and a smaller ruby fragment, and contains the power to harness lingering spirit essences from powerful creatures, the lost art of blue magic.

As a Magic action, you can absorb the spirit essence of a CR 5 or greater creature that has died within the last 10 minutes. Ask the GM to pick a trait, attack, or ability of its of the GM's choice. The attack or ability must take an Action, Bonus action, or Legendary Action to use. That ability or trait is stored in the amulet, for you to use. Once you have stored an ability or trait, you cannot absorb the spirit essence from that statblock again.

As a Magic action, you can use one of the stored abilities or attacks, as you outwardly manifest the spirit of the creature for a brief period while you execute the ability. You revert back to your normal appearance after the action is taken. The spirit essence remains inside the amulet after use.
Once you manifest a spirit, you cannot manifest any spirits again until the next dawn.

When you finish a long rest while attuned to the amulet, you can choose one of the stored traits. You gain the benefits of that trait while you are attuned to the amulet, or until you choose a different stored trait.
{@i Repassed from S3, made by Aris}

item.name: Antimagic Shackles
entries: These adamantine manacles are forged in the reaches of Ysgard and are used in the prisons of the Seven Heavens.

You can take a Utilize action to place these shackles on a creature that has the Incapacitated condition. The shackles adjust to fit a creature of Small to Large size. The shackles prevent the bound creature from creating or being affected by any magic effect whether it be from spells, items, or other innate abilities other than the shackles.

You and any creature you designate when you use the shackles can take a Utilize action to remove them. Once every 30 days, the bound creature can make a DC 30 Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the creature breaks free and destroys the shackles.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Caduceus
entries: This sentient white ash staff with silver inlaid runes appears carved as a caduceus, that of snakes carved into its shaft and its tip adorned with wings. This staff functions as a Staff of Healing, but it has the additional properties:

{@b Ease Pain}. As a Magic action, you can expend a charge of the staff and end a magical pain effect on a creature such as Power Word Pain or Symbol (Pain). You cannot use this feature again until dawn.

{@b Vision of Heaven}. As a Magic action, you can expend a charge of the staff and force one Fiend or Undead within 30 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target creature is Dazed until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature three times, regaining expended uses daily at dawn.

{@b Sentience}. Caduceus is a sentient Neutral Good staff with an Intelligence of 14, a Wisdom of 14, and a Charisma of 10. Caduceus can hear and see with normal vision out to 120 feet. It communicates verbally and can be heard by any nearby creature. It knows Celestial, Common, and Infernal.

{@b Personality}. Caduceus has no memory of its origin, but it chats incessantly about the many great heroes it has helped over the years. The staff abhors bloodshed and violence, and it sometimes elects to not heal wounds it feels are justified or deserved.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Casus Belli
entries: {@i There is always a reason for war. If none can be found, you will make one.}
You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
{@b Pressure.} This weapon does an extra {@dice 2d6} Psychic damage to any creature it hits. This damage is increased to {@dice 3d6} if you hit the same creature on your previous turn.
{@b Hungry For Battle.} If you would roll Initiative due to your hostile actions towards another creature, you have Advantage on that check.
{@b Many Forms of War.} As a Bonus Action, you may change the form of this weapon into any weapon of your choice.
{@b Sentience.} Casus Belli is a sentient chaotic evil weapon with an Intelligence of 15, a Wisdom of 18, and a Charisma of 20. It has hearing and darkvision out to a range of 120 feet.
The weapon communicates telepathically with its wielder and can speak, read, and understand Common and Celestial.

{@b Personality.} Casus Belli is cold, deliberate, and utterly certain. It is always working to push its wielder towards resolving any conflict with swift violence, judging any attempt at diplomacy as a sign of weakness and refusing to engage in such. It will deceive its wielder when possible in order to push them towards violence.
{@i Made by Tyler}

item.name: Chromatic Cold Brew
entries: {@i This special kaeth has been steeped with the elements to give it a unique tangy taste.}
For 1 minute after you drink this potion, you have Resistance to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, and Thunder damage. Additionally you emanate a faint rainbow aura for the duration.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Cloak of the Frenzied Lord
entries: While attuned to this mantle, you have Resistance to Psychic damage and your melee Weapon Attacks deal an additional {@dice 1d6} Psychic damage.

{@b Wave of Madness}. As a Magic action, you unleash a pulse of befuddlement and paranoia. Each creature within a 20-foot Emanation centered on you must make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, they are Confused for the next minute. An affected creature can repeat the save at the end of each of their turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you use this feature you cannot do so again until the next dawn.

{@b Expand Potential}. You tap into the powers present within the Far Realm. When you use this feature, you take {@dice 6d6} Psychic damage and cast one of the following spells on yourself without taking a Magic action:
Blur, Mirror Image, Haste, Stoneskin

When you cast them this way, your Wisdom score is reduced by the spell’s level. If your Wisdom score is reduced to 0 this way, you die. When you are targeted with Greater Restoration only restores two points of Wisdom rather than the entire reduction. Long Rests do not restore reduced Wisdom score from this item.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Dart of the Phoenix
entries: This red and gold dart has a tail made of phoenix feathers.

When you hit a creature with this dart, that creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a fail, it dies. On a success, it takes {@dice 12d6} Fire damage. If a creature dies or is reduced to 0 Hit Points in this way, it is reduced to a pile of ash. Creatures with Immunity to Fire damage automatically succeed on the saving throw.
After 10 minutes, any creature reduced to a pile of ash by this dart is reformed in the same state it was in when it died in the spot in which it died, or the nearest unoccupied space.
After you throw the dart, your Strength score is reduced by {@dice 1d4}. This reduction only heals 1 point every Long Rest or Greater Restoration cast on you, rather the entire ability score.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Death Caller
entries: You have Resistance to Necrotic damage while you hold this staff. You have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon and it deals an additional {@dice 1d6} damage on a hit. When you deal damage with Death Caller, you can choose to deal either Necrotic or its normal damage type.

While holding this staff, you can cast Mortify (DC 30) with a range of 120 feet. Once you cast this spell, you are unable to use it again until a creature is killed with Death Caller, or the next dawn. Creatures within a 120ft Emanation of you are unable to become Stable.
While holding the staff, you can cast Toll the Dead at level 11 (DC 17).
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Dimensional Cannon
entries: This strange-looking firearm has swirling portals on both its sides and its front-flared muzzle. The weapon has an extradimensional space within it that can store a single Tiny object weighing up to 5 lbs. Whenever you would make an attack with the firearm, you instead cast the Catapult spell on an object loaded within the space.

You can make melee attack rolls using the firearm as if it was a melee weapon. On a hit, the weapon deals no damage, but if the firearm's extradimensional space is empty, the creature must make a DC 17 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, you can immediately cast the Catapult spell flinging the creature instead of an object.
Whenever you cast Catapult using the Dimensional Cannon, it has a dimensional recoil. You teleport 5 feet in the opposite direction that you fire the weapon.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Drilling Ammunition
entries: You can ignore Half Cover and Three-Quarters Cover from sand, earth, mud, or ice on attacks made with this ammunition. Additionally you can target a creature behind Full Cover from sand, earth, mud, or ice  that you are aware of using this ammunition, however the creature gains a +5 bonus to AC against the attack.
Once it hits a target or is shot through Cover, the ammunition is no longer magical. This ammunition is typically found or sold in quantities of ten or twenty pieces.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Eternal Sending Stone
entries: Eternal Sending Stones come in pairs, with each stone carved to match the other so the pairing is easily recognized. While you touch one stone, you can cast Sending from it. The target is the bearer of the other stone. If no creature bears the other stone, you know that fact as soon as you use the stone, and you don't cast the spell.
If one of the stones in a pair is destroyed, the other one becomes nonmagical.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Finality Circuit
entries: A metal wafer with impossibly small magic sigils running across it. Repaired and given new strength, both it and you can be realized to the fullest potential.
{@b Integrated.} While you live, this item can't be removed from you against your will.
{@b Extinctor Protocol.} You gain a +1 bonus to your attack and damage rolls with Unarmed Strikes. Additionally, your Unarmed Strikes deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage of the same type on a hit.
{@b Absolute Directive.} Alien logic flows through your brain, supporting your aims with its cold calculus. You have Advantage on Intelligence saving throws.
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Gauntlets of Grounding
entries: These black metal gauntlets are built to help a brawler get their hands on a mystically slippery foe and keep them there.
When you’re Grappling a creature, they cannot teleport, travel to another plane, or change their shape.
{@i From S3, by Lily}

item.name: Gunslinger’s Eye
entries: This prosthetic eyeball’s pupil is in the shape of a glowing crosshair, belying its design as a gunslinger’s aide. The small copper sphere functions as a normal eye while placed in a socket. While seeing through the eye, you treat any Pistol you hold as if it had a range of 60/180.
{@i Made by Lily}

item.name: Mystery Potion (Very Rare)
entries: {@i Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.}
When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 25 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.
{@dice 1d100} | Potion Effect
1     | Potion of Climbing
2     | Potion of Comprehension
3     | Potion of Healing
4     | Potion of Watchful Rest
5     | Bottled Breath
6     | Oil of Slipperiness
7     | Philter of Adoration
8     | Potion of Acid Resistance
9     | Potion of Advantage
10    | Potion of Animal Friendship
11    | Potion of Cold Resistance
12    | Potion of Fire Breath
13    | Potion of Fire Resistance
14    | Potion of Force Resistance
15    | Potion of Greater Healing
16    | Potion of Growth
17    | Potion of Hill Giant Strength
18    | Potion of Lightning Resistance
19    | Potion of Necrotic Resistance
20    | Potion of Poison
21    | Potion of Poison Resistance
22    | Potion of Polychromy
23    | Potion of Psionic Fortitude
24    | Potion of Psychic Resistance
25    | Potion of Pugilism
26    | Potion of Radiant Resistance
27    | Potion of Thunder Resistance
28    | Potion of Water Breathing
29    | Elixir of Health
30    | Oil of Etherealness
31    | Potion of Clairvoyance
32    | Potion of Diminution
33    | Potion of Fire Giant Strength
34    | Potion of Frost Giant Strength
35    | Potion of Gaseous Form
36    | Potion of Heroism
37    | Potion of Invisibility
38    | Potion of Invulnerability
39    | Potion of Mind Control (beast)
40    | Potion of Mind Control (humanoid)
41    | Potion of Mind Reading
42    | Potion of Stone Giant Strength
43    | Potion of Superior Healing
44    | Oil of Sharpness
45    | Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
46    | Potion of Flying
47    | Potion of Greater Invisibility
48    | Potion of Longevity
49    | Potion of Mind Control (monster)
50    | Potion of Speed
50    | Potion of Supreme Healing
51    | Potion of Vitality
52-00 | Roll Again
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Humming Anamnesis
entries: While held, this peculiar dodecahedron emits hums and clicks of an alien language.

As a Magic action, you can activate the Anamnesis and gain a +10 bonus to Study actions you make for the next minute. After activation, the hums and clicks of the Anamnesis crescendo and invade your senses, as if trying to communicate with you; when you take the Search action or make a Wisdom saving throw, you subtract {@dice 1d6} from the roll. This penalty remains until you finish a Long Rest. The Humming Anamnesis cannot be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Horn of Fiendish Calling
entries: As a Magic action, you can blow this horn to cast one of the following spells: Summon Fiend, Incendiary Cloud, Wall of Fire (level 6), or Scorching Ray (level 6). Once blown, the horn turns to ash. Use +9 spell attack and DC 17 spell save DC for the spells.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Lesser True Polymorph Potion
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effects of the True Polymorph spell for 1 hour (no concentration required). The creature you turn into is chosen by rolling on the following table and ignores the Challenge Rating restriction.
When you transform, you gain a max number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 30 + twice your level (or 30 + twice your Challenge Rating if you don't have a level).
{@dice 1d100}
1   | Gray Slaad
2   | Larva
3   | Bronze Dragon Wyrmling
4   | Warrior Commander
5   | Sphinx of Secrets
6   | Dretch
7   | Mammoth
8   | Fiend Cultist
9   | Giant Ape
10  | Steam Mephit
11  | Awakened Tree
12  | Doppelganger
13  | Swarm of Venomous Snakes
14  | Cloaker
15  | Blue Dragon Wyrmling
16  | Violet Fungus Necrohulk
17  | Mimic
18  | Azer Pyromancer
19  | Tyrannosaurus Rex
20  | Warhorse
21  | Ankylosaurus
22  | Assassin
23  | Commoner
24  | Lemure
25  | Displacer Beast
26  | Kuo-toa Monitor
27  | Basilisk
28  | Giant Squid
29  | Salamander
30  | Kuo-toa Whip
31  | Half-Dragon
32  | Reef Shark
33  | Kenku
34  | Umber Hulk
35  | Roper
36  | Bandit Captain
37  | Galeb Duhr

60  | Modron Quadrone

63  | Draft Horse
64  | Animated Rug of Smothering
65  | Triceratops
66  | Merfolk Skirmisher

68  | Red Slaad
69  | Piercer
70  | Magma Mephit
71  | Harpy
72  | Phase Spider
73  | Priest
74  | Green Dragon Wyrmling
75  | Sahuagin Warrior
76  | Bullywug Warrior

78  | Swarm of Stirges

80  | Killer Whale
81  | Giant Seahorse

92  | Owl
93  | Tough Boss

95  | Treant
96  | Brazen Gorgon
97  | Black Dragon Wyrmling
98  | Manes Vaporspawn
99  | Seahorse
100 | Giant Shark
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Mole Suit
entries: This bulky suit, which fully encases your head and body, takes 1 minute to don or doff. While worn, it enables you to breathe in an airless environment and renders you immune to the harmful effects of any gas that surrounds you. The suit also grants you a Burrow Speed equal to your Speed while more than 100 feet underground.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Pipe Organ of Villainous Motivations
entries: This Pipe Organ weighs 10,000+ lbs. When played, its music can be heard up to 300 feet. When you play particular songs with this pipe organ, you can choose to radiate various emotional effects. Choose one of the effects below. Any creature within a 300-foot Emanation that hears your music for an entire minute must succeed on a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or be subjected to the effect. A creature that succeeds is immune to the effects of your music for 24 hours.
{@b Loud or Enthralling}. A target has a −10 penalty to Wisdom (Perception) checks and Passive Perception until the music ends.

{@b Frightening}. A target is Panicked or Frightened until the music ends.  If the creature ends its turn in a location where it doesn't hear the music, the creature can make a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the condition ends.
{@b Despairing}. The target has Disadvantage on {@dice D20} Tests until the music ends.
{@b Charming}. The target has the Charmed condition until the music ends or you or your allies damage it. While Charmed, it is Friendly to you
{@b Calming}. The target has Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions until the music ends. If the creature was already Charmed or Frightened, those conditions are suppressed while you play.

{@b Apathetic}. The target becomes Indifferent about creatures of your choice that it’s Friendly or Hostile toward. This indifference ends if the target takes damage or witnesses its allies taking damage. When the music ends, the creature’s attitude returns to normal.
{@b Sorrowful}. The target has the Depressed condition until the music ends.
{@b Annoying}. The target has the Enraged condition until the music ends.
{@b Uplifting}. The target has the Blissful condition until the music ends.
{@b Disorienting}. The target has the Confused condition until the music ends.
{@b Lullaby}. The target has the Asleep condition until the music ends or it is woken up.
{@b Discordant}. The target argues with other creatures for 1 minute. During this time, it is incapable of meaningful communication and has Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks.
{@i Based on 3.5e, Made by Aris}

item.name: Pocket Organ
entries: {@i Thousands of pipes shrunk to fit into your pocket.}

As a Magic action, you can place this 1-foot pipe on the ground and using a command cause it to grow into a large pipe organ. Repeating the command word causes the organ to revert to a pipe. Each creature in the area where the organ appears is pushed to an unoccupied space outside but next to the organ. Objects in the area that aren't being worn or carried are also pushed clear of the organ.

The organ is 15ft wide, 5ft thick, and 30ft tall. At the base of the organ rests manuals and a pedalboard that are used to play the organ. Magic prevents the tower from being tipped over. The organ has an AC of 20; HP 100; Immunity to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage except that which is dealt by siege equipment; and Resistance to all other damage. Only a Wish spell can repair the tower (this use of the spell counts as replicating a spell of level 8 or lower). Each casting of Wish causes the tower to regain all its Hit Points. If the organ is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it reverts into a pipe and cannot be grown until repaired.

When played, the organ can be heard up to half a mile away. As a Magic action, you can pull out all the stops and begin to play incredibly loud requiring Concentration. The music fills a 60-foot Emanation origination from the organ. Whenever a creature enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, the creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@dice 5d6} Thunder damage and has the Deafened condition until the start of your next turn. Creatures that are Deafened automatically pass the saving throw.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Shield for the Many.
entries: While attuned to the Shield for the Many, it functions as a +1 Shield.

When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to move up to your movement in a straight line towards that creature, and if you end your movement within 5 feet of that creature, you are hit by the attack instead. You can use this feature three times and regain all expended uses at dawn.
{@i Made by Vic.}

item.name: Skull of Tragic Pasts
entries: {@i Delicately etched with strange marks.}
Whenever you make an Intelligence {@dice D20} Test, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 3-4, add +1 to the {@dice D20} Test. On a 5-6, add +2 to the {@dice D20} Test.
If you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, roll {@dice 3d6}. On a 1-8, you die. On a 9+, you instead drop to 1 Hit Point.
{@i Based on Betrayal at the House on the Hill, Made by Aris}

item.name: Staff of Pestilence
entries: {@i This wooden staff is covered in diseased sores that ooze yellow pus. It gives off a horrible odor of rotten flesh}
You have Immunity to the effects of Poisons and non-magical diseases while you hold this staff.
The staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC:
{@i  Infestation (0 charges)
{@i  Allergy Field (5th-level version, targets air instead of plants; 1 charge)
{@i  Befoul (6 charges).
{@i  Blindness/Deafness (5th-level version; 1 charge)
{@i  Contagion (3 charges)
{@i  Harm (3 charges)
{@i  Insect Plague (2 charges)
{@i  Pox (1 charge)
{@i  Ray of Sickness (5th-level version; 1 charge)

The staff regains {@dice 1d6} + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1, you are inflicted with Flesh Rot until cured, as the staff rots away into a puddle of diseased pus.
{@i Heavily adapted and changed from 3.5e Book of Vile Darkness, Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3}

item.name: Sun Pellet
entries: This small ceramic ball is specially coated with enchanted oil. As a Magic action, you can crack a sun pellet or throw it up to 100 feet, whereupon it releases a brilliant magical light. This light is equivalent to a Sunburst spell (save DC 15) but has a radius of 20 feet.
{@i From Dragon+, Made by Aris}

item.name: The Bagman’s Gambit
entries: This Handy Haversack has a strange connection to the horrifying Bagman.

Anything put inside Bagman’s gambit is magically concealed and can’t be detected, divined, or scried upon. Additionally, it’s been filled with a variety of useful consumables. At any time, as an action, a character may reach inside and pull out one of the following magic items:

items: One dose of Keoghtom’s Ointment
One potion of Greater Healing
One Potion of Heroism
One Potion of Vitality

entries: Once the bag produces an item, it can't produce another until the next dawn.

When you pull any item out of the haversack, you must roll a {@dice d10}. On a 1, the Bagman comes out of the haversack and attacks you and your allies. If the Bagman is defeated, he reforms inside the backpack the next day, ready to wreak havoc again. For more information about the Bagman, see Van Richten’s Guide to Ravenloft.
{@i Adapted from Adventurer's League, Made by Aris}

item.name: Tol-kendar
entries: The Tol-kendar (“body wrecker” in Infernal) is a magic weapon that grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. Wielded by the asymmetrical yagnoloths, it takes the form of a 10-foot-long steel Pike topped by a metal cup ringed with blades, with a harpoon point emerging from the cup’s center. The {@i Tol-kendar} has the statistics of a Pike, but each time you hit, you can choose to do either Piercing damage with the harpoon or Slashing damage with the blades.

When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with the {@i Tol-kendar}, the weapon pierces deep into the target’s body to deal an extra {@dice 2d8} Piercing damage. A target that isn’t a Construct or Undead must then succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be Stunned until the start of your next turn.
{@i From Dragon+}

item.name: Van Richten's Guide to Ravenloft
entries: When this book is consulted, you gain a +5 bonus to Intelligence (Arcana, History, Nature, or Religion) checks you make regarding the Domains of Dread or its inhabitants. You gain this benefit whether or not you are attuned.

{@b Character Creation.} While attuned, you can gain the benefits of 1 [Dark Gift](https://5e.tools/charcreationoptions.html#watchers_vrgr,flstfeature%20type:dark%20gift=1) of your choice (choose from the Dark Gifts in Van Richten's Guide to Ravenloft). When you die while attuned to this item, you can choose to come back as a Dhampir, Hexblood, or Reborn.
{@b Creating Domains of Dread.} While attuned, you can cast the Mordenkainen's Magnificent Mansion spell. The item can't cast the spell again until the next dawn.

{@b Domains of Ravenloft.} While attuned, you have a +5 bonus to Wisdom (Survival) checks made to navigate the Domains of Dread. In addition, you immediately know when you see a Mist Talisman, and what domain it leads to. In addition, whenever you and your group roll on the Wandering the Mists table, you can roll twice and choose either result.

{@b Horror Adventures.} While attuned, the first time you would gain a level of Stress after you take a Long Rest, you can choose to not gain a level of Stress. While attuned you also have Advantage on saving throws against Fear, Curses, and Haunted Traps (see the optional rules in VRGR).

{@b Monsters of Ravenloft.} While attuned, as a Bonus Action you can make a Study action against a creature within 30 feet of you. On a success, you also learn its Condition Immunities, Damage Vulnerabilities, if the creature is Shape-shifted, and its traits.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Antibarrel
entries: This barrel is able to hold up to 40 gallons or up to 4 cubic feet of anything that isn't a liquid or dry good.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Censer of Blood
entries: This brass incense burner hangs on a brass chain, and is covered with fiendish symbols and the names of ancient vampires. While the censer is on your person, you have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track by scent and Wisdom (Perception) checks to detect smells. In addition, you are aware of the exact location of any creature within 60 feet of you that is below its hit point maximum.

As a Magic action, you can spill one drop or more of your own blood into the censer, causing it to steam. You suffer one level of Exhaustion, and the censer issues forth {@dice 1d6} vampiric mists that appear within 10 feet of you. You can repeat this process to create multiple mists, taking yourself to a maximum of five levels of Exhaustion. You can have no more than 50 vampiric mists created at a time with this item. The vampiric mists don’t appear if you are in sunlight.

The summoned vampiric mists understand your language and obey your spoken commands. If you issue no commands, they attack all nearby humanoids except yourself, only sparing those that you specifically command them to.
You reduce levels of Exhaustion imposed by the censer of blood in the normal fashion. Each mist lasts until it is destroyed, until you die, or until you dismiss it as a Bonus Action.
{@i From Dragon+, Made by Aris}

item.name: Cheese With Everything
entries: {@i “Everything?”}

This pizza carries with it extra cheese, the wonders of the earth, and the beauty of the stars. As part of a short or long rest, you may eat this pie. The pie sates you for time immemorial. You no longer need to eat.
{@i Made by Miel}

item.name: Duelist's Grace
entries: {@i This ornate rapier of elven make has been corrupted by the negative energy of its wielder.}
This rapier has a +3 bonus to its attack and damage rolls. On a hit, it deals an additional {@dice 2d6} Necrotic damage.

When you are targeted by a ranged attack, you can take a Reaction to brandish Duelist's Grace and add {@dice 1d4} to your Armor Class until the end of the turn. If the attack misses, the ranged missile is deflected back at the attacking creature, and that creature takes {@dice 3d8} damage of the same type of the attack you deflected; if there is more than one type of damage present in the triggering attack, the GM decides what damage the deflected attack does.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Fish Lighter Classic
entries: This Tiny automatic torch is shaped like a fish, and is fitted with extremely bright magical lights. A creature holding this item can use a Utilize action to make it start or stop shedding Bright Light for 40 feet and Dim Light for 40 feet further. While it is shedding light, it also emits an extremely loud noise of a screaming human, which can be heard from up to 300 feet away.
Any creature within 5 feet of this object while it is shedding light is Blinded and Deafened until it is either no longer within 5 feet or the torch is deactivated.
{@i Stolen from V, renamed by June}

item.name: Handkerchief of Darkness
entries: This black velvet handkerchief is inscribed with a repeating pattern of closed eyes. The handkerchief normally appears to be the size of a napkin, but it can be unfolded to the size of a tablecloth.

When the handkerchief is folded to the size of a napkin, you can drop or throw it as a Magic action, creating an area of magical darkness around the handkerchief identical to that created by a Darkness spell. The Darkness endures until the handkerchief is picked up by a creature.

When the handkerchief is unfolded to the size of a tablecloth, you can throw it as an action to have it magically fly high in the air. The handkerchief hovers 50 feet above you and a shadowy fog billows out of it, filling a cylinder 100 feet high and 1,000 feet across, with the handkerchief at its center. The area of the fog is lightly obscured, and creatures that suffer negative effects while in sunlight ignore those effects while within the fog. The fog lasts until the handkerchief is picked up by a creature (typically one that can fly).
{@i From Dragon+, Made by Aris}

item.name: Helm of the Slumbering Lord
entries: While attuned to this helm, your attack rolls and saving throws only suffer a -1 penalty per level of Exhaustion, you do not need to sleep, and are immune to the Asleep condition.

As a Bonus Action, you may gain a level of Exhaustion and inflict the following effects on any number of creatures you can see within a 20-foot Emanation, based on how many levels of Exhaustion you have. These effects resolve before you gain the level of Exhaustion. Any levels of Exhaustion gained in this way can be reduced by finishing a Long Rest or being revived from death, and cannot be removed in any other way.
1+ levels of Exhaustion: The target must make a DC 17 Intelligence saving throw, taking {@dice 2d6} Psychic damage per level of Exhaustion you have, halved on a success.
2+ levels of Exhaustion: The target must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or have Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the end of your next turn.
3+ levels of Exhaustion: The target must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or have the Immobilized condition until the end of its next turn.

4+ levels of Exhaustion: The target must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or have the Asleep condition for 1 minute. While asleep, the target cannot be woken up by taking damage. At the end of each of its turns, it can repeat the save, ending the condition on a success.
5+ levels of Exhaustion: The target must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, gaining 2 Exhaustion levels on a failure, or 1 on a success.

6 levels of Exhaustion: If the target has the Asleep condition and has less than 250 Hit Points, it must succeed on a DC 17 Charisma saving throw or be killed instantly. Otherwise, it takes {@dice 20d10} Psychic damage.
{@i Made by Ellie}

item.name: Imminent Light
entries: This staff is finely crafted of silvered oak and adorned with a headpiece centered around a large amethyst. It has 25 charges and can be wielded as a magic Quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. While you hold it, you gain a +2 bonus to AC and have the benefit of a Protection from Evil and Good spell. While attuned to the staff, you do not need to eat or drink.

{@b Spells.} While holding the staff , you can cast one of the spells on the following table from it. Use +5 for the spellcasting ability modifier. The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.
{@b Spell - Charge Cost}
Cure Wounds - 1 per spell slot level, up to 4
Lesser Restoration - 2
Mass Cure Wounds - 5

{@b Radiant Wave.} As a Bonus action, by expending 10 charges from the staff, you cause a wave of radiant energy to sweep out from you in a radius of 50 feet. Any Undead or Fiend in the area takes {@dice 2d8} Radiant damage and suffers the effect of a Divine Word spell.
{@b Regaining Charges.} The staff regains {@dice 4d6} + 2 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 20, the staff regains {@dice 1d12} + 1 charges.

{@b Curse.} This staff is cursed and possessed by the will of Zariel. Becoming attuned to it extends the curse to you. While you are cursed, any effect that restores Hit Points (whether magical or mundane) restores only half the normal number of Hit Points to you. Your scars and bruises remain visible for some time even after you are at full Hit Points.

If you are a Paladin or Cleric, you gradually lose your connection to your god or source of divine inspiration. For each month that you possess the staff, any Paladin or Cleric class features that are divinely granted (including Spellcasting) function at one level lower than normal. A Paladin or Cleric who remains cursed by the staff long enough eventually loses all their Spellcasting and divine class features.

{@b Eternal Damnation.} The routine effects of the curse can be ended simply by ending your attunement to the staff. However, a creature cursed by the staff has its soul bonded to Zariel until another creature attunes to the staff and gains the curse. Upon the bonded creature’s death, its soul travels to Avernus and becomes the property of Zariel, with results determined by the DM.
If a creature willingly retains the curse by maintaining attunement to the staff, then dies with the staff still attuned and in hand, the curse is permanently removed from the staff.
{@i From Dragon+, Adapted by Aris}

item.name: Lunistra, the Heartstar
entries: Lunistra is a sentient golden +2 breastplate shaped in the likeness of a lupinal guardinal’s visage, with a golden eight-pointed star adorning its forehead.

While wearing Lunistra, you are immune to effects that would instantly kill you, such as Power Word Kill or a Vorpal Sword's Critical Hit, and you cannot accrue Exhaustion levels. When you reach 0 Hit Points while wearing Lunistra, Lunistra heals you as though it casted Cure Wounds (level 3 version), using its Charisma as its spellcasting ability. Once Lunistra heals you this way, it cannot do so again until dawn.

{@b Sentience}. Lunistra, the Heartstar is a sentient Neutral Good breastplate with an Intelligence of 12, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 12. Lunistra can hear and see with normal vision out to a range of 30 feet. It communicates with its wielder by transmitting emotions. It understands Celestial and Common.
{@b Personality}. When donned by a creature of good alignment, Lunistra exudes unflinching confidence in its ability to protect the wearer from grave harm.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

item.name: Mystery Potion (Legendary)
entries: {@i Typically made as a byproduct by alchemists, this potion is fully unpredictable. Swirling this liquid causes its color to change rapidly between a variety of vibrant colors. Its smell is purely alchemical, providing no insight as to what could be inside.}
When you drink this potion, roll on the table below, gaining the effects of drinking or applying the rolled potion.

A creature can spend 1 hour studying the potion to attempt to identify and stabilize its effects. After the hour is done, the creature must make a DC 30 Intelligence (Alchemist's Supplies) check. On a pass, roll on the table transforming the Mystery Potion into the rolled potion.
{@dice 1d100} | Potion Effect
1     | Potion of Climbing
2     | Potion of Comprehension
3     | Potion of Healing
4     | Potion of Watchful Rest
5     | Bottled Breath
6     | Oil of Slipperiness
7     | Philter of Adoration
8     | Potion of Acid Resistance
9     | Potion of Advantage
10    | Potion of Animal Friendship
11    | Potion of Cold Resistance
12    | Potion of Fire Breath
13    | Potion of Fire Resistance
14    | Potion of Force Resistance
15    | Potion of Greater Healing
16    | Potion of Growth
17    | Potion of Hill Giant Strength
18    | Potion of Lightning Resistance
19    | Potion of Necrotic Resistance
20    | Potion of Poison
21    | Potion of Poison Resistance
22    | Potion of Polychromy
23    | Potion of Psionic Fortitude
24    | Potion of Psychic Resistance
25    | Potion of Pugilism
26    | Potion of Radiant Resistance
27    | Potion of Thunder Resistance
28    | Potion of Water Breathing
29    | Elixir of Health
30    | Oil of Etherealness
31    | Potion of Clairvoyance
32    | Potion of Diminution
32    | Potion of Fire Giant Strength
33    | Potion of Frost Giant Strength
34    | Potion of Gaseous Form
35    | Potion of Heroism
36    | Potion of Invisibility
37    | Potion of Invulnerability
38    | Potion of Mind Control (beast)
39    | Potion of Mind Control (humanoid)
40    | Potion of Mind Reading
41    | Potion of Stone Giant Strength
42    | Potion of Superior Healing
43    | Oil of Sharpness
44    | Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
45    | Potion of Flying
46    | Potion of Greater Invisibility
47    | Potion of Longevity
48    | Potion of Mind Control (monster)
49    | Potion of Speed
50    | Potion of Supreme Healing
51    | Potion of Vitality
52    | Potion of Dragon's Majesty
53    | Potion of Giant Size
54    | Potion of Storm Giant Strength
55-00 | Roll Again
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Octavo of Keeping
entries: In the arcanaloths’ endless quest for knowledge and power, no magic item is as highly sought after as the Books of Keeping, in which the true names of all yugoloths are scribed. Over eons of research, a few arcanaloths have managed to record the true names of some of their weaker kin in smaller tomes, creating tiny versions of the Books of Keeping for their own purposes. Each octavo of keeping contains the names of {@dice 1d4} + 1 yugoloths, as well as ritual instructions for summoning them written in a complex code combining Infernal and Abyssal. These special instructions are unique to each yugoloth. For each name in an octavo, roll a {@dice d6} to determine what kind of yugoloth it is:
{@b {@dice d6} Yugoloth}
1 Mezzoloth
2 Nycaloth
3 Dhergoloth
4 Canoloth
5 Hydroloth
6 Yagnoloth

A creature that can read both Infernal and Abyssal must succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence (Arcana) check to decode each ritual in the {@i Octavo of Keeping}. Any creature that decodes a ritual can use the octavo thereafter to summon the yugoloth named by the ritual. This requires 1 hour, rare incense and alchemical powders worth 5,000 gp, and the blood sacrifice of a creature of at least Medium size. When the ritual is completed, the yugoloth appears from the Lower Planes and the summoner must make an Intelligence (Arcana) check contested by the yugoloth’s Intelligence check. If the sacrificed creature was a humanoid of Intelligence 4 or greater, the summoner has advantage on the check.

If the yugoloth wins the opposed check, the ritual was performed improperly and the fiend is free to act as it wishes. This typically involves attempting to kill the summoner and destroy the octavo (see below). If the summoner wins or ties the check, the yugoloth cannot attack or cast spells on the summoner (though any other creatures in the area are fair game) and is bound to obey the summoner for 24 hours. However, the yugoloth always bends and misinterprets the summoner’s commands if it can, and the DM might call for Charisma checks to threaten, coax, or trick the yugoloth into doing exactly what the summoner wants. After 24 hours, the yugoloth vanishes back to Gehenna.

If an octavo of keeping is used multiple times, the same yugoloths are summoned again and again, even if they were previously destroyed. This greatly annoys the affected yugoloths, which constantly scheme for revenge. If a summoned yugoloth can get its hands on the octavo, it can tear the book in half as an action, destroying it and summoning all the yugoloths whose names are written in the book. Those fiends appear at the start of the yugoloth’s next turn, work together to kill the summoner and its allies, then go on a rampage of murder and destruction before returning to Gehenna 24 hours later.
{@i From Dragon+, Made by Aris}

item.name: Orb of Greening
entries: As a Magic action you can throw this orb at a point within 120 feet causing it to explode. Each creature in a 60-foot-radius Sphere must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw, gaining the Green story record on a failure.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Prismatic Dragon Scale
entries: When you cast Prismatic Wall while holding this scale, you can choose to destroy it and imbue the spell with its power. The duration of the spell becomes Until Dispelled.
{@i Based off 3.5e lore. Made by June}

item.name: Rainbow Prism
entries: The Prism can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells. While holding it, you have Resistance to Radiant damage and emit a 15-foot Emanation of sunlight. The sunlight's area is Bright Light and sheds Dim Light for an additional 15 feet.

{@b Spells.} The prism has 15 charges. While holding the prism, you can cast one of the spells on the following table from it, using your spell save DC and spell attack modifier. The table indicates how many charges you must expend to cast the spell.
Spell               		| Charge Cost
Prismatic Spray     	| 7
Sunbeam             	| 6
Wall of Light       	| 5
Fount of Moonlight  	| 4
Hypnotic Pattern    	| 3
Moonbeam            	| 2
Daylight            	| 2
Color Spray         	| 1
Light               		| 0

{@b Regaining Charges.} The prism regains {@dice 1d6} + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, the prism shatters into shards of light and is destroyed.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Ring of Blueberry Resistance
entries: While attuned to this ring, you gain resistance to Blueberry damage. You cannot benefit from another Ring of Resistance while attuned.

item.name: Rod of Nightmares
entries: This rod is constructed of an ebony material and takes the form of a horrible, writhing form with a skull-like unearthly head that is unsettling to behold.

{@b Nightmare.} Any creature that ends its turn within 20 feet of the rod feels a grave sense of unease. That creature must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or receive the effects of the Dream spell the next time it falls asleep, where it is visited by a terrifying messenger. If you are attuned to the rod, you are immune to this effect. A creature that passes its saving throw automatically passes all saves against this feature for the next 24 hours. Remove Curse or similar magic can remove the nightmare.

{@b Wail of the Banshee.} You can utter a command word that causes the rod to emit a horrid, inhuman scream. This has no effect on Constructs and Undead. All other creatures (including you) within a 30 foot radius of this terrible wail and can hear it believe that their worst nightmares have become reality. Each affected creature must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, if the target has 100 Hit Points or fewer, it drops to 0 Hit Points. On a success, a creature takes {@dice 3d6} Psychic damage. This wail can be used 3 times and regains all uses daily at dawn.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Aris}

item.name: Soda of Devastation
entries: {@i soda pop}
As a Magic action, you can shake the soda. Everytime you shake it, there is a percent chance equal to the times its been shaken that it explodes.
When you take the Attack action, you can replace an attack with throwing the can up to 300 feet, where it instantly explodes.

When the can explodes, all creatures within a 5 foot radius must make a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw. The radius increases by 5 feet every time it is shaken. On a failure it takes {@dice 1d10} Force damage per time the can was shaken, or half as much on a success.
A nonmagical object that isn't being worn or carried also takes the damage if it's in the area.
Every day at dawn, the can settles down, resetting as if it had not been shaken.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: True Polymorph Potion
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effects of the True Polymorph spell for 1 hour (no concentration required). The creature you turn into is chosen by rolling on the following table and ignores the Challenge Rating restriction.
When you transform, you gain a max number of Temporary Hit Points equal to 70 + twice your level (or 70 + twice your Challenge Rating if you don't have a level).
{@dice 1d100}
1   | Dracolich
2   | Spy Master
3   | Ultroloth
4   | Treant
5   | Aberrant Cultist
6   | Death Tyrant
7   | Noble Prodigy
8   | Young Gold Dragon
9   | Arcanaloth
10  | Young Red Dragon
11  | Cockatrice Regent
12  | Balor
13  | Vampire Umbral Lord
14  | Death Knight
15  | Green Slaad
16  | Efreeti
17  | Goristro
18  | Brazen Gorgon
19  | Cyclops Oracle
20  | Sphinx of Lore
21  | Vampire Nightbringer
22  | Death Slaad
23  | Shadow Dragon
24  | Adult Bronze Dragon
25  | Sphinx of Valor
26  | Ice Devil
27  | Adult Red Dragon
28  | Erinyes
29  | Glabrezu
30  | Githyanki Knight
31  | Planetar
32  | Iron Golem
33  | Abominable Yeti
34  | Pirate Admiral
35  | Beholder
36  | Tyrannosaurus Rex
37  | Roc
38  | Death Cultist
39  | Aboleth
40  | Cloaker
41  | Ancient Brass Dragon
42  | Stone Golem
43  | Elemental Cultist
44  | Young Green Dragon
45  | Spirit Naga
46  | Young Bronze Dragon
47  | Bone Devil
48  | Nycaloth
49  | Adult Blue Dragon
50  | Adult Silver Dragon
51  | Purple Worm
52  | Young Blue Dragon
53  | Adult Copper Dragon
54  | Mummy Lord
55  | Remorhaz
56  | Adult Gold Dragon
57  | Ancient White Dragon
58  | Dragon Turtle
59  | Young Silver Dragon
60  | Sphinx of Secrets
61  | Hezrou
62  | Dire Worg
63  | Demilich
64  | Frost Giant
65  | Storm Giant
66  | Haunting Revenant
67  | Vampire
68  | Bandit Crime Lord
69  | Hydra
70  | Archpriest
71  | Chain Devil
72  | Warrior Commander
73  | Pit Fiend
74  | Marilith
75  | Yochlol
76  | Adult Brass Dragon
77  | Thri-kreen Psion
78  | Guardian Naga
79  | Gray Slaad
80  | Marid
81  | Salamander Inferno Master
82  | Gulthias Blight
83  | Horned Devil
84  | Behir
85  | Cloud Giant
86  | Assassin
87  | Deva
88  | Nalfeshnee
89  | Djinni
90  | Githzerai Psion
91  | Fomorian
92  | Adult White Dragon
93  | Dao
94  | Archmage
95  | Rakshasa
96  | Clay Golem
97  | Berserker Commander
98  | Fiend Cultist
99  | Cultist Hierophant
100 | Gnoll Demoniac
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Ultroloth’s Eye
entries: Resembling a large, pale fire opal, this gem is actually a magically preserved eye from an ultroloth—one of the yugoloths’ secretive lords.

When you hold the ultroloth’s eye up to one of your own eyes and look through it, you feel terrible pain, and you take {@dice 1d6} Poison damage and {@dice 1d6} Acid damage at the end of each of your turns in which you look through the eye. While looking through the eye, you are colorblind but have Truesight out to a range of 120 feet.
{@b Fiendish Insight.} You also gain an additional benefit based on which eye you hold the ultroloth’s eye up to. (If you have more than two eyes, the DM rolls randomly to determine the effect.)

If the ultroloth’s eye is held to your left eye, you can use a Bonus Action to look at a creature of Intelligence 4 or greater. While doing so, you learn the name and appearance of the mortal being that creature most loves and respects. If the ultroloth’s eye is held to your right eye, you can use a Bonus Action to look at a creature of Intelligence 4 or greater. While doing so, you learn the thing that creature most fears.
If a creature you look at with either of these features has telepathy, it is aware of what you’re doing and can shield itself from your scrutiny with a successful DC 17 Wisdom saving throw.

{@b Permanent Placement.} While attuned to the ultroloth’s eye, you can gouge out your own eye and press the gem into the empty socket. You take {@dice 6d6} Acid damage and {@dice 6d6} Poison damage by doing so, but if you survive, the eye grafts itself into your head. A grafted eye gives you the permanent benefit of its features (depending on which eye socket it replaces) and does not deal damage when you use it. It no longer counts against the number of magic items you can attune to, and remains a part of you until you die.
{@i From Dragon+, Made by Aris}

item.name: Wheel of Interplanar Travel
entries: This wheel must be installed into the helm of a vehicle. While attuned to the wheel and holding onto it, you can cast Planar Navigation (Save DC 25). When cast this way, it can only target the vehicle it is installed in. Additionally, it can be teleported onto the land, or into the air depending on the vehicle. Once used, this spell cannot be cast this way for {@dice 3d4} days.

When cast this way, you still need to maintain Concentration on the wheel. If you are forced to make a Constitution Saving throw to maintain Concentration on the wheel, you can instead roll a Constitution (Navigator's Tools) check against the save DC for the Concentration save. If you are forced to roll on the mishap table and roll a 1, the wheel breaks and has to be fixed, costing 100 gp for every hit point of damage dealt in materials and 3 days of work.
As a Magic action you can touch a willing spellcaster. That creature attunes to the wheel immediately, and your attunement to it ends.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Bag of Rats
entries: {@i "Only a fool would open this bag" - Elminster}

{@i Sewn from the flesh of Sneel, former deity of rats, this bag is constantly thrashing. The inside of the bag has never been seen, but some say it is a portal to the Black Sewers, where Raxivort spends his days making rats from pure chaos.}
The Bag of Rats has an AC of 11, and 100 Hit Points. On Initiative 0, the Bag of Rats repairs itself, regaining all of its Hit Points.

{@b Interplanar Nature.} The Bag of Rats is unable to enter any space that prevents interplanar travel. If the Bag of Rats is in an area and a Gate Seal or similar magic is cast on its space, it is summoned to the nearest place on the same plane that isn't protected from interplanar travel. If no such space exists on the current plane, the Bag of Rats is immediately summoned to a random plane of existence.
{@b Pack Nature.} All Rats and Swarms of Rats are friendly to all other Rats and have no allegiance to a creature that lets them out of the bag, often being hostile to it.

{@b Quick Grab.} As a Magic action you reach into the bag, taking {@dice 1d4-2} (min 0) Piercing damage from being bit, to summon a Rat in your hand. As part of the action you can throw the rat up to 60 feet.

{@b Deploy Rodents.} As a Magic action you can attempt to quickly open and close the bag, launching out a swarm of Rats at a point you can see within 30 feet. A Swarm of Rats is summoned from the bag at the point. Additionally make a DC 15 Strength (Athletics) check. On a failure, you cannot close the bag. Every turn the bag is open, a Swarm of Rats is summoned at the entrance of the bag. A creature can make a DC 15 Athletics check to attempt to close the bag.

Swarm Breach. If the bag remains open for 1 minute or has 0 Hit Points, it ruptures exploding into a 5-foot-diameter portal. The portal functions as a permanent gate spell. One per round on Initiative count 20, the portal summons a Swarm of Rats. The Bag of Rats reforms {@dice 4d10} days after it ruptures, turning a random Swarm of Rats on any plane into a new bag, ending the gate.
{@b Destroying the Bag.} The bag of rats can only be destroyed if Raxivort is killed while the Swarm Breach gate is open, or a creature holds the bag shut for 5 years.
{@i From WM3, by June}

item.name: Chaos Emerald
entries: The Chaos Emeralds are seven magical gemstones, usually scattered across the realms. They are strong alone, but become more powerful together. If a creature attunes to multiple Chaos Emeralds at once, they share an attunement slot. Each gem has minor unique powers, and gathering them offers greater power. Any spells cast through the emerald have a DC of 14 + the number of attuned Emeralds, and a to-hit bonus of 6 + the number of attuned emeralds.

Each Emerald has two properties based on its color. While attuned to an Emerald, you gain the creature type listed for its color, in addition to any types you had before, and you can cast the listed spell at Level 5. Once you've cast any of the spells, you can't cast another until the next dawn.
{@i Pink:} Humanoid - Hold Person
{@i Orange:} Construct - Summon Construct
{@i White:} Celestial - Jallarzi's Storm of  Radiance
{@i Blue:} Dragon - Scrying
{@i Cyan:} Elemental - Songal's Elemental Suffusion
{@i Red:} Fiend - Infernal Calling
{@i Green:} Undead - Danse Macabre
If you attune to multiple Emeralds at once, you gain additional benefits as listed below.
{@b 1+ Emeralds:} When you hit a creature with an attack roll, it takes an additional {@dice 1d4} Force damage.
{@b 2+ Emeralds:} You gain Resistance to Force damage.
{@b 3+ Emeralds:} Your Speed increases by 5 feet.
{@b 4+ Emeralds:} You can cast Steel Wind Strike at will.
{@b 5+ Emeralds:} You gain a Fly Speed of 30 feet.
{@b 6+ Emeralds:} You gain Immunity to Force damage.
{@b 7 Emeralds:} You can cast Wish. Once you do so, the seven emeralds leave your possession and scatter across random locations throughout the multiverse.

The Chaos Emeralds are naturally drawn to each other. If you are attuned to at least one Emerald, you become aware of any Emeralds within one mile of you, but not where they are or who holds then. If you have at least three, you can vaguely sense the location of any Emerald within a hundred miles - roughly well enough to pinpoint a city or town, but no narrower than that. If you hold five, you can sense Emeralds anywhere on the same plane of existence as you. If you hold six, you can sense exactly where the final Emerald is, and who has it, as long as you're on the same plane of existence.
{@b Destroying the Emeralds.} The Emeralds can only be destroyed if a creature were to gather all seven, then use them to Wish themselves out of existence. Doing so instantly kills the caster.
{@i By Lily}

item.name: Egg of Lolth
entries: The egg of Lolth is a potent artifact with a long history. Originally nothing more than a cursed item that compelled its wielder to fling himself through a gate to the Abyss, its long exposure to the chaotic energies of the Demonweb Pits has transformed it into the deadly device it is today.
This artifact functions as a Material component for the purpose of casting Plane Shift, but only if the destination is the Demonweb Pits.
While inactive, the artifact appears to be a smooth, fist sized platinum egg. When touched, it vibrates slightly, and faint sounds of scratching can be heard coming from within.

To activate this item, a casting of Remove Curse on it is required. When done so, it turns into a shuddering, tumescent bag of spider silk. On the following round, the bag tears itself open, summoning 5 Swarms of Spiders (Swarm of Insects with the Fiend type, MM'25) and 1 Darkweaver (with the Fiend type). Then, for the next minute, during each round, 1 Giant Spider (with the Fiend type) is summoned by the egg. After which, the artifact reverts to inactivity and cannot be activated again until the next Midnight.

Creatures summoned by the egg appear in an unoccupied space on the ground within 10 feet of the egg and are Hostile to non-drow creatures. After 10 Minutes, all spiders summoned by the egg are cast back into the Abyss.
While the artifact is activated, any creature that touches it is automatically transported to the Demonweb Pits.
Every time the artifact goes inactive, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, it is instantly teleported to a location in the Demonweb Pits or the Material Plane of Lolth's choosing.
{@b Destroying the Artifact}. The egg has to be embraced by Corellon as one of his own and is destroyed.
{@i Made by Chrys}

item.name: Mystery Potion (Artifact)
entries: {@i Mystra help you. This potion was made by the world's greatest alchemist when she accidentally inverted her bag of holding full of supplies into Iggwilv's Cauldron. The resulting concoction was bottled many times and transported across the realms.}
When you drink this you gain the effects of every potion on the Mystery Potion (Legendary) table (ignoring roll again).
{@b Destroying the Potion.} The potion can only be destroyed by being drunk by a creature, where it immediately becomes an empty bottle.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Staff of the Lower Planes
entries: Fiendish lore says that only one other yugoloth was ever powerful enough to challenge the General of Gehenna: an enormous, magically augmented oinoloth named Anthraxus the Decayed, lord of plagues. Anthraxus’s fate is lost to time, but its diabolical essence survives, soaked into the wood of the staff it once bore.
The Staff of the Lower Planes is made of gray ash seamlessly grafted to a ram’s skull, both as hard as iron. It can magically change in size to better conform to the grip of its user.

{@b Dark Attunement.} The process of attuning to the staff involves first soaking it in the water of the River Styx. This causes the staff to momentarily animate, whereupon the ram’s head bites the nearest creature (+10 to hit, {@dice 1d8} Piercing damage plus {@dice 3d6} Necrotic damage). If this damage reduces the creature to 0 Hit Points, it dies instantly. Otherwise, that creature becomes attuned to the staff and gains instinctive knowledge of its powers.

{@b Magic Quarterstaff.} Whether the wielder is attuned or not, the Staff of the Lower Planes functions as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. In addition, any creature hit by the staff is affected as if it had been hit by the Contagion spell (save DC 17).
Any creature that possesses the staff for more than one hour without attuning to it is affected as if it had been hit by the Contagion spell (save DC 17).
{@b Random Properties.} The Staff of the Lower Planes has the following random properties:
1 minor beneficial property
1 major beneficial property
1 minor detrimental property
1 major detrimental property

{@b Spells.} The staff has 8 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. While holding it, you can use an action and expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it (save DC 17): Dominate Beast (1 charge), Dominate Monster (3 charges), Dominate Person (1 charge), Geas (2 charges), Giant Insect (2 charges), Insect Plague (2 charges), Suggestion (1 charge), or Mass Suggestion (3 charges).
Spells cast from the staff that impose the charmed condition can affect Fiends normally immune to that condition.

{@b Styx Master.} While you hold the staff, you are immune to the negative effects of the River Styx. If you drink from that river, you know the location of all creatures currently on or within its waters, and you can read the memories of any creature whose name you know and which has been subject to the river’s mind-draining effects within the last 24 hours. The dissolved memories are murky and fragmentary, and the DM might call for Intelligence checks to read specific memories.

{@b Master of the Lower Planes.} While you are holding the staff, you can use a Magic action to focus and sense the direction to any portals leading to, from, or between the Lower Planes within 1,000 feet of you. You can also sense the specific plane that each such portal leads to.

{@b Punish Fiends.} If you reduce a Fiend to 0 Hit Points with a weapon attack made using the staff, that Fiend is permanently transformed into a larva when it returns to its home plane. All intelligent Fiends know and fear this power of the staff.

{@b Final Wish.} While you are holding the staff, you can cast a Wish spell as a Magic action. Doing so causes you to lose your Attunement to the staff, and you are stricken by the detrimental effects as if you had drunk from the River Styx (no saving throw). Only another Wish or similar effect can cure this affliction. If you use this feature to wish for Anthraxus to be resurrected, your soul goes to Gehenna and your body transforms into a magically augmented Oinoloth, becoming the corporeal form of Anthraxus the Decayed.

{@b Undone by Disease.} While you are attuned to the staff, you become immune to all Magical Contagions. However, if the staff ever leaves your side, you immediately suffer from some of the terrible afflictions that the item’s power has long held at bay. At the end of each turn when the staff is more than 5 feet away from you, you must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw or be inflicted by the Contagion spell. Roll a {@dice d6} to determine what ability you gain Disadvantage on saving throws for.

Once you are suffering from 3 Magical Contagions, you stop gaining new ones. No effect short of a Wish spell or similar magic can remove or ameliorate these Magical Contagions until the staff is returned to you.

{@b Destroying the Staff.} The Staff of the Lower Planes is destroyed if it is submerged for one week in the water of a particular magic stream in Elysium. The name of this stream is recorded in only a few places in the multiverse, including the Book of Exalted Deeds.

Each round that the staff is submerged in the stream, larvae and other unintelligent fiends emerge from the wood of the staff and attack any creatures they can see. All yugoloths in the multiverse of Intelligence 18 or higher become aware that the staff is in danger, and they attempt to converge on Elysium to rescue it.
{@i From Dragon+, Ported by Aris}

item.name: Teeth of Dahlver-Nar
entries: The following are additional teeth for the bag:
| Azahu's Tooth (needle-sharp demon fang) | 3 Bodaks | Your skin becomes cold to the touch, and the inside of your mouth turns black. You count as 1 size larger for the purposes of grappling. |

| Shami-Amorae's Tooth (ivory canine) | 1 Succubus | Your skin becomes pale and your hair golden. The tooth has 3 charges. As a Magic action, you can expend 1 charge to cast Suggestion (DC 18). The tooth regains all expended charges daily at dawn. You gain the following flaw: "I desire companionship and attention more than anything else." |

| Ansitif's Tooth (charcoal demon fang) | 1 Balor | The tooth has 3 charges. As a Magic action, you can expend 1 charge to cast Desecrate (DC 18). The tooth regains all expended charges daily at dawn. |

| Astaroth's Tooth (fiendish cuspid) | 1 Glabrezu | The tooth has 3 charges. As a Magic action, you can expend 1 charge to cast Divination (DC 18). The tooth regains all expended charges daily at dawn. |
| Cabiri's Tooth (alien demon tooth) | 1 Sibriex | You can use the tooth to cast the Clairvoyance or Scrying spell. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn. |
{@i From Savage Tides}

item.name: The Apparatus
entries: Little is known about the legendary alchemist who created the very first Apparatus. Many Mordentish folktales confuse him with Count Strahd von Zarovich, who is also said to have visited the region at the time. The alchemist came to Mordentshire when it was still part of the Material Plane. Haunted by Lord Godefroy, he crafted this terrible artifact he called the Apparatus, which could separate the soul from a living body, translocate souls from one body to another, and otherwise manipulate the substance and energy of spirits both living and undead. Lord Godefroy hoped the Apparatus would send all spirits (including him) to their deserved rests, but the artifact had the opposite effect: its necromantic energy washed through the region around Mordentshire, killing every living being in it, and destroying the Apparatus. As the Mists rose, Lord Godefroy became lord over a land of ghosts, haunted by the spirits whose deaths he had caused. Now all living souls that dwell in Mordent are doomed to haunt the domain's Darklord long after the demise of their mortal bodies.

This towering machine is over 30 feet tall and is 20 feet wide at its base. A glass globe with a 15-foot diameter is supported 15 feet above the floor by a massive tripod of wood and steel. The globe encases a large, spinning ball of sulfur. A network of cold-forged steel rings tops the sphere. Two lesser glass globes, each with a 6-foot diameter, suspended below. A narrow neck connects the lesser globes, like an hourglass on its side. A 3-foot trapdoor in the bottom of each lesser globe allows entry. A hollow steel arch links each lesser globe to the mother sphere above. A ring of small glass spheres, each ranging from 1 to 3 feet in diameter, surrounds the base of the tripod. A convoluted network of metal and glass rods, wires, tubes and plates joins the small spheres to the two lesser globes.

To use any of the Apparatus' powers, it must first be fully charged by receiving 12+ lightning strikes within a span of 3 hours. This must be natural, nonmagical lightning, so the Apparatus is usually connected to numerous lightning rods. This electrical energy is then converted into arcane power.
The Apparatus has several powers, as listed below:

{@b Transpossession.} The most basic of these is transpossession. A creature must be placed in each of the lesser globes. On the 12th lightning strike, the spirits of these subjects are exchanged (no saving throw). This functions like the magic jar spell for the effects of switching spirits. Both subjects fall Asleep for {@dice 1d4} x 10 minutes.

{@b Split Spirit.} A second ability ascribed to the Apparatus also requires the use of a Rod of Rastinon, a minor artifact created by the alchemist as a form of key for his machine. If a Rod of Rastinon is inserted in the Apparatus, then on the 12th strike of lightning a single subject in one of the lesser globes is split into two separate creatures, one Evil, the other Good. The creatures have identical game statistics and are nearly identical physically, though their appearance may subtly reflect their inner nature. Both creatures are always true to their moral alignment (One of these becomes an NPC under the GMs control, or an NPC under the player's control with GM supervision). Each also despises the other.

{@b Conjoin Spirit.} If two subjects are placed in the lesser spheres when the crystalline Rod of Houtras is used, then the Apparatus merges their spirits in a single body. If the Apparatus had previously split the subjects, then the original creature is restored. If two individuals are merged, then both minds occupy the body of the subject with the more forceful personality (contested Charisma check). The abandoned body is reduced to a lifeless shell.

{@b Destroying the Apparatus.} Unlike most artifacts, the Apparatus is actually extremely fragile. Each time it is used, its builder must make an Intelligence check (DC 18). If the builder fails this check, the Apparatus explodes. All creatures in a 30-foot Emanation must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 25d6} Lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. All objects in the area are also damaged.

The Rod of Rastinon and Rod of Houtras cannot be destroyed, but are useless without the Apparatus. Many occult scholars believe that the tome containing the alchemist's schematics is the true cursed artifact, not the Apparatus itself.
{@i From Ravenloft, Made by Aris}

item.name: The Sword of Eons' End
entries: The end of an era, and the beginning of one new. The Sword of Eons' End is a blade of pure white. Forged from the divine spark of an artificial god, it gleams brilliantly in the sun.
You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
{@b One for All.} When you hit an enemy with this weapon, its All as One trait is negated until the end of your next turn (if it has that trait).

{@b Destroying the Sword.} What was forged by a god of law can be undone only by a god of chaos. The Sword of Eons' End can be damaged and destroyed as normal by a Lesser or Greater Deity of Chaotic alignment (ignoring its Artifact indestructibility).
{@i Made by Christopher}

item.name: Caligrostro Blade
entries: Caligrostro blades are not like other Legacy items, and many such items exist. They result from a Caligrostro demon possessing a magic weapon, changing and warping it with demonic power. Casting Dispel Evil and Good on the blade forces the demon to leave it. The blade then becomes a +1 weapon with no other properties.
The blade can choose to instantly attune to a creature it touches, ending any current attunements to it.
You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
{@b Brutal Feast (Level 3).} When you kill a creature with this weapon, you gain 10 Temporary Hit Points. Alternatively, you can feed one of the creature's Hit Die to the blade.
{@b Keen (Level 6).} Attacks with this weapon score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.

{@b Fiendish Shade (Level 6).} The Caligrostro can expend 6 Hit Dice that it has consumed, it creates a sword wraith under its control in an unoccupied space within 5 feet, and the blade it is possessing flies into the sword wraith's grasp and immediately attunes to the sword wraith (but it keeps its hit dice).

The sword wraith has the Fiend (Demon; Loumara) creature type, and it resembles its previous wielder but a more fiendish version. It also has a +2 bonus to attack rolls against its previous owner. It is Resistant to all damage except Poison damage (which it is Immune to), and any damage it takes, its previous wielder also takes.
While wielded by the Sword Wraith, the blade has the benefits of all its Hit Dice. Once slain, the blade acts under its wielder’s control for the next 24 hours.

{@b Demonic Strikes (Level 9).} Attacks with this weapon deal an extra {@dice 1d10} Necrotic damage on a hit. On a hit, you can choose to expend 1 Hit Die of the blade and roll it, and the target takes extra Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled.
{@b Black Possession (Level 12).} While Incapacitated, you ignore the effects of being Incapacitated and act under the blade's control.

{@b Dance Partner (Level 15).} As a Bonus Action you can release the blade. When you do so, the weapon begins to hover, flies up to 30 feet, and attacks one creature of your choice within 5 feet of itself. The weapon uses your attack roll and adds your ability modifier to damage rolls.

{@b Abyssal Doom (Level 18).} The weapon now ignores Resistance to Necrotic damage. When you use this weapon to attack a creature and roll a 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20} for the attack roll, you can instantly slay the creature and send its soul to the Abyss. A creature is immune to this effect if it has Immunity to Necrotic damage, lacks a soul, or is a Construct, Ooze, or Undead. Such a creature instead takes an extra 30 Necrotic damage from the hit. If the creature has Legendary Resistance, it can expend one daily use of that trait to avoid dying, taking the extra damage instead.
