{@b Legacy Rituals.} This weapon feeds on the lifeforce of its wielder, empowering the blade. Only one ritual is needed to unlock the abilities of the Caligrostro Blade, though two exist. The Blade’s features are only unlocked if the Caligrostro blade is fed a number of Hit Dice equal to the Level prerequisite. The blade loses Hit Dice when the attuned creature finishes a long rest, or the creature becomes unattuned. Certain features also cause the blade to lose hit dice.
{@i  {@b Bloodfed Blade:} As a Magic action, you may choose to lose up to three unexpended Hit Dice, feeding them to the blade.

{@i  {@b Lifeblood Thief:} At any time but no more than once per round, the blade can choose to force you to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, you must expend 1 Hit Die and roll it, and you take Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled. The blade gains 1 Hit Die. If you have no Hit Die to spend, you drop to 0 Hit Points.

{@b Curse.} While attuned to this weapon, you have Disadvantage on attack rolls made with weapons other than this one, and you can't willingly end your attunement to it. The Caligrostro is Chaotic Evil and has its own personality. It can telepathically communicate with creatures within 120 feet of it, including its wielder. It seeks to be fed, and will forcibly drain Hit Dice if it is not fed by its wielder. It will gladly betray its wielder for another stronger creature if it thinks it can be fed more, but shows of strength can impress it, and it can be won over. Some partnerships between wielder and Caligrostro have been known to form. The demon has 16 Int, 15 Wis, 19 Cha, 120 feet Darkvision, Chaotic Evil, and can speak Common and Abyssal. It cannot be detected by Divination magic.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

item.name: Facade
entries: The amulet appears however you choose, and you can change its appearance freely. It can be used as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

{@b Manifestation (Level 6).} As a Bonus Action, you can force any creatures of your choice within the area of an Illusion of yours to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, a target is either Frightened or Poisoned (your choice) until the end of your next turn, or until it leaves the area of the Illusion. This only affects creatures that can perceive the Illusion.

{@b Resonant Illusions (Level 7).} You can create illusory sounds to accompany any illusions you create. The sound cannot be loud enough to deal Thunder damage or Deafen a creature. You can choose for Tremorsense to detect your Illusions as if they were real. In addition, the Deafened condition is added as an option for Manifestation.
{@b Far Fetched (Level 8).} The range of your Illusion spells are doubled.
{@b Blind Spot (Level 10).} You can choose for Blindsight to detect your Illusions as if they were real. In addition, the Blinded condition is added as an option for Manifestation.

{@b Noble Phantasm (Level 13).} As a Bonus Action, or as part of the Bonus Action you use for Manifestation, you can deal {@dice 4d6} Psychic damage to each creature you choose that you are targeting with an Illusion or that are within the area of an Illusion of yours or within 5 feet of one, provided that the Illusion is of a creature or hazard that could logically deal damage, such as by attacking. The target perceives the damage as a type appropriate to the Illusion. Creatures that cannot perceive the Illusion are unaffected.

{@b Truth be Told (Level 15).} Physical interaction with your Illusions of your choice does not reveal them to be Illusions. You can choose for Truesight to detect your Illusions as if they were real, and creatures with Truesight do not automatically save against your Illusions. In addition, the Charmed condition is added as an option for Manifestation.

{@b Shadowshaper (Level 17).} You can use spell slots of any level to cast the Creation spell, not just Level 5 or greater slots, and you can cast the spell as a Magic action (instead of its normal casting time). In addition, the Grappled and Restrained conditions are added as options for Manifestation.
{@b The Prestige (Level 21).} When one of your Illusion spells ends, you can use your Reaction to choose one of the following effects:

All creatures within 5 feet of the illusion that ends must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is either Charmed, Confused, Dazed, Asleep, Frightened, Blinded, or takes {@dice 5d10} Psychic damage (your choice). If you choose a condition, it lasts until the end of the creature's next turn.
You can immediately cast another Illusion spell, but the spell must be of a higher level than the Illusion that ended, and have a casting time of 1 action.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} These three rituals must be completed to unlock Facade's Legacy features.
{@i  {@b Utilitarian Fabrication:} Use an illusion to save someone from grave danger, without bringing harm to anyone else. This unlocks its level 6-10 features.
{@i  {@b Hedonistic Fabrication:} Use an illusion to maximize your happiness and wellbeing to the utmost, perhaps even endangering others gravely. This unlocks the level 13 feature.

{@i  {@b Act Utilitarianism:} Complete an extremely deadly encounter with the caveat of only using illusory magic. You may only use spells of the Illusion school of magic for this combat. This unlocks it's level 15-21 features.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Family Fortune
entries: The fortune is a treasure worth 100gp. A creature attuned to the fortune can take a Magic action to magically "sell" the treasure, converting it into a pile of gold coins equal to its value, destroying the fortune.

While attuned the fortune begins to increase in value. Every bastion turn, it increases in value by 50gp to a max value of 300gp. If a creature dies while attuned to the fortune, the treasure disappears and stops increasing in value. If the creature is brought back to life within 1 month, they immediately reattune to the fortune and the fortune reappears in the space that it was left.
{@b Poor (Level 3).} The fortune now increases by 100gp per bastion turn, to a max of 600gp
{@b Livable (Level 5).} The fortune now increases by 200gp per bastion turn, to a max of 1,000gp
{@b Alright (Level 8).} The fortune now increases by 300gp per bastion turn, to a max of 3,000gp
{@b Comfortable (Level 11).} The fortune now increases by 500gp per bastion turn, to a max of 5,000gp
{@b Wealthy (Level 15).} The fortune now increases by 1,500gp per bastion turn, to a max of 15,000gp
{@b Rich (Level 17).} The fortune now increases by 3,000gp per bastion turn, to a max of 30,000gp
{@b 1% (Level 20).} The fortune now increases by 10,000gp per bastion turn, to a max of 50,000gp
{@b Cursed.} This fortune is cursed. Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by a Remove Curse spell or similar magic. Whenever your attunement ends to the item, the fortune vanishes.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} All of the abilities of the item are unlocked, requiring no rituals.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Hatchling's Blanket
entries: This small blanket is sized for a newborn snakelet, but it can change its size to fit whoever is wearing it, even if not attuned.
{@b Denial (Level 3).} While you are wrapped in the blanket, you are kept warm, and you have Resistance to Fire damage, and a +1 bonus to AC.

{@b Anger (Level 6).} As a Magic action, you can wave this blanket and cast Gust of Wind (DC 8 plus your Strength or Charisma modifier and Proficiency bonus). When you cast it in this way, it doesn't require concentration, but it lasts until the start of your next turn.

{@b Bargaining (Level 9).} As a Magic action, you can throw this blanket so that it lands in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you, causing the blanket to assume the form of a python in that space. The blanket uses the Rug of Smothering statblock. The blanket is under your control and shares your Initiative count, taking its turn immediately after yours. On your turn, you can mentally command the blanket (no action required) if it is within 60 feet of you and you don’t have the Incapacitated condition. The form ends if the blanket is reduced to 0 Hit Points or if you end it as a Bonus Action. The blanket can't assume the form again for 1 hour.
{@b Depression (Level 12).} You have Advantage on saving throws while you wear this blanket. In addition, while wearing the blanket, you are always kept dry, even while raining or submerged in water.

{@b Acceptance (Level 15).} As a Bonus Action while holding the blanket, you can cause the blanket to fly up to 30 feet to another creature and wrap itself around the creature. The creature gains the benefits of the Denial and Depression features while it is wrapped by the blanket. As a Bonus Action, you can call the blanket to you, where it ceaselessly tries to return to you, despite intervening distances. When it reaches you, it wraps around you.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} One ritual is needed to unlock the abilities of the Hatchling’s Blanket.
{@i  {@b Five Movements of Grief.} Complete the Symphony of Dreamers. Each movement of the symphony unlocks its corresponding feature.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Heart of Gears
entries: {@i This large metal amalgamation of electrum gears empowers any construct it is installed into. The nature of the gears causes constructs to take on a more mechanical nature, allowing greater consistency in all that they do.}
When attuned to, the Heart of Gears is installed into the creature, disappearing and becoming one with it. When unattuned, the Heart of Gears reappears next to the creature.
Your movement speed is increased by 10 feet. You have Advantage on saving throws against the Prone condition.

{@b Engrained Talent (Level 5).} The Heart of Gears holds mechanical knowledge on tools. When you finish a Short or Long Rest, choose a tool that you aren't Proficient with. You gain proficiency with that tool until the end of your next Short or Long Rest. This feature starts unlocked.

{@b Clockwork Consistency (Level 7).} The Heart of Gears gains the ability to give consistency to attacks you make. It has 4 charges and regains {@dice 1d4} charges daily at dawn. When you make an attack roll, you can expend a charge to forgo rolling the {@dice d20} to get a 10 on the die.
{@b Mechanical Nature (Level 9).} The Heart of Gears learns to adapt to absorb attacks made against you. Any Critical Hit against you becomes a normal hit.

{@b All-Around Programming (Level 11).} The Heart of Gears gains the ability to provide consistency in all that you do. You can use Clockwork Consistency on any {@dice D20} Test you make, not just attack rolls.
{@b Proper Oiling (Level 15).} The Heart of Gears improves, having 6 charges instead of 4, regaining {@dice 1d4+2} charges daily at dawn.

{@b Fine Tuned Machinery (Level 17).} The Heart of Gears improves allowing consistency all of the time. It has 8 charges instead of 6, and regains all charges daily at dawn. When you roll Initiative while the heart has no charges remaining, it regains {@dice 1d4+1} charges.
{@b Clockwork Mastery (Level 20).} The Heart of Gears learns when it is needed. When you make a {@dice D20} test, you can use a reaction to expend a charge to replace the number you roll with a 10.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} Three rituals are needed to unlock the abilities of the Heart of Gears.
{@i  {@b Advanced Parts:} You must defeat a Construct of CR 6 or higher, and spend an hour to incorporate its components into the heart. This unlocks the Level 7-9 features.

{@i  {@b Mechanus Machinery:} You must defeat a Modron of CR 10 or higher, and spend an hour to incorporate its components into the heart. Alternatively, you can incorporate 10k gp worth of parts bought from Mechanus. This unlocks the level 11-15 features.
{@i  {@b Inevitability:} You must defeat an Inevitable of CR 20 or higher, and spend an hour to incorporate its components into the heart. This unlocks the level 17-20 features.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Mystery Potion (Legacy)
entries: {@i Passed down from generations from one great alchemist to another. Its like a forever stew.}

This potion is filled with a Mystery Potion (Uncommon). Once drunk in this form, it instead is filled with Mystery Potion (Common). When drunk as a Mystery Potion (Common), the potion becomes empty, refilling as the highest unlocked rarity mystery potion at the next dawn.
{@b Crazy (Level 5).} The potion starts filled with Mystery Potion (Rare). When drunk in this form, it is refilled with Mystery Potion (Uncommon).
{@b Crazier (Level 11).} The potion starts filled with Mystery Potion (Very Rare). When drunk in this form, it is refilled with Mystery Potion (Rare).
{@b Craziest (Level 17).} The potion starts filled with Mystery Potion (Legendary). When drunk in this form, it is refilled with Mystery Potion (Very Rare).
{@b Legacy Rituals.} Three rituals are needed to unlock the abilities of the Mystery Potion (Legacy).
{@i  {@b A bit of this.} You must mix in the hair of a CR 5 or higher creature that is chaotically aligned. This unlocks the level 5 feature.
{@i  {@b Tad of that.} You must mix in the nail of a CR 11 or higher creature that is chaotically aligned. This unlocks the level 11 feature.
{@i  {@b And a drop of whatever.} You must mix in the blood of a CR 17 or higher creature that is chaotically aligned. This unlocks the level 17 feature.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Nimbus Bow
entries: The Nimbus Bow is a beautiful weapon, made of fine wood carved with spiraling motifs and encrusted with diminutive turquoises. The string sparkles and shimmers with electricity. You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. It has the Finesse and Push properties.
{@b Shocking (Level 5).} This weapon deals an extra {@dice 1d6} Lightning damage on a hit, and the target can’t make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn.

{@b Olman Hero (Level 7).} The weapon’s +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls becomes a +2. While you hold this weapon with two hands, you gain a +2 bonus to your AC and you have Advantage on saving throws made to avoid or end Frightened condition.
{@b Shocking Burst (Level 9).} Whenever you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this weapon, the target and creature of your choice within 5 feet of the target takes 5 Lightning damage.
{@b Bane of Scale and Stone (Level 11).} This weapon deals an extra {@dice 3d6} Lightning damage if the target is a Dragon, Elemental (Earth), or a Dinosaur.

{@b Windcaller (Level 13).} As a Magic action, you can wave the bow overhead and cast Control Winds or Warding Wind. Your ranged attack rolls are unaffected by the spells. Control Winds can’t be cast again until the next dawn.
{@b Warwood (Level 14).} As a Magic action, you can strike the bowstring to cast Shillelagh on the bow. While under the spell, you can also wield the bow as a Quarterstaff.

{@b Summon Arrowhawk (Level 15).} You can take a Magic action to fire an arrow at an Unoccupied space within 150 feet, where it transforms into a four-winged, four-eyed elemental hawk that crackles with electricity. The elemental uses the Lightning Hulk statblock, except its size is Medium and its Illumination trait is sunlight. The elemental understands your languages, obeys your commands, and takes its turn immediately after you on your Initiative count. The elemental disappears after 1 hour, when it dies, or when you dismiss it as a Bonus Action. This feature can’t be used again until the next dawn.

{@b Blessing of Sun, Moon, and Sky (Level 20).} As a Magic action, you can cast one of the following spells, firing an arrow imbued with exalted power that transforms into the spell’s effects: Sunbeam, Maddening Darkness, or Whirlwind. This feature can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} Four rituals are needed to unlock the abilities of the Nimbus Bow.
{@i  {@b Rite of the Sky.} Gain the blessing of Quetzalcoatl. This unlocks the level 13 feature.
{@i  {@b Rite of the Moon.} Gain the Blessing of Tezcatlipoca. This unlocks the Level 14 feature.
{@i  {@b Rite of the Sun.} Gain the blessing of Tonatiuh. This unlocks the Level 15 feature.
{@i  {@b Hero of Thanaclan.} Save the Olman city of Thanaclan, and purge the evil that dwells within. This unlocks the Level 20 feature.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e Savage Tides}

item.name: Princess Sparkletoes
entries: This beige patchwork stuffed bunny has bead-studded feet that sparkle in the light. A willing ally holding it can take a Magic action to fall Asleep, even if not attuned.
{@b Denial (Level 3).} While the toy is on your person, you have Advantage on saving throws to avoid or end the Frightened condition.

{@b Anger (Level 6).} As a Magic action, you can summon the spirit of a feline hunter. You cast the Summon Shadowspawn spell with the emotion Fury at level 4. The spell can't be cast again until the spirit kills an enemy or the next midnight.

{@b Bargaining (Level 9).} When you fail a saving throw, you can choose to reroll the saving throw at Advantage, and one of the two sparkling feet vanishes. You cannot use this ability if both the rabbit’s feet are missing. Any vanished feet reappear at the next midnight.

{@b Depression (Level 12).} As a Magic action, you can hug the rabbit. Doing so gives you Advantage on all Saving throws and all attack rolls made against you have Disadvantage. Both benefits last until the end of your next turn. In addition, if you have the Depressed or Frightened condition, it ends on you.

{@b Acceptance (Level 15).} If you wink at the rabbit while within 30 feet of it, it says “I love you.” If you wink twice at the rabbit, it hums a melody, which lasts for 5 minutes or until you wink at it again. While it hums, all allies within a 30-foot Emanation originating from the rabbit that can hear the song feel loved, gaining a +{@dice 1d6} bonus to all saving throws. The rabbit can't hum the melody again until the next midnight. Winking doesn't require any action economy.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} One ritual is needed to unlock the abilities of Princess Sparkletoes.
{@i  {@b Five Movements of Grief.} Complete the Symphony of Dreamers. Each movement of the symphony unlocks its corresponding feature.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Remembrance of the Vaati
entries: {@i Most cannot recall the height of the Wind Duke’s empire. However, this circlet, over a millennia of age, remembers it well; both the empire’s gilded heights and its bloody fall during the war between Law and Chaos.}
While you are wearing and are attuned to the circlet, you gain the following benefits:
You can speak and understand Primordial (Auran). You can cast Gust, Fog Cloud, and Dust Devil from the circlet.
The spell save DC for spells attached to this item is equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or your Charisma modifier (You choose when you attune to this item).
{@b Wergadeam (Level 5)}. You take on an innate quality of the Wind Dukes. Your falls count as having fallen 60 less feet for the purposes of taking damage from falling (to a minimum of 0 feet).

{@b Trygrideam (Level 7)}. You recall the practices of the druidic sect of Wind Dukes that followed nature’s order. You can cast Druidcraft and Gust of Wind from the circlet. The circlet can't cast Gust of Wind again until the next dawn.

{@b Haikjadeam (Level 9)}. You recall the wrath of the Wind Dukes. When you hit with a weapon or spell attack, you deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Thunder damage. Against the following types of creatures, you instead deal {@dice 2d6} Thunder damage: Aberrations, Celestial Eladrin, Fey, and Demons.
{@b Kheirdeam (Level 11)}. You recall the counsels of the Wind Dukes. You can cast Spare the Dying and Calm Emotions from the circlet. The circlet can't cast Calm Emotions again until the next dawn.

{@b Bledrudeam (Level 15)}. Your mind is more resilient against the forces of chaos. You have Advantage on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma Saving Throws on effects originating from the following types of creatures: Aberrations, Celestial Eladrin, Fey, and Demons.

{@b Wendeam (Level 17)}. You recall the powers the exiled sect Wind Dukes dedicated to tracking the agents of chaos. You can cast Lightning Lure and Mind Spike from the circlet. The circlet can't cast Mind Spike again until the next dawn.

{@b Vindeam (Level 20)}. You take on more qualities of the Wind Dukes. You can take a Magic action to give yourself a Flying speed equal to 60 feet for the next hour. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so for another {@dice 2d6} hours.
{@b Legacy Rituals}. Three rituals are needed to unlock the abilities of the Remembrance of the Vaati.
{@i  {@b Law’s Vengeance}: You must defeat a Demon of CR 5 or higher and douse its corpse in axiomatic water. This unlocks the Level 7-9 features.
{@i  {@b Elemental Unity}: You must convince an Elemental of CR 6 or higher to swear fealty to you. This unlocks the level 11-15 features.
{@i  {@b Ancient Enemy}: You must defeat an avatar of either the Queen of Chaos or Miska the Wolf Spider. This unlocks the level 17-20 features.
{@i Made by Chrys}
item.reqAttune: by a creature of non-chaotic alignment

item.name: Sojourner's Jacket
entries: This purple leather jacket is new and yet well worn. You have a +1 bonus to Charisma (Intimidation) checks while you wear the jacket even when not attuned.

{@b Denial (Level 3).} While wearing this jacket, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class. You can also take a Bonus Action to cause the jacket to assume the appearance of a normal set of clothing or some kind of armor. You decide what it looks like—including color, style, and accessories—but the outfit retains its normal bulk and weight. The illusory appearance lasts until you use this property again or remove the jacket.

{@b Anger (Level 6).} As a Bonus Action, you can cast the Ashardalon's Stride spell at level 5. If you are mounted, you can cast the spell on your mount instead of you. Once you cast the spell, you can't cast it again until the next dawn.

{@b Bargaining (Level 9).} When you fail a saving throw, you can choose to sacrifice some of your memories to succeed instead. You become memory drained until you finish a Long Rest. While memory drained, you must subtract a {@dice d4} from your {@dice D20} Tests. Each time you are memory drained beyond the first, the die size increases by one: the {@dice d4} becomes a {@dice d6}, the {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}, and so on until the die becomes a {@dice d20}, at which point you become Unconscious for 1 hour. You can't use this feature if the die is a {@dice d20}. Greater Restoration or Heal removes any memory drain.
{@b Depression (Level 12).} You have a +{@dice 1d4} bonus to {@dice D20} Tests while you have no allies other than your mount.

{@b Acceptance (Level 15).} You can take the Study action as a Bonus Action, drawing upon your experiences you've been through since you first acquired this jacket. If you roll a 20 or higher, you learn an additional piece of information, and if the subject of your action is an enemy, the enemy's attack rolls against you have Disadvantage until the start of your next turn.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} One ritual is needed to unlock the abilities of the Sojourner’s Jacket.
{@i  {@b Five Movements of Grief.} Complete the Symphony of Dreamers. Each movement of the symphony unlocks its corresponding feature.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: Sword of Cerastium
entries: This Longsword is forever stained with silver blood. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this longsword. An Aberration hit with this longsword takes an extra {@dice 1d12} Slashing damage. You get both benefits whether or not you are attuned.

{@b Denial (Level 3).} If an Aberration you hit with this weapon is currently Grappling a creature, the Aberration must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or release each creature it is Grappling.

{@b Anger (Level 6).} When you start your turn within 30 feet of an Aberration, you can gain the Enraged condition until the start of your next turn. While Enraged, your attacks with this weapon deal an additional {@dice 1d12} (total {@dice 2d12}) damage to Aberrations.

{@b Bargaining (Level 9).} You can take a Bonus Action to toss this magic weapon into the air. When you do so, the weapon begins to hover, flies up to 30 feet, and attacks one creature of your choice within 5 feet of itself. The weapon uses your attack roll and adds your ability modifier to damage rolls. After the attack, the blade falls to the ground.
{@b Depression (Level 12).} When you would be Charmed, Confused, or Stunned by an Aberration, you can choose to instead be Depressed for the duration of the effect.

{@b Acceptance (Level 15).} As a Magic action, you can begin a sword dance. A 15-foot Emanation from you is filled with Dim Light while the dance lasts, and the light is moonlight. All creatures in the Emanation gain a +2 bonus to their AC if they aren't wearing armor, and they gain a +{@dice 1d4} bonus to attack rolls and saving throws. A creature that starts its turn in the Emanation has its Speed increased by 10 feet until the end of its turn. Flowers sprout and bloom in the Emanation. The dance lasts until you lose Concentration.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} One ritual is needed to unlock the abilities of the Sword of Cerastium.
{@i  {@b Five Movements of Grief.} Complete the Symphony of Dreamers. Each movement of the symphony unlocks its corresponding feature.
{@i Made by Aris}

item.name: The Bug Zapper
entries: You can use this Pistol as a Spellcasting Focus for your Sorcerer spells. The Pistol has the Light property. Use your spell save DC for all DCs of this weapon.
{@b Storm Shot (Level 3).} The pistol magically produces its own ammunition. When you attack with the pistol you can choose to deal Thunder, Lightning, or its normal damage type.
{@b Channeled Shot (Level 6).} You have a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.

When you cast a spell with a range of at least 30 feet, you can choose to fire it through the pistol. The range of the spell becomes 90 feet unless it is already greater, and you can add the pistol’s attack roll bonus to the spell attack roll.

{@b Localized Storm (Level 9).} While your Innate Sorcery is active or you are in a storm, you can cause a storm cloud to appear above you, filling a Cylinder 40 feet tall with a 20-foot radius around with rain and lightning.

When you use Heart of the Storm while your Localized Storm is active, you can choose for any creatures in the storm to also take the damage. When a creature other than you in your Localized Storm takes Lightning or Thunder damage, they become Deafened until the start of your next turn. You can choose if a creature or object in the storm gets wet.

{@b Bolt-action (Level 12).} When you hit an attack with the pistol while your Localized Storm is active, you can take a Bonus Action to immediately cast Lightning Arrow without expending a spell slot. When cast in this way, you can choose to expend a spell slot to cast it at a level equal to the spell slot expended.
You now have a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this weapon.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} A multitude of repairs and upgrades must be made to the pistol to fully unlock the features.
{@i  {@b Sisterly Love.} “Borrow” some components from Tan’nik's gun and install them into yours. This unlocks the Level 3-6 features.
{@i  {@b Storm Tamer.} Sail into the eye of a storm and end it. This unlocks the level 9 feature.
{@i  {@b YEEE-OWCH!!} Get struck by nonmagical lightning. This unlocks the level 12 feature.

{@i  {@b Storm’s Wrath.} Experience 5 unique Eldritch, Extraplanar, or Supernatural Storms. Alternatively, defeat a notable Pirate’s fleet (Any canon pirate fleet counts, GM fiat otherwise).This unlocks the level 15 feature.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Weaver’s Heart
entries: This set of ancient Weaver’s Tools hums to the same resonance as the will of its wielder. When you attune to the Weaver’s Heart, you have Expertise in Weaver’s Tools.

{@b Bind Wounds (Level 7).} You have 0 Pulse. Whenever you hit a creature with an attack, you gain 1 Pulse. Pulse is gained after the attack is resolved. You can have a maximum of 12 Pulse at a time. At the end of every 10 minutes, you lose 6 Pulse.
As a Bonus Action, you can expend 9 Pulse to heal yourself. You roll up to 4 Hit Point Dice and regain Hit Points equal to the roll's total plus your Proficiency Bonus; those dice are then expended.

{@b Song of Peace (Level 9).} You may use Weaver’s Heart as an instrument. When you take the Influence action with the intent to calm down the creature, you may expend 1 Pulse to make the roll with Advantage, and target a number of creatures equal to your Proficiency Bonus, instead of 1. You must be playing Weaver’s Heart in order to use this feature.

{@b Thread Storm (Level 12).} When you hit with a melee attack, you can expend 5 Pulse to deal {@dice 4d8} Force damage to all creatures of your choice within 5 feet of you. You may only use this feature once on each of your turns.
{@b Swift Step (Level 14).} When you take the Dash or Dodge action, you may expend 1 Pulse point to increase your Speed by 20 feet until the end of your turn.

{@b Cross Stitch (Level 17).} When taking the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 6 Pulse to forgo an attack. When you do so, you may add half of your proficiency bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn, and the next time a creature misses its attack against you before the start of your next turn, you may take a Reaction to make a melee attack against the creature. This attack deals an additional {@dice 4d8} Force damage. You may only use this feature once on each of your turns.
{@b Legacy Rituals.} Three rituals are needed to unlock the abilities of Weaver’s Heart.
{@i   {@b Hymn of Temperance.} Resolve a violent conflict without the use of violence. This unlocks the level 7-9 features.

{@i  {@b Weave of Pressure.} Keep up the pressure in combat. Take the Attack action for three consecutive turns in a combat against a Hostile creature and do not miss or forgo a single attack roll. This unlocks the level 12-14 features.

{@i   {@b Monument to Greatness.} Weave a grand tapestry that displays the legendary acts you’ve committed. Sewing a tapestry of this grandeur takes 336 hours of downtime. Upon completion, this unlocks the level 17 feature.
{@i Made by Chrys}
item.reqAttune: by someone with Proficiency in Weaver’s Tools

item.name: Bayonet
entries: A blade meant to be attached to the muzzle of a firearm. You can take a Utilize action to attach or deattach a Bayonet to a weapon that has the Loading property. A weapon can only have one Bayonet attached at a time.

When you make an attack roll with a weapon that has a Bayonet attached to it, you can instead choose to make a melee attack with the Bayonet's blade against a creature within 5 feet of you. When you do so, you deal {@dice 1d8} Piercing damage instead of the weapon's normal damage.
A Bayonet is considered a Martial weapon with the Finesse property, the Slow weapon mastery and has a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with it determined by its rarity.
{@b Rarity    Bonus}
Common    +0
Uncommon    +1
Rare    +2
Very Rare    +3
{@i Made by Denny}

item.name: Training Manual: [Talent/Perk]
entries: A creature can study the manual in order to learn a new Talent. The amount of time it takes depends on the rarity of the manual. Once a manual is used, it becomes nonmagical. If a creature does not meet the prerequisites for the Talent, it does not gain the Talent and the manual is still consumed.
The Talent the manual gives is chosen by the GM when it is given out. The item is of the same rarity as the Talent. Perk training manuals have a rarity of common.
Perk: 1 hour
Common: 1 day
Uncommon: 2 days
Rare: 3 days
Very Rare: 4 days
Legendary: 5 days
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Anklet of Nature's Passage
entries: While you wear this anklet, your walking speed is increased by 10 feet.
{@i Uncommon (+1), Rare (+2), Very Rare (+3)}

item.name: Bracelet of Nature's Will
entries: While wearing this magic item, you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your Druid spells. The bonus is determined by the item's rarity.
While you wear this bracelet, you can use your Wild Shape feature without expending one of the feature's uses. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Uncommon (+1), Rare (+2), Very Rare (+3)}

item.name: Fortune Clover
entries: You gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your Rogue features and your Weapon Masteries. The bonus is determined by the item's rarity.
When you hit with an attack roll, if you haven't used the Sneak Attack feature this turn, you can apply your Sneak Attack. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Uncommon (+1), Rare (+2), Very Rare (+3)}

item.name: Medal of Valor
entries: You gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your Fighter features and your Weapon Masteries. The bonus is determined by the item's rarity.
When you use your Action Surge feature, you have Advantage on your next Attack roll before the end of your next turn.
{@i Uncommon (+1), Rare (+2), Very Rare (+3)}

item.name: Rhythm-Maker's Instrument
entries: As an Action, you can touch the item and transform it into any type of instrument of your choice.
While holding this instrument, you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls and the saving throw DCs of your Bard spells. The bonus is determined by the item's rarity.
As an Action, you can play this instrument to regain one use of your Bardic Inspiration feature. This property of the instrument can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Uncommon (+1), Rare (+2), Very Rare (+3)}

item.name: Vital Bandages
entries: You gain a bonus to the saving throw DCs of your Barbarian features and your Weapon Masteries. The bonus is determined by the item's rarity.
While you wear these bandages, you can use your Rage feature without expending one of the feature's uses. Once this property is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Uncommon (+1), Rare (+2), Very Rare (+3)}

item.name: Worn Card: 0 - The Fool
entries: {@i 'Where journey begins.'}

As a Magic action, you may brandish this card to cast Word of Recall without any components. If you do not have a designated sanctuary, the spell fails and the card is not consumed. Once you use the card, it disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Worn Card: I - The Magician
entries: {@i 'May you never miss your goal.'}
As a Magic action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to gain advantage on attack rolls for 1 minute. Once you use the card, it disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Worn Card: II - The High Priestess
entries: {@i 'Mother is watching you.'}

As a Magic action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to summon a Gargantuan-sized, heeled, and disembodied leg to stomp a point you choose, creating a 10-foot-radius, 30-foot-high Cylinder extending upwards from that point. Any creatures within this space must make a DC 18 Dexterity Saving Throw or take {@dice 12d6} Bludgeoning damage and be knocked Prone. Creatures that save take half damage and are not knocked Prone. Once this resolves, the leg rises up and disappears, and the card disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Worn Card: V - The Hierophant
entries: {@i 'Two prayers for the lost.'}
As a Magic action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to gain 10 Temporary Hit Points. Once you use the card, it disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac Repentance.}

item.name: Worn Card: VI - The Lovers
entries: {@i 'May you prosper and be in good health.'}

As a Magic action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to spend an amount of your hitdice less than or equal to your proficiency bonus. For each Hit Point Die you spend in this way, roll the die and add your Constitution modifier to it. You regain Hit Points equal to the total (minimum of 1 Hit Point). Once you use the card, it disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on the Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Worn Card: VII - The Chariot
entries: {@i 'May nothing stand before you.'}
As an Action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to gain the following effects until the end of your next turn:

items: Your movement speed becomes 60 feet.
You cannot cast spells, concentrate on spells, or make ranged attacks.
The first melee attack you hit as part of an Attack action deals an extra {@dice 1d8} damage of the weapon's damage type.

entries: Once you use the card, it disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Worn Card: II - The High Priestess?
entries: {@i 'Run.'}

As a Magic action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to call upon a Gargantuan-sized, heeled, and disembodied leg that attempts to stomp you, hopefully to your benefit. You are affected by the following for 1 minute.

At the start of your turn, choose a point to create a 10-foot-radius, 30-foot-high Cylinder extending upwards from that point. You must be within the resulting cylinder’s space in order to choose that point. A shadow looms over that area for the duration of the turn.

At the end of your turn, any creatures within this space must make a DC 18 Dexterity Saving Throw or take {@dice 12d6} Bludgeoning damage and be knocked Prone. Creatures that save take half damage and are not knocked Prone. Once this resolves, the leg rises up and disappears.
Once the effect ends, the card disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Worn Card: V - The Hierophant?
entries: {@i 'Two prayers for the forgotten.'}

As a Magic action or Bonus Action, you may brandish this card to gain 5 Temporary Hit Points. When these Temporary Hit Points are reduced to 0, you may negate any additional damage dealt to yourself. Once you use the card, it disintegrates into dust.
{@i Based on The Binding of Isaac: Repentance}

item.name: Soul Doll
entries: {@i Created by a demonic ritual, soul dolls are an inherently Evil magic. They are used by those who seek to control and manipulate others.}

When created, a Soul Doll is linked to a specific creature. The Doll shares the Hit Points of the linked creature, but has an AC of 10 (When the creature or the dolls hit points changes, the other ones change to match).
When you cast a spell with the range of Touch that targets a creature, you can instead target the doll. When you target the doll, the linked creature suffers the effects of the spell.
While holding the doll you know the location of the linked creature, unless it is protected from Divination magic.

If the linked creature dies, the doll is submerged in Holy Water, or if Dispel Evil and Good is cast on the doll, the doll loses its magic. If the doll is destroyed while still magical, the linked creature dies a painful death that can only be prevented by Wish or the direct intervention of a deity. The creatures death counts as if it was from the source that destroyed the doll.

The soul doll only functions if the linked creature is on the same plane of existence. While the linked creature is on a different plane, the doll’s magic is suppressed. A casting of Dispel Magic suppresses the doll's magic for {@dice 1d4} minutes.
If a Good aligned creature intentionally causes harm to a soul doll, its alignment changes to Evil.
{@i Made by June}

item.name: Night Terror
entries: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo is your fears manifested, inscribed on your skin. To use the tattoo, you must hold the needle against your skin and speak the command word. The needle turns into ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin in a shadowy design emblematic of the fear. Once the tattoo is there, you cannot gain the Frightened condition from that source of fear. The tattoo wriths while in presense of that source of fear. The source of the fear can't be so generic as a specific spell, but it can be a kind of creature, a Phobia, a Seed of Fear, or similar.
When you apply the tattoo, your Hit Point maximum decreases by 1. Only a wish spell (with stress) can undo this reduction to your Hit Point maximum, which removes the tattoo.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Blazing
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
When you score a Critical Hit on an attack roll made with the item, the target starts Burning.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Cast-Off
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor}
You can doff this armor as an action.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Chilling
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
When you score a Critical Hit on an attack roll made with the item, the target's speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Darksteel
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon or Armor}
Weapons made of Darksteel deal an additional +1 Lightning damage with their attack rolls.
When the wearer of Darksteel armor takes Acid damage, the Acid damage the wearer receives is reduced by 2.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Edible
entries: The item is able to be eaten, providing as much nourishment as 1 day of Rations.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Elastic
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
When you score a Critical Hit on an attack roll made with the item, the target is pulled up to 10 feet in a straight line toward you.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Feathered
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor}
When the wearer of the item takes Bludgeoning damage from falling, the Bludgeoning damage the wearer receives is reduced by the wearer's Proficiency Bonus.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Focused Focusing Focus
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting Focus, Requires Attunement}
If you roll a 1 on the {@dice d20} for a Constitution save to maintain concentration, you can treat it as a 2 instead.
Once you have used this property, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Imbued Wood
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting Focus, Requires Attunement}

When you cast a damage-dealing spell while attuned to the focus, you gain a +1 bonus to one damage roll of the spell, provided the damage is of the type associated with the item's wood. The types of wood and their associated damage types are listed in the Imbued Wood table.
{@b Wood}                              {@b Damage Type}
Infernal Ash                     Fire
Solarian Rosewood         Radiant
Vudran Manchineel         Acid or poison
Pandemonian Oak          Lightning or Thunder
Death Ebony                   Necrotic
Frostfellian Pine              Cold
Acheronian Birch             Force
Eldritch Wenge                Psychic
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Mending
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor or Clothing}
When you gain experience, you can reduce the number of experience points you regain to repair this item. It costs 10 experience points per Hit Point of the item.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Moonblood
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor or Clothing}
Each time a Fiend hits the wearer of the item with a Natural Weapon or Unarmed Strike, or casts a touch spell on the wearer, it takes {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Netherite
entries: The item cannot be damaged or destroyed by fire or lava. If the item is submerged in lava, it floats to the surface after 1 round.
Netherite armor confers Advantage on saving throws or ability checks made to resist being pushed, pulled, or knocked prone.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Ough I'm so green
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Casus Belli}
Somehow it has become green.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Restorative
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting Focus, Requires Attunement}
While you are attuned to the focus and cast a spell that restores Hit Points using a spell slot, you can choose to regain a number of Hit Points equal to the spell's level, up to a max of 5.
Once you have used this property, it can't be used again until the next dawn.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Returning
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Thrown Property}
This weapon returns to the wielder's hand immediately after it is used to make a ranged attack.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Shock Absorber
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor}
When you are targeted by Chromatic Orb or Chaos Bolt, it is unable to leap to another target. When you are targeted by Green-Flame Blade, the spell does not leap to an additional target.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Sweeping Edge
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon with the Cleave Mastery}
When you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using this item, the range of Cleave's additional attack increases to a creature within 10 feet of the original target and within your reach.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Unbreaking
entries: This item has twice the number of Hit Points.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Zardazil
entries: As a Magic action, you can cause the item to become mostly intangible to you, allowing it to be safely concealed inside your body. You become Immune to all damage caused by the weapon, including spell effects on it. Despite being intangible, you are able to equip and hold the item as normal. This effect lasts until another creature uses this ability.
{@i From Volo's Guide to All Things Magical (2e). Made by June}

reward.name: Accurate
entries: The item has an additional +1 bonus to attack rolls made with it.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Celestial Bronze
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ammunition, Thrown Weapon, Melee Weapon, or Armor}
Thrown Weapons, Melee Weapons, or Ammunition made of Celestial Bronze deal an additional {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage on attack rolls made against Celestials, Dragons, Fiends, Giants, and Undead.

When the wearer of Celestial Bronze armor takes Necrotic damage, the Necrotic damage the wearer receives is reduced by {@dice 1d10} and the wearer can use a Reaction to deal {@dice 1d4} radiant damage to any creature of their choice within a 10 foot radius centered on themself.
{@i Inspired by Percy Jackson, Made by Krypt}

reward.name: Defensive
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Requires Attunement}
While Attuned, you gain a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Efficient
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Axe, Pickaxe, Paxel, or Shovel}
The item displaces material at twice the speed it usually would.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Fleet
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Requires Attunement}
While attuned to the item, the wielder gains an additional 5 feet of movement.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Gruumsh-Branded
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Boots of Orcish Kind}
The item no longer confers Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Gruumsh’s Finest
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Orc Stone}
The item summons a second Tough Boss that is permanently Stunned and Paralyzed. In order to attune to the item, the wielder must save the item's bestower's life a second time.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Heroic
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
Attack rolls made with the item deal an additional {@dice 1d4} damage while the wielder is Bloodied.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Holding Cabin
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Folding Boat}

When the Folding Boat folds back into a box, any objects in the vessel that can't fit inside the box are transported to an extradimensional space. These objects cannot be retrieved until the boat is unfolded again.

When the boat is unfolded, any objects stored in the extradimensional space reappear in the same part of the boat they were in when the boat was folded up. If the new form of the boat does not have the part or is too small the fit the objects, they remain in the extradimensional space until the boat takes a form that can hold them.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Nuclear
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Staff of Power}
The item is considered to have 15 additional charges for the purpose of breaking it with Retributive Strike.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Orcslayer
entries: When an Orc touches an object made of Orcslayer or is hit by an attack made with an Orcslayer melee weapon or Orcslayer ammunition, it must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. Failure: The Orc takes {@dice 2d6} Poison damage and is Poisoned for 1 minute. Success: Half damage and the Orc becomes Immune to Orcslayer for 1 hour.
{@i From 2e. Made by June}

reward.name: Starmetal
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
The item deals an additional {@dice 1d6} damage on attack rolls made against Aberrations, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, and Fiends.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Alien
entries: When the item's time is up, it is stolen away by horrible entities and never seen again.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Bouncy
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor}
When you are targeted by Chromatic Orb or Chaos Bolt, it always leaps to another target.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Encumbering
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor}
The item takes twice as long to don and doff.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Orc's Gift
entries: The item can only be Attuned to by a creature that was gifted the item by an orc after saving its life. If the item previously did not require Attunement, it now does.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Sponge
entries: The item is able to soak up half a cubic foot of liquid. This liquid can be squeezed out as a Utilize action.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Wet
entries: This object is always wet.

reward.name: Woke
entries: Whenever you say something insensitive, this item will speak aloud and correct you.
If you are deaf or otherwise do not understand verbal language, the woke item will speak telepathically to correct you.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Barbaric
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
When you miss with an attack roll made with this weapon, it becomes mad until the end of your next turn. You cannot maintain Concentration while holding this weapon if it is mad.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Bell-Adorned
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor or Clothing}
The item is covered in magical bells that jingle loudly. While wearing the item, the wielder has Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Bonecrafted
entries: This item can only be wielded by Undead.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Boulder Magnetizing
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Requires Attunement}

While attuned to the item, the wielder must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw each time they finish a Long Rest as a boulder magically materializes and falls on them. On a failure, they take {@dice 5d10} Bludgeoning damage.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Damocles’
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon, Requires Attunement}

While attuned to the item and in initiative, the wielder rolls a {@dice d20} at the start of each of their turns. On a 1, the weapon flies towards the wielder, they draw the weapon if possible and make the Attack action against themselves, using the weapon if it is successfully drawn.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Elven Secret
entries: This item requires Attunement by an Elf.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Hunter Hunter
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
This item can only be used to harm Alakmur’ss, otherwise known as Akormur’ss, otherwise known as Drizzt “Alakmur’ss” Do’Urden

reward.name: Jabberwhacked
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
Your weapon is a little jabbered. A little wocky, even.
When you make an attack with this weapon, if the attack isn't a Critical Hit, it is treated as a Critical Miss.
{@i Made by Christopher E}

reward.name: Kid-friendly
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
This weapon deals no damage on a hit. Additional damage cannot be added onto its attack rolls.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Penetrable
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting Focus, Requires Attunement}

If you cast Shield or Mirror Image while attuned to the focus, you take {@dice 10d6} + 40 Force damage.  If this damage reduces you to 0 Hit Points, you and everything nonmagical you are wearing and carrying are disintegrated into gray dust. If disintegrated in this way, you can be revived only by a True Resurrection or a Wish spell.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Plaid
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Armor or Clothing}
The item is covered in rather unfashionable plaid patterns magically enchanted to be unsightly. While wearing the item, the wielder has Disadvantage on Charisma-based skill checks.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Scattered
entries: {@i Prerequisite: House of Cards}

When the shelter's duration ends, instead of transforming into a deck of cards and appearing in your hand, it collapses into a pile of cards that takes 10 minutes to collect. Strong winds or other hazards may result in the House of Cards being irrecoverable.

reward.name: Self Conscious
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}

When you roll a 1 on the {@dice d20} of an attack roll with this weapon, it becomes sad. While sad you have Disadvantage with all attack rolls using it. A creature can cheer up the weapon as an Influence action while holding it, ending its sadness.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Stone-like
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Weapon}
You must save an orc's life to make an attack roll with this weapon. After making an attack roll with it, you must save another orc's life in order to make another attack roll with it.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: The Best Idea
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Lets you cast Wish}
When you cast Wish using this item, you can only wish to give yourself a sandwich.

reward.name: 🤔
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Investigation proficiency}
You are Proficient with Thinker's Tools
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Abyssal Influence: Bloody Ichor
entries: When you die, you leave a puddle of demon ichor in your space. It dissolves after 24 hours.
(can only be given with the players' permission)
{@i From WM3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: A Little Version of Me
entries: As a Magic action, you can touch a creature to grant it the Silly Guy Perk, taking the form of a spirit resembling you. They keep this perk until you use this action again.
{@i Inspired by Smiling Friends. Made by June}

reward.name: All Edge
entries: You've taken the first steps to becoming a true Lord of Edge.

{@b Edging.} As a Magic action, you can transform your body into an embodiment of edge, which lasts until you end it (no action required). Any number of your physical features such as fingers, limbs, or other body parts become or sprout blades. While this way, you can deal Slashing damage with your Unarmed Strikes, and you can use your body parts as any melee weapon that deals Slashing damage, even if your hands are empty.

{@b Form of Edge.} As a Magic action, you can take the form of a Greatsword (if you are Medium or larger in size), a Longsword (if you are Small), or a Sickle (if you are Tiny). While in this form, you are Incapacitated and cannot move. This transformation lasts until you choose to end it (no action required).

reward.name: Anonymity
entries: You have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in crowds of people.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Anuran Throat
entries: You can inflate your throat like a frog's. In addition, you can perfectly mimic the ribbit of a frog.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Arborea Influence: Merry Music
entries: Whenever you cast a spell or make a spell attack, the faint sound of merry music can be heard by you and those affected by your magic.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Attack Power
entries: Your prowess in martial combat can be given numerical value as Attack Power, or AP. It is determined based on class and has no mechanical impact.
The AP of a Barbarian, Fighter, Monk, Paladin, Ranger, or a Rogue is equal to their class level.
The AP of an Artificer, Bard, Cleric, Druid, Sorcerer, Warlock, or a Wizard is equal to half their class level, rounded down.
{@i AP and Multiclassing}
The Attack Power of a character with multiple classes is the sum of the AP from their class levels. For example, a Paladin 6/Warlock 1/Sorcerer 1 has AP 6, and a Fighter 10/Rogue 5 has AP 15
{@i Inspired by BAB tracking from 3.5e, Made by V}

reward.name: Beast Treats
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Beast Master}
You can take a Magic action to feed your Primal Companion treats and expend a spell slot. After 1 minute, the beast has all its Hit Points restored.
(you can already do this but you have to kill the beast to do it normally)
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Beyond the Grave
entries: When you die, you can appear as a visible spirit. While in this form, you are intangible and invulnerable and you do not occupy your space. While in this ghostly form, you cannot affect objects or creatures and you are Incapacitated. You have a Fly Speed of 10 feet, but you are tethered to your body, unable to move more than 30 feet from it.
While you cannot speak due to being Incapacitated, You can gesture, pantomime, and otherwise visually interact with others, who can perceive you as a translucent, ghostly apparition of yourself.
This form lasts until you are revived, or 10 days, whichever comes first.
If your body is destroyed or your soul is not free, you disappear completely, unable to manifest as a spirit.
{@i From S3}

reward.name: Best Buds
entries: You have Advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks to determine a Plant's mood. You also have Advantage on any Influence action made against a Plant.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Bigby's Foot
entries: When you cast Bigby's Hand, you can make it look like a foot. When you make it a foot it is unable to use the Grasping Hand part of the spell.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Bigfoot Enthusiast
entries: You have Advantage on Wisdom (Nature) checks made to identify footsteps of bare feet.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Big Foot Enthusiast
entries: As a Magic action, you can make your feet an inch longer. This can only be undone by Wish.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Birthday Blessing!
entries: While it is your birthday (the PC's birthday), you add {@dice 1d4} to {@dice D20} Tests.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Blessing of Corellon
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Any Elf}

{@i Ever changing, mirthful, and beautiful, the primal elves could assume whatever sex they liked. When they bowed to Lolth's influence and chose to fix their physical forms, elves lost the ability to transform in this way. Yet occasionally elves are born who are so androgynous that they are proclaimed to be among the blessed of Corellon—living symbols of the god's love and of the primal elves' original fluid state of being. Many of Corellon's chief priests bear this blessing.}

{@i The rarest of these blessed elves can change their sex whenever they finish a long rest-a miracle celebrated by elves of all sorts except drow. (The DM decides whether an elf can manifest this miracle.) Dark elves find this ability to be terrifying and characterize it as a curse, for it could destabilize their entire society. If Corellon's blessing manifests in a drow, that elf usually flees to the surface world to seek shelter among those dedicated to Corellon. (From MTF)}

You have become among the blessed of Corellon. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your sex as well as minor characteristics about yourself such as your height, facial features, sound of your voice, and hair length. You cannot change your height by more than 6 inches and your size category stays the same. Whatever changes you make, you are still recognizable as yourself.
[{@i Adapted from Mordenkainen's Tome of Foes}](https://5e.tools/rewards.html#the%20blessed%20of%20corellon_mtf){@i , Made by June}

reward.name: Bloody Mess
entries: You cannot make your attacks non-lethal, and creatures you kill with a critical hit cannot be revived via Revivify or Raise Dead.
{@i Inspired by Fallout, Made by Sam}

reward.name: Bodybuilder
entries: You have Advantage on Strength (Performance) checks. If you lack proficiency in Performance, you can still add your Proficiency Bonus to Strength (Performance) checks.
{@i Repassed by from WM3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Bottleable
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ooze}

As a Magic action you are to shrink your form in order to fit into an open bottle, flask, vial, or other tiny container. While in the bottle, you are able to be concealed by another creature, allowing you to share its Stealth (Dexterity) checks to remain unseen. If the container is open, you can leave it using half of your movement. If the container is nonmagically sealed, you are unable to leave but can use an action to make a DC 10 Strength saving throw in order to break out of the container. If the container is magically sealed, you are unable to break out in this way.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: By the Nine! I'm Tweaking!
entries: If you would be forced to roll on the Potion Miscibility table as a result of drinking a potion while already under the effects of another potion, you may choose to treat your {@dice d100} roll as a 50. When you do so, you become Paralyzed until the end of your next turn and fall Prone.
{@i Made by Patrick}

reward.name: Carceri Influence: Lockdown
entries: All Ropes, Chains, Manacles, and Nets while within 10 feet of you have a +5 bonus to DCs made to escape the object.
(can only be given with the players' permission)
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Cat Tamer
entries: You gain a +5 bonus to Animal Handling checks made to Influence Cats.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Cat's Cooking
entries: You have Advantage on checks made to make biscuits.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Cling Grip
entries: You can hold onto walls to prevent yourself from falling. If climbing a wall would require a Strength (Athletics) check to climb, you make the check when you cling onto the wall, falling if you fail.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Corellon's Other Blessing That No One Really Cares That Much About
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Elf}

You have unlocked full range of control over your ears, whether it be rotating them, pinning them against your head, having them droop or perking them up. There have been legends that the elves of yore used this ability when temperatures dropped in order to keep their ears warm.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Create Wine
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Create or Destroy Water}
When you cast Create or Destroy Water, and choose the Create Water option, you can choose to make wine instead of water. It functions the same as the water, except it cannot put out exposed flames.
Additionally, you can choose how alcoholic it is. If you choose to make it excessively alcoholic, any creature who drinks it must make a Constitution saving throw or become Poisoned for 1 hour.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Creature Feature
entries: “Little did I know, It would be a creature feature. Featuring… the creature.”
You have advantage on Charisma (Performance) checks to portray yourself as feral or monstrous.
{@i Made by Miel}

reward.name: Creep
entries: You can perfectly perform this song. When you make a Charisma (Performance) check to play it, you can treat a roll of 9 or lower as a 10.
(This Song for Reference)
{@b Creep}
{@i By Radiohead}

When you were here before Couldn't look you in the eye You're just like an angel Your skin makes me cry You float like a feather In a beautiful world I wish I was special You're so fucking special But I'm a creep, I'm a weirdo What the hell am I doing here? I don't belong here I don't care if it hurts I want to have control I want a perfect body I want a perfect soul I want you to notice When I'm not around You're so fucking special I wish I was special But I'm a creep, I'm a weirdo What the hell am I doing here? I don't belong here She's running out the door She's running She run, run, run, run Run Whatever makes you happy Whatever you want You're so fucking special I wish I was special But I'm a creep, I'm a weirdo What the hell am I doing here? I don't belong here I don't belong here
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Critical Thinking
entries: When you hit a willing creature, the hit can be a Critical Hit.
If you hit with an attack that is a Critical Hit, you can choose for it to be a normal hit instead.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Cubist
entries: As a Magic action, you can change your form to a more cube-like shape. Your shape becomes a 5-foot Cube. While in this form, your weight doubles, and you are Immune to the Prone condition and cannot be pushed. However, your Speed is reduced to 5 feet and you cannot take any actions except a Magic action to leave this form.
{@i Inspired by Gris}

reward.name: Defender… Treats?
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Battle Smith Artificer}
You can take a Magic action to repair your Steel Defender and expend a spell slot. After 1 minute, the Steel Defender has all its Hit Points restored.
{@i Inspired by Beast Treats by Aris, Made by Denny}

reward.name: Dice Goblin
entries: You can attune to multiple Charlatan's Die at once, and they all count as the same attunement.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Digital Display
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Common Sign Language, Construct}
A part of your body has been transformed into a screen. You are able to cause text to appear on the screen in any language that you understand (No action required).
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Doohickey Savant
entries: You have Advantage on checks using Tinker’s Tools to make completely “useless” contraptions, gizmos (not Gizmo!) and gadgets.

reward.name: Door Buster
entries: {@i You are adept in knocking doors off their hinges.}
You gain a +4 bonus to Strength checks to break down doors. This bonus does not stack with a Portable Ram.
{@i Perk of Portable Ram, Made by June}

reward.name: Double-Jointed
entries: Your body is jointed in ways it shouldn't be. The difference isn't noticeable until you start moving them, but you have more joints than most and more range in the ones most people have. You have Advantage on checks made to squeeze through small spaces, and you're capable of unnatural movements like spinning your head 180 degrees, bending your knee forwards, or similar slightly uncanny movements.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Draconic Diet
entries: You can consume treasure and currency of equal value to the Rations you would need to eat when taking a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Elminster Discerning Protocol
entries: You can choose to gain a +1 bonus on an ability check made to discern whether someone with the first name Elminster is the Elminster of legend. Once you do so, you cannot use this perk again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Elysium Influence: Adequate Chef
entries: No matter how low you roll for Wisdom (Cook's Utensils) to prepare a meal, your food cannot taste terrible. It tastes mediocre at worst.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Enjoy the Show
entries: As a Reaction to seeing shit going down, you can conjure a red and white striped bucket full of buttery popcorn in your hand.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Ethereal Connection
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Manifest Mind}

Your Awaked Spellbook does not immediately vanish when you move to the Ethereal Plane. While you are on a different plane than it, you lose control of it and cannot use or benefit from any abilities that require it to be manifested.
The Awakened Spellbook stops manifesting after 1 minute unless you return to the same Plane as it.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Evil Brand
entries: You are physically marked forever as a servant of an evil power greater than yourself or as a villain who does not care who knows that you seek only death, destruction, and misery for others. The symbol is unquestionable in its evil, and all who see it know beyond a doubt that the bearer is forever in the sway of the blackest powers.

Evil creatures automatically recognize the symbol now emblazoned upon you as a sign of your utter depravity or discipleship to a powerful patron, although the specific identity of the patron is not revealed.
You gain a +2 bonus on Persuasion and Intimidation checks made against evil creatures.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Evil Brand: Mark of Chains
entries: You are physically marked forever as a servant of an evil power greater than yourself or as a villain who does not care who knows that you seek only death, destruction, and misery for others. The symbol is unquestionable in its evil, and all who see it know beyond a doubt that the bearer is forever in the sway of the blackest powers.
If you already have Evil Brand when you receive this Perk, you can choose for this Perk's features to replace Evil Brand's; else, this Perk has no effect.

Evil creatures automatically recognize the chain link symbol now emblazoned upon you as a sign of your utter depravity or discipleship to a powerful patron, although the specific identity of the patron is not revealed.
You gain a +1 bonus on your save DCs to Grapple and Escape a Grapple.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Expressionist
entries: You can change the colors of your body and what you wear or carry as you will. The colors could be whatever combination of shades, tints, and patterns, including solids, stripes, and spots, that you choose.
An object reverts to its original colors if you are no longer wearing or carrying it.

reward.name: Food Feelings
entries: When you take the Search action, you can target a creature and make a Wisdom (Insight) check against their Passive Charisma (Deception). On a success, you learn what their Super All-Time Favorite Food is, and their Worst Most Hated Food ever is. On a failure, that creature becomes immune to this ability for 24 hours.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Footsy
entries: When a creature uses Evasive Footwork within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to kick them in the feet to stop them from receiving any benefit from it.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Furry Feathered Familiar
entries: When you cast Find Familiar, you can summon a Tressym.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Fur Coat
entries: As part of a Short Rest, you can grow a thick coat of fur on your body. While you have this fur and remain dry, you automatically succeed on saving throws against the effects of extreme cold, as if you were wearing cold weather clothing. This fur remains until you cut it off.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Get me my keys
entries: While having the Poisoned condition as a result of being drunk, you have a +1 to checks to operate vehicles.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Gorg is this real?
entries: When you take the Study action, you can ask [Gorg](https://forgottenrealms.fandom.com/wiki/Gorg) for help. He responds if he happens to be within earshot.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Gork is this real?
entries: As a Magic action you can ask the legendary Gork to answer your question. He responds if he happens to be within earshot.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Hand-Eye Coordination
entries: Your hands function as a spyglass when positioned in front of an eye as one.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Harmless Theatrics
entries: When you cast a spell, you can choose to deal no damage with it.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Help! Help! I’m being compressed!
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Theme Music}

When you use Theme Music, you can choose to make the tone of the sound very muddy and overall compressed. You may also choose for your appearance to be compressed in a similar way, but does not make you harder to be identified or seen.

reward.name: Horrible Entity
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Aberration}
You have become a horrible entity. As an Action, you can steal away an Alienist whose time has come. When you do so, they are never seen again.
This perk does not make you immune to getting taken away by other, more horrible entities.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: I'm out here pondering my orb
entries: You can use Orbs as Thinker's Tools.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: I'm So Hungry...
entries: As a Magic action, you can cast Locate Creature targeting the nearest horse. While you are Concentrating on the spell, the horse gains the benefits of Locate Creature as if cast targeting you and is aware you are tracking it.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: I'm So Thirsty...
entries: As a Magic action, you can cast Locate Creature targeting the nearest creature with the Potable Healer perk. While you are Concentrating on the spell, the creature gains the benefits of Locate Creature as if cast targeting you and is aware you are tracking it.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: I'm So Hungry...
entries: As a Magic action, you can cast Locate Creature targeting the nearest horse. While you are Concentrating on the spell, the horse gains the benefits of Locate Creature as if cast targeting you and is aware you are tracking it.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: I am a wolf.
entries: {@i On all levels except physical...}
You can perfectly recreate the howl of a wolf.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Idea of Evil
entries: When you contemplate or undertake a malevolent act, your pangs of conscientiousness are enhanced.
{@i Made by Aris, cannot be given without explicit permission from her}

reward.name: Idea of Good!
entries: When you contemplate or undertake a benevolent act, your pangs of conscientiousness are enhanced! Yay! :D
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Idea of Self
entries: When you contemplate or commit an act that is true to yourself and your own desires, your pangs of conscientiousness are enhanced.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: If you Give an Adventurer a Cookie...
entries: As a Magic action, you can summon a cookie. This cookie has no mechanical benefit, but it can be a different flavor whenever you summon it, such as chocolate chip, or snickerdoodle... maybe even one of those Crumble cookies, if you feel fancy.
Once you use this ability, you cannot summon another cookie again until the end of a Short Rest.
{@i Made by Vic}

reward.name: I'm an Imp-Path
entries: You have Advantage on any Wisdom (Insight) check to determine the mood of any imp within 30 feet of you. Additionally, imps may move through your space freely, regardless of if they are an ally.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Introspection
entries: You have Advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks made against yourself.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: It's high enough.
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Trance of Order}
When you make a {@dice D20} Test while your Trance of Order is active, you can forgo rolling the {@dice d20} to get a 10 on the die.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: I Hired This 🦀 To Stare At You
entries: As a Magic action, you can summon an Aldani in an unoccupied space that you can see within 15 feet. The Aldani remains there for 1 minute, in which it uses its time to stand there and stare at you. Once you use this perk, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: I Hired This 🦞 To Stare At You
entries: As a Magic action, you can summon a Crab Folk in an unoccupied space that you can see within 15 feet. The Crab Folk remains there for 1 minute, in which it uses its time to stand there and stare at you. Once you use this perk, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: I Own a Musket For Home Defense
entries: You have Advantage on attack rolls with muskets while in your home, as the founding fathers intended.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: I know what I am
entries: You can cast Detect Pronouns at will. When cast in this way, its duration is Instantaneous and you only learn your own preferred pronouns.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: I know what you are
entries: You can cast Detect Pronouns at will.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: i to am in this episode
entries: When you, the player, are spectating a session, you may, with GM permission, have yourself (the character) appear or coincidentally arrive for 1 minute in an area determined by the GM. While in this state, you cannot take actions or movement, but may talk freely. At the end of the duration, or whenever you take damage, you disappear or otherwise leave back to wherever you were before activating the perk.
You may only use this perk once per session.
{@i Based on that one House M.D. gif}

reward.name: I Want My Mummy
entries: You can wear linen wraps as Desert Clothing.

In addition, as an Influence action, you can immediately befriend one Mummy within 30 feet of you that can see or hear you. The mummy immediately becomes Friendly to you. You can only take this action once ever.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Juggler
entries: When you hit another creature of one size larger than you or smaller with a weapon attack, you can cause that creature to not fall until the end of your next turn.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Kyton's Chains
entries: You can use Manacles or Chains on a creature within 10 feet of yourself, rather than 5.
Additionally, you can use Chains as a Whip, dealing either Bludgeoning or Slashing damage on a hit (depending on the nature of the chain).
{@i Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3}

reward.name: Laborer’s Weapons
entries: You can use any of the following tools as the associated weapon listed below.
Axe - Battleaxe
Broken Bottle - Dagger
Crowbar - Mace
Fire Poker - Spear
Gaff - War Pick
Hammer - Light Hammer
Hatchet - Handaxe
Hoe - Quarterstaff
Knife - Dagger
Meat Cleaver - Handaxe
Pickaxe - War Pick
Pitchfork - Trident
Scythe - Glaive
Shovel - Quarterstaff
Skillet - Club
Sledgehammer - Warhammer
Threshing Flail - Flail

reward.name: Let’s Get This Out Onto A Tray
entries: You never tire of eating rations and have Advantage on Saving Throws against the Poisoned condition from consuming spoiled or old rations. Nice.
{@i From WMS3}

reward.name: Legacy Carpenter
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Carpenter's Tools proficiency}
{@b Fortify.} With 1 minute of work and raw materials, you can make a door or window harder to force open. Increase the DC needed to open it by 5.

{@b Temporary Shelter.} As part of a Long Rest, you can construct a lean-to or a similar shelter to keep your group dry and in the shade for the duration of the rest. Because it was fashioned quickly from whatever wood was available, the shelter collapses {@dice 1d3} days after being assembled.
{@i From 2014, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Legacy Mason
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Mason's Tools proficiency}
{@b Demolition.} Your knowledge of masonry allows you to spot weak points in brick walls. You deal double damage to such structures with your weapon attacks.
{@i From 2014, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Legacy Tinkerer
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Tinkerer's Tools proficiency}

{@b Repair.} You can restore 10 Hit Points to a damaged object for each hour of work. For any object, you need access to the raw materials required to repair it. For metal objects, you need access to an open flame hot enough to make the metal pliable.
{@i From 2014, ported by Aris}

reward.name: LEVITATION!!!!
entries: {@i Prerequisite: TRANSFORMATION\!\!\!\! perk}

While in your TRANSFORMATION!!!!, you can take a Magic ACTION!!!! (action) to shout “LEVITATION!!!!”, causing your legs to raise up into your body, leaving it hovering in the air. Your movement speed is 0 while in this form. You can shift back to your original TRANSFORMATION!!!! as a Magic ACTION!!!! (action).
{@i Made by Miel}

reward.name: Liquid Food Categorization Specialist
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Chef’s Utensils Expertise, 22 Intelligence}
“She said chili! Chili’s not a soup! The woman has named something that’s not a soup!”
