In addition while carrying this toy, when you make a saving throw to avoid or end the Frightened condition, you can choose to roll at Advantage. You can gain this benefit up to three times.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Mists of the North Wind
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Wild Shape feature}

As a Bonus Action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to take on a gaseous shape. This functions as the Gaseous Form spell, except it targets only you, and does not require Concentration to maintain. It lasts for a number of hours equal to half your Druid level (rounded down). You can revert to your normal form earlier by using a Bonus Action on your turn. You automatically revert if you fall Unconscious, drop to 0 Hit Points, or die.

reward.name: Breeze of the South Wind
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Cunning Action feature}

You can use the Bonus Action granted by your Cunning Action to cast the Zephyr Strike spell. When you cast it in this way, it doesn't require components nor a spell slot, and while under its effects you leave no tracks. Once you cast the spell, you cannot do so again with this talent until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

reward.name: Gale of the East Wind
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Arcane Recovery feature}

When you gain the benefits of your arcane recovery feature, you can choose one of your spells that you have prepared that requires concentration. The next time you cast that spell and Concentrate on it, you also gain the benefits of the Warding Wind spell for its duration.

reward.name: Breath of the West Wind
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Channel Divinity feature}

As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Channel Divinity, and bestow yourself or a creature within 30 feet of you the freedom of the winds. The creature gains the benefits of the Freedom of Movement spell, which lasts for 10 minutes.

reward.name: Abyss-bound Soul
entries: You have pledged your immortal soul to a particular demon lord in return for a gift that aids your evil works in life.

You are branded with the personal symbol of your demon lord patron, who watches over you from the Abyss. You gain a +2 bonus on saving throws made against good aligned spells. Additionally, a tiny portion of your patron’s power infuses your body, granting you an additional benefit. The exact benefit gained depends on who your patron is, as given below.

{@b Baphomet.} If you move at least 20 feet straight toward a creature and then hit it with an attack on the same turn, the target takes an extra {@dice 2d8} damage. You can gain this benefit only once per turn.
{@b Dagon.} You can breathe water as well as you can breathe air, and you can cast Hunger of Hadar once. You must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it again in this way.

{@b Demogorgon.} You can cast Summon Greater Demon once. You must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it again in this way. Additionally, the CR of the Demon summoned cannot be higher than your Proficiency Bonus. The Demon has Disadvantage on its saving throw to resist.
{@b Fraz-Urb’luu.} Any spell slot you expend to cast an Illusion spell counts as a spell slot of one level higher.
{@b Graz’zt.} You have Immunity to the Frightened condition.
{@b Juiblex.} You gain Resistance to Acid damage.
{@b Kostchtchie.} You gain Resistance to Cold damage.
{@b Malcanthet.} Any spell slot you expend to cast an Enchantment spell counts as a spell slot of one level higher.
{@b Miska.} You gain a Climb Speed equal to your walking speed, and can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
{@b Obox-ob.} You know the Infestation cantrip, and you are resistant to Poison damage. You can cast Giant Insect once. You must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it again in this way.
{@b Orcus.} Any spell slot you expend to cast a Necromancy spell counts as a spell slot of one level higher.
{@b Pale Night.} You have Immunity to Insanity, Madness, and Confusion effects, including the Confused condition.
{@b Pazuzu.} While airborne, you have a +2 bonus to your Dexterity saving throws.
{@b Yeenoghu.} You gain a +1 bonus on attack and damage rolls made with your natural weapons.
{@b Zuggtmoy.} You have Immunity to additional damage from Critical Hits and sneak attacks.
{@b Abyssal Doom.} When you die, your soul becomes claimed by your demon patron. You can be restored to life only by True Resurrection or Wish.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for any spells in this talent.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e by Aris}

reward.name: Archaeomancy
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting and proficiency in History}
{@b Focus of Study.} You can use any Treasure (Art Object) that is at least 1000 years old, and any Artifacts, as a spellcasting focus for your spells.

{@b Unearth Ruins.} As a Magic action, you can magically raise broken, ancient ruins in a 20-foot-radius, 60-foot-high Cylinder centered on a point you can see within 120 feet. Ground in the Cylinder becomes Difficult Terrain. Each creature in the Cylinder when it appears must make a Strength saving throw (DC is your spellcasting DC). On a failure, the creature takes {@dice 3d8} Bludgeoning damage and is knocked Prone.
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again unless you expend a Level 2+ spell slot to regain the use of it (no action required).
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Blessing of Nikolaus
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Kindness of Nikolaus Talent}
You have gained the favor of the Interloper god Nikolaus.
You gain a +2 bonus to AC when underwater and can breathe underwater.
{@i Made By Krypt}

reward.name: Brutal Stomping
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Brutal Strike (9th-level Barbarian)}
When you use the Forceful Blow option of your Brutal Strike, if the triggering attack is an unarmed strike, the damage die for that Brutal Strike becomes a {@dice d12}.

Instead of pushing the target away, you can move up to half your Speed, and you can move the target with you up to 15 feet during that movement. The target must remain adjacent to you throughout this movement. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.
{@i Made by Miel}

reward.name: Bounding Leap
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Cere-many
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Ceremony}
When you cast Ceremony, you may replace the Wedding rite with the following:

{@b Wedding.} You touch adult humanoids willing to be bonded together in marriage. For the next 7 days, each target gains a +2 bonus to AC while they are within 30 feet of another target. This effect cannot increase a creature's AC by more than 2. A creature may be married to a number of creatures equal to its proficiency bonus, and any two creatures married to each other cannot benefit from this rite together again unless they are widowed by the other.
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Commander? I Hardly Know ‘Er
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Command}
When you cast Command, you may use the 2014 wording of the spell.
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Cynosure
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting, can cast Teleport}
When you cast Teleport and roll on the Teleportation Outcome table, you can expend a level 4+ spell slot to roll twice and use either number.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Deathly Dance
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ghost Pipes}

You can spend 1 minute haunting an Instrument you touch, which requires Concentration. At the end of the minute, the instrument becomes haunted by the [Danse Macabre](https://5e.tools/trapshazards.html#danse%20macabre_vrgr) Haunted Trap. You and creatures you designate do not trigger the trap. The trap is disarmed for 1 hour after the Performer stops playing. The trap follows the [Haunted Trap](https://5e.tools/variantrules.html#haunted%20traps_vrgr) rules.
The item remains haunted until you haunt another Instrument. Once you haunt an Instrument, you cannot haunt another Instrument until you finish a Long Rest.

reward.name: Disc of Obliteration
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Disc of Destruction talent}
When you use Disc of Destruction, you can target objects. When you target an object, you can expend an additional Focus Point to deal double damage.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Discombobulate
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Cunning Strike}
You gain the following Cunning Strike option.
{@b Discombobulate (Cost: {@dice 4d6}).} The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or its Concentration is broken and it cannot Concentrate until the end of its next turn.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Feet of Elvenkind
entries: While barefoot, your steps make no sound, regardless of the surface you are moving across. You also have Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.
{@i Talent of Boots of Elvenkind. Made by June.}

reward.name: Fire Arm
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Construct}
You’ve integrated a firearm into your arm, which you can use whenever the hand is free.

It functions as a +1 Pistol that cannot be disarmed. If you load no ammunition in the weapon, it produces its own, automatically creating one piece of magic ammunition when you load it. The bullet vanishes the instant it hits or misses a target. It has six charges.

{@i Finger Blast.} When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a shell launched from your Fire Arm. All creatures within a 10-foot radius sphere around a point you choose within 60 feet must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 2d10} Force damage, taking half damage on a pass. This costs two charges.

{@i Muzzle Punch.} You can use the Fire Arm to make a damaging Unarmed Strike by attempting to zap someone point blank with the muzzle flash, dealing {@dice 2d8} Lightning damage. This costs one charge.

{@i Misfire.} When you use Finger Blast or Muzzle Punch, roll a {@dice d10}. On a 3 or lower, the arm misfires, dealing {@dice 2d6} Force damage to the wielder immediately after the feature is used. If the weapon misfires, the attack still works as normal.
The Fire Arm regains all expended charges when you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Adapted from the S3 Handgun, both by Lily.}

reward.name: Ghostform
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Etherealness}

When you cast the Etherealness spell on yourself, you can choose to instead only partially affect your form, making you incorporeal. Instead of the normal benefits of the spell, you gain the following:
{@b Ethereal Sight.} You can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when you are on the Material Plane.

{@b Incorporeal Movement.} You can move through other creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. You take {@dice 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside an object. You have Immunity to the Grappled and Restrained conditions.
{@b Ethereal Visage.} You are visible in the Border Ethereal while on the Material Plane, but you can't affect or be affected by anything on the other plane. Your appearance is spectral.
When the spell ends, if you are in an occupied space, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take Force damage equal to twice the number of feet you are moved.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Go for the Throat
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Cunning Strike}
You gain the following Cunning Strike option.
{@b Stifle (Cost: {@dice 3d6}).} The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or it has the Silenced condition until the end of its next turn.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Heated Sorcery
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Sorcerer}
While your Innate Sorcery is active, you gain the following benefits:
{@i  At the end of each of your turns, each creature of your choice in a 5-foot Emanation originating on you takes {@dice 1d4} Fire damage.
{@i  A creature that touches you or hits you with a melee attack takes {@dice 1d6} Fire damage. A creature can only take this damage once per turn.,
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Helm Breaker
entries: Once per turn, when you hit an airborne creature one size larger than you or smaller with a melee weapon attack, you can push the creature up to 30 feet downward. If the creature collides with a solid surface, it takes damage as though it had fallen the distance it was pushed.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: I AM THE ANGRY PUMPKIN
entries: When a creature makes a Dexterity saving through to avoid being fallen upon by you, they have Disadvantage. Upon impact with another creature after falling, as a reaction, you can choose to roll maximum damage.
{@i Made by Sam}

reward.name: I Can Be Your Angle...
entries: When you make a ranged attack or a thrown-weapon attack, you can target a point on a surface within the normal range instead of targeting a creature. The attack then ricochets harmlessly off that surface and continues toward a creature of your choice within 10 feet of that point.
The ricochet attack may ignore cover provided by the original target’s position. You make the attack roll for the ricochet with Disadvantage. The surface takes no damage from this attack.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Lightning Lance
entries: When you take the Attack action and throw a weapon at a target no farther than 120 feet from you, you can forgo making a ranged attack roll and instead turn the weapon into a bolt of red lightning. This bolt forms a 5-foot-wide Line between you and the target. The target and each other creature in the Line (excluding you) makes a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 4d6} Lightning damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Immediately after dealing this damage, the weapon reappears in your hand. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining expended uses whenever you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Living Weapon
entries: Your body has been tempered like a magic weapon.
You have a bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with your Unarmed Strikes, determined by the talent’s rarity. Those strikes deal your choice of Force damage or their normal damage type.
{@b Rarity		Bonus}
Uncommon	+1
Rare		+2
Very Rare	+3
You cannot benefit from Wraps of Unarmed Power items if you have this talent. You cannot get multiple versions of this talent, but you can be upgraded from one rarity to another.
{@i From S3, Talent of Wraps of Unarmed Power}

reward.name: Maybe the back door is unlocked?
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Lucky Thief}
If life gives you a locked door, find another one.
You can use your Lucky Thief talent an unlimited number of times, but only once per door.
{@i Talent of Ring of Mystery Keys, Made by June}

reward.name: Mental Mark
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Tireless}

When you cast Hunter's Mark using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can mentally mark a creature. While marked, you always know the target's location until the spell ends, but only while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. While you have this knowledge, the target can't become hidden from you, and if it has the Invisible condition, it gains no benefit from that condition against you. You can only have one creature marked in this way.
When you cast Hunter's Mark using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you are able to track the target's location across planes of existence.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Mimic Familiar
entries: When you cast Find Familiar, you may optionally target a Medium or Small object within 15 feet of you. The familiar will then take the form of a Juvenile Mimic which is currently replicating that object.
{@i Made by Gavin}

reward.name: Mole Claws
entries: Your hands have transformed into mole's claws.

Your hands are a natural weapon with the Finesse and Light properties, and deal {@dice 1d4} Slashing damage on a hit. While attuned, you gain a Burrow Speed equal to your Speed and Blindsight of 15 feet.

As a Magic action while Attuned, you can invoke your claws to bolster yourself with the sturdiness of the earth. Spend and roll a number of your unspent Hit Dice up to a maximum equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You then regain a number of  Hit Points equal to the total roll plus your Constitution modifier.
Once the claws have been invoked, you can't invoke them until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Talent of Delver's Claws, Made by June}

reward.name: Mole Training
entries: As a Utilize action, you can gain a 5 foot Burrow Speed until the start of your next turn.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Noxious Fumes
entries: As a Magic action, you can begin to emit a 15-foot Emanation of poisonous gas around you. Whenever the Emanation enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, the creature must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. A Poisoned Creature automatically fails. On a failed save, the creature takes {@dice 4d4} Poison damage or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn. You can dismiss this Emanation at any time (no action required).
{@i Adapted from STS2 by Landon}

reward.name: Phantasmal Conjurer
entries: When you cast one of the following spells, you can change the nature of the spell to exist only in a single creature’s mind. You must be able to see the target creature within range of the spell when you cast it. The spell’s school becomes Illusion, and the summoned spirit gains the following properties:

items: Creature Type changes to Aberration
All damage dealt by the spirit is Psychic damage
The spirit can’t affect or be affected by any creature that isn’t the designated target.

entries: {@b Affected Spells:} Giant Insect, Summon Aberration, Summon Beast, Summon Celestial, Summon Construct, Summon Dragon, Summon Elemental, Summon Fey, Summon Fiend, Summon Plant, Summon Shadowspawn, Summon Undead
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Planar Body
entries: Your body has been suffused with extraplanar energies much like one of its native metals.
You learn an extraplanar property corresponding to a Material item property, chosen when you receive this talent. The property must be applicable to weapons or armor.
When you make an Unarmed Strike, you can choose to apply one of your learned material item properties as if you made an attack with a weapon that possesses that property.

While you aren't wearing armor and aren't benefiting from any other material item properties, you can choose to benefit from one of your learned material item properties as if you are wearing Light Armor that possesses that property.
This talent's rarity is considered Rare if the learned property is a Minor Beneficial Property, and Very Rare if the learned property is a Major Beneficial Property.
You can receive multiple versions of this talent, but you can only benefit from one learned material property at a time and you can change your active material property after finishing a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Possess Object
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Possession, Ghost Ride, or another possession ability.}
You can use your possession ability to possess objects. Doing so still counts as a use of your possession ability.

As a Magic action, you can use your Possession ability to possess a Huge or smaller object within 5 feet of you. You and your items disappear as you inhabit the object. You now control the object. You can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, except ones that specifically target your creature type. You can use your normal senses to perceive from the object, and if you have telepathy, you can use it to communicate with other creatures.

You can move movable parts of the object, such as a piano’s keys and petals, a clock’s hands, a crossbow’s firing mechanism. If an object has speeds, then you can move as the object using those speeds, such as moving a Wagon’s wheels or a Boat’s oars or sails.
The possession lasts until the object drops to 0 Hit Points or you leave as a Bonus Action. When the possession ends, you and your items appear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the object.

reward.name: Potion Thrower
entries: {@i You have mastered the art of throwing bottles into open mouths.}

As a Utilize action, you can throw a potion you are holding at a creature. Target one creature within 20 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or suffer the effects of drinking the potion. After being thrown, a potion is destroyed.
At the end of an affected creature's turn, it can make a Constitution saving throw to end the effects of the potion. The DC for this save depends on the rarity of the potion.
Rarity     | DC
Common     | 11
Uncommon   | 13
Rare       | 15
Very Rare  | 17
Legendary  | 19
Other      | 15
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: [ PROTOCOL ] - Core Overextension
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Construct, Giant Barbarian}
{@i You have learned a special technique that allows you to push past your limits, and extend your body to the MAX}

When you Rage or as a Bonus Action while your Rage is active, you can expend a use of your Rage to become larger. Until your Rage ends, you gain the effects of the Enlarge option of Enlarge/Reduce and gain a {@dice d4} bonus to Strength ability checks and saving throws.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Radiant Beams
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Eldritch Blast}
You can deal Radiant damage instead of Force when you cast Eldritch Blast. If you change the damage type this way, the damage die becomes {@dice 1d8}.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Landon}

reward.name: Rain of Lightning
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Lightning Lance Talent}

When you take the Attack action with a ranged weapon at a target no farther than 120 feet from you, you can forgo making an attack roll and instead summon a bolt of lightning to rain from above centered at the target. The target, and each creature within 5 feet of the target, must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@dice 4d10} lightning damage on a failure and half of that on a success. When you use this feature, you expend a use of your Lightning Lance.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Revenant Oath
entries: If you are killed by a creature, you can choose to sacrifice your chance at revival to seek vengeance on those who killed you. When you do so, your spirit cannot be revived by any means short of True Resurrection until you are revived.

24 hours later, your spirit repossesses your original body and you rise as a Revenant. If your body is destroyed or otherwise unavailable, your spirit animates a Humanoid corpse elsewhere on the same plane of existence and rises as a Revenant.
Your game statistics are replaced by the Revenant statblock, though you retain your alignment, personality, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores.
You remain as a Revenant for 366 days, or until you have exacted vengeance on those who have wronged you. While you are a revenant, you pursue exacting that vengeance to the best of your ability.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Sixfold Path: Third Principle (Speech) / [Sunrise of Revelations]
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Zone of Truth}

When you verbally ask a creature a question immediately before hitting the creature with a weapon attack, the creature must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target truthfully answers your question in one word telepathically to you, even if the target is unwilling. If the target could not hear your question, it succeeds on the saving throw.
You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all uses when you finish a Long Rest.
The DC is 8 plus your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (choose when you gain this talent).
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Sorrow Taint
entries: {@i Tears streak down your face, staining your skin; Your sorrows and inhibitions heighten.}

{@b Weight of Grief.} As a Magic action, you point at a creature within 60 feet of you, filling it with deep sorrow. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have Disadvantage on {@dice D20} tests until the start of your next turn.

{@b Heightened Empathy.} Your constant grief deepens your understanding of others’s feelings. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks made to discern a creature’s emotions. If you roll a 20 on the die when making a Wisdom (Insight) check, you are Dazed and Immobilized until the end of your next turn, as your own sorrows overtake you.
All DCs from this talent are equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (your choice when you receive this talent).
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Spirit Trap
entries: As a Magic action, you can create a zone of manifestation in a 30-foot Emanation for 10 minutes or until your Concentration ends. All creatures in the area with the Incorporeal Movement trait cannot benefit from that trait and do not benefit from any Resistances to damage. Additionally, creatures in the area cannot enter the Ethereal Plane, and creatures cannot enter the area from the Ethereal Plane.
While this magic is active, however, your soul's protection is weakened. You have Disadvantage on saving throws against possession.
You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Adapted from Ghost Trap from 3.5e, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Spiritual Medium
entries: As a Magic action, you become possessed by the nearest creature or force with the ability to possess you within 1000 feet of you, even if the creature or force is unwilling. The possessing force cannot willingly end the possession until the end of your next turn, and the possession can only end through the normal means of its possession (such as a Bonus action for ghosts).
{@i From S3}
{@i Talent of the Rod of Possession, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Stone Faced
entries: As a Magic action, you can become Petrified until the start of your next turn. While Petrified, creatures have Disadvantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks made against you.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Swift Animation
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Ability to cast Animate Dead}

When a Small or Medium Humanoid dies within 30 feet of you, you can spend a Magic action on your next turn to cast Animate Dead, targeting the corpse of the dead Humanoid. This version of Animate Dead ignores the usual casting time (taking only an action), and has a range of 30 feet instead of 10.
{@i From S3, Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Trick Shot
entries: Once on each of your turns when you make a ranged weapon attack while airborne, you can attempt to perform an elaborate trick.

Choose one of the below DCs to determine the difficulty of the trick, and make a Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. On a success, you gain the associated bonus to your attack roll. On a failure, your attack misses.
{@b DC - Bonus}

reward.name: Triple Jump
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Double Jump}
You can jump with the Double Jump talent twice before you must touch the ground.
{@i Adapted from S3; Made by Aris}

reward.name: Violent Thrust
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Telekinesis}
When you move a creature or object with Telekinesis, if the target comes into contact with a hard surface or heavy object, it takes {@dice 1d6} Bludgeoning damage per 10 feet moved.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Web Walker
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spider Climb}
You ignore movement restrictions caused by webbing.

reward.name: Arena Blood
entries: {@i The blood of an arena champion flows in your veins, giving you a thirst for glory and a drive to succeed.}

You get a +2 bonus on Charisma (Intimidation) checks. In addition, if you successfully intimidate a Hostile creature using the Influence action in combat, you gain {@dice 1d4} Temporary Hit Points. You lose these Temporary Hit Points at the start of your next turn.

While fighting for the entertainment of onlookers, such as a fight in an arena or a sparring match, you gain a +2 bonus to your DCs to Grapple or Shove, and a +2 bonus to any Battlemaster Maneuvers or Weapon Masteries. The GM can determine what fights this applies to.
{@i From 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Child of the Shadow
entries: {@i You're used to the streets and back alleys and have refined several talents and tricks valuable to survival in rougher neighborhoods.}

You know Sasserine’s black market quite well, and need not make checks to locate its outlets. Further, in other cities, you have a knack for rooting out the underground markets. You gain a +2 bonus on Charisma (Persuasion and Investigation) checks made to learn about a city’s black markets.

Your experience has also equipped you with several tricks you can use to ensure survival. You gain a +2 bonus to Initiative in urban environments. You are also adept at fighting in confined areas, such as narrow city alleyways or crowded taprooms. Your melee weapon attacks and Unarmed Strikes ignore Half and Three-Quarters Cover.
{@i From 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Knack for Magic
entries: {@i Magic is not that mysterious to you, and its gifts have made your life a little easier.}
You get a +2 bonus to Intelligence (Arcana) checks made to identify spells. In addition, you have an innate talent for magic. You can cast the following spells:
Prestidigitation,
Light,
Detect Magic.,

Once you cast a spell with this talent, you cannot cast that spell again until you finish a Long Rest. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this talent's spells (choose when you gain this talent).
{@i From 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Merchant’s Tongue
entries: {@i Your practices in trade made you glib and gave you a keen eye for value.}
You have a +2 bonus to checks made to identify an object, and you can automatically determine the monetary value of any treasure (art object) you see.
In addition, whenever you sell an object, you can take the Influence Action (DC 20) as if the creature was Hesitant. On a success, you can sell the object for up to 20% over its asking price.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Student of Nature
entries: {@i The wild has always excited you with its simple beauty and majestic creatures. It is no wonder that many of your talents let you better enjoy the natural world.}

You get a +1 bonus to Wisdom (Animal Handling or Survival), and Intelligence (Nature) checks. You also get a +2 bonus on Constitution saving throws made to resist effects originating from a Plant creature.
{@i From 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Suspicious Eye
entries: {@i You are always on the lookout for treachery and wrongdoing.}
You gain a +2 bonus to Wisdom (Insight) checks made to determine a creature's intentions with you.
In addition, you have a +5 bonus to your Passive Perception against attempts to pickpocket or steal from you. If you notice the creature, you can make an Opportunity Attack against that creature.
{@i From 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Water Rat
entries: {@i You've learned to swim well, and take to the water as easily as land.}

You gain a +2 bonus on Strength (Athletics) checks made to swim. When wearing light or no armor and not Encumbered or Exhausted, you have a Swim Speed equal to your walking speed. If you already have a Swim Speed, it is increased by 10 feet. While swimming, you gain a +2 bonus to Initiative.
{@i From 3.5e, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Warning Shot
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Intimidation Proficiency}

While holding a Pistol, you can replace an attack by firing into the air or intentionally missing your foe by a narrow margin. Make a Charisma (Intimidation) check, the DC being its Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma score, whichever is highest. On a success, the creature becomes Frightened of you for one minute. A Frightened creature makes a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. In addition, you deal an extra {@dice 1d10} damage on your next hit with a pistol against the Frightened target.
Once you have used this ability, you can’t do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Movement I: Denial
entries: As a Magic action, you can surround yourself in a pocket of Antimagic, as you reject the world around yourself, entering a state of denial. Your space acts as if under the Antimagic Field spell while in denial. While in denial, you are Blinded, Deafened, Incapacitated, and Immobilized, seeing and hearing only a white void. The state of denial ends if you are reduced to 0 Hit Points or if you choose to end it. When it ends, you remain Incapacitated until the end of your next turn.
While in denial, your space is bleached of color, leaving only black and white.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Movement II: Anger
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can become Enraged for 1 minute, as red sand swirls around you like a dust devil. While Enraged, as a Magic action you can Fly up to 15 feet towards a creature or object within 15 feet of you and slam into it. If the target is a creature, it must succeed a DC 15 Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away. If the target is an object, it takes {@dice 10d10} Force damage, and if it isn't anchored, it is pushed up to 15 feet away from you.
You can't use this feature again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Movement III: Bargaining
entries: In moments of need, your character can attempt to bargain with the fabric of reality, sacrificing something of themselves for a miracle.

Once per session, you can ask to make a deal with the GM. You and the GM can discuss the terms, but the trade should always be for a short term benefit (not lasting beyond the scope of the session), and the cost must be something sacrificed for a long term. You can make requests and offers, but the GM ultimately chooses the benefit and its cost, and it should not be fair (the cost should always be greater than the benefit). You (and the character) are fully aware of both the benefit and the cost, and you can ultimately choose not to make the deal.
The events of the bargaining happen in an instant in world. Plants sprout around you when you use this ability.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Movement IV: Depression
entries: As a Magic Action, you can become Depressed for 1 minute While Depressed, you are surrounded in a 15-foot Emanation of heavy rainfall, Lightly Obscuring the area and extinguishing exposed flames. As a Magic action while Depressed, you can force all creatures in the Emanation to make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a failure, a creature becomes Depressed until the end of your next turn.
A Depressed creature that fails its saving throw is filled with melancholy, and has Disadvantage on {@dice D20} Tests until the end of your next turn.

A creature filled with melancholy that fails its saving throw becomes hopeless. Until the end of your next turn, it can't attack or target any creature with harmful abilities, spells, or other magical effects.
You can't use this feature to become Depressed again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Movement V: Acceptance
entries: As a Magic action, you can gain the Blissful condition for 1 minute. While Blissful, whenever you succeed on a saving throw against a harmful effect, you can choose to fail instead, and you gain a Heroic Inspiration if you didn't already have one. You lose the Heroic Inspiration when the condition ends.
You can't use this feature to become Blissful again until you finish a Long Rest. While Blissful, shimmering celestial lights surround you.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Astral Ascendance
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Superior Defense}

You can expend 8 Focus Points to cast the Astral Projection spell without material components. You can target only yourself when you do so. Once you cast it in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i From 5e14 Monk}

reward.name: Binary Star: EVE Docent
entries: {@i Your life has been saved by this sentient docent implanted into your head. You're not getting rid of her so easily... she's here to help.}
You are always attuned to this Talent, and cannot unattune.

Sentience. EVE is a sentient docent with an Intelligence of 16, a Wisdom of 14, and a Charisma of 14. She perceives the world through your senses. She communicates telepathically with you and can speak, read, and understand Elvish, Netherese, and Common.

Personality. A docent is designed to advise and assist the construct it's attached to.  The docent's properties are under her control, and if you have a bad relationship with EVE, she might refuse to assist you.

Do you Require my Assistance? When you make a skill check or tool check you are proficient in, you may roll {@dice 2d6}. You take Force damage that cannot be reduced equal to the number rolled, and may add a {@dice d8} to your skill check. No matter if you succeed or fail at this skill check, you take this damage You may use this ability three times per Long Rest.
Migraine. You take a -1 penalty to Intelligence saving throws.
{@i Made by Vic}

reward.name: Consume Magic
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Dispel Magic}

When you cast Dispel Magic and dispel effects from spells, you regain Hit Points equal to the total number of spell levels dispelled at once, to a maximum of 15. Once you use this talent, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Embercleave
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Fire Rune, Great Cleave}

You can imbue your mighty slashes with roaring flame. When you use Great Cleave, you can expend a use of your Fire Rune. When you do, the damage dealt by the cleave is Fire damage, it deals an extra {@dice 2d6} Fire damage, and it deals half damage to creatures that pass the saving throw.
{@i Name taken from Magic: The Gathering. Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Familiar, Familiar.
entries: When you cast Find Familiar, your familiar is able to speak the languages you know.
{@i Made by Vic}

reward.name: Fists of Grounding
entries: Your hands are built to help a brawler get their hands on a mystically slippery foe and keep them there.
When you’re Grappling a creature, they cannot teleport, travel to another plane, or change their shape.
{@i Talent of Gauntlets of Grounding, made by Lily}

reward.name: I Hear Carceri is Nice This Time of Year
entries: When you cast Plane Shift using a spell slot, you can modify it to banish an unwilling creature to another plane. Instead of its usual effects, make a melee spell attack (using your spell attack modifier) against a creature within your reach. On a hit, the creature must make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. If the creature fails the save, it is transported to a random location on a different plane of existence that you specify. You must have a tuning fork attuned to the specified plane, as is normal for the spell.
{@i From 2014 Plane Shift}

reward.name: Oath of Blood
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Geas}

If a creature dies while under the effects of your Geas, and your Geas was to carry out some service, then the creature’s spirit rises as an Indentured Spirit (CR 1). While as a spirit, it pursues the service to the best of its ability. When the service is completed, the spirit “dies” and the soul passes on.
You choose if this talent applies to the Geas when you cast the spell.
{@i Inspired from Oath of Blood from 3.5e, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Sentinel's Shadow
entries: You can cast the Tiny Hut spell without a spell slot or components. When you cast it in this way, the spell ends if you move or are moved from the space you cast the spell. You can also choose if the Emanation is opaque or transparent from the outside. You cannot cast the spell again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Shroud Soul
entries: As a Magic action, you can target a creature within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC = 8 + Prof Bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be unable to perceive you or your soul by any means for the next minute. This effect ends early on the target creature whenever you deal damage or interact with that creature in any way. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Spiral Taint
entries: {@i A constant and ever changing fractal has taken hold of your mind; spiral patterns form on your body with no consistency.}
{@i Sight of the Abyss}. You can cast Vision of Entropy at will.

{@i Touch of Insanity}. Once on your turn, when you hit a creature with a melee attack or Unarmed Strike, you can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature is Confused until the start of your next turn.

{@i Fractal Mind}. There is nothing left to break. You automatically succeed on saving throws made to retain your sanity. You have a -3 penalty on all Wisdom based ability checks and saving throws; whenever you roll a 1 on a {@dice D20} test, you are Confused until the end of your next turn.
All DCs from this talent are equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (your choice when you receive this talent).

reward.name: Shadow Clone Jutsu
entries: As a Magic action, you can cast the Triple Mask spell without a spell slot or Material Components. When you cast it in this way, the spell’s duration is Concentration, up to 10 minutes, and you can independently move all of your duplicates as a Magic action when you cast it in this way. However, you cannot cast spells from your duplicates’ spaces.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Ghoulish Spirit
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Humanoid}
When you drop to 0 Hit Points or make a Death Saving Throw, you can choose to instead die.

When you die, your body rises as a Ghoul under your control. Your body uses the Ghoul statblock, however it maintains your appearance, Size, Alignment, and Languages. Your body rolls its own Initiative and acts on its own turn. If your body is reduced to 0 Hit Points, it returns to normal.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Massive Brian
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Proficiency in Thinker's Tools}
You gain Expertise in Thinker's Tools.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Seismic Empath
entries: You've cultivated a deep connection with the earth and its volcanic power.

{@i Forewarning}. When you see a volcano that's at least 1 mile away, you can take the Search action to make a DC 20 Wisdom (Insight) check. If you succeed, you know if the volcano will erupt within the next 7 days.
{@i Subterranean Heat Endurance}. You are Immune to the effects of Extreme Heat while underground.
{@i Seismic Detection}. You have Tremorsense out to 60 feet. In addition, you detect the presence of seismic activity with your Tremorsense, to a depth that reaches the center of Toril.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Adaptable Artillery
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
You can treat every ranged weapon as if it were a Simple melee weapon of your choice.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Assassin's Mark
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Hunter's Mark}
If you hit a creature marked by your Hunter's Mark with a weapon attack, you can choose to end the Hunter's Mark on the creature. If you do so, your attack deals an extra {@dice 4d6} damage.
If you have Foe Slayer, this instead becomes {@dice 4d10} damage.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Blessing of Increased Ability
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
Choose an Ability Score. It increases by 2, up to a maximum of 20.
When you finish a Long Rest, you can change which Ability Score you increase with this gift.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Brutal Thrower
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
You treat every melee weapon as if it has the thrown property with a range of (20/60). After you make an attack, the weapon magically returns to your hand.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Bulwark
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Unarmored Defense}
You add your Strength Modifier to your Unarmored Defense calculations.
{@i Made by V}

reward.name: Celestial Familiar
entries: You can cast the Find Familiar spell as a ritual without using any material components. When you cast the spell in this way, your familiar always is a Celestial.

When you cast Find Familiar, you can choose for it to take the form of a Lantern Archon, Musteval Guardinal, Giant Owl, or Giant Eagle. If you choose one of these forms, it is of the Celestial creature type.
Additionally, when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your familiar to make one attack of its own with its Reaction.
{@i Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3}

reward.name: Cerebrant
entries: {@i You've called out to the Far Realm as a hope to harness some of its power for yourself; the Far Realm responded.}

{@b Aching Dread}. Unfelt, your form subtly alters the psychological landscape of the surrounding aether. As a Magic action, you can cause the alterations to flare. Creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Frightened of you until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining expended uses after you complete a Short or Long Rest.

{@b Call Farspawn}. You call upon horrible entities from the Far Realm to your aid, casting Summon Aberration (Level 4 version). Once you cast this spell, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

All DCs in this gift are equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (determined by you when you receive this gift), spellcasting ability is equal to your Proficiency Bonus + the same ability modifier as the DCs.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Close Quarters Defense
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

When a creature within 5 feet of you attempts to Grapple you or subject you to an effect that would Grapple you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. If you hit, you deal {@dice 1d6} extra damage, and you automatically succeed against the effect.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Cram Study
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

When you finish a Long Rest, you can choose Arcana, History, Investigation, Nature, or Religion. If you do not have proficiency in that Skill, you gain proficiency with that skill until the end of your next Long Rest. If you did have proficiency, when you take the Study Action, you have Advantage on Intelligence checks involving that Skill.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Dirty Tactics
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

As a Bonus Action, you can throw irritating particles at a creature within 10 feet of you. It must make a Dexterity Saving throw, or become Blinded until the end of your turn. The saving throw is equal to 8 + Your Proficiency Modifier + Your Dexterity modifier.
Additionally, you have a +1 bonus to weapon damage rolls against Prone creatures.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Dropkick of Doom
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Special Technique: Dropkick\! talent}

When you dropkick a creature, the creature is pushed 5 feet away from you if it is no more than one size larger than you. In addition, you may make any of your Unarmed Strikes into a dropkick attack, instead of one per turn.
{@i Made by Patrick}

reward.name: Dungeon Adept
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

items: You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to detect the presence of secret doors.
You have Advantage on saving throws made to avoid or resist traps.
You have Resistance to the damage dealt by traps.

entries: {@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Eldritch Akimbo
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Pact of the Blade Invocation}
Your Pact of the Blade invocation can sustain two weapons at the same time, allowing you to keep up the pressure with dual-wielded weapons.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Empowered Turning
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Channel Divinity (Turn Undead)}
The range of your Turn Undead is increased to 60 feet, and undead within 15 feet of you have Disadvantage on the saving throw.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Enduring Focus
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Flurry of Blows}
The first time you use Flurry of Blows after a Short or Long Rest, you can do so without expending Focus Points. Additionally, you gain 2 more Focus Points to your total pool.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Expanded Cunning Strikes
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Cunning Strike}
You have the following options added to your Cunning Strike feature:

items: {@i Lacerate (cost {@dice 1d6}).} The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or suffer {@dice 3d6} Piercing damage at the start of its next turn.
{@i Hamstring (cost {@dice 2d6}).} The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have its speed reduced by 15 feet.

entries: {@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Eyes of Light and Darkness
entries: {@b Ebon Gaze}. As a Magic action, you can tune your eyes to see through the darkest darkness. For the next 10 minutes, you can see in Darkness—both magical and nonmagical—within 60 feet of yourself as if it was Dim Light. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

{@b Dazzling Gaze}. As a Magic action, you can cause your eyes to erupt as pools of radiance. For the next minute, your eyes sheds Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet; as an action on each of your turns, you can concentrate your gaze onto a creature within 40 feet of you. That creature has to succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + prof bonus + con mod) or be Blinded until the end of its next turn. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Familiar Familiarity
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
You always have the Find Familiar spell prepared. Additionally, you can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Fast Reader
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Wizard}
You can read twice as quickly as most people, and it takes you only half the time to copy spells into your book.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: First Move
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
Your movement Speed increases by 10 feet. Whenever you take the Disengage action, you are able to move through all creatures as if they were your allies.
Whenever you roll Initiative, you can immediately move up to your movement Speed.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Flurry of Blades
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Flurry of Blows, Level 7 Reward}
You can use attacks with a Monk weapon in place of the Unarmed Strikes from Flurry of Blows.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Hefty Spellbook
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward; Wizard}
You can prepare an extra number of spells equal to your Intelligence modifier.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Hotswapper
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
Whenever you roll Initiative, you may immediately draw a weapon, and the first attack roll you make that combat has Advantage. Additionally, you can don or doff a shield as an object interaction.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Malleable Illusions
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Spellcasting}
{@i Made by Gavin}

reward.name: Metamancer
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Sorcery Incarnate, Level 7 Reward}
While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you can use up to three of your Metamagic options on each spell you cast.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Modular Design
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward; Magic Item Tinker}
You can take your Transmute Magic Item ability as a Bonus Action. Additionally you can use Transmute Magic Item twice per Long Rest
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Negotiator
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

You do not suffer Disadvantage on Influence checks you make against Hostile creatures. Whenever you take an Influence action to make a Charisma (Persuasion) and pass, you gain Advantage on the next Charisma (Persuasion) check you make against that creature in the next hour.
Whenever you roll Initiative, you gain the effects of Sanctuary for 1 minute (DC equals 8 + your Proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier).
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Paladin's Wrath
entries: You always have the Wrathful Smite spell prepared. When you cast Wrathful Smite, you deal Radiant damage instead of Necrotic, and you cast it as though it used a spell slot 2 levels higher, to a maximum of level 5. In addition, you can cast it without using a spell slot, but you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it this way again.
{@i Made by Gavin}

reward.name: Poison Expert
entries: {@i Prerequisite: 7th level reward, Poisoner's Kit proficiency}
The saving throw DCs of the Poisons you use are increased by 2. If the Poison deals damage, the damage increases by {@dice 4d6}.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Polyglot
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can choose a Language to know until you finish another Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Powerful Shove
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
Whenever a creature fails the saving throw against being Shoved by you, you can have it take Bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Skim Reader
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward; Memorize Spell}

As a Study action, you can study your spellbook and replace one of the level 1-5 Wizard spells you have prepared for your Spellcasting feature with another level 1-5 spell from the book. The next time you finish a Short Rest, you do not benefit from Memorize Spell. You cannot take this action again until you finish a Short Rest.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: So much to do...
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

During your turn, you can take an extra Bonus Action. This Bonus Action cannot be used to make an attack roll, cast a spell, or deal damage. Once you use this Bonus Action, you cannot use it again until you roll Initiative or finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Sparks of Magic
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

You always have Dancing Lights, Elementalism, Mending, Prestidigitation, and Thaumaturgy prepared. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this Gift).
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Spellbreaker
entries: {@i Prerequisite: level 7 reward}

When a creature within reach casts a spell, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack with a weapon or an Unarmed Strike against that creature. On a hit, it must make a saving throw to maintain Concentration. On a failure, the creature's spell fails and has no effect. The action, Bonus Action, or Reaction used to cast it is wasted and, if the spell was cast using a spell slot, the slot is also wasted.
As with any saving throw to maintain Concentration, the DC equals 10 or half the damage the creature takes, whichever number is higher, to a maximum DC of 30.
{@i Adapted from S3. Made by V}

reward.name: Spellheart
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting feature}
If you have no spell slots remaining for an entire minute, you regain a 1st level spell slot.
Sorcerers cannot convert slots regained in this way into Sorcery Points. These slots cannot be used to refuel uses of other features, such as Wild Resurgence or Font of Inspiration.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Strong Upbringing
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}
You gain an Origin feat of your choice.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Tactical Opportunist
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward}

Whenever you make an Opportunity Attack against a creature, you can choose to roll at Advantage. If you land this attack, all allies within 15 feet of the creature gain a +1 bonus to their attack and damage rolls against the creature until the start of the creature's next turn.
You can use this ability a number of times equal to half of your Proficiency Bonus (rounded down), and regain expended uses at the end of a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Tool Expert
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Flash of Genius}
You gain Expertise with every tool you are Proficient in.
{@i Based on Tool Expertise from 2014e. Made by June}

reward.name: Vestigial Wings
entries: Your High Jump is increased by 20 feet, and your Long Jump is increased by 40 feet. In addition, if you fall and your speed is not 0, you can slow your rate of descent to 60 feet per round. When you land you can take no damage from the fall and can land on your feet.
{@i Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3}

reward.name: Vexing Mark
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 7 Reward, Hunter's Mark}
Creatures affected by your Hunter's Mark subtract {@dice 1d4} from their saving throws against your spells.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Who?
entries: {@i "I'm so sorry, what was your name again?"}

Unknown. You are immune to spells and effects that target you using your name or description unless the creature speaks your true name (Such as Psychic Lance). You are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type.

Forgettable. You have Advantage on Charisma (Deception) checks. As a Magic action, you can attempt to force a creature within 120 feet to forget who you are. It must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 + your Charisma modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, you permanently eliminate all memories it has of you that took place in the last 8 hours. On a success, this creature is immune to this effect for 24 hours.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Winter's Gift
entries: {@i You've learned to harness some of the powers of winter.}
{@b Frost Treader}. You ignore the penalties of Difficult Terrain imposed by snow or ice.

{@b Icebrand}. As a Bonus action, you can infuse your Unarmed Strikes with chilling cold. For the next minute, your Unarmed Strikes deal an additional {@dice 1d6} Cold damage. This ends early if you are subject to Fire damage. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Blessing of Memory
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting}
When you finish a Long Rest, you can choose a number of spells equal to your Proficiency Bonus that you can prepare. You have those spells prepared until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Adapted from S3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Blessing of the Sailor
entries: The ocean called your name and you've accepted its blessing.

{@b Captain's Command.} Pistols you wield gain the Light property. You can Load, Aim, and Fire a Cannon or similar object as a single action. While on a Vehicle (Water) you gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed, have Advantage on all weapon attack rolls, and Advantage on Charisma ability checks,

{@b One Person Crew.} You count as a crew of 20 skilled hirelings towards the number needed to crew a ship. You can repair 1 Hit Point of damage to a ship in 1 hour and it only costs 10 GP in materials.

{@b Overboard.} You have a Swim Speed equal to your Speed. In addition, if you start your turn underwater with 0 Hit Points, you immediately regain {@dice 1d4} Hit Points. Once used, you cannot use this ability against until you finish a Long Rest
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Blessing of the Witches Extraordinaire.
entries: {@i The Witches Extraordinaire offered you a gift, and you accepted. A spark of possibility exists within you, and with your careful nurturing, it can explode into great power.}

{@b Latent Possibility.} When you receive this gift, choose one of your skill proficiencies. When you make an Ability Check associated with the skill, instead of adding your ability modifier and Proficiency bonus, you roll a {@dice d6} and add it to the {@dice d20}. If you roll a 6 on the {@dice d6}, the dice explodes, allowing you to roll another {@dice d6} and add it to the result, possibly exploding again.

{@b Spark of Heroism.} After making the Ability Check, if the extra dice exploded, the dice size changes. A {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}, a {@dice d8} becomes a {@dice d10}, and a {@dice d10} becomes a {@dice d12} (Exploding on a roll of 8, 10, and 12 respectively). Whenever you use Latent Possibility, you roll the new dice instead of the {@dice d6}. If the dice was a {@dice d12}, instead of increasing in size, you gain Heroic Inspiration, the dice becomes a {@dice d6}, and you cannot benefit from Spark of Heroism until you finish a Short Rest.
{@i Made by Vic}

reward.name: Cerebrant Fester
entries: {@i You've called out to the Far Realm as a hope to harness some of its power for yourself; the Far Realm responded.}

{@b Aching Dread}. Unfelt, your form subtly alters the psychological landscape of the surrounding aether. As a Magic action, you can cause the alterations to flare. Creatures of your choice within 20 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Frightened of you until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining expended uses after you complete a Short or Long Rest.

{@b Call Farspawn}. You call upon horrible entities from the Far Realm to your aid, casting Summon Aberration (Level 4 version). Once you cast this spell, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

{@b Dimensional Rift}. You can cast Dimension Door. When you do so, roll {@dice 1d100}. On a 1, you are stolen away by horrible entities forever; if another creature was brought along by the spell, they arrive at the target destination. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

All DCs in this gift are equal to 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (determined by you when you receive this gift), spellcasting ability is equal to your Proficiency Bonus + the same ability modifier as the DCs.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Possession
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Aberration, Fey, Fiend, or Undead}

As a Magic action, you can force a Humanoid creature you can see within 5 feet to make a Charisma Saving Throw. On a failure, the target is possessed by you; you and your items disappear as you inhabit the body. The target has the Incapacitated condition and loses control of its body. You now control the body, but the target retains awareness. You can't be targeted by any attack, spell, or other effect, except ones that specifically target your creature type. Your Hit Points, Hit Point Dice, Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Speed, and senses are replaced by the target's. You otherwise keep your game statistics.

The possession lasts until the body drops to 0 Hit Points or you leave as a Bonus Action. When the possession ends, you and your items appear in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target, and the target is immune to your Possession for 24 hours. If the target succeeded on the Charisma saving throw, it is also immune to your Possession for 24 hours.
You must finish a Short or Long Rest before you can attempt a possession again.
The DC is 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

reward.name: Mystic Brew
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 2 Monk, Proficiency in Brewer's Supplies}

Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest while holding Brewer’s Supplies, you can use that tool to magically produce one magical beverage, chosen from the following options: Cinnamon Dragon, Heavenly Spirit, or Refreshing Dip. The beverage appears in a bottle or cask, and the bottle vanishes when the beverage is drunk or poured out. If any beverage remains when you finish a Short or Long Rest, the beverage and its container vanish.

{@b Drinking a Magical Beverage.} Only you can gain the benefit of drinking the beverage, and you must drink the entire beverage before gaining its benefit. You can spend 1 minute drinking the beverage. You then gain the benefits of the beverage for 1 hour or until you finish a Short or Long Rest, as described below. If the benefit requires a saving throw, the DC is equal to your Monk's Focus DC.

items: {@b Cinnamon Dragon.} You can take a Magic action to exhale toxic flames in a 30-foot Cone. Each creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes Fire damage equal to four rolls of your Martial Arts die and has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.
{@b Heavenly Spirit.} You gain Resistance to Psychic and Radiant damage.
{@b Refreshing Dip.} Whenever you regain Hit Points, you regain additional Hit Points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die.

entries: {@b Enhancing a Magical Beverage.} When you create a magical beverage with this gift, you can expend 3 Focus Points. When you do so, the benefit’s duration extends to 8 hours.
{@i Adapted from the Warrior of Intoxication UA}

reward.name: Vistani Evil Eye
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Spellcasting Class Feature}

{@b Vistani Curse Magic}. You learn the spell Bestow Curse and always have it prepared. When you cast it this way, you instead make a Ranged Spell Attack with a Range of 30 feet, casting it at its Level 3 version. When the target fails the saving throw, you alternatively choose the nature of the curse from the following options:

{@i  The target is unable to perform a certain kind of act involving fine motor control, such as tying knots, writing, playing an instrument, sewing, or casting spells that have Somatic components. When this curse ends, you take {@dice 2d6} Psychic damage.

{@i  The target's appearance changes in a sinister yet purely cosmetic way. For example, the curse can place a scar on the target's face, turn the target's teeth into yellow fangs, or give the target bad breath. When this curse ends, you take {@dice 2d6} Psychic damage.

{@i  A nonmagical item in the target's possession (chosen by the DM) disappears and can't be found until the curse ends. The lost item can weigh no more than 1 pound. When this curse ends, you take {@dice 2d6} Psychic damage.
{@i  The target has Disadvantage on ability checks and saving throws tied to one ability score of your choice. When this curse ends, you take {@dice 6d6} Psychic damage.
{@i  The target's attunement to one magic item of your choice ends. When this curse ends, you take {@dice 10d6} Psychic damage, and the target automatically reattunes to the item.
{@i  The target is Blinded, Deafened, or both (your choice). When this curse ends, you take {@dice 10d6} Psychic damage.

{@b Evil Eye}. As a Magic action, you can target a creature within 10 feet that you can see. This magical ability, which the Vistani call the Evil Eye, duplicates the duration and effect of the Animal Friendship, Charm Person, or Hold Person spell (your choice), but requires neither Somatic nor Material components. If the target succeeds on the save, you are Blinded until the end of your next turn. When you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
The DC for these effects uses your spellcasting DC.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Whirlwind Strike!
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Extra Attack}
{@i don't cry because it's over. attack. attack everything around you. do spin attacks}

You can do spin attacks. You can use an Action to make a Melee Weapon Attack against any number of creatures within 5 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target. You can make this attack twice, regaining one of its expended uses when you finish a Short Rest, and all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Words of Creation
entries: You have learned a few of the words that were spoken to create the world. You can use the Words of Creation to enhance the music of Bards, empower the creation of new things, to help cast good spells, and to harness more power of True Names. The four basic uses are detailed below.

{@b Celestial Choir}. These Words of Creation can be used only by Bards: once per turn, when your Bardic Inspiration Die is rolled, you can add a +{@dice D4} bonus to the roll. You must then roll another Bardic Inspiration Die and reduce your Hit Points by the amount rolled.

{@b Creation}. When you cast a Transmutation, Conjuration, or Illusion spell, you can double the spell's duration. If you speak these words while rolling an ability check that uses your Proficiency with a tool, you gain a +5 bonus.

{@b Exalted Power}. By incorporating Words of Creation into the Verbal component of a spell, you increase the level the spell is cast at by 1. You lose {@dice 1d4} Hit Points per level of the spell when you do so.
{@b True Names}. As a Magic action, you can reduce your Hit Points by {@dice 5d4} and speak the true name of a creature to apply one of the following effects to it for the next minute:
The creature has a -4 penalty on all saving throws it makes against a specific effect or spell of your choice for the next minute.
The creature loses its Magic Resistance trait, or its damage Resistances
You can cast the Teleport or Plane Shift spell on it without including yourself with the spell
The creature has Disadvantage on Charisma saving throws to resist your spells.
If this gift reduces your Hit Points to 0, you are stabilized.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3}

reward.name: Accomplished Adventurer
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}
You gain an Origin, General, or Fighting Style feat of your choice for which you meet the prerequisites.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Always Prepared
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}

You can retroactively buy mundane non-exotic adventuring gear, spending the currency to buy it when you need to use it. You can buy up to 10 items this way, after which you cannot use this feature until you visit a place where you have the time and money to purchase goods.

When you finish a Long Rest, you can produce either up to 2000 gp worth of Poisons or Explosives, or one Potion or non-Spell Scroll of up to Rare rarity. The items must be from DMG'24. The item lasts until you finish another Long Rest, at which point the item disappears or otherwise becomes unusable.
{@i Partly adapted from I Knew I Would Need This by V from S3}

reward.name: Brutal Grip
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Strength 13+; Level 16 Reward}
{@b Heavy Duelist.} You can wield a Melee weapon with the Two-Handed property in one hand.
{@b Versatile Dual Wielder.} While wielding a Melee weapon that lacks the Two-Handed property in one hand, the weapon has the Light property for you.
{@i Adapted from VSS:PP}

reward.name: Chronomaster
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Must have suffered the Chronal Anomaly curse}
{@i Multiple time streams flow through you, and you've learned to master them all.}

{@b Moment of Skill}. When you roll an ability check, you may use your Reaction to reach into a possible future where you do well. Instead of rolling a {@dice d20}, you may instead treat the roll as if you rolled a 15. When you do so, you age by {@dice 2d20} years. You must use this feature before you roll the ability check. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so until you finish a Long Rest.

{@b Withering Touch}. You've learned to manipulate the time streams of creatures. Once on each of your turns, you may take a Magic action to make an Unarmed Strike against a creature. Instead of taking damage, that creature must make a Charisma saving throw (DC = 8 + Con mod + Prof Bonus). On a failed save, that creature suffers 1 Exhaustion level and is aged {@dice 2d4} years, and you decrease in age by the same amount. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw has Immunity to this feature. You may only affect a creature with this feature once every hour.

{@b Flood of Time}. You've learned to manipulate the time streams of objects. As a Magic action, you can fling objects forward in their time streams in a 10-foot Cube. Each nonmagical object that isn't being worn or carried within the Cube has their Maximum Hit Points reduced by {@dice 2d6}. If this would reduce an object's Maximum Hit Points below 0, the object is destroyed. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all uses once finishing a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Collective Predation
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward, Hunter's Mark}

Whenever a creature other than you hits the target of your Hunter's Mark with an attack roll, they deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Force damage to that target. Multiple instances of Collective Predation do not stack.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Curiosity
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}

You always have Augury, Divination, Commune, Contact Other Plane, and Legend Lore prepared. You can cast one of those spells without expending a spell slot or material components. Once you cast a spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Eldritch Gatling
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Warlock, Level 16 Reward, Eldritch Blast}
Your Eldritch Blast fires an additional beam.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Enhanced Pact Magic
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Warlock, Level 16 Reward}
Your number of spell slots from Pact Magic is increased by 1.
{@i Made by V}

reward.name: Enduring Capacity
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}
If you are Incapacitated while under an effect (except for Concentration) that would end early if you are Incapacitated, you can choose to have the effect not end.
{@i Made by V}

reward.name: Expert Bonus
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}
Your Proficiency Bonus increases by 1.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Gork's Cracked the Code
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Orc}
{@i "Little thing I learned back in the day. Elves don't want you to know this." - Gork}

You have stolen one of elves' most well kept secrets and figured out how to finally be good at shooting. You gain the Elven Accuracy feat, ignoring its prerequisite. You have Disadvantage on all Influence checks towards an elf.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Horde Slayer
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}

When an attack you make reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points, you can target another creature within range and, if the original attack roll can hit it, applies any remaining damage to it. If that creature is likewise reduced to 0 Hit Points, repeat this process, carrying over the remaining damage until there are no valid targets, or until the damage carried over fails to reduce a creature to 0 Hit Points.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Indominus
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}

At the start of your turn, if you aren't Incapacitated, you may gain Temporary Hit Points equal to twice your Constitution modifier (minimum of 2). Additionally, you can add your Constitution modifier to your Death Saving Throws.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Intelligent Napper
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Wizard, Level 16 Reward}
When you use Arcane Recovery, the number of levels you can recover increases by your Intelligence Modifier. Additionally, Arcane Recovery can recover your sixth-level slot.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Master of All Weapons
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Fighter Level 16 Reward}
You have mastery in all weapons.

A Little Bit of Everything: You may unequip and equip a weapon after each attack when you take the Attack Action on your turn. Once per turn, when you do this, you may attack an additional time if the weapon you equipped has a different mastery property than the one you unequipped.
{@i Made by Gavin}

reward.name: Planar Voyager
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}

You always have the Plane Shift spell prepared. When you cast it, it doesn’t require components. Additionally, you can cast it without expending a spell slot, but you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this gift (choose when you receive it).
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Positional Master
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}
Immediately after you roll Initiative, you can take a Reaction to cast Scatter without expending a spell slot.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Positive Energy Flux
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward; Bard, Cleric or Druid}

When you restore Hit Points to a creature with a spell, any excess healing (Hit Points not healed due to being at maximum health), the target instead receives a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to the excess healing. These Temporary Hit Points cannot exceed the target's maximum Hit Points.
These Temporary Hit Points remain for 1 minute.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Psionic Reach
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}
You gain Resistance to Psychic damage.
You always have Telekinesis prepared. You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot. Once you cast this spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
The spellcasting ability is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma when you cast the spell with this gift (choose when you gain the gift).
{@i Adapted the Draconic Gift. Made by Landon.}

reward.name: Sickening Greed
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Artificer, Level 16 Reward}
You can attune to an additional magic item.
You can use a Magic Action to touch a magic item and immediately attune to it. Once you do so, you can't again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Sneakier
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Rogue, Level 16 Reward}
Your Sneak Attack dice increases by {@dice 4d6}.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Soul Exalt: Cherished Companionship
entries: When you roll Initiative, you can take a Reaction to cast one of the following spells without spending a spell slot: Beacon of Hope, Calm Emotions, Circle of Power, Fizban's Platinum Shield. You expend Hit Dice equal to the spell’s level.
If you took your Reaction to cast one of those spells, allied creatures under the effects of that spell have a +1 bonus to saving throws.

When you or a creature you can see that's under the effects of your spells fails a saving throw, you can take a Reaction to expend up to 5 Hit Dice and add the number of Hit Dice expended to the result.
If you do not have enough Hit Dice to expend, you can instead take Exhaustion levels. Each Exhaustion level you take is worth up to 4 Hit Dice.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Soul Exalt: Loyal Guardian
entries: When you roll Initiative, you can choose an ally you can see to be your charge.
While within 10 feet of your charge, you have a +1 bonus to your attack rolls, and can do the following:

{@i  When your charge is hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to expend up to 5 Hit Dice and add the number of Hit Dice expended to your charge’s AC until the start of your charge's next turn.

{@i  When your charge takes damage, you can take a Reaction to expend 2 Hit Dice to have your charge to instead take half that damage and you take the other half. The damage you take cannot be reduced by any means.
{@i  While your charge is Bloodied, your +1 bonus to attack rolls becomes a +3 bonus.
{@i  You can cast the following spells targeting your charge, expending a number of Hit Dice equal to the spell’s level: Greater Restoration, Lesser Restoration, Remove Curse, Heal.
If you do not have enough Hit Dice to expend, you can instead take Exhaustion levels. Each Exhaustion level you take is worth up to 4 Hit Dice.
{@i Made by Chrys}
