reward.name: Soul Exalt: Unbreakable Champion
entries: When you roll Initiative, you can take a Reaction to cast one of the following spells on yourself without spending a spell slot: Death Armor, Elminster's Effulgent Spheres, Freedom of Movement, Masochism. You expend Hit Dice equal to the spell’s level.
While under the effects of those spells, you have a +1 bonus to damage rolls. While Bloodied, the +1 bonus instead becomes +3.
When you miss an attack roll, you can expend up to 5 Hit Dice and add the number of Hit Dice expended to the attack roll.

When you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can expend up to 15 Hit Dice. You gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 3 times the Hit Dice expended. While you have these Temporary Hit Points, you have Immunity to the Unconscious condition and your weapon attacks deal an extra {@dice 1d6} Force damage.
If you do not have enough Hit Dice to expend, you can instead take Exhaustion levels. Each Exhaustion level you take is worth up to 4 Hit Dice.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Tougher
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Level 16 Reward}

Your Hit Point maximum increases by an amount equal to twice your character level when you gain this gift. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your Hit Point maximum increases by an additional 2 Hit Points.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Perfect Order
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Trance of Order}

Before you make an attack roll while your Trance of Order is active, you can instead use your perfect order for the attack. If the target's AC is equal to or less than 10 + the would be bonus to the attack roll, your attack automatically hits, otherwise it misses. When you deal damage for the attack you don't roll damage, instead you treat any roll of a {@dice d4} as a 3, a {@dice d6} as a 4, a {@dice d8} as a 5, a {@dice d10} as a 6, a {@dice d12} as a 7, and a {@dice d20} as an 11 as if you had rolled.
{@i Based off of Inevitables. Made by June}

reward.name: Supernatural Boobs
entries: {@i Made by Tres, Repassed from WM3}

reward.name: This Coughing Baby Got Hands!
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Constitution score of 13 or less}
{@i Born Carrier}. You can take an action to replicate the effects of the Influenza (DC 19) spell. Once you use this feature 3 times, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Natural Rapper}. You have a +5 bonus to Charisma (Intimidation and Performance) checks when you perform in a rap battle.

{@i Survivor in Desolation}. Once per Long Rest, when you would be reduced to 0 Hit points from a single source of damage that exceeds your Hit Point maximum, you can take a Reaction to fall to 1 Hit Point instead.
{@i Underdog Victory}. You are immune to hydrogen bombs.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: All That's Left
entries: You are unable to turn right.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: As Intended
entries: {@i We did that all in an hour!}

You are incapable of playing D&D for longer than an hour, that's how long the game is supposed to last! Every hour which passes in a session, you receive a cumulative -1 penalty to all {@dice D20} Tests. This effect ends when the current session is declared over by the GM. This curse can only be removed by completing a session in less than an hour.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Aw...
entries: If you critically miss on an attack roll, you are Depressed until the start of your next turn.
{@i Made by V}

reward.name: Back in My Day!
entries: One of your features or spells, decided by the GM when this curse is given out, is replaced by its version from 5e 2014 Edition.
{@i Made by V}

reward.name: Big and Full
entries: Every sentence you say must begin with "Ouuuh I'm so ___"
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Blessing of Amiability
entries: The cursed creature, regardless of species, class, background, or alignment becomes incredibly nice, kind, and thoughtful. They do everything possible to help those around them and fall in with their plans and suggestions. They freely give away their possessions, bestow beneficial spells and resources, try to be friendly with everyone (even monsters and enemies), helps with chores. They are appalled at theft and refuse to take what isn't theirs, and they refuse to attack or harm creatures. If they or a party member is attacked, they will offer gifts and seek peace. The character tries to explain to the attacker why everyone should live in love, peace, and harmony.
The curse cannot compel the cursed creature to take actions that directly cause harm to themself.
The bearer of this curse gains the ability to conjure 6 chocolate chip cookies or a handful of flowers as a Magic action. The cookies go stale after 24 hours.
{@i From WM3, Based on the 2e Amulet of Amiability, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Cat got your tongue?
entries: You cannot speak, only meow.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Curse of Euclid
entries: When playing on a square grid, the way you move diagonally changes, becoming less simple.

When measuring range or moving diagonally on a grid, the first diagonal square counts as 5 feet, but the second diagonal square counts as 10 feet. This pattern of 5 feet and then 10 feet continues whenever you're counting diagonally, even if you move horizontally or vertically between different bits of diagonal movement.
For example, you might move one square diagonally (5 feet), then three squares straight (15 feet), and then another square diagonally (10 feet) for a total movement of 30 feet.
{@i Repass from S3}

reward.name: Cyclonically Rupturable
entries: You are considered to be a Pistol for the purposes of the Cyclone Rupture Talent.
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Dark Gift of Grackle-lung
entries: Your lungs burn as if from acute smoke inhalation, and you exhale wisps of smoke with every breath. You suffer Disadvantage to all Dexterity (Stealth) checks due to a constant, irritating cough brought on by this affliction.

As an action, you may cough up a billowing cloud of foul-smelling smog in a 10 ft. radius Emanation centered on you, which remains in place. This smog's area is Heavily Obscured, and dissipates after 1 minute (or 1 round in a moderate or stronger wind). Once this ability has been used, you must finish a Short or Long Rest before it can be used again.
{@i By Adventurer’s League, ported by Aris}

reward.name: Druidic Runescorned
entries: While these runes are branded on your body, creatures automatically recognize you to be an oathbreaker, corruptor of sanctuaries, defiler of the sacred groves, and outcast. You have Disadvantage on Charisma based ability checks while these runes are on your body.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Drums of the Abyss
entries: You can hear your own heartbeat. In addition, you are obsessed with your own heart, and you are convinced there is something deeper in the pattern of heart beats. Perhaps it intrigues you, perhaps it terrifies you, but you can't stop thinking about it.
Sometimes, you want to rip out your heart, either so that you can hear the beat more clearly, or so that you can examine it closer.
{@i From S3, Made by Aris}

reward.name: Dysphoria
entries: An overwhelming dissatisfaction engulfs you. You have Disadvantage on all Ability Checks and Attack Rolls. Participating in an activity that brings you joy lifts the curse for 1 minute.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Felt by its Embrace
entries: {@i Have my company. Feel my warmth. You are alone no longer.}

Whenever you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points, you instead drop to 1 Hit Point and transform and lose control. Your skin begins to constantly shed blood and transform into an Aberrant Cultist as if through True Polymorph.

While in this form, whenever you choose a creature as a target, you must choose the target at random from among the creatures you can see within range of the attack, spell, or other ability it's using. If an enemy provokes an Opportunity Attack, you must make the attack if you are able to.
If you lose the Temporary Hit Points granted by the transformation, you die and your body returns to normal ending the Curse.
{@i Made by Althaea and June}

reward.name: Full Round Attack
entries: If you moved more than 5 feet and take the Attack action, you cannot benefit from the Extra Attack feature until the end of your turn.
Additionally, if you use the Attack action to make more than one attack using the Extra Attack feature, your speed is reduced to 5 feet until the end of your turn.

reward.name: Game Balanced
entries: You must power through a proper Adventuring Day™ before you’re tired enough to rest. After you finish a Short or Long Rest, you must roll initiative at least twice before you can take a Short Rest. After you finish a Long Rest, you must roll initiative at least seven times before you can Long Rest again.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Impotence
entries: You are incapable of Zerking it (or Zoarkin'auffe).
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: It’s Always Cooler to Call Your Attacks
entries: Whenever you cast a spell, you are compelled to yell the name of the spell as part of casting. If you are unable to do so, the spell fails. If the spell had a verbal component, this replaces it.

reward.name: It's just gonna ask you a couple of questions...
entries: Whenever you make a purchase, you have to pay an additional 20%.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Movement Phase
entries: You can no longer split your movement between your actions. Once you move in a turn using your speed, you cannot move again unless you use an Action or a Bonus Action to move.

reward.name: Multiple Attack Penalty
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Extra Attack Feature}
When you make multiple Attacks in one turn as part of the Attack action, each attack after the first receives a cumulative -5 penalty to hit. (Second attack has -5, Third has -10, etc.)
{@i Inspired by PF2E/D&D 3.5e, Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Nonverbal
entries: You cannot speak or perform verbal components, and any telepathic communications from you become garbled into emojis.
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Oiled Up
entries: Your hair and skin is constantly oily. You sweat oil.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Ough, so covered in honey...
entries: You are covered in honey, thus attracting many insects- especially bees- and the occasional bear. This is just unpleasant.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Ow the Edge
entries: Whenever you would deal a damage type other than Slashing, you deal Slashing damage instead.
{@i Try not to cut yourself.}
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Pacifist
entries: You are unable to take the Attack action and cannot knowingly do harm to other creatures.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Phantom Pains
entries: You still feel the pain of what you once lost. Any Prosthetic Arm you have equipped does not function with the same strength as your original. This arm can manipulate an object, open or close a door or container, pick up or set down a Tiny object, or wield a weapon that has the Light property.
This Curse can be removed with the Regenerate spell or a 7th level Remove Curse
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Pig Snout, Eternal
entries: You grow a beautiful, snuffling pig snout. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell. In addition, you gain great fondness of truffles; when you catch a whiff of one, you cannot help but disregard everything you were doing just to find and devour it.
{@i Based on Pig Snout, Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Pirate Speak
entries: Yargh, yer language been set to Sea Dog! Ye gotta speak like ye've got scurvy now!
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Pretty Bad Medicine, Actually
entries: {@i This is serious, asshole.}
Whenever you, the player, laugh, your character takes {@dice 1d6} psychic damage.
{@i Inspired by Legends of Avantris, Made by Lily}

reward.name: Pretty Discerning
entries: ‘Don’t feed a guy a sponge, Bobby!’
You attempt to eat anything offered to you as food, even if it isn’t.

reward.name: Rainy Day
entries: A rain cloud follows above you, constantly keeping you soaked. When the water touches any creature or object you aren't carrying, it vanishes. If you get angry, the cloud starts to storm.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Regressive Tendencies
entries: If you can change your list of prepared spells whenever you finish a Long Rest, the way you prepare spells changes.
When you finish a Long Rest, for each of your spell slots, you must dedicate one spell you have prepared. You can only use that spell slot to cast that spell.
Repass from S3

reward.name: Rule of Hospitality
entries: You are forced to be very polite. Say please and thank you. Don't start fights in a home. Open doors for others. If you are in someone else's home, you must defend them from attackers.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Rule of Ownership
entries: You are unable to take anything that isn't yours without permission from the owner.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Rule of Reciprocity
entries: Whenever someone gives you a gift, favor, or similar, you must give them something equal in return.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Slop.
entries: {@i Step 1: Slop. Step 2: Slop. Step 3: Slop. Repeat from Step 1 until thoroughly Slopped.}
Whenever you craft an item from an Artisan's Tool, it turns into useless{@i  Slop.
{@i Note: Not all might find this useless
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Stonepilled
entries: Every time you say a noun, you must append it with 'stone'.
{@i Ex. "The quick brown foxstone jumps over the lazy dogstone."}
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Terminal Terminal Velocity
entries: {@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: The Eeper
entries: When you fall Unconscious, you are unable to be woken up until you have slept for 8 hours.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: The Influencerrr
entries: The only Action you can take is the Influence action.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: The MacLeod Effect
entries: Whenever you succeed on a Dexterity (Stealth) check made to Hide, Sneaky Snitch plays from around you and can be heard in a 30 foot Emanation originating from yourself.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: The Unknown
entries: Anytime you leave a creature's perception, they forget who you are. The next time they see you, they believe it's the first time you have met.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Think Before You Speak
entries: You can only speak when what you're about to say aligns with the attribute(s) determined by the GM when you receive this curse (ie. true, helpful, inspiring, necessary, kind).
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: This is so glad…
entries: Every time you are glad, Happy plays loudly.

reward.name: This is so sad…
entries: Every time you are sad, Despacito plays loudly.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Time to Get a Watch
entries: You always know the location of the nearest clock and can hear it ticking, as long as there is a clock on the same plane of existence as you
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Tongue of Lily
entries: When annoyed or angry, you must speak with a southern accent.
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Uncertain
entries: You are only able to speak in questions. Failing to do so causes you to take {@dice 1d6} Necrotic damage that cannot be reduced, and prevents any other creature from hearing what you said.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Visions of Battle
entries: Your mind is clouded with thoughts of bloodshed, all you see is death. Any Ersatz Eye you have equipped does not function as well as your original. You have Disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks as well as Wisdom (Insight) checks to determine a creature's hostility.
This Curse can be removed with the Regenerate spell or a 7th level Remove Curse
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Vivziepop Writing
entries: Profanity is funny. You feel compelled to include at least one of Carlin's seven dirty words in every sentence you say. You take {@dice 1d4} Psychic damage for every sentence that does not include those words. Other profane words may be acceptable substitutes at the GM's discretion.
{@i Made by VP Ryan Gabrillo, Repassed from WM3}

reward.name: Voice of the Nerd
entries: You must speak with a nasally voice. In addition, you have an intense urge to correct people when they're wrong, and often start such corrections with "ermm, actually".
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: War Weariness
entries: You can not move as you used to, there is a crushing weight on you at all times. Any Prosthetic Leg you have equipped does not function with the same strength as your original. Your Speed is reduced by 5ft and you may not take the Dash action.
This Curse can be removed with the Regenerate spell or a 7th level Remove Curse.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Welcoming Party
entries: You are unable to enter a room without being invited in. You are compelled to invite anyone you see into your home.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Wither and Wither More
entries: When Blight is cast targeting you, you don't make a save and simply wither and die.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: You First.
entries: Whether it's because you are aura farming, are overly polite, or just have a godlike reaction speed, whenever you would roll Initiative, you instead get an Initiative of 0. If you still go before someone else, you can point and laugh at them.
{@i From WM3, by Christopher.}

reward.name: Lycanthropy (Bat)
entries: You are heavily swayed to a Neutral Evil alignment, and gain minor bat features.

{@b Shape-shift.} As a Bonus Action, you can shape-shift into a Large Giant Bat or Medium Bat-Hybrid.  While a Giant Bat or Medium Bat-Hybrid, you have a Blindsight of 60 feet and a Fly Speed of 60 feet.  Your game statistics are otherwise the same in each form, though you cannot speak if you are a Giant Bat. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying isn't transformed. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it's practical for the new form to wear or wield a piece of equipment. You can use another Bonus Action to shape-shift back.
{@b Sunlight Sensitivity.} While you are a Giant Bat or Bat Hybrid and are in sunlight, you have disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

{@b Bite.} While you are a Giant Bat or Bat Hybrid, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you add your Dexterity modifier to the attack and damage when you attack with it.

It deals {@dice 1d6} piercing damage. A damaged creature is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC10. Failure: The target gains this curse. Success: The target is immune to your curse for 24 hours. A creature automatically succeeds if it cannot become a Lycanthrope, like most non-Humanoids.
{@i Formatted like Landon’s Lycanthropy curse, Made by Marvin}

reward.name: Lycanthropy (Rat)
entries: You are heavily swayed to a Lawful Evil alignment, and gain minor rat features.

{@b Shape-Shift.}  As a Bonus Action, you can shape-shift into a Small Rat or Medium Rat-Hybrid. Your game statistics are the same in each form, though you cannot speak if you are a Rat. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying isn't transformed. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it's practical for the new form to wear or wield a piece of equipment. You can use another Bonus Action to shape-shift back.

{@b Bite.} While you are a Rat or Rat-Hybrid, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you add your Dexterity modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it.

It deals {@dice 1d6} piercing damage on a hit. A damaged creature is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 11. Failure: The target gains this curse. Success: The target is immune to your curse for 24 hours. A creature automatically succeeds if it cannot become a Lycanthrope, like most non-Humanoids.
{@i Formatted like Landon’s Lycanthropy curse, Made by Marvin}

reward.name: Lycanthropy (Shark)
entries: {@i You are heavily swayed to a Neutral Evil alignment, become a Monstrosity (Lycanthrope), and gain minor shark-like features.}

{@b Shape-Shift.} As a Bonus Action, you can shape-shift into a Shark or Shark-Hybrid. While a Shark or Shark-Hybrid, you have a Blindsight of 30 feet, a Swim Speed of 40 feet. While as a Shark-Hybrid, you can breathe air and water. While as a Shark, you are Large and can breathe only underwater. Your game statistics are otherwise the same in each form, though you cannot speak if you are a Shark. Any equipment you are wearing or carrying isn't transformed. Worn equipment functions as normal, but the DM decides whether it's practical for the new form to wear or wield a piece of equipment. You can use another Bonus Action to shape-shift back.

{@b Bite.} While you are a Shark or Shark Hybrid, you manifest a natural weapon. It counts as a simple melee weapon for you, and you add your Strength modifier to the attack and damage rolls when you attack with it, as normal. You have Advantage on attack rolls with this weapon against Bloodied creatures.

It deals {@dice 2d8} Piercing damage on a hit. A damaged creature is subjected to the following effect. Constitution Saving Throw: DC 14. Failure: The target gains this curse. Success: The target is immune to your curse for 24 hours. A creature automatically succeeds if it cannot become a Lycanthrope, like most non-Humanoids.

{@b Transformation.} When you start your turn next to a bloodied enemy, you must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you lose control of yourself and shape-shift into a Shark or Shark-Hybrid, and cannot shape-shift back into your true form until there are no Bloodied creatures other than you within 120 feet of you. You have no memory of your actions during this time, the GM having control over those actions instead.
{@i Formatted like Landon's Lycanthropy curse, Made by Aris}

reward.name: A Name Given
entries: You have given your name to a fey. You have Disadvantage on all attack rolls made against the fey and Disadvantage on saving throws against the fey's magic. No one is able to remember your name, or speak it. Any written mention of the name becomes incomprehensible.
As a Magic action, the fey is able to perfectly disguise itself as you. It can drop the disguise at any time (no action required).
This curse can only be cursed by dealing with the fey who took the name, or by casting Legal Name Change, permanently replacing the stolen name with a new one.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Absolution
entries: You lose all memory of every Evil deed you've ever committed. In addition, you cannot knowingly commit Evil deeds, as you lack the desire to.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Boneless
entries: Your bones have been removed from you, but you still live. You are Prone, and cannot stop being Prone while you have this curse.
{@b Removing the Curse.} Regain your bones.

reward.name: Chronal Anomaly
entries: You are caught between multiple time streams, all flowing in different directions.

When you finish a Long Rest, roll {@dice 2d10}. If the total is an even number, your age decreases by that amount, leaving you no younger than 15 years old. If the total is an odd number, you increase in age by that amount.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Clinging Chaos
entries: When you cast a spell, roll {@dice 1d20}. On a 1, roll for a random effect on the Wild Magic Surge (2014e) table. In addition, if you roll a 1 on a {@dice D20} test, a vortex of erratic magic surrounds you in a 10-foot Emanation for 1 minute. Each creature, including yourself, that starts its turn within the Emanation is subject to the effects of the Wildstorm table.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Ego Death
entries: You have lost who you are. Your memories feel disconnected and unlike yourself. A part of you is missing. You and everything you carry appears in grayscale.
This curse can only be cured by finding yourself.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Hard Mode
entries: Whenever you make a {@dice D20} Test, you roll a {@dice d12} instead of a {@dice d20}.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Nature's Calling
entries: While you are cursed this way, you replace all your class levels with levels in Ranger. The curser decides what Ranger Subclass.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Landon}

reward.name: One Smart Feller...
entries: You feel compelled to say "One smart feller, he felt smart" three times fast. This curse is cured when you do so successfully.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Tears of Sorrow
entries: You cannot stop crying. Everything that was; every wish for what could've been; the yearn of what you could be; all of these reverberate through your mind- through each action you take- as an incessant reminder of your loss and grief. These tears are shed and you cannot bring yourself to cease them.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: The Cheese Touch
entries: When you touch another creature, they gain this curse and you lose it. You cannot ~~teansfer~~ {@b transfer} the curse to the creature who bestowed it onto you.
This curse cannot be cured by any means.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Whimsical
entries: You are so full of whimsy! You love frolicking in the fields, laughing with friends, and being a part of the community! You don't understand violence and why people would hurt others. You have Immunity against effects that would make you sad or mad. In addition, you cannot willingly harm other creatures, opting to talk things out in violent situations!
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: 🗿
entries: As a Magic action you can expend this charm to give yourself the Petrified condition.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: 🥒
entries: {@i Funniest shit I've ever seen}
As a Magic action you become a pickle. This can only be undone by Wish.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Carcinization
entries: {@i The Crab Cycle: there is only one step and it is crab.}
As a Magic action, you can cast True Polymorph on yourself into a Crab. When you do so, the duration of the spell is Until Dispelled. Once the charm is used, it vanishes from you.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Charm of Acheron, the Infernal Battlefield
entries: When you score a Critical Hit, you can expend the Charm (no action required) to deal an extra 10 damage of one of the types dealt by the attack.
{@i Taken from Manifest Charm of Shavarath, the Battleground from EFA}

reward.name: Chaos Charm
entries: As a Magic action, you can expend this charm to roll on the Wild Magic Sorcery's Wild Magic Surge table, creating the corresponding magical effect.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: Charm of Inspiration
entries: {@i You did something so inspiring, you deserve to take it into the next session.}
If you lack Heroic Inspiration, you can expend this charm to gain it (No action required). You can only have one Charm of Inspiration at a time. (edited)
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Death Charm
entries: As a Magic action, you can expend this charm to instantly die.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: I'll Do It for You
entries: {@i Made by Gavin}

reward.name: Love is in the Air?
entries: As a Magic action you can expend this charm to fill a 30-foot radius Sphere centered on yourself with Poisonous Gas. The gas lasts for 1 minute or until it's dispersed by a strong wind.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Minor Blessing
entries: A god has noticed your prayers or some great deed you've undertaken, and given you their divine blessing.

When you receive this charm, the GM determines which ability is blessed. When you would fail a Skill Check which uses the blessed ability, you may use this charm to increase the result of the roll by 10.
{@i Made by Gavin}

reward.name: Nah, I Can't…
entries: As a Bonus Action, you drop to 0 hit points. Once you do so, this Charm disappears from you.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: No, Thank you!
entries: Too Scary! - As a Reaction to anything, you can immediately teleport to back to the guild and leave the session. You don't get any Rewards. After you use this ability, the charm fades away.
{@i From S3 By Landon}

reward.name: Nothing Ever Happens
entries: {@i Well, would you look at the time...}

When a GM rolls on a random encounter table, you can expend a charge of this charm to instead have them roll a {@dice d100}. On a 1-36, nothing happens. On a 36-75, nothing happens. On a 75-99, nothing happens. On a 100, something happens, and they can roll again on their table.
While you have this charm, burgers are tasteless.
This charm comes with {@dice 1d4} charges. Once the last charge is expended, it disappears in a thoroughly disappointing and anticlimactic way.
{@i Made by Ryan}

reward.name: Rainbow Charm
entries: As a Magic action you can expend this charm to cast Rainbow Bridge. When cast in this way, it does not require Concentration.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Something Just Happened
entries: If you, the player, are aware of something happening that your character isn't, such as due to a party split, you can use an action to use this charm to roll a DC 15 Constitution (Perception) check. On a success, your character knows that Something Just Happened, as well as a general direction to where it happened, but absolutely no further information. Once used, this charm vanishes from you.
{@i Made by V}

reward.name: Something Will Happen
entries: When a GM rolls for a Recharge ability, you can use this charm to have it automatically recharge the ability instead. Once you use this charm, it vanishes from you.
{@i I think something will happen! ☝️🤓}
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Sorgy...
entries: As a Magic action you can cast Ceremony targeting yourself, using the rite of Atonement. Once used the charm is expended.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Wait!
entries: As a Reaction to a creature making an attack or casting a spell, you can take an Influence action against that creature. Afterwards, it can continue its course of action, or lose the attack or spell. If it chooses to lose the attack or spell, the charm is expended. Either way, you cannot use this charm again until you finish a Long Rest.

reward.name: We Can Be Bees
entries: {@i This is good news.}

As a Magic Action, you can activate this charm, casting the Animal Shapes spell. When you cast it this way, the creature that is chosen to transform into is a giant bee (using Giant Wasp statistics). The charm vanishes after you activate it.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

reward.name: What are we gonna do?!?
entries: You can cast Divination as a Magic Action with this charm. When you do so, you can only ask about how to accomplish a goal or activity within the next 30 minutes. Once you do so, this Charm disappears from you.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Wurm
entries: As a Magic action you become a worm. After a minute you revert back to your normal form.
This charm disappears after you become a worm.
{@i Made by Laz}

reward.name: You can be a bridge
entries: As a Magic action, you can become a bridge.
# 📚 Story Records
# Table of Contents {#table-of-contents-6}
[{@b Table of Contents	1}](#table-of-contents-6)
[{@b Story Records	1}](#story-records)
[Lineage of Steel	11](#lineage-of-steel)
[Scholar Exploration Corps	12](#scholar-exploration-corps)
[The Scholars of Millleniums Past	13](#the-scholars-of-millleniums-past)
# Story Records {#story-records}

reward.name: 7 Thousand People in the World and I Saved Them All
entries: {@i You achieved your admirable ambition. You saved them all.}
You saved everyone in the world. You count as the Recipient of every Stone.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Angrycada
entries: You've angered the squidcada community. All squidcada's Attitudes towards you are initially Hostile. This lasts for 5 years (when the current generation of squidcada dies out), or until you make 10 successful Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks towards different squidcada with the intention to calm or befriend it.
{@i Made by Chrys}
{@i ![][image8]![][image9]}

reward.name: Arms Dealer Dealer
entries: You’ve earned the respect of Castil, premier gunsmith of Westgate. He offers a small discount at his shop, access to more select merchandise, and more jobs when he has them.
{@i By Lily.}

reward.name: ASYS Trial Participant
entries: You have participated in an ASYS Corporation lab trial, contributing to the research of magic. Thank you for your efforts towards the betterment of society.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: A Voice for a Life
entries: {@i You've given a cat your voice to sing in exchange for one of its lives.}
Whenever you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can choose to lose this Story Record and instead drop to 1 Hit Points and gain 40 Temporary Hit Points which last for 1 minute.
While you have this Story Record, you have the Cat got your tongue? Curse,
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: A Voice for the Restless Spirits
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Bard}
While you are in the lands of Mordent, your Bard subclass is temporarily changed to the College of Spirits.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Blessing of the Orc Stone
entries: I have saved the life of an Orc but died in the process. In return I have been reincarnated into a Tough Boss. My species has been replaced by:
{@b Tough Boss}
Creature Type: Humanoid
Size: Small or Medium
Speed: 30 feet
{@b Bully.} You gain proficiency in Intimidation.
{@b Combat Knowledge.} You gain proficiency with a Martial weapon of your choice. When you finish a Long Rest, you can swap this proficiency to a different weapon.

{@b Stone-Called Killer.} When you finish a Long Rest, you can create a magic stone. When you make a stone, all stones you previously made turn to dust. A creature other than yourself can attune to this stone. You have Advantage on attack rolls against a creature if an ally attuned to your stone is within 5 feet of it, and the ally does not have the Incapacitated condition. While attuned, the creature can take a Magic action to teleport you to a point they can see within 15 feet of themselves. Once this action is used three times, the stone turns to dust.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Bought a Trip to Wildspace
entries: You bought a trip to Wildspace! Nice.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Demon Spawn
entries: For whatever benign reason, you have been transformed into a creature that carries the essence of evil itself. The traits gained from your species are replaced with the following:
{@i Creature Type}: Your creature type is Fiend (Demon).
{@i Size}: Medium or Small.
{@i Speed}: 30 feet.
{@i Abyssal Fortitude}: You are Resistant to Cold, Fire, and Lightning Damage.
{@i Alignment}: Your alignment is Chaotic Evil.

{@i Abyssal Corruption}: You gain a randomly determined flaw from the [Abyssal Corruption](https://5e.tools/tables.html#optional%20rule%3a%20abyssal%20corruption%3b%20abyssal%20corruption_dmg) table, ignoring rolls of 10. You yearn for the Abyss; while not in the Abyss, you have a -1 to all {@dice D20} tests.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Dingy Hospitality
entries: You have the protection of Balabar Smenk, an upstart criminal from Westgate. When in Westgate, you can find a place to hide, rest, or recuperate within the criminal network, unless you have shown yourself to be a danger to those who are sheltering you. They will shield you from the law or anyone else searching for you, though they will not risk their lives for you.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Dog Veteran
entries: You have served in the Dog Wars in the Beastlands. Dogs that have been to the Beastlands are compelled to salute you upon recognizing you.
{@i Made by Denny}

reward.name: Efreeti's Serf
entries: {@i Your soul is binded to an Efreeti in order to do its whims.}
You are Charmed by that Efreeti. They have a telepathic link with you.
As a Magic action, that Efreeti can deal {@dice 5d10} Fire damage to you. This damage cannot be reduced in any way.
When you deal Fire damage to a creature, you can have that creature start burning. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Forever Torn
entries: Any body parts you are missing when you receive this story record are unable to be regrown.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Heart of the Fragaria
entries: Thanks to your actions, you have been blessed by the great Ananassa. You are Immune to Strawberry damage, however, you are vulnerable to Blueberry damage.
{@i Made by Vic}

reward.name: Known by its Embrace
entries: {@i Without me, you are nothing. In my arms, you are everything. To bleed is to feel. To feel is to see. To see is to know. To know is to love.}
Expect to know its embrace once again.
{@i Made by Althaea}

reward.name: The Gift of Lolth
entries: Your magic now relies on the Demon Weave, instead of the Weave.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Grand Theft Westgate
entries: You took part in a high profile burglary in Westgate, which resulted in multiple deaths. Local authorities are on the lookout for you, making you no longer welcome in the city.
If you take the Visit Westgate bastion turn, roll a {@dice d20}. On a 1 you are found by authorities and arrested.
{@i Made by Andrew}

reward.name: Greased Hog Lightning
entries: The Hoggler is known throughout Raven's Bluff to have killed a goblin under the Crossed Quils' House while covered in Grease. Local legends are formed around this event.
{@i Made by Patrick}

reward.name: Green
entries: Somehow, you have become green.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Half-Blood
entries: You have gained the respect of a demigod by aiding them. The demigods appreciate your help and respect you deeply (at least some of them).
{@i Made by Krypt}

reward.name: Honorary Hatter
entries: You’ve passed the Trial of Hats and garnered Maddison Milner’s respect.
{@i Made by Marvin}

reward.name: Horrible Husband
entries: You've married an uvuudaum, a horrible entity formed from a thought of the Far Realm. Your time left ticks ever faster.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Is That a Dead Wizard in Your Pocket?
entries: You are playing host to the spirit of a long dead—and rather eccentric—ghost. While the ghost does not control you, you pick up some of its characteristics. As long as you have this Story Record, you gain a personality trait, ideal, bond, or flaw of your choice that reflects the ghost’s subtle influence on your personality. You may remove this Story Record at any time by informing the ghost that he or she is no longer welcome (politely or otherwise).
{@i From Adventurer's League}

reward.name: Legendary Importance
entries: You are considered to be of legendary importance for the purposes of the legend lore spell.
{@i Originally made by Aris}

reward.name: Marketable Plushie
entries: {@i You've managed to give up your merch rights.}
You have allowed a creature to sell merch of your likeness. You occasionally find it for sale when shopping.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Mist Wanderer
entries: The Mists whisper to you and guide you through their mysterious eddies. You immediately know if an object you're touching is a Mist talisman. You recognize where the talisman originates from if you have been to its domain of origin.
{@i From VRGR}

reward.name: Mole Veteran
entries: You fought in the mole war.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Mole Champion
entries: You have won the mole war. As a result you can take the Mole prestige class ignoring its special prerequisites.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Omg kitty
entries: じしˍ,)ノ
Somehow you have turned into a Cat. You species is replaced with the following:
{@b Cat}
Creature Type: Beast
Size: Tiny or Small
Speed: 40 feet, Climb Speed equal to your Speed
{@b Cat's Claws.} You can use your claws to make Unarmed Strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals {@dice 1d4} + your Strength modifier Slashing damage.
{@b Darkvision.} You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in that darkness only as shades of gray.

{@b Feline Agility.} Your reflexes and agility allow you to move with a burst of speed. When you move on your turn in combat, you can double your speed until the end of the turn. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you move 0 feet on one of your turns.
{@b Precise Mouth.} You can hold items in your mouth as if it was a hand.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Return to Love
entries: {@i You have traded a language with "yourself"}
You can speak and understand Sylvan. In return, you can no longer speak or write Common but still understand it.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Sacred Vow of Abstinence
entries: You have taken a vow of abstinence, and have chosen to abstain from certain worldly pleasures or courses of action such as alcohol or stimulants, sex, material possession, or violence. What you abstain from is determined when you receive this Story Record.
If you ever break your abstinence, you suffer a -1 penalty on all {@dice D20} tests until you earn forgiveness from those you swore abstinence to.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Savage Creature
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Aberration, Beast, Dragon, Fey, Giant, Humanoid, or Monstrosity.}

{@b Savage Frenzy.} You are Chaotic Evil. You are Hostile to all creatures that lack this Story Record and that aren't Demons. In addition, you have Advantage on Strength and Constitution {@dice D20} Tests, and Disadvantage on Intelligence and Charisma {@dice D20} Tests.
{@b AC.} Your AC while not wearing armor is increased by 1
{@b Speed.} Your walking Speed is increased by 10 feet.
{@b Darkvision.} You gain Darkvision of 60 feet.
{@b Resistance to Acid.} You are Resistant to Acid damage.
{@b Tireless.} You are immune to Exhaustion, Stunned, Magical Contagions, and the Charmed condition.
{@b Bite.} You gain a natural weapon with which you can make Unarmed Strikes. On a hit, it deals {@dice 1d8} Piercing damage.
Those you bite must make a Constitution saving throw (DC is 8 + your Constitution modifier + your Proficiency Bonus). On a failure, the target contracts a Magical Contagion called Savage Fever.
{@b Death Throes.} When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you can make an Opportunity Attack with your bite against a creature within reach before you die.

When you are reduced to 0 hit points, you die, and your body melts into a puddle of goo, leaving only your gear and your skeleton, if any. All creatures within a 5-foot Emanation of you must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 1d6} acid damage. A pool of acid remains on the ground in your former space, and any creature that enters the space takes {@dice 1d6} acid damage. After 1 minute, the puddle dries up into a harmless green powder.

{@b Removing the Story Record.} This story record and the transformations wrought by it are removed by a Greater Restoration or Wish spell. They can also be removed by killing the creature and then bringing it back to life.
{@i Made by Aris, from Dragon 140}

reward.name: Saved Up Enough To Finally Go to Wildspace
entries: If you have 10,000 gp, you can spend that much gp and a Bastion Turn to take a vacation to Wildspace. You receive a Fish Suit as a souvenir and this Story Record is replaced with Bought a Trip to Wildspace.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: SEAF Recruit
entries: You’ve successfully finished a dive with the SEAF. You’ve earned a small modicum of their trust, and contributed to Managed Democracy.
{@i Made by Lily}

reward.name: Spire of Sasserine
entries: A medal awarded to those who perform great services to the city of Sasserine. You have a +5 bonus to Charisma (Persuasion or Investigation) checks made in Sasserine.
{@i Made by Aris, from Dragon 139}

reward.name: Teardrop's Echo
entries: You've formed an empathetic link with the Mistake that lies within the deeps of Belierin. You can innately sense its presence, the accuracy of this sense intensifies the nearer and nearer you are to it- or it to you. Sometimes you feel how it feels, sometimes you can see what it sees- though, not at will and occurring at random. This link overcomes planar barriers, a permanent mark on your soul.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: The Gift of Lolth
entries: Your magic now relies on the Demon Weave, instead of the Weave.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Touched by the Mists
entries: The Dark Powers of Ravenloft raised you from the dead for purposes unknown. The experience has placed a heavy burden on your soul, leaving you tainted by evil. Now cats hiss as you approach and milk sours at your touch. You detect as an Undead to the Detect Evil and Good spell.
{@i From Adventurer's League}

reward.name: Villain
entries: You are a villain.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Vow of Kindness to Beasts
entries: You have promised to never harm a beast.
You have a +1 to Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks. If you ever willingly harm a beast except in self-defense, you instead have a -5 penalty to Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: WSS Membership Card
entries: With this card, you are considered an honorary member of the Westgate Sanitary Society (AKA the Rat Bastards). While this doesn't do much, members of the Rat Bastards will respect you a bit more than usual.
{@i Made by Sam}

reward.name: [LOS] Breaker of Silence
entries: You've felled the third Machine, destroyed the Mass Shadow Engine, and returned both light and water to the streets of Perago.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: [LOS] Colossus Crusher
entries: You carry the renown of stopping the second Machine's rampage. The people of the Northern regions will speak of your great deed for many years.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: [LOS] Midnight Alliance
entries: In a fervent night of poison, lies, and illusion, you claimed victory over the fifth Machine. In doing so, you have not only thwarted the plans of life's enemy, but earned valuable allies in both Talia and Raul.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: [LOS] Oceanic Conqueror
entries: You've ventured into the heart of the tempest and defeated the fourth Machine. The souls of the lost at sea can know peace, and the Westmarch castle is safe from destruction...well, from this particular opponent, at least.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: [LOS] Starmantle's Gratitude
entries: You've extinguished the inferno and felled the first Machine. As such, you have the deepest thanks of Starmantle's people.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: [LOS] Village Champions
entries: You have saved the Myrmalons of Epoch, both from the dragon Wrathgut and the sixth Machine. They sing your praises, and hold you in high regard. You get a 10% discount on purchases made in their village.
{@i Made by Christopher}

reward.name: [SEC] Corp Commander
entries: {@i You have shown yourself to be a top member of the Scholar Exploration Corps. You have been put in charge of your own Corp under its mantle.}
Every bastion turn, you gain an item of your choice from the Guildhall: Dungeoneer bastion turn table. Whenever you are in a Dungeon (GM’s discretion), you have a +1 bonus to all ability checks.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: [SEC] Dungeoneer
entries: You have become an official member of the Scholar Exploration Corps and are able to go on expeditions with them.

Whenever you take the {@b Guildhall: Dungeoneer} bastion turn, you can choose to go on an expedition with SEC. If you do so, roll on the list twice and pick one of the items you rolled to receive (Rerolling duplicates).
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: [SEC] Expedition Leader
entries: {@i You have shown yourself to be a valuable member of the Scholar Exploration Corps. You are entrusted to devise and lead expeditions.}

Whenever you take the Guildhall: Dungeoneer bastion turn, you can choose to lead an expedition with SEC. If you do so, roll on the list twice and gain both of the items you rolled. (Rerolling duplicates). Whenever you are in a dungeon (GM’s discretion), you have a +1 bonus to all ability checks.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: [SMP] Banned for Life
entries: You have committed a dastardly crime against The Scholars of Millenniums Past, and have been banned from their libraries permanently. If you are found out to be banned while trespassing onto their property, hostile action will be taken. While other libraries don't immediately take this as proof of bad book lending, many will be hesitant to let you visit.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: [SMP] Card Holder
entries: You have been recognized as responsible enough to check out books from any Scholars of Millenniums Past' library. As a highly prestigious library organization, many non-associated libraries accept this membership as well.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: [SMP] Scholar
entries: Due to your dedication or assistance to The Scholars of Millenniums Past, you have shown yourself to be trustworthy. You receive preferential treatment over simple Card Holders. Additionally, you are allowed to start your own library under their name, and they will provide aid to do such.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Greenplague
entries: {@i I'm so green}

Any creature that is wounded by a creature with Greenplague must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, or become infected with Greenplague. A creature that is infected suffers the following effects immediately:
{@b Green.} Your body and everything you wear or carry becomes green.

{@b Fighting the Contagion.} When the creature finishes a Long Rest, it must make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw. On a success the contagion ends. Magic such as Heal or Greater Restoration cures a creature of the contagion
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Mad Rat Disease
entries: When large quantities of rodents gather in areas of intense magic, they can start going insane, infecting the area with mad rat disease. Any beast or humanoid that is wounded by a creature that carries the contagion or comes into contact with contaminated filth must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw, or become infected. A creature that is infected suffers the following effects after {@dice 1d8} minutes.

{@b Maddening Failure.} Whenever an infected creature fails a {@dice D20} test, it must roll on the madness table below. If it rolls a 5 or lower, it does not roll on the table again until the start of its next turn.
| `{@dice 1d10}` | `Effect` |
| `1` | `The creature is Stunned until the end of its next turn.` |

| `2` | `The creature becomes Frightened of all creatures (other than itself) until the end of its next turn. On its next turn, it uses its action to make an attack against a random creature within its range. If no creature is within range, it can’t take an action this turn.` |
| `3` | `The creature becomes Dazed until the end of its next turn.` |
| `4` | `The creature falls Prone, and cannot get up until the end of its next turn.` |
| `5` | `The creature is Poisoned until the end of its next turn.` |
| `6-10` | `No Effect` |

{@b Fighting the Contagion.} When the creature finishes a Long Rest, it must make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a success the contagion ends. Magic such as Heal or Lesser Restoration cures a creature of the contagion.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Orc Stoned
entries: I saved the life of an orc, who gave me drugs with the symbol of the Many-Arrows tribe (a humanoid skull pierced by three arrows) carved into them. The effects are a magical contagion that only I can be inflicted by.

{@b Trip Sitter.} As a Magic action, I can summon the spirit of an orc warrior, which appears within 30 feet of me. The spirit uses the Tough Boss stat block in the Monster Manual and disappears after 10 minutes or when reduced to 0 hit points. The spirit understands any language I speak and obeys my commands.
{@b Fighting the Contagion.} After Trip Sitter is used three times, I lose the contagion, as it turns to dust in my lungs.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Savage Fever
entries: Created and spread by the hateful energies of a savage tide, this contagion embodies the Evil and Chaos of the Abyss. A creature suffers the following effects 1 minute after infection:
{@b Fatigue.} The creature gains 1 Exhaustion level.
{@b Bony Protrusions.} The creature's flesh breaks out into a terrible black rash that causes bony protrusions to extrude from the affected area.
{@b Savage Insanity.} While the creature has any Exhaustion levels, it has Disadvantage on all Intelligence {@dice D20} Tests.
{@b Restlessness.} While the creature has any Exhaustion levels, finishing a Long Rest neither restores lost Hit Points nor reduces the creature’s Exhaustion level.

{@b Fighting the Contagion.} Daily at dawn, an infected creature makes a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature gains 1 Exhaustion level as its fatigue worsens. If the creature reaches 6 levels of Exhaustion instead of dying, it collapses into a coma-like state and gains the Savage Creature Story Record. When it wakes up after a Short Rest, it immediately seeks out fresh prey.
{@i Made by Aris, from Dragon 140}

reward.name: Seed of Evil
entries: Within every creature contains the capacity for evil; an idea of it. With the right cultivation, the right exposure, that evil within can grow and germinate- and eventually overpower. This Magical Contagion is unable to be cured by magical means (see Fighting the Contagion). A creature suffers the following effects immediately upon infection:
{@i Thrall to Evil}. The creature has Disadvantage on Wisdom saving throws against effects originating from Demons.

{@i Germination}. The creature's alignment slowly shifts towards Chaotic Evil. Whenever it fails the saving throw against Fighting the Contagion, its alignment moves one step towards Chaotic Evil. A Lawful Good character for instance would become either Neutral Good or Lawful Neutral, at the choice of the creature. This alignment change cannot be restored until the disease is cured.

{@i Fighting the Contagion}. Every 7 days, an infected creature makes a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the creature grows more chaotic and evil (see Germination). If the creature becomes Chaotic Evil, they gain the Demon Spawn story record. If you bathe yourself in the light of three of the Celestial Heavens, make physical contact with a Talisman of Pure Good, or undergo a purging ritual listed in the Book of Exalted Deeds, the contagion is cured.
This contagion affects all creatures that aren’t Demons, ignoring Immunity to Magical Contagions.
{@i Made by Chrys and Aris}

reward.name: Split Shadow
entries: {@i Legend has it that sometimes a shadow will break free from a creature and consume it, taking its place. The name is actually a misnomer, as split shadow causes a creature to be transformed into a shadowy form instead of having its shadow split off from it.}
Creatures are only able to contract this contagion through magic as it does not spread naturally. A creature with Split Shadow suffers the following effects:
{@b Shadow Form.} The creature and everything they wear or carry appears to be made out of shadows. While in Dim Light or Darkness, the creature is Invisible.
{@b Light Sensitivity.} While in sunlight, the creature has Disadvantage on all {@dice D20} Tests. The creature has Vulnerability to Radiant damage.

{@b Fighting the Contagion.} Whenever an infected creature finishes a Long Rest that took place entirely in sunlight, it makes a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a successful save, the contagion ends on a creature.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Total Heart Failure
entries: {@b Death}. You die. Afterwards, you lose this Magical Contagion.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Weave Fray
entries: {@i It's unknown why, but occasionally when a creature returns from a dead magic zone, they don't properly return to the fabric of magic.}

This Magical Contagion is able to affect all creatures, even those typically immune to Magical Contagions, and is unable to be cured through magical means. A creature suffers the following effects immediately upon infection.
{@b Fatigue.} The creature gains 1 Exhaustion level. Every day the creature spends not in an Antimagic field, it gains another level of Exhaustion.

{@b Magic Sickness.} Whenever the creature attempts to cast a spell, has a spell cast on it, or takes a Magic action, it takes {@dice 2d6} Necrotic damage per level of Exhaustion it has. This damage cannot be reduced.
Additionally when it casts a spell, or takes a Magic action it must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, or it fails wasting any resources expended.

{@b Fighting the Contagion.} A creature must spend a day in an Antimagic Field, after which it can exit and attempt to properly connect to the Weave. It must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a success the contagion is cured. On a failure, it continues to suffer the effects and must wait another day in order to attempt the save again.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Anatidaephilia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn within 60 feet of a goose or duck that you can see. On a failed save, you are Charmed by the creature. While Charmed you must do everything in your power to move next to the goose or duck. Once you are next to the creature you can repeat the saving throw, ending the Mania on a success. If you succeed against this save, you are immune to the Mania for 1 hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Ailuromania
entries: じしˍ,)ノ

When you start your turn and can see or hear a cat, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed of the creature. While Charmed of the creature, you must spend your turn moving as close to it as possible in order to pet it. Once you pet the cat, the Charmed condition ends. On a success, you are immune to Ailuromania for 1 hour.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Aquaphobia
entries: When you start your turn while in water, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be Panicked, moving away from the water. You can repeat the saving throw as an action while you are not in water. On a success, you are immune to Aquaphobia for 1 hour.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Cucurbitomania
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn within 60 feet of a pumpkin or gourd. On a failed save, you are Charmed by the object. While Charmed you must do everything in your power to move next to the pumpkin. Once you are next to the object you can repeat the saving throw, ending the Mania on a success. If you succeed against this save, you are immune to the Mania for 1 hour.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Goner
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn within 10 feet of a Holy Symbol. On a failed save, you are Charmed by the object. While Charmed you must do everything in your power to zerk it. Once you zerk it, you can repeat the saving throw. If you succeed on any saving throw against this Mania, you automatically succeed on saves against it for the next hour.
{@i Made by Ellie}

reward.name: Helminthomania
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn within 30 feet of a worm that you can see. On a failed save, you are Charmed by the worm. While Charmed, you must use your movement on your turns to get as close as possible to the worm, moving by the safest route. If you are within 5 feet of the worm, you cannot willingly move away. At the end of each minute, you can reroll the saving throw. If you succeed on any saving throw against this Mania, you automatically succeed on saves against Helminthomania for the next hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Merinthokleptomania
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn within 30 feet of a Small or smaller chain or binding implement that you can see and that isn't being worn or carried. On a failed save, you are Charmed by the object. While Charmed you must do everything to steal the object. Once you have the object you can repeat the saving throw. On a failure, you are unwilling to part with the object. If you succeed on any saving throw against this Mania, you automatically succeed on saves against Kleptomania for the next hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Nyctomaniac
entries: When you enter an area of Darkness, you must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you are unwilling to enter areas of Dim Light and Bright Light; If you are forced into such an area of brightness, you must devote your time snuffing the light out, if that is possible. You may repeat the saving throw at the end of every Short or Long Rest. Once you succeed on any saving throw against this Mania, you automatically succeed on saves against it for the next 24 hours.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Petramania
entries: When you start your turn and can see or hear a creature in need of saving, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed of the creature. While Charmed of the creature, you must do everything in your power to save their life. When you save their life, you must ask for their Stone, in which the Charmed condition ends. On a success, you are immune to Petramania for 1 hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Pyromania
entries: When you start your turn within line of sight or within earshot of a fire, you must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition. While Charmed, you cannot willing put out the fire and you must, on each of your turns, either do everything in your power to spread the fire or take an Action to reroll the saving throw. On a success, you are immune to Pyromania until the end of your next Long Rest.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: So big and full of pickles
entries: Whenever you see a pickle, you must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed of the pickle. While Charmed, you must devour the pickle. You can take an action on each of your turns to repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success and being unaffected by this Mania for the next hour. If you end your turn in a space where you don't have line of sight of the pickle, you are no longer Charmed.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Astraphobia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn in a storm, see a storm from a distance, or hear a storm from a distance. On a failed save, you are Frightened of the storm. As an action, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on a success. If you succeed, you automatically succeed on saves against Astraphobia for the next hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Carnophobia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you see meat being consumed. On a failed save, you are Frightened. As an action, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on a success. If you succeed, you automatically succeed on saves against Carnophobia for the next hour.
{@i Made by Tyler}

reward.name: Chronomentrophobia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn and can see a clock. On a failed save, you are Frightened of the object. As an action, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on a success. If you succeed, you automatically succeed on saves against clocks for the next hour
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Megalophobia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn within 300 feet of a Huge or greater sized creature that you can see or when you see a Colossal or greater sized creature from a distance greater than that. On a failed save, you are Frightened of that creature. As an action, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on a success. If you succeed, you automatically succeed on saves against Megalophobia for the next hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Melophobia
entries: Whenever you hear music playing, you must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be Frightened of the source of the song. You can repeat the saving throw as an Action on each of your turns or at the end of your turns when you can no longer hear the music. On a success, you are immune to this Phobia for the next hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Merinthophobia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn Grappled or Restrained. On a failed save, you are Frightened of the source of the condition. As an action, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on a success. If you succeed, you automatically succeed on saves against Merinthophobia for the next hour.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Panphobia
entries: You must make a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw whenever you start your turn. On a failed save, you are Frightened of everything. As an Action, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the condition on a success. If you succeed, you automatically succeed on saves against Panphobia for the next hour.
{@i Made by Landon}

reward.name: Petraphobia
entries: When you start your turn and can see a Stone, you must succeed on a DC 20 Wisdom saving throw or be Panicked, moving away from the nearest Stone. You can repeat the saving throw as an action when there are no Stones in sight. On a success, you are immune to Petraphobia for 1 hour.
Stone refers to Wondrous magic items that Requires Attunement by a Recipient Only.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Tubariphobia
entries: Whenever you see a pickle, you must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be Panicked of the pickle. If you end your turn in a space where you don't have line of sight of the pickle, you make a Wisdom saving throw. On a success, you are no longer Panicked.
{@i Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Greenzone
entries: Everything in your Lair becomes green until it leaves
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Known Bigfoot Hunter
entries: Bigfoot avoids your lair, refusing to enter it.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Subtle Obstruction
entries: Rocks within 6 miles of your lair sometimes move of their own accord, usually when no one is watching. Often the rocks obstruct the

approach to the lair, with boulders moving to block narrow defiles, way-markers tumbling off the path, or smaller stones shifting beneath travelers' feet to send them tumbling down slopes or into rivers.
{@i From Ancient Emerald Dragon. Made by June}

reward.name: Devolve
entries: {@i Prerequisite: Can cast Evolve}
Beasts who have been made into Humanoid through the Evolve spell can take a Bonus Action while in your lair to switch between their original animal form and their humanoid form.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Fouling Blight
entries: Within 6 miles of the lair, ability checks made to forage for food or water have Disadvantage, and magic that creates food or water only produces half the normal amount.
{@i From The Beast of Blight. Made by Chrys}

reward.name: Luxury Spider Resort
entries: Nearby Spiders are attracted to your lair, a large number appear in your home each day.
{@i Made by June}

reward.name: Pure Waters
entries: Water flows pure within 3 miles of your lair. Any purposeful corruption of the water lasts for no longer than 3 minutes.
{@i From Ki-Rin}

reward.name: Enervating Domain
entries: Whenever a creature other than you or one of your allies finishes a Long Rest within 1 mile of the lair, the creature must succeed on a DC 20 Constitution saving throw or have its Hit Point maximum reduced by {@dice 1d4}. This reduction lasts until the creature finishes a Long Rest outside that area.
{@i From Demilich}

reward.name: Run of Bad Luck
entries: Within 1 mile of the lair, a creature that rolls a 1 on a {@dice D20} Test must subtract {@dice 1d4} from its next {@dice D20} Test.
{@i From The Grinning Sinner}

reward.name: Crackling Thunder
entries: Thunderstorms erupt in a 5-mile radius centered on your lair, creating deafening cracks of thunder and constant lightning. Every ten minutes the storm rages, creatures standing beneath the open sky have a five percent chance of being struck by lightning. A creature struck by lightning must make a DC 21 Constitution saving throw, taking 21 ({@dice 6d6}) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
{@i From Yan-C-Bin}

reward.name: Skysea
entries: You infuse the sky with power from the Plane of Water. The air within 1 mile of your lair can be breathed by creatures that can breathe water. In addition, creatures in the area gain a Fly Speed equal to their Swim Speed, if any.
In addition, aquatic wildlife can thrive in the area, and other strange features may persist in the area, such as bubbles forming that rise upwards, or water-like light refraction.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Time Distortion
entries: While within 1 mile of the lair, creatures age at one-tenth the usual rate.
{@i From Spirit Dragon}

reward.name: Trapped Spirits
entries: After 24 hours, the spirit of anyone who dies within 1 mile of your lair reappears as a Ghost, a Specter, or another incorporeal Undead near where they died (determined by the GM). These spectral dead can be magically returned to life as normal, but those who aren't restored to life linger as Undead until they're destroyed.
{@i Made by Aris}

reward.name: Travel Ward
entries: Creatures can't use teleportation or planar travel to enter or exit the lair.
{@i From Demilich}

spell.name: Approximate
entries: Your magic quickly flows over an area to help you count and catalog. Name a particular type of object you are looking for within the area. You gain an instant estimate of the quantity of the chosen objects that are clearly visible within the target area. The number is rounded to the largest digit. For example, you could look at a pile of 180 copper coins, and you would learn that it held about 200 coins, but you couldn't determine there were exactly 180 coins.

The type of object you name can be as specific or general as you like—“dented copper coins” is as viable as “coins”—but the distinguishing features must be obvious at a glance, and the spell is automatically fooled by objects disguised as other objects. For instance, the spell would register copper coins plated in gold as gold coins, not copper coins.
{@i Adapted from Pathfinder by Landon}

spell.name: Bigby's Slapping Hand
entries: You create a tiny hand of shimmering, translucent force next to a creature that you can see within range and smacks it. Not enough to hurt it, but enough to distract it. If the target creature is concentrating on a spell, it must make a successful Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or lose concentration.
The slap can be heard up to 100 feet away.
{@i Mechanics heavily adjusted from 3.5e for 5e. Adapted by Aris}

spell.name: Caustic Blade
entries: You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects and then becomes coated in acid. At the beginning of your next turn, the spell ends and the target takes {@dice 1d4} Acid damage unless it used an action to remove the acid.
{@i Made by Ellie}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The attack and the acid deal extra Acid damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d8} and {@dice 2d4}), 11 ({@dice 2d8} and {@dice 3d4}), and 17 ({@dice 3d8} and {@dice 4d4}).

spell.name: Frostbitten Blade
entries: You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects and then becomes wreathed in frost until the start of your next turn. If another creature than you hits the target with an attack before then, it takes an additional {@dice 1d6} Cold damage, and the spell ends.
{@i Made by Ellie}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The attack and the frost deal extra Cold damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d8} and {@dice 2d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d8} and {@dice 3d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d8} and {@dice 4d6}).

spell.name: Instant Beer
entries: You create a beer in your hand. It disappears once it leaves your hand or you drink it.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: When you reach level 11, you can create two beers.

spell.name: Overcharged Blade
entries: You brandish the weapon used in the spell's casting and make a melee attack with it against one creature within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects and then becomes haloed with electricity until the start of your next turn. If the target misses an attack roll before then, it takes {@dice 1d6} Lightning damage and the spell ends.
{@i Made by Ellie}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Cantrip Upgrade
entries: The attack and the electricity deal extra Lightning damage when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 1d8} and {@dice 2d6}), 11 ({@dice 2d8} and {@dice 3d6}), and 17 ({@dice 3d8} and {@dice 4d6}).

spell.name: Vile Dagger
entries: A shard of evil appears within your hand. You are Proficient with this dagger, and no other creature is Proficient with it.
All damage dealt by the dagger is vile damage.
(vile damage is just like other damage but that is very difficult to heal)
When you reach 5th level, you gain a +1 to attack and damage rolls made with the dagger. This bonus increases to +2 at 11th level, and +3 at 17th level.
{@i Repassed for season 4, made by Aris}

spell.name: Aberrate
entries: You transform one willing creature into an Aberration. Its flesh twists and warps into thick, mutated hide. The target's creature type changes to Aberration and it gains a +1 bonus to AC while not wearing armor for the duration of the spell.
{@i Repassing from WM3, from 3.5e. Made by Chrys.}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: If you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the AC bonus increases by 1 for each slot level above 1st.

spell.name: Advanced Scurvy
entries: The target must succeed on an Constitution saving throw or contract an advanced form of scurvy, becoming constantly fatigued. While afflicted with scurvy, the creature has Disadvantage on Strength and Dexterity ability checks. Scurvy can be treated magically or can be overcome with proper nutrition; eating the right foods ends the condition after the creature's next Long Rest.
{@i Adapted from PF1e by Jackson}

spell.name: Allergy Field
entries: You target a patch of plant life within range, causing the plants to produce pollen, antigens, or similar allergens.

The plants release these allergens in a 5-foot-radius Sphere. When a creature enters the spell’s area for the first time or starts its turn there, that creature must make a Constitution saving throw. Creatures that don’t need to breathe or have Immune to Poison damage or the Poisoned condition automatically succeed on this saving throw.

On a failed save, the creature takes {@dice 2d4} Poison damage and is Poisoned for 1 minute. A Poisoned creature repeats saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the condition on a success.
Creatures that succeed on the saving throw are immune to the effects of the allergens for the duration of the spell.
The field of allergens lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it.
{@i Adapted from 2e, Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The field's radius increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Animate Crawling Claw
entries: This spell binds the life force of a murderer to its severed hand, haunting and animating it. Choose a severed hand you can see within range. The hand must have been severed from the body of a Humanoid murderer that has been dead no longer than 10 days.
The target hand becomes a Crawling Claw. It is under your control for 24 hours, after which the hand becomes inactive and can't be animated again.

On each of your turns, you can take a Bonus Action to mentally command any Crawling Claw you made with this spell if the creature is within 60 feet of you (if you control multiple crawling claws, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move on its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as guard a chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the claw moves towards the nearest creature that isn't an Undead and takes the Attack action against it. Once given an order, the claw continues to follow it until its task is complete.
Subclasses: Oathbreaker Paladin
{@i Adapted from online and Animate Dead, uncredited, by Aris}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: If cast using a level 6+ spell slot, the crawling claw remains active until slain.

spell.name: Book Sense
entries: When you cast this spell you can name a topic. For the duration, you can see a faint aura on any books, tomes, or other objects that contain writing related to the topic within 60 feet of yourself. If it is written in a language you do not understand or is concealed by magic, you are unable to sense it.

Additionally you can take the Magic action to touch a book, tome, or other object containing writing. If it is in a language you understand and not concealed by magic, you learn a brief summary of what the writing is about.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Complex Idea
entries: You attempt to send a complex thought into the mind of a creature. The target makes an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@dice 3d6} Psychic damage and has Disadvantage on Intelligence checks until the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Cryptmaster's Call
entries: Convening with The Cryptmaster, played by the GM, the caster may write a single word, and The Cryptmaster creates a magical effect based on the word, or rejects the request completely.
Whatever word the caster writes must be able to replicate the effects of a cantrip or 1st-level spell, or have an equivalently powerful impact.
Examples of words to use include:
CRABS - Summon two CR 0 Crab monsters as an ally (Duration 10 mins, Concentration).
BLOCK - Adds a +2 Magic Bonus to your Armor Class until the start of your next turn.
ZAP - Make a ranged spell attack against a target within 60 ft. The target takes {@dice 2d8} Lightning damage.
TRIP - Force a target within 30 ft. to make a Strength saving throw. On a fail, the target becomes Prone.
LOOK - The Cryptmaster describes an object or location around you that you are unable to perceive via sight.

If you cast the spell more than once before finishing a Long Rest or make a request that displeases the Cryptmaster, there is a cumulative 25 percent chance for each casting after the first that you become unable to cast this spell again until the next dawn (The GM might also deny further castings at their will).
{@i Inspired by Cryptmaster. Made by Patrick}

spell.name: Detect Bees
entries: For the duration, you can detect the presence, but not exact location, of all honey producing insects within 10 feet.
The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
Subclasses: Cleric (Nature)
{@i Made by Tyler}

spell.name: Detect Pronouns
entries: For the duration, you know the preferred pronouns of all creatures and objects within 30 feet of you.
The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
{@i Repass from S3, by Chrys}

spell.name: Exposition
