entries: You touch a willing creature and link your minds to mentally impart to that target an amount of information in an instant that could otherwise be communicated in 10 minutes. The spell fails if you do not share a language with the target creature.
Subclasses: Sorcerer (Aberrant), Warlock (Great Old One)
{@i Made by V, Inspired by Mindlink from PF2e}

spell.name: Insect
entries: You summon an insect. A bug even. It manifests in an unoccupied space you can see within range and uses the Spider stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, the creature shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Kill Bird
entries: You will a Beast with a Flying Speed within range to die instantly. If the creature you chose has 10 hit points or fewer, it dies. Otherwise the spell has no effect.
{@i Inspired by Landon, Made by Tyler}

spell.name: Meat Bubble
entries: You create a spectral globe of raw meat around your head. The globe is filled with raw meat that lasts until the spell ends. If you have more than one head, the globe of meat appears around only one of your heads.
Subclasses: Cleric (Nature)
{@i Inspired by Air Bubble, Made by Denny}

spell.name: Ping
entries: You learn your current location and the plane of existence you are on. You can choose to inform a creature within 1 mile of you that you are nearby, however they don't know your location.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can inform the creature of your exact location when you use a spell of level 3+. The distance you can inform a creature increases with a spell slot of level 3-4 (up to 10 miles), 5-6 (same plane of existence), or 7+ (any creature across any plane).

spell.name: Placebo
entries: A creature you touch gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

This spell only affects creatures that are missing Hit Points, and the number of Temporary Hit Points cannot exceed the number of Hit Points the creature is missing. The creature loses these Temporary Hit Points when it regains any of its Hit Points.
The creature believes that it regained Hit Points via the Cure Wounds spell, instead of gaining Temporary Hit Points.

The target can take a Search action to examine their own status with a Wisdom (Medicine) check against your spell save DC. If the check succeeds, the target realizes that the healing was fake, and it loses its Temporary Hit Points.
{@i Made by Aris}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Temporary Hit Points increase by {@dice 2d8} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Pointed Question
entries: You ask a creature within range a question in a language you understand. It must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it must answer the question though it does not have to answer truthfully. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks made to determine if the answer is a lie.

A creature automatically passes the save if it is Immune to Charmed, doesn't understand the language, or does not speak any language. If a creature passes the saving throw, it is Immune to your casting of this spell for 10 minutes.
{@i Inspired by Pointed Question from pf2e. Made by June.}

spell.name: Preserve Work

components.m: A piece of fresh parchment, a bottle of ink, a lead weight, and a block of clear wax

entries: When you cast this spell, you touch a nonmagical book, tome, artwork, or other physical work. The work becomes protected from damage and wear. The work is unable to be damaged by nonmagical means. Whenever the work is damaged by magical means, it reforms the damaged section to its exact form when the spell was cast over the course of 10 minutes.
If the work is fully destroyed before it has time to reform, the spell ends and the work is destroyed.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Puppet
entries: Your gesture forces one Humanoid you can see within range to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target must move up to its Speed in a direction you choose. In addition, you can cause the target to drop anything it is holding. This spell has no effect on Humanoids that have Immunity to the Charmed condition.
{@i From 5e UA, Repassed from WM3. Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Quincy’s Quick Riser
entries: You touch a Prone creature. If this creature is capable of standing, it may do so, using its Reaction and expending no movement.
{@i Made by Tyler}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Random Action
entries: You attempt to taint a creature you can see within range with delusion. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the target is Confused until the end of its next turn. A target with an Intelligence score less than 4 automatically succeeds on the save.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Adapted from 3e, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Rotting Ward
entries: You shroud yourself in deathly energy to protect yourself from enemies. If a creature hits you with a melee attack, it must make a Constitution save. On a fail, it takes {@dice 1d6} Necrotic damage.
{@i Made by Lily}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, attackers take {@dice 1d6} more damage for every two slot levels above 1st.

spell.name: Scholar's Touch

components.m: a scrap of parchment, a pinch of tinder, and a thin crystal disk worth at least 50 gp

entries: You touch a book or scroll and absorb the knowledge contained within as if you had just read it. This is equivalent to a solid reading but not deep study—you do not gain perfect recall of the information, only whatever you would have gotten from reading it completely once.
If you can't read the language of the source, the spell has no effect. This spell cannot be used to prepare spells or to cast magical scrolls, nor does it have any effect when reading a magical book
{@i From 3.5e, Ported by Aris}

spell.name: Shred Arcane
entries: You shred a page of your spellbook, spraying it in a 30-foot cone. When you cast this spell, pick a spell you have prepared from your spellbook of 1st-level. The spell is immediately unprepared and removed from your spellbook. If the chosen spell cannot be unprepared or removed, the spell fails. If you don't have any spells you can pick, this spell fails without expending the slot.
Each creature of your choice in the cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, it takes {@dice 4d6} Slashing damage. On a success, it takes half as much damage.

If for any reason this spell fails to take effect, you do not unprepare nor remove the chosen spell from your spellbook. If a creature attempts to cast Counterspell in reaction to the casting of this spell, you automatically succeed on the saving throw against it.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Your chosen spell must be of the same level of the slot used. The damage increases by {@dice 3d6} for each spell slot level above 1.

spell.name: Shush
entries: One creature that you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or it has the Silenced condition.

A Silenced target makes another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a success the spell ends. If the creature fails against this spell three times, it stops making saving throws to break free, being Silenced until the spell ends normally.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 3+ (1 hour) or 5+ (8 hours), or 7+ (24 hours).

spell.name: Stone Bubble
entries: I create a spectral globe of stone around my head. The globe is filled with stone that lasts until the spell ends. If I have more than one head, the globe of stone appears around only one of my heads.
{@i Inspired by Air Bubble, Made by Denny}

spell.name: 52 Pickup
entries: You spray a 15-foot cone of spectral cards. Each creature in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Prone condition until the spell ends. A Prone creature can take an action to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check against your spell save DC. On a success, it frees itself from the task of picking up cards an can get up from Prone.
At the end of each of its turns and each time it takes damage, it makes another Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the spell ends on that creature.
{@i Made by Marvin}

spell.name: Appraise
entries: You touch an object. If the object is a magic item or some other magical object, you learn its properties and how to use them, whether it requires Attunement, and how many charges it has, if any. You learn whether any ongoing spells are affecting the item and what they are. If the item was created by a spell, you learn that spell's name.

If you instead touch a creature, it can make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, you reveal which ongoing spells or magical effects, if any, are currently affecting it, as well as one of the following characteristics of your choice:

items: Strength Score
Dexterity Score
Constitution Score
Intelligence Score
Wisdom Score
Charisma Score
Current Armor Class
Current hit points
Total class levels, if any
One of its Traits
What spells it can cast

entries: This information appears as a screen in front of you in a language that you know, following you for the duration of the spell unless you dismiss it, ending the spell (no action required).
{@i Classes: Bard, Cleric, Druid, Paladin, Ranger, Warlock, Sorcerer, Wizard}
{@i Using a Higher-Level Slot}. If you cast this spell using a level 3 spell slot or higher, you can learn an additional characteristic for each level above 2nd.
{@i Made by Landon}

spell.name: Chain of Conviction
entries: You hurl a spectral, barbed chain at a creature that you can see within range. The target makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@dice 2d6} Force damage, is pulled up to 30 feet toward you, and is bound by the chain. While bound by the chain, the creature is tethered to you and can't move or teleport to a space more than 30 feet away from you, and you have Advantage on attack rolls made against the creature.

On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. The bound target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success. If you are ever more than 30 feet away from the target, the spell ends.
{@i From The Crooked Moon}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Consecrate

components.m: a vial of holy water and powdered silver worth at least 25gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You choose a point within range and bless an area around it with positive energy. The area can have an Emanation up to 20 feet, and the spell fails if the Emanation includes an area already under the effect of a Consecrate spell or the Hallow spell. The affected area is subject to the following effects.
The ground within the area of this spell is considered consecrated ground. Undead standing on consecrated ground have Disadvantage on all saving throws.
In addition, undead cannot be raised or created within, or summoned into the area.
A vial of unholy water defiles a 10-foot-square area of consecrated ground when sprinkled on it, and a Hallow (unholy) spell defiles consecrated ground within its area.

If the area contains an altar, shrine, or other permanent fixture of a deity, pantheon, or higher power other than your patron or deity, the consecrate spell instead curses the area, cutting off its connection with the associated deity or power. This secondary function, if used, does not grant the penalties to undead listed above.
If you cast consecrate on an area that includes desecrated ground from a Desecrate spell, the Desecrate spell is dispelled.
{@i Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3, Adapted from 3.5e}

spell.name: Damn
entries: This spell snatches the soul of a creature (if it had one) as it dies and sends it to the Lower Planes, dooming it. The target can be revived only by a True Resurrection or a Wish spell, or by rescuing its soul from the Lower Planes.
Class List: Cleric, Paladin, Wizard, Warlock
{@i Made by Landon}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: If you use a Level 5 or higher spell slot, you can cause casting to affect each creature of your choice that dies during this turn.

spell.name: Desecrate

components.m: a vial of unholy water and powdered silver worth at least 25gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You choose a point within range and infuse an area around it with negative energy. The area can have an Emanation up to 20 feet, and the spell fails if the Emanation includes an area already under the effect of a Desecrate spell or the Hallow spell. The affected area is subject to the following effects.
The ground within the area of this spell is considered desecrated ground. Undead standing on desecrated ground have Advantage on all saving throws.
In addition, an undead creature raised or created within, or summoned into the area gains a number of Temporary Hit Points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.
A vial of holy water purifies a 10-foot-square area of desecrated ground when sprinkled on it, and a Hallow (holy) spell purifies desecrated ground within its area.

If the area contains an altar, shrine, or other permanent fixture of a deity, pantheon, or higher power other than your patron or deity, the desecrate spell instead curses the area, cutting off its connection with the associated deity or power. This secondary function, if used, does not grant the benefits to undead listed above.
If you cast Desecrate on an area that includes consecrated ground from a Consecrate spell, the Consecrate spell is dispelled.
{@i Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3, Adapted from 3.5e}

spell.name: Drawmij’s Scent Mask
entries: The touched creature emanates no scent for the duration of the spell. The creature cannot be tracked by scent and cannot make use of scent-based attacks. Any creature choosing to resist this effect may make a Constitution saving throw. On a success, it is unaffected by the spell.
{@i Made by Tyler, Adapted from 2e}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The number of targets is increased by 1 and the duration is increased by 10 minutes for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Entice Gift
entries: By asking nicely, you can compel a non hostile creature to give you what it's holding. The creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it must spend its next turn moving as close to you as it can and hand (or toss if it's too far to hand) it to you.
Creatures immune to the Charmed condition automatically succeed the saving throw.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Eyes of the Avoral
entries: For the duration, a willing creature you touch has Advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight. In conditions of clear visibility during the day, the affected creature can make out details of even extremely distant creatures and objects as small as 5 feet across, if said creatures and objects are in Bright Light.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Adapted by Chrys}

spell.name: Flash
entries: You conjure a node of incredibly bright light. Until the end of your next turn, you shed Bright Light in a 30-foot radius, and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet. All creatures of your choice in the Emanation must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they take {@dice 3d6} Radiant damage and are Blinded until the end of your next turn.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The radius of the Emanation, Bright Light, and Dim Light increase by 15-feet for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Mesmerizing Glare
entries: You direct your gaze at a point within range. Each creature that can see you within a 5-foot Emanation centered on that point must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Incapacitated until the start of its next turn.
{@i Made by Chrys, Adapted from 3.5e Spell Compendium}

spell.name: Psychometry
entries: You sense psychic impressions left by events in an area around you. The types of events most likely to leave psychic impressions are those that elicited strong emotions: battles, betrayals, marriages, murders, births, great pain, or any other event where one emotion dominates. Everyday occurrences leave little residue for you to detect.
The vision of the event is dreamlike and shadowy. Your vision is vague but you might be able to read large banners or other writing if they are in your language.

You can spend a minute studying, and make a DC 20 Intelligence (History or Investigation) or Wisdom (Insight or Perception) check (your choice). On a successful check, you can choose to learn one of the following pieces of information about the event:
{@i  The number of creatures to pass through the area.
{@i  The type of creatures to pass through the area.
{@i  The length of time the creatures were in the area.
{@i  The general alignment of the creatures that were in the area.
{@i  What direction the creatures came from to get to the area.
{@i  What the creatures did while they were in the area.
{@i  What direction the creatures went when they left the area.
{@i  The dominant emotion experienced by the creatures.

You sense the most recent of such an event. After concentrating on an event for at least 10 minutes, you can shift to sensing the next most recent distinct event, if any such events exist to be sensed.
Creatures under the Mind Blank spell currently or while they were in the area in the past, are not detected by Psychometry.
{@i By Aris, repassed from S3.}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can become more attuned to this sense. You can choose to sense either psychic impressions of powerful emotions, or psychic impressions of the most recent creatures to pass through the area.

spell.name: Radiant Lattice
entries: You unleash a blinding mote at a creature you can see. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@dice 2d8} Radiant damage, is Blinded until the end of its next turn, and Restrained for the duration as the light unfurls into a golden net.
A Restrained creature can use its action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC, ending the condition on a success.
{@i Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Snow Cloud
entries: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere cloud of snow centered on a point within range. The Sphere is Heavily Obscured. It lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as one created by Gust of Wind) disperses it.
Each creature that starts its turn in the Sphere must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@dice 2d4} Cold damage and have its Speed reduced by 15 feet until the start of its next turn.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The fog's radius increases by 10 feet for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Spider Legs
entries: You grow four long spider legs from the sides of your torso. You can use these legs to move at a Speed of 30 feet if your Speed is less than 30 feet. These legs are subject to the same restrictions as the rest of you, and can't move you if an effect reduces your Speed to 0.
In addition, you gain the ability to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You also gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed.
From 3.5e

spell.name: Sticks to Snakes
entries: You transform a nonmagical stick, spear, or staff into a Venomous Snake, Giant Venomous Snake, Flying Snake, Constrictor Snake.

If the target item is being held by another creature and the creature is unwilling for their item to be transformed, that creature can make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the item is transformed.

The snake is Friendly to you and your companions, and acts at the end of your turn. It understands and obeys any verbal commands that you issue to it (no action required by you). If you don't issue any commands to it, then it defends itself from hostile creatures, but otherwise takes no actions.
If you cast this spell on a stick, spear, or staff each day for 30 days, it permanently becomes a snake, though it no longer has to be Friendly or obey your verbal commands.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can choose up to 2 more sticks, spears, or staves for each spell slot level above 2.

spell.name: Summon Elysian Thrush
entries: You summon an Elysian Thrush within range. The Elysian Thrush is friendly to you and your allies. It obeys your commands, though it refuses to fight in combat, instead fleeing from perceived danger.

The Elysian Thrush uses the raven statblock but is a Celestial, and their song is magically enchanting and beautiful. Any creature that finishes a Short Rest while within 30 feet of a singing Elysian Thrush rolls the maximum number on any hit dice it rolls to regain Hit Points.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Aris}

spell.name: Summon Plant

components.m: A pot of dirt worth 100+ gp

entries: You call forth a Plant spirit. It manifests in an unoccupied space that you can see within range and uses the Plant Spirit stat block (see below). When you cast the spell, choose Fungus (Small), Tree (Large), or Vine (Medium). The creature resembles a Plant of that kind, which determines certain details in its stat block. The creature disappears when it drops to 0 Hit Points or when the spell ends.

The creature is an ally to you and your allies. In combat, it shares your Initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It obeys your verbal commands (no action required by you). If you don't issue any, it takes the Dodge action and uses its movement to avoid danger.
![][image6]
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: Use the spell slot's level for the spell's level in the stat block.

spell.name: Tunnel Vision
entries: You attempt to constrain the senses of one creature within range. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, it cannot benefit from any special senses for the duration of the spell.
{@i Made by Lily}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: If you cast this spell with a slot of 4th level or higher, the duration becomes Concentration, up to 10 minutes. If you use a slot of 6th level or higher, the duration becomes Concentration, up to 1 hour.

spell.name: Vision of Entropy
entries: You form a vision of the seething, maddening chaos of the Abyss within a creature's mind. Choose a creature within range. At the start of each of the creature's turns, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature becomes frightened for the duration. If it fails a second time, it is no longer frightened, and becomes confused instead. If it fails its saving throw for a third time, it is no longer confused but gains a randomly determined short term madness for the duration.
Casting lesser restoration, dispel magic, or any spell that removes madness from a target creature ends the effects of the spell.

Demons and creatures under the effects of the calm emotions or protection from evil and good spells automatically succeed on all saving throws against the spell, as do creatures immune to frightened, confused, or madness.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, repassed from WM3. Made by Aris}

spell.name: Yoke of Mercy
entries: You compel a creature up to a CR of 5 (or level 5 if it doesn’t have a Challenge Rating) to fight in a merciful manner. The target creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be unable to cast damaging spells; when it reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points with a melee attack, the creature is instead knocked out. Furthermore, the affected target creature cannot perform a coup de grace on an Unconscious creature. The affected target creature can reroll the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
You must not have dealt damage, by spell or any other means, to another creature within the last 8 hours before casting this spell.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: For each level above 2 that this spell is cast, the upper limit of the target creature’s CR (or level) is increased by 2.

spell.name: Arcane Sight
entries: For the duration, you sense the presence of magic within 120 feet of you, and you can see an aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears magic. You can determine the school of magic, if any. As a Magic action you can determine if a creature within range that you can see can cast spells, and if it can, you determine the level of the highest level spell it can cast.
The spell can penetrate most barriers, but it is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt.
{@i From 3.5e and S3}

spell.name: Awaken Sin
entries: You show a creature the error of its ways. Choose a Humanoid, Fey, Giant, or Monstrosity within range. If it is Evil, it must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a Success, it is momentarily distracted by the weight of its sins and has a -1 penalty to {@dice D20} tests until the end of its next turn. On a Failure, it is instead Stunned until the end of its next turn.
{@i Adapted from Dragon Magazine 315. Made by Landon}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 2nd. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

spell.name: Bloodspear

components.m: a glaive, halberd, pike, or spear

entries: You channel the fury of Gruumsh into the god’s favored weapon—a spear. The weapon used in the spell's casting’s tip begins to drip blood, and the spear acts as if it had the properties of a Sword of Wounding for the duration of the spell, except that the extra Necrotic damage on a hit is reduced to {@dice 1d6}. The spell is automatically canceled after the weapon leaves your hand.
Casting this spell cancels all other Bloodspear spells that you have cast.
Subclasses: Paladin (Conquest)
{@i Adapted from 3.5e Champions of Ruin, Made by Denny}

spell.name: Elation
entries: An aura radiates from you in a 30-foot Emanation for the duration. While in the aura, you and your allies become elated, full of energy and joy, gaining a +1 bonus to Strength and Dexterity based {@dice D20} tests, has their Speed increased by 5 feet, and is treated as if having one less Exhaustion level for the purpose reducing penalties from {@dice D20} tests and Speed by having cumulative levels of Exhaustion.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Ethereal Shunt

components.m: an object from the Material Plane

entries: When you cast this spell, you momentarily gain the ability to see onto the Ethereal Plane allowing you to target creatures on it with this spell.

You force a creature in range to make a Charisma saving throw, or be forced out of the Ethereal Plane back to the plane they were previously on. The creature is unable to return to the Ethereal Plane until the end of your next turn.
{@b Using a Higher Level Spell Slot.} The duration of the spell increases with a spell slot of level 5-6 (1 Minute), 7-8 (1 Hour), or 9+ (24 Hours)
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Investiture of the Kyton
entries: With a rush of red light and the faint clanking of many chains, you invest the target creature with the power of a chain devil. The target creature gains Resistance to Fire damage and once on it's turns, can choose the following option when taking the Attack action (The DC equal to your spell save DC):

{@b Conjure Infernal Chain}. You target a creature within 60 feet of you as you summon a fiery chain to bind it. That creature must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@dice 2d4} + your Strength modifier Fire damage and is Restrained until the end of your next turn. If the target is Large or smaller, you move the target up to 30 feet straight toward yourself to an unoccupied space.
{@i Based off of Investiture of the Chain Devil (3.5e), Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Lesser Permanency

components.m: a rune-carved emerald worth at least 500gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You touch a spell effect that can become permanent if cast repeatedly (such as Private Sanctum, Galder’s Tower, or Teleportation Circle), and the time remaining before it becomes permanent decreases by one week. You must provide any material components that would be required to cast the spell for a week.
{@i Made by Lily}

spell.name: Magic Vestment
entries: You touch a nonmagical armor or shield. Until the spell ends, that armor or shield becomes a magic armor or shield with a +1 bonus to AC. The spell ends early if you cast it again.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The bonus increases to +2 with a level 4-6 spell slot. The bonus increases to +3 with a level 7+ spell slot.

spell.name: Masochism
entries: For every 20 points of damage you take, you gain a +1 bonus to all {@dice D20} Tests you make before the end of your next turn, when the bonus resets. The more damage the caster takes, the greater the bonus, to a maximum of +5. If the bonus would become a +6 or greater from taking damage, your senses become overwhelmed and you become Dazed until the end of your next turn.
{@i Adapted from WM3 and 3.5e Book of Vile Darkness, Made by Aris}

spell.name: Pox
entries: You shout "A Pox on you!", and target a Humanoid, Giant, or Beast of your choice within range. The target creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become infected with a Magical Contagion. When you cast this spell, choose the contagion from one of the following options:
{@i  Cackle Fever
{@i  Blue Mist Fever
{@i  Sewer Plague
{@i  Shivering Sickness
{@i  Mudpox
{@i  Lichen Plague

Alternatively, you can disease a body of water. You make exposed water inside an area you choose that is a Cube up to 100 feet on a side diseased. The water becomes diseased with Sight Rot or Throat Leeches (your choice). The entire area of water can be purified with a single casting of Purify Food and Drink cast using a 5th level slot or higher.
At the DM's option, you may choose an alternative Magical Contagion effect, but it should be no more powerful than those described above. The DM has final say on such a contagion's effect.
{@i Repassed from WM3, Adapted from 3.5e by Aris}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 3rd. The creatures must be within 10 feet of each other when you target them.

spell.name: Putrid Aura
entries: You emit a putrid scent, filling a 10-foot Emanation originating from you for the duration. Whenever a creature enters the Emanation or starts its turn there, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature becomes Poisoned until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned in this way, it can't willingly move closer to you.
If a creature Poisoned by this spell ends its turn in the Emanation, it takes {@dice 6d6} Poison damage.
A creature makes this save only once per turn. When you cast this spell, you can designate creatures to be unaffected by it.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Emanation increases by 10-feet for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Rainbow Bridge
entries: Choose two points within range. You create a bridge in a 15-feet wide Line made of rainbows between the two points which creatures can walk on. The bridge can arch up to half of its length up or down, but is unable to curve otherwise. The spaces where it appears must be unoccupied. The bridge hovers in the air, is immovable, and is made of magical force. The bridge emits Dim Light within 30 feet of itself.
Creatures are able to choose to move through the bridge, preventing it from being used as a blockade. The bridge is unable to provide Cover.

{@b Using a Higher Level Spell Slot.} When cast using a level 5 spell slot or higher, you can choose for only creatures you designate when you cast the spell to be able to walk on it, with all other creatures passing through it as if it wasn't there. When cast using a level 7 spell slot or higher, the duration of the spell becomes Until Dispelled if you maintain Concentration on it for the entire 10 minutes.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Soul Charge

components.m: A piece of crystal worth at least 100 gp

entries: You steal a portion of a soul of a Humanoid that you touch and use it to activate a wand or other spell-trigger item. The spell bestows 1 level of Exhaustion to the target and transfers the soul's energy to a crystal you carry. A crystal can only have one charge at a time. While the crystal holds a charge, when you cast a Level 3 or lower spell using an item and expend 1 or more charges of the item to do so, you can expend the crystal’s charge to reduce the charge cost of casting the spell by 1, to a minimum of 0 charges.
Once you cast this spell, you cannot cast it again for the next 6 hours.
Deity: Vhaeraunan
{@i Made by Aris}

spell.name: Spikeskin
entries: Until the spell ends, one willing creature touch grows sharp quills to ward off attackers. Whenever a creature hits the warded creature with a melee attack, it takes {@dice 2d8} Piecing damage.
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for every two levels above 3.

spell.name: Torrent of Tears
entries: You force a creature within range to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you force the target creature to cry by drawing forth the water of its tears, making them Blinded and Poisoned for the duration of the spell. The affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
Subclasses: Cleric (Grave)
{@i From 3.5e Complete Mage, Adapted by Chrys}

spell.name: Unliving Weapon

components.m: a drop of bile and a bit of sulfur

entries: You touch an Undead creature and attempt to turn it into a bomb. The creature must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the creature becomes a bomb.

The bomb explodes when the creature takes damage. When you cast the spell you can also choose a time in which the bomb will explode. When the Undead explodes, all creatures in a 10-foot Emanation (including the Undead) take {@dice 3d6} Fire damage and {@dice 3d6} Necrotic or Radiant damage (chosen when you cast the spell).
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The Fire and Necrotic/Radiant damage increase by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.

spell.name: Wizard Blade

components.m: sharpening stone and a wooden handle

entries: You magically transform into a large sword of force. While in this form, you are unable to cast spells, have a Speed of 0, a Fly Speed of 30ft, and Immunity to the Prone condition, but otherwise your stats remain unchanged. As a Magic action on your turn, you are able to make a spell attack with yourself against a creature within 5ft of you. On a hit the target takes Slashing or Force damage (chosen when you cast the spell) equal to {@dice 3d6} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.

Additionally, another creature can carry and wield you as a martial melee weapon. The sword has a bonus to attack and damage rolls equal to half your spellcasting ability modifier (rounded down). On a hit, it deals {@dice 3d6} Slashing or Force damage (chosen when you cast the spell).
{@i Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for every two levels above 3.

spell.name: Celestial Brilliance
entries: You touch an object sheds light brighter than bright sunlight, channeled directly from the celestial realms. The object emits Bright Light in a 60-foot Emanation and Dim Light an additional 60 feet. This light is considered sunlight.
An Undead that starts its turn within the Bright Light takes {@dice 1d6} Radiant damage. A Fiend that starts its turn within the Bright Light takes {@dice 2d6} Radiant damage.
If any of this spell's area overlaps with an area of Darkness created by a spell of level 4 or lower, that other spell is dispelled.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Dream Walk
entries: Yourself, a willing creature you touch, or up to eight willing creatures who link hands in a circle, are transported to a dreamscape of an Asleep target that is dreaming within 30 feet. If there is no such creature, the spell fails and the slot is wasted. You have no control over the dream. When the dreaming creature wakes up, the transported creatures reappear in the nearest unoccupied spaces to the waking creature.
Subclasses: Druid (Dreams)
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, Ported by Aris}

spell.name: Illusory Wall
entries: You create the illusion of a wall, floor, ceiling, or similar surface. You can choose the dimensions and shape of the illusion, but it must fit within a 5-foot Cube. The image appears at a spot that you can see within range and lasts until it is dispelled.

Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, for things can pass through it. A creature that takes a Study action to examine the image can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If it succeeds, it becomes aware that the wall is illusory.
{@i From 3.5e, Ported by Aris}

spell.name: Influenza
entries: With a mighty sneeze that can be heard up to 300 feet away, you blast forth a 30-foot Cone of phlegm, bad breath, and viral pathogens. Each creature in the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 4d6} Poison damage and is Poisoned for the duration. On a successful, the creature takes half as much damage only. A Poisoned creature can make a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

While Poisoned in this way, a creature must roll {@dice 1d12} at the start of each of its turns. On a 3 or lower, it is racked by a coughing fit and becomes Incapacitated until the start of its next turn. Each creature within 5 feet of a creature when it becomes Incapacitated in this way must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or also become Poisoned in this way for the spell's duration.
{@i From Heliana's Guide to Monster Hunting, Ported by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of level 5 or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} and the value a Poisoned creature must roll on the {@dice d12} to cause a coughing fit increases by 1 for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Missile Storm
entries: You brandish a piece of nonmagical ammunition with duplicative magic. The first time you use this ammunition for an attack roll using a ranged weapon, you can instead make that attack roll against up to 12 creatures within a Cone a number of feet equal to the normal range of the weapon. You use the same attack roll for each target.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, Made by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When casted using a level 5 spell slot, the number of creatures you can target increases to 15.

spell.name: Proliferate
entries: You worsen a creature within range's afflictions. The target must make a Constitution saving throw, being affected depending on its affliction:

items: If the target is Poisoned, it takes {@dice 10d6} Poison damage and has Disadvantage on its next saving throw against the Poisoned condition on a failure, or half damage only on a success.
If the target is Burning, it takes {@dice 10d6} Fire damage on a failure, or half as much on a success

If the target is Confused, on a failure whenever it rolls Confusion behavior at the start of its turn, it rolls {@dice 2d10} instead of {@dice 1d10} and takes the lower roll to determine its behavior. This lasts until the Confusion ends.
If the target is Dazed, on a failure it becomes Slowed while it is Dazed.
If the target has a level of Exhaustion, it gains 1 Exhaustion level on a failure.
If the target is Frightened, on a failure it becomes Panicked while it is Frightened.
If the target is Slowed, on a failure it becomes Immobilized while it is Slowed.
If the target is Stunned, on a failure it becomes Paralyzed while it is Stunned.

entries: If a target is afflicted by multiple options, choose one to proliferate.
{@b Using a Higher Level Spell Slot.} You can target an additional creature in range for each spell slot level above 4.
{@i Inspired by Mewgenics. Made by June}

spell.name: Sensory Deprivation

components.m: black silk cloth tied like a blindfold

entries: Rather than creating an illusion of something, this spell creates an illusion of absolute nothingness. A creature of your choice must make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the target only perceives a clinging darkness, and is Blinded and Deafened for the duration. The creature cannot benefit from Blindsight, Tremorsense, or scent.
An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, Repassed from WM3, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Radiant Fog
entries: You create a 20-foot-radius Sphere of thick, shimmering fog centered on a point within range. The Sphere remains for the duration, and its area is Lightly Obscured and Difficult Terrain.

When the fog appears, each creature in it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be Blinded for the duration. A creature also makes this save when it enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
You must not have cast Darkness or a Necromancy spell within the past 24 hours before casting this spell.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Ask Anoth'a'deyem

components.m: a snack worth 2 sp, which is consumed

entries: You ask a different GM than the one running the game for advice. Note, they have no obligation to be truthful, or even answer at all. If they don't respond within the duration of the spell, nothing happens.
If you cast the spell two or more times before finishing your next long rest, there is a chance that your GM gets even more annoyed. Rocks might fall on you, killing you instantly.
{@i Made by Landon, Repassed from WM3}

spell.name: Control Construct

components.m: A small gear, and a small wrench

entries: You attempt to command up to 3 Constructs (CR 7 or less) within range that can hear you. Each creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Charmed by you for the duration. On your turn you can issue a simple verbal command (no action required) to each of the charmed Constructs, and on their turn they will follow it as closely as possible. If the creature completes the command and doesn’t receive further direction from you, it defends and preserves itself to the best of its ability.

Each time a target takes damage, it repeats the Wisdom saving throw, ending the effect on a success. If the creatures end their turn more than 60 ft from you, they additionally repeat the Wisdom saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
{@i Based off of Control Undead which was made by June. Adapted by Lily.}

spell.name: Cyclonic Blast
entries: You send a twisting torrent of wind, not unlike a tornado turned on its side, in a 120-foot-long, 5-foot-wide Line out from you in a direction you choose. Each creature in the Line must succeed on a Strength saving throw, taking {@dice 10d6} Thunder damage and falling Prone on a failed save, or half damage only on a success.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, Made by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Flowsight
entries: You touch a body of water, allowing you to sense creatures and objects in contact with it. You become aware of the presence of any creature in contact with the water within 1 mile of where you touch. You learn their positions, size, and direction they are moving. If you are familiar with one of the creatures, you can recognize it in the water.

As a Magic action you can peer into the water and attempt to scry a creature you are aware of via this sense. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. If a creature knows you are casting this spell, they can choose to fail.
On a successful save the target isn't affected and you can't target them again for 24 hours, and the spell ends.

On a failure, you can see through the water near them as if you were there. Your vision moves with the target, remaining within 10 feet of it for the duration. A creature that can see invisible objects can see your vision as a pair of eyes in the water watching them. If the targeted creature leaves the water, the spell ends.
{@b Using a Higher Level Spell Slot:} The distance you can sense increases by 2 miles for each slot level above 5.
{@b Spell Lists:} Druid, Ranger, Sorcerer, Wizard, Visionary Seeker
{@i Adapted from 3.5e by June}

spell.name: Forbidden Speech
entries: You make it impossible for a single creature to speak about a single topic. The topic should be as narrow as possible for the spell to work properly. An appropriate topic might be events that occurred while the creature was in captivity, the whereabouts and names of tormentors, the secret location of a hidden treasure, the password into a guarded compound, the command word of a magic item, or the explanation behind some major occurrence. Invalid topics include verbal components of spells, a creature's whole life history, or anything too broad for a reasonable definition of a single topic. Whether a topic is too broad is up the DM, who can cause the spell to fail and the slot to be wasted.

The affected creature cannot communicate regarding the topic in any way. Speech becomes gibberish, writing is reduced to an indecipherable scrawl, gestures are impossible, and even telepathy (including Detect Thoughts) is scrambled. The creature has not forgotten the information, however, and it can be a maddening occurrence for the creature to be unable to communicate information that might be of life-and-death importance to friends and allies.
A Remove Curse or Greater Restoration spell cast on the target restores the creature's ability to speak about the topic.
{@i From 3.5e, Adapted by Aris}

spell.name: Morality Undone
entries: You target one creature within range and attempt to turn the target evil. The creature must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, the target becomes evil. The chaotic/neutral/lawful component of the target's alignment is unchanged.

The target retains whatever outlook, allegiances, and outlooks it had before, so long as they do not conflict with the new alignment. An Evil creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against this spell.
{@i Made by Aris}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: If you use a level 6+ spell slot, the duration becomes 8 hours (level 6-7 slot) or 24 hours (level 8 slot). If you use a level 9 spell slot, the spell lasts until dispelled.

spell.name: Pacify
entries: Each creature in a 30-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point you choose within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be affected by the following effects:
{@i  The creature has Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions until the spell ends. If the creature was already Charmed or Frightened, those conditions are suppressed for the duration.
{@i  The creature becomes Indifferent towards all creatures it's Hostile toward.
{@i  The creature is unable to willingly make attack rolls, cast spells, or deal damage.
{@i  The creature can be passed through as if they were your ally.
At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success. The spell ends early if the target takes damage or is unwillingly targeted by a spell.
{@i Vaguely from 2e. Made by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The radius increases by 10 feet for each spell slot level above 5.

spell.name: Mortify

components.m: A piece of flesh from a creature that died in the last day

entries: You attempt to cause a creature that you can see within range to die. The target must make a Death Saving Throw. If the creature is not at 0 Hit Points, the DC for this save is 10, regardless of your normal spell save DC. On a failure, it has the Slowed condition for the duration. Undead and Constructs automatically succeed on the save.

A Slowed creature makes another Death Saving Throw at the end of each of its turns. If it successfully saves against this spell three times, the spell ends. If it fails its saves three times, it dies, requiring Raise Dead or stronger magic to revive it. The successes and failures needn't be consecutive; keep track of both until the target collects three of a kind.

A creature at 0 Hit Points counts any failed Death Saving Throws against dying normally to the number of failures of this spell, and cannot become Stable. A casting of Spare the Dying on the creature counts as one save towards ending the spell.

Casting as a Circle Spell. Casting this as a Circle spell requires a minimum of four secondary casters. The spell's range increases to 300 feet, it no longer requires Concentration, and the spell uses the caster's normal spell save DC even if the creature is not at 0 Hit Points. If any caster who participated in the casting or the target dies before the spell ends, their body is destroyed requiring True Resurrection or a Wish spell to be revived and the spell ends.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Spirit Away
entries: You instantly die, and your spirit rises as a Ghost.

For the duration, your body is protected from decay and can't become Undead. The spell also allows you to be revived by any spell, including Revivify, as your spirit is safely pulled back into the body. Once the spell ends, your spirit form must be put to rest before you can be revived.

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: You can target up to three willing creatures (including yourself) for each spell slot level above 6. The creatures must be within 10 feet of you when you cast the spell.

spell.name: Sympathetic Vibration
entries: You strike a tuning fork and attune yourself to the natural resonance of a freestanding structure you are touching, such as a building, bridge, or dam. If the target is not freestanding, such as part of a larger mass of ground or embedded in surrounding rock, the spell fails and has no effect.
You can choose to end the spell early at any time.
The structure begins to vibrate violently. At the start of each of your turns, the target takes {@dice 2d10} Thunder damage. This damage ignores any damage threshold the structure may have.
This spell has no effect on creatures, including Constructs.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, Made by Laz, Repassed from WM3}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a level 7+ spell slot, the duration increases by 1 minute for each slot above level 6.

spell.name: Vile Blade
entries: This spell draws out and temporarily crystallizes a sword-shaped portion of your soul that springs out from an empty hand. You are proficient with this weapon, and no other creature is proficient with it.

When you cast the spell, you can choose the form of the weapon: either a Shortsword, Longsword, Rapier, Glaive, or Greatsword. The weapon is magical, and you have a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it.
When you make a weapon attack with this weapon, you use your spellcasting ability modifier, instead of Strength or Dexterity, for the attack and damage rolls.
All damage dealt by the weapon is Vile Damage.

The Vile Blade deals an additional {@dice 1d8} damage to creatures with an opposing alignment of the caster, or {@dice 2d8} if both alignments are opposing. If the weapon scores a Critical Hit against a creature, the creature must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failure, a portion of the caster's personality infects the wounded creature. Its alignment changes to match yours, and it is under the effects of a Charm Monster spell. Both effects last for the duration of this spell.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e, Repassed from WM3, Made by Aris}

spell.name: Wages of Sin
entries: You force a creature of Evil alignment within range that you can see to make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the affected creature’s own evil thoughts and impulses blossom in their minds into intense suspicion and distrust, and it attacks the nearest Evil aligned creature on each of its turns. The affected creature does not completely ignore other threats, but attacks Evil aligned creatures in preference to any other foes. An affected creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success.
You must refrain from intoxicants and stimulants for one week before casting this spell.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 6.

spell.name: Wall of Iron

components.m: A small iron sheet plus gold dust worth 50 gp

entries: A nonmagical wall of iron springs into existence at a point you choose within range. You can make the wall up to 100 feet long, 20 feet tall, and 2 inches thick. If the wall is 50 feet or less in length, you can instead make it up to 4 inches thick. The wall is able to pass through objects and terrain, anchoring them together. Additionally, you can choose to anchor the wall to any object or terrain touching it. While an object is anchored to the wall, it cannot be moved.

If the wall cuts through a creature's space when it appears, the creature is pushed to one side of the wall (you choose which side). The wall is a slippery surface requiring a Strength (Athletics) check with a DC equal to your spell save DC to climb.

The wall is an object that can be damaged and thus breached. Each 5-foot-square of the wall has AC 19 and 80 Hit Points per inch of thickness, and it has Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. Reducing a square of the wall to 0 Hit Points destroys it, and unanchors the wall to any object or terrain that square was touching unless it is touching another part of the wall.

If the wall is not anchored to terrain or objects large enough to hold it in place (DM’s discretion), a creature within 5 ft of the wall can take the Attack action to attempt to tip it over its length. The creature must make a DC 30 Strength (Athletics) check. On a success, the wall falls over onto its side away from the creature. All creatures in the area it lands must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a success, it can move up to its Speed out of the area. If it is unable to leave the area, it suffers the effects of failing. On a failure the creature is crushed, taking {@dice 5d6} Bludgeoning damage per inch of thickness and have the Prone condition. Gargantuan creatures are immune to being crushed.
If you maintain your Concentration on this spell for its full duration, the wall becomes permanent and can't be dispelled. Otherwise, the wall disappears when the spell ends.
{@i Adapted from 3.5e by June}

entriesHigherLevel.name: Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot
entries: The wall can be up to 1 inch thicker for each spell slot level above 6.

spell.name: Alter Creature

components.m: At least 5000 gp worth of reagents, which are consumed

entries: You transform a target creature's appearance that is within range for the entire casting time of the spell. The target decides what they look like, including height, weight, facial features, sound of voice, hair length, coloration, and distinguishing characteristics, if any. They can make themselves appear as a member of another species, though none of their statistics change. They also don't appear as a creature of a different size category, and their shape stays the same.
{@i Made by Landon, Repassed from WM3}

spell.name: Barghest's Feast

components.m: A diamond worth 5000 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: Your magic consumes a corpse of a creature you touch with black flames, utterly consuming it. The corpse is destroyed, and thus the target can be revived only by a True Resurrection or a Wish spell. The target's soul also becomes trapped; any magic that tries to restore the soul to life has a 50 percent chance of failing and being wasted.
{@i From 3.5e, Adapted by Aris}

spell.name: Constricting Chains

components.m: a small silver chain worth 10 gp

entries: You call upon the heavenly powers to bind a creature of your choice that you can see in writhing chains of scintillating light. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be Restrained by the chains for the duration. While Restrained by the chains, if the affected creature attempts to cast a spell, it must first succeed on a Constitution saving throw or the spell dissipates, and the Action, Bonus Action or Reaction taken to cast it is wasted.

The chains have 20 AC and 80 Hit Points, have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. Half of the damage dealt to the chains is transferred to the creature Restrained by them.
A creature Restrained by the chains can take an Action to make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell save DC to escape the chains.
{@i From the Book of Exalted Deeds, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Stonemulacrum

components.m: powdered orc stones worth 1,500+ GP, which the spell consumes

entries: I saved the life of a wizard, who gave me this spell. I can create a simulacrum of one Orc that is within 10 feet of me for the entire casting of the spell. I finish the casting by touching both the creature and a pile of ice or snow that is the same size as that creature, and the pile turns into the orc spirit, which is a creature. It the uses the Tough Boss statblock (From the Monster Manual) except it is a Construct.
The spirit is Friendly to me and speaks my languages. It obeys my commands and acts on my turn in combat. The spirit can't gain levels, and it can't take Short or Long Rests.

If the spirit takes damage, the only way to restore its Hit Points is to repair it as I take a Long Rest, during which I expend orc components worth 100 GP per Hit Point restored. The orc spirit must stay within 5 feet of you for the repair.
The spirit lasts until it drops to 0 Hit Points, at which point it reverts to snow and melts away. If you cast this spell again, any orc spirit you created with this spell is instantly destroyed.
{@i Adapated from DnD 5e 2024, Made by Denny}

spell.name: Befoul

components.m: a dead fish and a drop of poison

entries: You make exposed water inside an area you choose that is a Cube up to 800 feet on a side foul and poisonous. Underwater, this effect lightly obscures the area until a strong current clears it away. Water in containers remains corrupted until it evaporates.

A creature that consumes this foul water or swims in it must make a Constitution saving throw. On a successful save, the creature is immune to the foul water for 24 hours. On a failed save, the creature takes {@dice 4d6} Poison damage and is Poisoned for 1 minute. At the end of this time, the Poisoned creature must repeat the saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@dice 4d8} Poison damage and is Poisoned until it finishes a Long Rest.

If you only affect part of a large body of water, the befouled water mixes with the pure water. If the entire body of water is no more than four times the size of the affected area, all the water in the body of water is befouled 24 hours later, but the damage from drinking or swimming in the water is only half the damage dice. If the body of water is more than four times the affected area but less than twenty times the affected area, after 24 hours all of the water tastes foul, but is not poisonous. If the entire body is larger than twenty times the affected area, the fouled water mixes with the clean water and loses all effect after 24 hours.
A strong magical current, such as a Control Water spell, can purify areas the spell affects of the befoulment.
{@i From 3.5e and the Wastrilith 5e statblock, Made by Aris, Repassed from WM3}

spell.name: Evolve

components.m: Organic material from a Humanoid, a miniature tool, and a pearl worth 500+ gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You touch a Beast and cause it to begin to transform into a Humanoid lifeform. This spell has no effect on a creature transformed into a Beast (such as through Shapechange or Polymorph) or a Beast without a CR. A Beast with an Intelligence of 4 or higher can choose to be unaffected by this spell. The Beast is Unconscious for the duration of the spell.

If the spell lasts for its entire duration, then the creature is permanently changed, requiring Wish in order to undo. The target’s creature type becomes Humanoid (Evolved Beast) and its form changes to a Humanoid one retaining its original characteristics (Ex: wings, tails, hooves). No matter the original form, the new one has at least 2 arms and 2 legs. If the creature is Tiny, its size becomes Small. If the creature is Large or larger, its size becomes Medium.

Additionally all of the target’s ability scores increase to 10, unless they are already higher than that. The target gains the ability to speak all languages you know. It gains proficiency in Simple and Martial weapons, and training in Light, Medium, and Heavy armor and Shields.
The evolved target has the Charmed condition for 30 days or until you or your allies deal damage to it. When that condition ends, the evolved creature chooses its attitude toward you.
{@i From 2e. Made by June}

spell.name: Hornung's Random Dispatcher
entries: With the utterance of a few words, this spell can hurl a creature to a random plane of existence. The target creature makes a Charisma saving throw, and it can choose to fail. On a failed save, the creature is transported to a random plane determined by the table below. The exact destination on said plane is determined by the GM. If the random plane is the same as the one the creature is currently on, it is teleported to a random location on the same plane.
{@i Adapted from 2e by Chrys, Repassed from WM3}
{@i ![][image7]}

spell.name: Impenetrability

components.m: A small shield-shaped token made of mithral worth at least 500gp, which the spell consumes.

entries: A creature you touch gains Immunity to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage until the spell ends.
{@i Made by Lily}

spell.name: Crushing Foot of Spite

components.m: A severed foot from a good-aligned humanoid cleric.

entries: A foot composed of clouds or shadows appears 30 feet above the ground and begins smashing down with incredible power. All creatures and objects within a 5-foot radius Cylinder, 30 feet high below the foot must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes {@dice 20d6} Bludgeoning damage, or half on a successful save. On subsequent turns as a Bonus Action, you can move the foot anywhere else in range and immediately smash down. If you do not take this Bonus Action, it smashes down in the same place at the end of your turn.
Airborne creatures in the area are knocked Prone on a failed save and are pushed downward to the ground.
{@i Adapted from the Book of Vile Darkness by Lily, Turned to Feet by June}

spell.name: Elminster's Illusion
entries: You create an image of Elminster within range that lasts for the duration. The illusion also ends if you dismiss it as an action or cast this spell again.
Elminster spends the entire duration with his arms spread to either side and maintains a blank expression for the duration. He will not listen to or obey commands.
The image can't create sound or light, but does smell like old man. Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, because things can pass through it.

If a creature uses its action to examine the sound or image, the creature can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the illusion becomes faint to the creature.
{@i Made by Tyler}

spell.name: Elminster’s Shining Shield

components.m: A dragon’s scale worth at least 1,000gp

entries: A shining light appears around you in a 30-foot Emanation. Any creatures of your choice in this aura are Immune to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.
{@i Made by Lily}

spell.name: Legal Name Change

components.m: A stack of paperwork as tall as the target

entries: You endeavor to officially change the name of a willing creature you touch for the entire casting time, finishing by speaking their new name. This affects the creature's name, but not their magical true name, nor any titles and epithets. Any written mention of the original name is overwritten with the new one, and any memory of their old name is erased. Anyone who knew their old name now knows their new one, which functions as their real name for purposes such as the {@i gate} spell.
{@i Repass from S3, made by Lily.}

spell.name: Planar Navigation

components.m: The helm of a water vehicle, a sextant, and a metal rod worth at least 250 gp, attuned to a particular plane of existence

entries: You attempt to transport a ship no larger than 300ft in any dimension to a different plane of existence. While concentrating on this spell, the only actions you can take is to navigate the ship.

If you Concentrate on the spell for its full duration, you successfully transport yourself, the ship, and all of its contents to a different plane of existence. Any unwilling creature on board must make a Charisma saving throw, or be transported as well. While you cannot specify the specific location, the ship always appears on a body of water large enough for the ship to sit in safely, unless the destination plane has no body of water large enough to hold the ship, in which case the ship simply materializes right side up, lying on the ground. If the destination plane is one that possesses an ocean, the ship automatically appears there.
If your concentration is broken before the full duration of the spell, you leave the helm, or the ship is rendered immobile, you must roll on the mishap table.
{@b Mishap Table ({@dice d10})}
10   - The ship is successfully transported to the plane of your choice, as if you had maintained concentration for the entire duration.
6-9  - Nothing happens.
2-5  - You, the ship, and all of its contents are transported to a random plane of existence. (Chosen by the DM) Unwilling creatures are brought along.
1    - The ship is ripped apart by your magic, causing it to take {@dice 20d10} force damage, and you have to roll again (ignoring a roll of 1).
{@b Spell Lists:} Sorcerer, Wizard
{@i Adapted from the 3.5e spell by June}

spell.name: Timeless Body
entries: You step outside the flow of time and causality. Until the end of your next turn, you cannot affect or be affected by creatures, objects, and effects, even by yourself.
Horrible Entities can still steal you away when your time is up.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Aris}

spell.name: Assemble Allies
entries: {@b Development:} 130,000 GP, 300 hours

When you cast this spell, you can speak the names of up to 10 specific creatures (a pseudonym, title, or nickname doesn't work). If the creature is willing, a portal appears and transports it to a portal that opens within range, as if by the Gate spell.
You gain no special power over the creature, and it is free to act as the DM deems appropriate. It might leave, attack you, or help you.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Nailed to the Sky
entries: Development: 90,000 GP, 216 hours

You make a flicking gesture at a creature or an object within range that weighs up to 1,000 pounds. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target is flung straight up, teleporting through any obstacle, to a location far above the surface of the planet and at such speed that it keeps missing the surface as it falls back. Unless the target can fly, it is in an eternal orbit around the planet.

Depending on the world where this spell is cast, conditions that far above the surface may be deadly. If so, the target takes {@dice 4d6} Fire or Cold damage depending on the target's exposure to the sun at that location and {@dice 1d4} additional damage from the vacuum at the start of its turns. This environment is airless.
If the spell is cast while not on a spherical planetary body or on a plane that lacks gravity, the spell fails.
{@i From the Epic Level Handbook, Adapted by Chrys, Repassed from WM3}

spell.name: Unname
entries: Development: 168 hours, 65,000 GP
With this most powerful of spells, you erase a subject’s truename from existence itself.

You target a creature with more than 3 Intelligence that you know the truename of and must succeed on an Arcana check using your spellcasting ability score, the DC equal to 10 + the CR or level of the target creature (whichever is higher), to correctly speak the truename. On a success, the target creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be obliterated from existence. Likewise, all items being worn or carried by the target creature are erased with it.
{@i From 3.5e, Made by Chrys}

spell.name: Quicken Earth
entries: {@b Development:} 1,000 GP, 4 hours

When you cast this spell, you choose an area of loose earth or stone that you can see that fits within a 40-foot cube and is within range. The area turns into a Level 20 Quicksand Pit (See DMG for the hazard), with a Pit Depth equal to the depth of the cube you transformed.
{@i Made by June}

spell.name: Perfect Parry
entries: Once used, this technique cannot be used again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Adapted from Landon}

spell.name: Prismatic Guy
entries: 3    {@b Yellow.} {@i Failed Save:} {@dice 12d6} Lightning damage. {@i Successful Save:} Half as much damage.
4    {@b Green.} {@i Failed Save:} {@dice 12d6} Poison damage. {@i Successful Save:} Half as much damage.
5    {@b Blue.} {@i Failed Save:} {@dice 12d6} Cold damage. {@i Successful Save:} Half as much damage.

6    {@b Indigo.} {@i Failed Save:} The target has the Restrained condition and makes a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns. If it successfully saves three times, the condition ends. If it fails three times, it has the Petrified condition until it is freed by an effect like the Greater Restoration spell. The successes and failures needn’t be consecutive; keep track of both until the target collects three of a kind.

7    {@b Violet.} {@i Failed Save:} The target has the Blinded condition and makes a Wisdom saving throw at the start of your next turn. On a successful save, the condition ends. On a failed save, the condition ends, and the creature teleports to another plane of existence (DM’s choice).
8    {@b Special.} Reroll on this table.
You can use this technique a number of times equal to half your Spellcasting ability modifier (rounded down), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
{@i Made by Denny}

spell.name: The Doohickey
entries: 3    {@b 5G Waves.} You can take an Influence action against the creature as a free action.

4    {@b Green.} {@i Failed Save:} The creature gains the Green story record and has the Poisoned condition for one minute. At the end of each of their turns they can repeat the saving throw, ending the Poisoned condition on a success. {@i Successful Save:} The creature gains the Green story record for one minute.
5    {@b Corrosive.} {@i Failed Save:} The creature's AC is reduced by an amount equal to your Intelligence modifier until the end of your next turn.

6    {@b Glitter.} {@i Failed Save:} The creature is Blinded and under the effect of the Faerie Fire spell for one minute. At the end of each of their turns they can repeat the saving throw, ending it on a success.
7    {@b Standardized.} The creature is provided enough nourishment for one day and automatically succeeds saving throws against extreme cold and extreme heat for the next 24 hours.
8    {@b Special.} The Doohickey gains an additional feature. Roll twice on this table, rerolling any 8s.

Afterwards, choose a point within 120 feet of you that you can see. Each creature within a 30 foot Emanation centered on the point that you choose must make a Constitution saving throw against your Artificer spell save DC if the rolled effects call for it and consequently suffers the rolled effects.
You must finish a Long Rest before you can use this technique again.
{@i Made by Denny}

spell.name: Unerring Charm
entries: {@i  All creatures are Charmed by you
Once you use this technique, you cannot do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
{@i Made by June}
